name: Student Organizations
description:
Student Organizations in the Office of the Dean of Students provides several services to support to student organizations on campus. Student Organizations offers many services to student organizations, providing information through its publications, training through its workshops, one-on-one consultations and co-hosting events such as UT Marketplace, Party on the Plaza, Student Organization Fairs, and Evening of the Stars.

Student Organizations staff take great pride in our efforts to support The University of Texas at Austin's educational mission. The incredibly diverse range of organizations offer students many exciting opportunities to enrich their educational experiences and develop a variety of skills.

Office Hours

Fall Term Hours 
Monday: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.
Tuesday: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.
Wednesday: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.
Thursday: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.
Friday: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.
Saturday: Closed
Sunday: Closed

When the university is closed for a holiday (example: Labor Day) or an emergency (example: 2022's Winter Storm), our office will be closed. Please email us if you need assistance.


Contact Us

Consultation Scheduling: Please use our Bookings page to schedule a meeting with a staff member. These meetings are conducted via Microsoft Teams.

Phone: 512-471-3065, if we are not able to answer, please leave a voicemail with your name, student organization name, phone number, and request to studentorganizations@austin.utexas.edu please include your name, student organization name, and request.
summary: Student Organizations in the Office of the Dean of Students provides services to support student organizations on campus.

name: Recreational Sports
summary: Serving the campus community since 1916, Recreational Sports promotes physical fitness, healthy habits, and balanced behaviors through an array of recreation facilities, programs, services, special events, and student employment opportunities.

name: Sorority and Fraternity Life
description:
Our mission is to foster responsible leadership and personal development within the sorority and fraternity community through education, coaching, and collaboration with individuals and organizations. We are committed to advancing a supportive environment that strengthens a sense of belonging, and aligns members' actions with their shared values, ensuring a holistic and enriching college experience.

Quick Facts About the Community

As a Community We Value…

Academic Excellence

Leadership Development

Service and Philanthropy

Personal Responsibility

Sisterhood and Brotherhood
summary: Sorority and Fraternity Life within the Office of the Dean of Students provides programs, services and support to the 6000+ members of UT Austin's Sorority and Fraternity Community.

name: Volunteerism and Service-Learning
description:
Volunteerism and Service-Learning is The University of Texas at Austin’s central resource for those interested in service and community engagement throughout the UT and Austin communities. Our mission is to connect the resources of the university to the community to meet community priorities. We're here to help you make an impact while you're here on the forty acres!

VolunteerUT is the home base for anyone on campus looking to volunteer or organize volunteering on behalf of a group. VolunteerUT is an umbrella of resources including volunteer opportunity listings, group presentations and workshops, and recognition programs for those involved in service.

The Community Engagement Collective is the place for you if you're a student looking to be more involved in the community. This large network of students work together to get to know and make a difference in the Austin community. In the Collective you can volunteer regularly, gain logistical and leadership skills by planning service events for others, or travel outside of Texas to learn from other schools and communities how they approach service.

The Project and Alternative Breaks are both programs of the Community Engagement Collective.
summary: Learn Here, Serve Here! You'll find thousands of opportunities to volunteer and to give back to the Austin community through VSL's programs and resources. Volunteer on your own or with your group!

name: Sanger Learning Center
description:
The Sanger Learning Center is UT Austin's free academic support center, serving thousands of Longhorns every year. Whether you're struggling with a tough course or just want to study smarter, we have the resources to help you succeed.

 

We are located in Jester Center (JES) A332, 201 E. 21st St., Austin, TX 78705. Office hours are Monday–Friday, 9 a.m.–5 p.m. Many services, including tutoring, have extended hours.

 

Our services include:

Peer Tutoring – one-on-one and drop-in sessions for a wide range of courses

Academic Coaching – work with a peer coach or learning specialist on study skills, time management, and motivation

Academic Skills Workshops – interactive sessions on test prep, note-taking, and more

Math Refreshers & Reviews – review sessions for algebra, calculus, statistics, and more

Supplemental Instruction – weekly group study sessions for historically challenging courses
summary: Free academic support for all UT Austin students—tutoring, coaching, workshops, and more at the Sanger Learning Center.

name: Student Veteran Center
description: UT Austin's Student Veteran Center is a lounge and meeting space where student veterans can study, socialize and relax between classes. Equipped with Wi-Fi access, a public computer workstation and a flat screen television, the center offers events and programs specifically for student veterans. Come visit us on the fourth floor of the Student Services Building (SSB), 4.472.
summary: UT Austin SVC is a lounge and meeting space where student veterans can study, socialize and relax between classes. Equipped with Wi-Fi access, a public computer workstation and a flat screen television, the center offers events and programs for veterans.

name: Actuarial Science Club
description: The Actuarial Science Club at the University of Texas at Austin was established with the goal of enhancing opportunities for students interested in actuarial science. The club provides resources for these students to aid them in pursuing internships and full time jobs, preparing for actuarial exams, studying for their actuarial classes, and developing a social and professional network.
summary: Enhance opportunities for social interaction for students who are interested in actuarial science. All members gain experience and knowledge of the actuary field, its past, and its future.

name: United Black Collegiate Association
summary:
We are the financial and centralized voice on campus working to eradicate ignorance, equitize disparity, and promote positive change through education, acceptance, motivation, and advocacy for the Black students at UT Austin.

In Peace and Power.

name: AIESEC in Austin
description:
AIESEC is an international, student-run non-profit that operates as a hybrid between a not-for-profit organization and a student organization. Members build professional skills through delivering AIESEC's global exchange programs to UT students and corporations based in Austin. Through developing business skills and understanding the world more, AIESEC develops young leaders who are solution-oriented, self-aware, world-citizens, and who empower others to be the same.

 

Join if you are interested in:

 – Leadership development

 – Networking with professionals globally

 – Making an impact in our community

 – Cross-cultural awareness

 – Gaining practical business skills, such as in B2B and B2C sales, marketing, and finance

 

 

Ready to apply? Apply to join today at aiesecus.org/join (please copy and paste the URL in your browser if the link is not working). Contact vptm-austin@aiesecus.org for more information on membership! Students from all disciplines and majors are welcome.

 

Info Sessions:

– Tuesday 1/20 from 5:30-6:30 PM in Chicano Culture Room of the Texas Union

 – Thursday 1/22 from 5:30-6:30 PM in Chicano Culture Room of the Texas Union

 – Wednesday 1/28 from 5:30-6:30 PM in the African American Culture Room of the Texas Union

 

Or join us at our open LCM!

 – Monday 1/26 from 6-7 PM in room 3.112 in the William Powers Center

 

Believe in Leadership, Make an Impact
summary: We are the world's largest youth-led non-profit organization. Our mission is to provide youth with challenging, cross-cultural experiences so they may activate their leadership through a professional business setting and understand the world better

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society at UT Austin
description:
The Alexander Hamilton Society at UT Austin is the local affiliate of the Alexander Hamilton Society, a nonpartisan, not-for-profit national organization that promotes constructive debate on contemporary issues in foreign, economic and national security policy. Check out the main AHS website or email us at ahsutaustinchapter@gmail.com for more details on who we are and what we do. 
 
Our chapter brings world-renowned speakers to campus several times a year to discuss current issues in foreign policy and national security. These events are free and open to members and non-members alike. Event information will be posted here and on our social media accounts.
 
If you'd like to get more involved though (and we hope you do), you can sign up to be a chapter member. Chapter members are invited to chapter trivia nights and happy hours, get access to member-only career services, are eligible to attend the various conferences and workshops put on by AHS National, and more. Membership is free, although we do ask that members do their best to attend all chapter meetings and events. If you're interested in becoming a member, please (Form to come).
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society at UT Austin is the local chapter of an independent, non-partisan, not-for-profit membership organization that promotes constructive debate on contemporary issues in foreign, economic, and national security policy

name: Duren Residence Hall Council
description: Our purpose as a council is to represent the residents of Duren Hall in all their needs by sponsoring a wide array of social, academic, and self-interest/awareness activities. We will work efficiently to distribute funds equally to all types of hall programming. The council will continuously work to build a strong community within the residence hall, with University Housing and Dining (UHD), with the University Residence Hall Association (URHA) and, ultimately, The University of Texas at Austin.
summary: Our purpose as a council is to represent the residents of Duren Hall in all their needs by sponsoring a wide array of social, academic, self-interest, and self-awareness activities.

name: Bengali Students Association
description:
The Texas Bengali Students Association (BSA) is primarily a social and cultural organization that has been attracting & connecting the students of the Bengali diaspora since 2011 (and even as far back as 1998 in a different iteration/generation!). Since then, Texas BSA has promoted, nurtured, and preserved the rich Bengali culture around campus and within the greater Bengali community in Austin.

 

From philanthropy projects to celebrating genuinely traditional Bengali events, BSA serves as a platform to bring about a sense of unity and cultural awareness among its members. In being a part of this organization, BSA instills positive, culturally rich values amongst its members, offers avenues for leadership, and promotes self-betterment.

 

BSA is not exclusive only to students of Bengali heritage, we welcome everyone! Simply think of BSA as a big family that empowers and supports one another through the expression of Bengali culture and the interlinked histories of Bengali peoples in Bangladesh, West Bengal, Assam, Jharkhand and Tripura.

 

Major events to look forward to are our annual "Tour De Austin"/"Austin Ghure Berano" event where we tour the city of Austin, have a picnic, play games outside and take great pictures together, End-of-Semester Dawaats where we all dress up in cultural clothing and eat biryani and other Bengali foods, Intramural sports, performing dances at "Mock Mehndi", "Basant" and "Pohela Boishakh", and so many more events!



Of note, BSA also contributes to charities and helps to fund important causes such as raising awareness of children literacy, a major issue of the country of which we are culturally representing. Recently in 2019, BSA held samosa sales on campus and volunteered at a charity concert alongside Austin's "Bengali Association of Greater Austin" to raise money for the Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh. BSA also volunteered with UT Austin's "Sustainability Squad" to help sort and recycle waste produced by home football games to ensure a cleaner planet.

 

Follow us on Instagram and reach out to us to be added to our main source of communication, GroupMe!

Official Instagram: @texas.bsa

Official Email: texas.campus.bsa@gmail.com
summary: Texas Bengali Students Association (BSA) is a cultural & social organization that celebrates the rich culture of Bengal at UT Austin.

name: Best Buddies
description:
Best Buddies is a registered service organization that matches UT students with adults in the Austin community with Intellectual and Developmental Disabilities (IDD).  Through one-to-one friendships, fun events, leadership development, and self-advocacy workshops, we strive to empower individuals with IDD to become fully included members of our society. 

 

If you're interested in joining, please fill out this form! This will keep you in the loop.

2025-26 Membership Link
summary: Best Buddies strives to establish a global volunteer movement to create opportunities for one-to-one friendships, leadership development, and integrated employment for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities....

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society
description:
Beta Beta Beta (Tri-Beta) Biological Honor Society (Kappa Upsilon Chapter) is an honor and professional society for students of and interested in the biological sciences. Activities are designed to stimulate interest, scholarly attainment, investigation in the biological sciences, and to promote the dissemination of information and new interpretations among students for the life sciences.

As a nationally recognized honor society, we are heavily involved in research and will help you get involved in research at UT Austin. We aspire to have guest speakers at most meetings related to various fields of biology to inform, inspire, and connect. We have frequent socials, volunteer opportunities, game nights, Princeton Review and Kaplan test prep discounts, and more. If you are interested in joining, please fill out the Membership Application form under "Forms," and come to a meeting! Zoom links or locations will be posted prior to each meeting. Membership is not limited to only biology majors - we welcome all life science majors <3
summary: The purpose of the BBB Biological Honor Society shall be to function as an honor and professional society for students of the biological sciences. We host service, academic and social events and are open to any Undergraduate student interested in Biology

name: Beta Upsilon Chi- Brothers Under Christ
description: Establishing brotherhood and unity among college men, based on the common bond of Christ; service through a variety of activities including, but not limited to the following: service projects, parties, evangelistic outreaches, team events, Bible studies, etc.
summary: Establishing brotherhood and unity among college men, based on the common bond of Christ; service through a variety of activities including, but not limited to the following: service projects, parties, evangelistic outreaches, team events, Bible studie...

name: Creekside Residence Hall Council
description: Represent residents in University Residence Hall Association and raise money to be used in student activities and dorm improvements.
summary: Represent residents in SRH organization and raise money to be used in student activities and dorm improvements....

name: Dean's Scholars Student Association
description: The Dean’s Scholars Student Association is affiliated with the Dean’s Scholars Honors Program within the College of Natural Sciences. Our student organization focuses on preparing motivated students for successful research careers by providing enriching intellectual opportunities and fostering a strong sense of community between members, research faculty, and a vast network of alumni dating back to 1983.
summary: To support students in the Dean's Scholars Honors Program and provide them with the best undergraduate experience, while encouraging scientific engagement and undergraduate research across the university.

name: Dean's Squad (Fake Organization)
description: The Dean’s Squad serves the Office of the Dean of Students and UT Austin community in various capacities, including but not limited to representing DoS at activities/functions and supporting registered student organization leaders in their freedom of speech, expression and assembly.
summary: Put a detailed description of your organization

name: Chi Omega Sorority
description: We are an organization with high standards of scholarship and personnel. We promote friendship, campus activities, vocational goals, and social and civil service.
summary: We are an organization with high standards of scholarship and personnel. We promote friendship, campus activities, vocational goals, and social and civil service....

name: Chi Phi Fraternity
description: The Chi Phi fraternity is a social organization that is dedicated to fostering a supportive environment for men to develop to their greatest potential through college and beyond. We build better men through social and professional development. Our organization places emphasis on our chapter's values of Brotherhood, Authenticity, and Growth to guide our development.
summary: The Chi Phi Fraternity is a social fraternity at The University of Texas that is committed to cultivating the next generation of leaders through academic success, community engagement, and personal growth.

name: Chicano/Hispanic/Latino Law Students' Association
description: Foster communication of a bicultural nature in an effort to bring understanding of legal problems related to the Chicano/Hispanic community; provide common base of understanding for the Chicano/ Hispanic law students and provide them with support in overcoming the challenge of law school; stimulate awareness on the part of the faculty and administration to problems which are unique to the Chicano/Hispanic student and his/her society... (MORE IN FILE)
summary: Foster communication of a bicultural nature in an effort to bring understanding of legal problems related to the Chicano/Hispanic community; provide common base of understanding for the Chicano/ Hispanic law students and provide them with support in ov...

name: China Care
description:
Every year, thousands of Chinese children's lives are placed into overcrowded, under funded orphanages and abandoned because the parents cannot afford to care for more than one child. For children born with disabilities, the outlook is far grimmer.

Our mission is fundraise rehabilitation, nurturing, and educational programs for Chinese orphans. In the past, we have directly contributed to at least one medically fragile child every year and funded lifesaving surgeries for them. After their corrective surgery, the chance for them to be adopted increases significantly. Although we no longer directly fund surgeries for medically needy children, we host many fundraising events, ranging from panhandling, bake sales, and benefit nights to support their needs in the orphanages.
summary: China Care is a non-profit service organization that funds rehabilitation, nurturing, and education programs for orphans in China. We also interact with Austin's Chinese adoptee programs in addition to local volunteering.

name: Chinese Student Association
description:
Chinese Student Association is one of the oldest cultural student organizations on campus at the University of Texas at Austin. Since 1947, we have been dedicated to promoting Chinese culture and helping members find their home on campus. We welcome anyone who is interested in Chinese culture or wants to meet new people. We hope to see you at one of our general meetings or events soon!

 

If you want to stay up to date on all our meetings and activities, join our Discord and follow our Instagram.
summary: CSA is dedicated to promoting Chinese culture and creating a tight-knit community. We host social events, compete in intramural sports, and much more! One of our largest events includes the Mid-Autumn Festival!

name: Christian Legal Society
description: A multi-demoninational organization whose purpose is to provide Christian law students a source of fellowship, social events, Bible Study, and mutual Christian support and prayer. Our mission is to be a space where Christians can share values and community, and carry the Good News through the Law School and beyond.
summary: A multi-demoninational organization whose purpose is to provide Christian law students a source of fellowship, social events, Bible Study, and mutual Christian support and prayer.

name: Graduate Organization for Comparative Literature Students
description: Our purpose is to be a resource for new and continuing students, with our mentor program helping orient our new cohorts to life in Austin both on and off campus. GRACLS meetings are open to the community and an opportunity to create and maintain connections. The annual GRACLS conference fosters interdisciplinarity by engaging with our colleagues in COLA and at other universities. The GRACLS service program, Reading World Literature, allows students to give back to the community through teaching. By this fall, we hope to integrate the program into Comparative Literature curriculum and to make it an official COLA program. GRACLS is constantly working to improve our organization’s efforts, be they related to roundtables on the Qualifying exams, our conference, or social events. Since Comparative Literature is so diverse, the work of the GRACLS organization is imperative to keeping the community together.
summary: The mission of GRACLS (the Graduate Organization for Comparative Literature Students) is to foster a collegial and supportive Comparative Literature community that embraces the diversity of our scholarship.

name: Hispanic Association of Pharmacists
description:
The purpose of the student chapters of HAP is to encourage and promote unity among pharmacy students with common interests, values, and backgrounds. HAP provides a student support network on a social level aimed at achieving academic excellence while balancing a friendly, relaxed, and entertaining atmosphere outside of the classroom. HAP will focus on service projects throughout the year to educate, assist, and support Hispanic culture through health care and community development.

The organization at large strives to promote education services for the Hispanic community. It also provides a network of Hispanic pharmacy professional mentors for students. The organization will develop research methods and maintain documentation to better understand Hispanic use of medicines.

Mission:

To promote unity among pharmacy students with common interest, values, and backgrounds; thus, to support one another and the profession in general.

To strive for excellent scholarship via peer tutoring and counseling concerning academic problems.

To exchange information regarding professional career opportunities and/or postgraduate education.

To conduct fundraising activities to provide scholarship for members and contributions to projects sponsored by the organization.

To organize education programs regarding contemporary political issues affecting the practice of pharmacy and health care delivery systems.

To participate in community services involving health care education.

To assist in the recruitment and retention of qualified students, mainly serving as role models to high school and pre-pharmacy students.

To provide a center for the exchange and/or loaning textbooks, journals, articles, and other professional literature.

To support the College of Pharmacy in every way possible; to promote its academic excellence and public relations.

To celebrate the foregoing objectives as actual accomplishments, some social function should be in order either annually or every semester.
summary: Our organization strives to promote unity among pharmacy students with a common interest. Though we hold the Hispanic culture at our core, we invite all who are interested to join us as we serve our community and promote the practice of pharmacy.

name: Hispanic Business Student Association
description: Established in 1976, The Hispanic Business Student Association is committed to bringing out the best in its members and preparing them for success in the corporate world. Long regarded as one of the top organizations at The University of Texas at Austin, HBSA continues to provide its 250+ members with unparalleled professional and leadership opportunities. Through the numerous programs HBSA hosts throughout the year, including our signature events, Company Night and Scoring Careers, HBSA has helped increase the number of Hispanic professionals entering the workforce. Our consistent hard work has led to many accolades. The most recent accolades include The 2022 McCombs Most Outstanding Organization, Finalists for The 2022 Swing Out's Best Professional Organization and 2021 Most Impactful Event for the 45th Annual Company Night career fair at the McCombs School of Business. Our four pillars include Academics, Community Service, Professionalism, and Family.
summary: HBSA is dedicated to promoting professionalism, academics, community service, and family. Our mission is to provide a medium to enhance ourselves academically and culturally & serve as an information center for scholastic and occupational opportunity

name: Interfraternity Council
description:
The purpose of the Interfraternity Council (IFC) is to act as the governing body for the member organizations of the IFC. In conjunction with this purpose, the IFC also operates as a liaison for the fraternities and provides services to its member fraternities.

IFC is currently composed of 21 Greek organizations:

  1. Alpha Epsilon Pi (A-E-Pi)
  2. Beta Theta Pi (Beta)
  3. Beta Upsilon Chi (BYX)
  4. Chi Phi
  5. Delta Sigma Phi (Delta Sig)
  6. Delta Tau Delta (Delt)
  7. Delta Upsilon (DU)
  8. Kappa Alpha Order (K-A)
  9. Kappa Sigma (Kappa Sig)
  10. Lambda Chi Alpha (Lambda Chi)
  11. Phi Gamma Delta (Fiji)
  12. Phi Kappa Psi (Phi Psi)
  13. Phi Kappa Sigma (Phi Kap)
  14. Phi Kappa Tau (Phi Tau)
  15. Pi Kappa Alpha (Pike)
  16. Sigma Alpha Mu (Sammy)
  17. Sigma Phi Epsilon (Sig Ep)
  18. Tau Kappa Epsilon (Teke)
  19. Theta Chi
  20. Zeta Beta Tau (Z-B-T)
  21. Zeta Psi (Zetes)
summary: The purpose of the Interfraternity Council (IFC) is to act as the governing body for all IFC member fraternities. In conjunction with this purpose, the IFC also operates as the liaison between the fraternities and university administration.

name: International Affairs Society
description:
The International Affairs Society (IAS) is a professional and social organization at the University of Texas at Austin whose mission is to cater to students interested in international relations, global affairs, public service, and cultural interaction. We foster both professional and social relationships between students, academic faculty, and professionals from various fields. Whatever each member’s unique reason for joining the organization, the International Affairs Society provides opportunities to fit each member's interest. Whether hosting a current events discussion, career development workshop, distinguished speaker, or community service, IAS creates a society where students can thrive and become global citizens. Every meeting includes a guest speaker with a different internationally themed topic, ranging from study programs and internships abroad to presentations by international, non-governmental organizations and former intelligence community officials. IAS also hosts several events throughout the year, including the International Affairs Roundtable and memorable socials.

We meet every Monday from 6-7 pm in GSB 2.122!
summary: The International Affairs Society (IAS) is a professional and social organization that caters to students with an interest in international relations, global affairs, public service, and cultural interaction.

name: Longhorn Energy Club
description:
The Longhorn Energy Club (LEC) is the social hub for all things energy at the University of Texas at Austin. Open to undergraduate and graduate students from all university programs, our organization is focused on fostering connections between students and professionals for career development, academic collaborations, and interdisciplinary learning. We believe that the multifaceted nature of our global energy challenges demands cooperation across disciplines and technologies.

Based in the capital of Texas, we operate in close partnership with major stakeholders at the university, in government, and in industry. Our events highlight the leading role of Texas in advancing today’s energy systems and aim to prepare our members with well rounded perspectives on the energy sector, serving as a pipeline for students to become knowledgeable of both energy technology and policy.
summary: The social hub for all things energy at UT Austin. Open to both undergraduate and graduate students from all disciplines.

name: Longhorn Powerlifting
description:
Longhorn Powerlifting is a family that supports each other's goals inside and outside of the weight room. We are a nationally competitive team that promotes strength, academic success, and mental health! We are STUDENT athletes. 

This is a student run organization but we also have amazing coaches that help us succeed. We compete in local meets in the San Antonio and Houston areas in the fall, as well as at Collegiate Nationals in the spring. 

This is a sport in which lifters compete in weight classes within raw and equipped divisions; we value ALL of our lifers and accept beginners. We have a tryout process that is held every Fall to recruit strong minded UT students to join our growing team. 

Check out our website and follow us on our social media!
summary: Longhorn Powerlifting is a family that supports each other's goals inside and outside of the weightroom. We are a nationally competitive team that produces world champions! We promote strength, academic success, and mental health! We are STUDENT athletes

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America
description: As part of a national organization, we seek to facilitate the transition from student to professional via corporate networking events and professional development functions. The student chapter of ALPFA serves to enrich the academic, professional, and social growth of our members. We consider ourselves a family and we cultivate relationships outside the professional setting through participation in community service projects, Intramural sports, and mentorship programs.
summary: ALPFA serves to enrich the academic, professional, and social growth of our members. ALPFA is a national business organization aiming to progress future leaders. Please visit www.utalpfa.com to become involved!

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description: The Alpha Chi Omega Fraternity is devoted to enriching the lives of members through lifetime opportunities of friendship, leadership, learning and service. The main purpose shall be to encourage the spirit of true sisterhood, to develop through personal high moral and mental standards and to advance the appreciation and practice of allied arts among its members.
summary: The Alpha Chi Omega Fraternity is devoted to enriching the lives of members through lifetime opportunities of friendship, leadership, learning and service.

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description:
Founded in 1851, Alpha Delta Pi Sorority is the first secret society for women. The Delta Chapter of Alpha Delta Pi at the University of Texas at Austin was founded in 1906 and is the oldest surviving chapter of ADPi.

ADPi is built on a foundation of sisterhood, leadership, and service, values that continue to shape the experiences of its members today. The Delta Chapter fosters a community of amazing, creative, intelligent, and caring women who reflect the core principles of ADPi in everything they do. From lifelong friendships and unwavering support to opportunities for personal and leadership growth, ADPi provides a home where members feel valued, empowered, and inspired to give back. The chapter’s commitment to service is evident in its dedication to the Ronald McDonald House Charities, embodying the spirit of giving that defines ADPi.

More than just a sorority, ADPi is a lifelong sisterhood where members truly live out the motto, “We Live for Each Other.” The bonds formed within the chapter create a lasting impact, shaping the college experience and beyond. Through shared traditions, leadership opportunities, and meaningful connections, ADPi continues to leave a legacy of love, support, and excellence for generations to come
summary: Founded in 1851, Alpha Delta Pi Sorority is the first secret society for women. Alpha Delta Pi at the University of Texas at Austin was founded in 1906 and is the oldest surviving chapter of ADPi.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta - Pre-Health Honor Society
description:
Alpha Epsilon Delta is the nation's oldest and largest pre-health honor society. The Texas Alpha chapter of the University of Texas at Austin serves to prepare undergraduate students for their pursuit of a career in the health professions. AED offers opportunities for intellectual and professional development, provides a forum for students with common interests, and extends a program of service to benefit both the community and the individual.

Texas AED is also a part of the Healthy Student Organization Program!
summary: Alpha Epsilon Delta is the nation's oldest and largest pre-health honor society. The Texas Alpha chapter of the University of Texas at Austin serves to prepare undergraduate students for future professional school aspirations.

name: Alpha Epsilon Phi
description: The mission of Alpha Epsilon Phi Sorority is to inspire and support exemplary women dedicated to friendship and a lifelong commitment to Alpha Epsilon Phi
summary: To provide unity and sisterhood to all of its members

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi Fraternity
description: Alpha Epsilon Pi, the premier Jewish fraternity at The University of Texas at Austin, was founded to provide opportunities for a young man seeking the best possible college and fraternity experience.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Pi has the tradition of helping build the most successful gentlemen through the ideals of honesty, perseverance, mutual helpfulness, faith, humility, and Jewish values.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated - Delta Xi Chapter
description: To cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women, to maintain a progressive interest in college life and to be of service to all mankind.
summary: To cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women, to maintain a progressive interest in college life and to be of serv...

name: alpha Kappa Delta Phi Sorority, Inc.
description:
ABOUT US
 
NATIONAL HISTORY

alpha Kappa Delta Phi was established at the University of California at Berkeley in the Fall of 1989 and was recognized by the College Panhellenic Association on February 7, 1990. The founding sisters dedicated themselves to establishing a strong and lasting organization which would provide Asian American women the opportunity to participate in the Greek system. Soon after being recognized by the College Panhellenic Association the founding sisters brought together a diverse group of women who shared with them the goals of sisterhood, scholarship, and leadership. These ideals were to later set the foundation for alpha Kappa Delta Phi's purpose.

The sisterhood has since then spread to prominent university campuses, with over 60 chapters across the nation, and 2 chapters in Canada, making us the first and only international Asian-interest sorority. alpha Kappa Delta Phi is the largest and fastest growing Asian-American interest sorority in the nation. It is the sincere wish of the founding sisters and the charter class that alpha Kappa Delta Phi continues to promote Asian Awareness in the community and leadership opportunities for women by providing each woman with the resources, unique sisterly support, and understanding to achieve her goals in life.​

ETA CHAPTER HISTORY

The Eta Chapter of alpha Kappa Delta Phi was founded in the fall of 1993 at the University of Texas at Austin. Since then, alpha Kappa Delta Phi has grown and expanded into one of the most well-established Asian-American interest sororities on campus. The sisters of Eta Chapter of alpha Kappa Delta Phi are among the most active and visible student leaders within the sorority, the campus, and the community.

As a sorority, we work with a variety of other organizations on campus to promote Asian-Awareness, academics, and philanthropy. alpha Kappa Delta Phi is one of the few Asian-interest Greek organizations that make up the Texas Asian Pan-Hellenic Council (TAPC) along with the sisters of Alpha Sigma Rho, Kappa Phi Gamma, Kappa Phi Lambda, Sigma Phi Omega and the brothers of Gamma Beta, Delta Epsilon Psi, Lambda Phi Epsilon, and Omega Phi Gamma.

MISSION STATEMENT    

alpha Kappa Delta Phi Sorority, Inc. fosters the making of successful leaders by assisting and challenging our members to reach their maximum potential. We strive to maintain the highest levels of integrity and innovation in the promotion of sisterhood, philanthropy, scholastic excellence, and Asian-Awareness in the community while creating lifelong relationships.​

VISION STATEMENT    

alpha Kappa Delta Phi Inc. will be the most respected, premier network of innovative female leaders within the community by:

          • Encouraging the growth of our members through cultural diversity and the expression of individuality
          • Empowering our members to become pioneers for philanthropic, cultural, and scholastic programs worldwide
          • Developing strategic collaborations and partnerships
          • Supporting initiatives promoting the sustainability of our organization and its mission
summary: Our mission is to provide sisterhood among women, scholarship, leadership and Asian American Awareness in the University and community.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi - Prof. Bus. Frat.
description: Alpha Kappa Psi is the world's oldest and largest professional co-ed business fraternity spanning across all careers and backgrounds. We pride ourselves on our three pillars: Professional, Philanthropy, and Brotherhood. As a member, you will make your best friends at the University of Texas, while being provided with an incredible opportunity to develop professionally. Additionally, we have a vast network of alumni and brothers who are able to provide guidance, advice, and even job opportunities. While currently around 70% business students, AKPsi is open to all majors.
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is the world's oldest and largest professional co-ed business fraternity spanning across all careers and backgrounds. We pride ourselves on our three pillars: Professional, Philanthropy, and Brotherhood. We are open to all majors.

name: Alpha Lambda Delta Phi Eta Sigma - Freshmen
description: The purpose of the society is to encourage superior scholastic attainment among students in their first year and beyond in institutions of higher education; to promote intelligent living and a continued high standard of learning; and to assist students in recognizing and developing meaningful goals in life.
summary: The purpose of the society shall be to encourage superior scholastic attainment among women and men in their first year in institutions of higher education; to promote intelligent living and a continued high standard of learning.

name: Bilingual Education Student Organization
description:
The purpose of B.E.S.O. (Bilingual Education Student Organization) is to promote the content, the procedure, and the philosophy of bilingual-bicultural education at the University of Texas at Austin. Our organization supports professional development, community service, and continuing education for both colleagues and the community. The BESO program offers students the opportunity to learn, teach, and innovate strategies and activities. We are a community of learners and represent the bilingual education community at UT Austin.

If you are interested in joining BESO as a member in Spring 2025, please fill out the following Interest Form.
summary: The purpose of B.E.S.O. (Bilingual Education Student Organization) is to promote the content, the procedure, and the philosophy of bilingual-bicultural education at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Biomedical Optics Graduates Organization
description:
Registered Student Organization since 2007

Official SPIE Student Chapter at the University of Texas at Austin

Official OSA Student Chapter at the University of Texas at Austin

The Biomedical Optics Graduates Organization (BOGO) aims to provide students in the fields of biomedical optics and biomedical imaging with resources to attain success in both research and professional development. BOGO was started with the purpose of cultivating interest in biomedical optics and imaging research within the Department of Biomedical Engineering. BOGO hopes to provide an inter-departmental platform for graduate students to present their own research, gain exposure to the research of others, and network with fellow students and professors through a regular seminar series and social events. BOGO also provides an opportunity for undergraduate students to interact with graduate students and to learn about the research opportunities available to them.

Update August 2025: 

We are coming back! Following graduations from the Dunn Lab, new officers are taking over and will be re-initiating club activities over the 2025-2026 school year. Please click to join to be updated on events and news.
summary: The Biomedical Optics Graduates Organization (BOGO) aims to provide students in the fields of biomedical optics and biomedical imaging with resources to attain success in both research and professional development.

name: Black Business Student Association
description:
The Black Business Student Association (BBSA) of The University of Texas at Austin is a professional organization that was established to serve African Americans and business-minded students. Founded in October of 2005, BBSA is dedicated to providing academic, professional, and social support to students!


To achieve our goal, we implement a variety of programs that uniquely benefit each of our members. We invite students of all majors, backgrounds, and work experience in order to provide a welcoming and business-oriented environment for everyone interested in business.


The members of BBSA strongly adhere to the principles of achievement, unity, and service. We are a close-knit organization that strives to work together to encourage and support one another.


With corporate partners, BBSA implements worthwhile programs for our members and the university’s student body. We serve our campus and community through both corporate networking and social networking events, as well as various educational programs catering to professional development.
summary: The Black Business Student Association is a professional organization established to serve Black and business-minded students. BBSA aims to unite our members under the common goal of serving the African-American community through various initiatives.

name: Black Health Professions Organization- MAPS Chapter
description:
The purpose of the Black Health Professions Organization is to foster an environment that supports minority undergraduates in their efforts to enter into a healthcare related professional school. BHPO works to connect students with resources including networking, shadowing, volunteering, and test prep. Though we are not a social organization, members benefit from a close-knit group that uplifts one another beyond professional development and engages with the local community to improve health outcomes.

summary: Uplifting future health professionals and engaging with the community. Meetings every other Wednesday!

name: Delta Delta Delta
description: To develop womanly character through leadership and scholarship.
summary: To develop womanly character through leadership and scholarship....

name: Delta Gamma
description:
Delta Gamma became a part of the University of Texas in 1939. We strive to bring exceptional young women into a meaningful sisterhood that serves as a home away from home.

We have a vibrant, strong chapter, recognized both nationally and locally as a significant asset to our community. Our established philanthropy is Service for Sight, and we are so lucky to be able to serve the students at the Texas School for the Blind and Visually Impaired (TSBVI) here in Austin. Hands-on service allows our members to cultivate relationships with the students as well as each other, and many of our sisters find themselves volunteering at the school, beyond their requirement, as mentors or tutors. We are also passionate about our partnership with Guide Dogs of Texas. In addition to supporting them financially, we aid them in their fundraisers and community outreach efforts. 

We also have unique events on campus to raise money for the Delta Gamma Foundation. Each year we host a synchronized swimming competition called Anchor Splash, a concert called Anchor Bash, and a basketball tournament called Anchor Slam to benefit the blind and visually impaired. We also host a biennial lectureship series on The University of Texas' campus, hosting speakers to come and talk about values and ethics for no admission fee.
summary: NPC - Delta Gamma is a women's organization built on a foundation of fostering high ideals of friendship, promoting educational and cultural interests, a true sense of social responsibility and developing the best qualities of character in it's members.

name: Delta Phi Omega Sorority, Incorporated
description: Membership in our organization provides a lifetime of friendships, numerous opportunities for personal and professional growth, and an unforgettable collegiate experience that you can choose to continue after graduation, upon becoming an alumna.
summary: In addition to promoting sisterhood and unity, our mission is to fuse the cultures of east and west through community service and social service projects....

name: Delta Sigma Phi Fraternity
description: Delta Sigma Phi Fraternity is a non-hazing organization devoted to the cultivation of a cohesive brotherhood organized around ideals designed to promote leadership, scholarship, and loyalty.
summary: Delta Sigma Phi Fraternity is a non-hazing organization devoted to the cultivation of a cohesive brotherhood organized around ideals designed to promote leadership, scholarship, and loyalty.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description:
Delta Sigma Pi is a professional fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities; to encourage scholarship, social activity and the association of students for their mutual advancement by research and practice; to promote closer affiliation between the commercial world and students of commerce, and to further a higher standard of commercial ethics and culture and the civic and commercial welfare of the community.

For more information, please visit our website at https://www.jointexasdsp.com/

If you have any recruitment-related questions, please contact our Senior Vice President: dsp-svp@texasdsp.org 

Please note that in order to join Delta Sigma Pi, you must apply through our recruitment process (more info on our website). Unsolicited Membership Requests will not be accepted.
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is a professional fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities; to encourage scholarship, social activity and the association of students for their mutual advancement by research and practice; to promote closer af..

name: Gamma Beta Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Gamma Beta is one of the premier Asian-interest fraternities established at The University of Texas at Austin. We proudly enter our 20th year of excellence, leadership, lifelong friendships, and service to our community. Even after a decade, our ideals remain firm: Brotherhood, Morality, Discipline, Responsibility, and High Academic Standards. Termed the “Gentlemen” amongst the Asian Greeks, GBs excel academically, socially, culturally, and professionally. The experience of being a part of Gamma Beta is unlike anything that you can imagine. If you decide to become a part of our family, you will grow to become a Gamma Beta Gentleman; one who is mature, confident, and excels at all aspects of life.

The Gamma Beta Fraternity was created in the Spring of 2000 at the University of Texas at Austin. The original nine founders intended to incorporate the culture of the Asian community and the values and morals of a family to create an unparalleled brotherhood. However, due to complications with the University of Texas, Gamma Beta began as a student organization. After many long nights of planning, the initial ideas of brotherhood and unity changed from a dream to reality. With the dedication and commitment of these nine founders, the Gamma Beta Fraternity was officially established in the Summer of 2000. Gamma Beta had their first pledge class in the Fall of 2000, and with the Alpha class, began a tradition of brotherhood and unity that has been and will be passed on to all future brothers.
summary: The official purpose of the Gamma Beta Fraternity is to better each member as an individual and as a whole, and all those in contact with Gamma Beta. Through a brotherhood promoting and enforcing morality, discipline, responsibility, an unprecedented...

name: Graduate Association for Germanic-Language Students
description: GAGLS will be a platform for intellectual exchange among UT graduate students interested in Germanic languages, literatures, linguistics and cultures.
summary: GAGLS will be a platform for intellectual exchange among UT graduate students interested in Germanic languages, literatures, linguistics and cultures.

name: Gigglepants Improv Comedy Troupe
description:
Shortly after being founded in the harsh Austin winter of 2003, Gigglepants ascended to greatness to become UT's best (and only) short-form improv comedy troupe. We perform free shows to packed crowds on every other Friday night. Each show is completely made up on the spot, making each one a completely unique (and hilarious) experience. 

 

2025 Auditions to join Gigglepants will be October 2nd, 7th, and 9th (callbacks)! Location will be BUR 116 for the first round of auditions, and BEN 1.126 for callbacks! Updates will be posted on our Instagram! We will have two info sessions with our sister Improv troupe SNAFU on September 8th and the 15th (7 PM, CMA 3.114). Open practices will be held 9/16 and 9/30 in BEN 1.126 @ 7PM. Info sessions and open practices are not required to audition : )
summary:
Gigglepants is a short-form improv comedy troupe on the UT Campus
Every show is made up on the spot and this never-been-done-before comedy is all based off of audience suggestions! We believe in good, clean, fun and we do it all for free!

name: Queer Business Student Association
description:
The Queer Business Student Association is a professional organization that provides students of any major and identity with networking opportunities through corporate interaction and community-building events. Students will learn about career opportunities within varying industries, gain experience talking to professional recruiters, and ultimately work towards ending discrimination in the workplace.

 

QBSA is open to all majors and classifications! Our meetings are every Monday from 6-7 pm!!
summary: The Queer Business Student Association serves to curate an open, professional environment; promoting workplace equality for UT Austin students.

name: Graduate Engineering Council
description: The primary purpose of the GEC is to represent the interests of the graduate engineers at UT Austin. The GEC fulfills this purpose, serving as a liaison between the Office of the Dean of Engineering, the Office of the Dean of Graduate Studies and graduate engineers in the College of Engineering.
summary: The primary purpose of the GEC is to represent the interests of the graduate engineers at UT Austin. The GEC fulfills this purpose, serving as a liaison between the Office of the Dean of Engineering.

name: Graduate Public Affairs Council
description: Serves as the student government for students of the LBJ School of Public Affairs.
summary: Serves the needs of the students of the LBJ School of Public Affairs.

name: Graduate Student Art History Association
description: The Objective of the GSAHA are to encourage and develop relationships between M.A. and Ph.D. candidates, faculty, visiting scholars, alumni, and other professionals in the fields; to provide a forum through which graduates students may openly address issues related to their experience within the Dept. of Art and Art History; and to foster an alliance with other graduate students within the university.
summary: The objectives of GSAHA are to encourage and develop relationships between M.A. and Ph.D. candidates, faculty, visiting scholars, alumni, and other professionals in the fields; to provide a forum through which graduates students may address issues.

name: Graduate Student Assembly
description:
The Graduate Student Assembly provides a range of programming and events for the graduate student community including professional development, academic and departmental preparation workshops, social events. Our activities are meant to create a stronger graduate student community while also working to prepare them for their professional endeavors. Please visit the GSA's website to learn more about how to get involved, whether that be serving as a department representative, attending GSA events and meetings, or even becoming part of the leadership team!

If you would like to stay updated with the GSA, subscribe to out GSA Members ListServ. This is the list which receives updates in real time. Click <here> to subscribe.

For membership inquiries, such as being certified as a department representative or assembly attendance, please reach out to our membership director at GSA@austin.utexas.edu
summary: The Graduate Student Assembly (GSA) is the principal legislative student organization representing graduate students to the University's administration. The GSA meets every other week to discuss items of interest and concern to graduate students.

name: Iranian Students Academic and Cultural Organization
description:
ISACO is a student cultural organization at the University of Texas at Austin dedicated to developing and nurturing the Austin Persian community.

We aim to celebrate the Persian community and diaspora through routine organization events. From Nowruz celebrations to chai socials, we foster a diverse community of students who honor the Persian heritage.

 

ISACO is not associated with any political or religious beliefs and is committed to maintaining its culture of diversity and inclusion.

 

For questions, comments, or concerns please email us at isacotxpersians@gmail.com
summary: ISACO is a student cultural organization at the University of Texas at Austin dedicated to developing and nurturing the Austin Persian community.

name: Ismaili Muslim Students Association
description: Founded in 2010, the Ismaili Muslim Students Association is dedicated to championing pluralism and uplifting quality of life for all students at The University of Texas at Austin, regardless of personal belief or affiliation, by integrating the cosmopolitan ethics of the Ismaili faith in each of our community service, health and fitness, social, and outreach events.
summary: The Ismaili Muslim Students Association serves to build bridges and uplift the quality of life in the community by integrating the cosmopolitan ethics of the Ismaili faith in each of our community service, health and fitness, social, and outreach events.

name: J. Reuben Clark Law Society
description:
Our Mission: "We affirm the strength brought to the law by a lawyer's personal religious conviction. We strive through public service and professional excellence to promote fairness and virtue founded upon the rule of law."

The J. Reuben Clark Law Society has its roots in the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. Law students and friends of all faiths are invited to join with us in fellowship and service. We provide a space for students and their families to gather together in friendship and support.
summary: We affirm the strength brought to the law by a lawyer's personal religious conviction. We strive through public service and professional excellence to promote fairness and virtue founded upon the rule of law.

name: Longhorn Pre-Pharmacy Association
description:
PURPOSE

Our goal is to be an organization that promotes Pharmacy and gives its members deserved membership benefits. We support and actively participate in projects related to the advancement of career in Pharmacy. We keep our members informed about advancements in the Pharmaceutical world, with the hope to instill a deep and lasting pride and understanding of the profession. We promote closer relationships between Pharmacists and other Public Health workers. We care about each one of our members' success, and we want to do our best to help establish them as well-rounded individuals.

 

MEMBERSHIP

Longhorn Pre-Pharmacy Association is open to any University of Texas at Austin student who is interested in pursuing a career in Pharmacy.

 

FUNCTIONS

This organization hosts 6 general meetings per semester. Although meetings are not required, it is recommended that members attend. We invite guest speakers from pharmacy schools and various fields of pharmacy to attend our general meetings. We also host programs that enlighten our members' perspectives on pharmacy such as workshops and luncheons with professors. Additionally, we attend various volunteer, social and fund-raising events throughout the fall and spring semesters, which ensure lasting memories and friendships!

 

DUES

For Fall 2025, dues will be $35.

 

For more information on how to gain points and remain an active member, please visit our website at: https://www.txlppa.com
summary:
We are a 200+ member organization promoting Pre-Pharmacy to students at the University of Texas at Austin. 

Visit our website at: http://www.txlppa.com AND add us on Instagram: @texaslppa

name: The Longhorn Rocketry Association
description:
**Please note, we do not accept requests through Hornslink, so please fill out the application to be considered for a position in LRA.**

The Longhorn Rocketry Association is a student-run organization at the University of Texas which designs, builds, and tests high-power rockets. Through this, we aim to provide hands-on experience with industry-standard design processes, fabrication techniques, and testing and launch procedures.

 

We compete in the Spaceport America Cup, which is the world's largest intercollegiate rocketry competition.

 

More detailed information on us, our projects, and our history can be found on our website: longhornrocketry.org

 

Official LRA Application goes on a semesterly basis. Follow us on Instagram and feel free to join us for our semesterly info sessions!
summary: The Longhorn Rocketry Association is a student-run organization which designs, builds, and tests high-power rockets. We aim to provide hands-on experience with industry-standard design processes, fabrication techniques, and testing and launch procedures.

name: Longhorn Singers
description: Providing “Entertainment in the Finest College Tradition,” Longhorn Singers was founded in 1958 by Dr. Morris Beachy by joining together the men’s and women’s glee clubs of the school of music into one ensemble. He envisioned new group for the university, one that joined singers from all educational backgrounds to promote a sense of Texas pride—both for our school and our state. Now, half a century later, the group has evolved into who we are today: the premier show choir of The University of Texas at Austin.
summary: A co-educational show choir providing UT Austin with entertainment and UT Spirit.

name: Alpha Omega
description:
We are a fellowship of disciples of Jesus Christ, anchored in faith by our increasing knowledge of the grace and truth of Christ and the inspiring Word of God. We want to grow in our love for God and one another, focusing on growing in our faith, establishing a strong community and spreading the gospel to our campuses.

We have a midweek devotional for those wanting to worship, hear a lesson and fellowship!

Devotionals: Wednesdays 7:30 pm 

And for those looking for fun Friday night event, we got you covered!

Friday Event: Fridays 7:30 pm

Additional events and announcements can be found on our Instagram @atxalphaomega !
summary: We are a fellowship of disciples of Jesus Christ, anchored in faith by our increasing knowledge of the grace and truth of Christ and the inspiring Word of God. We want to grow in our love for God and one another, spreading the gospel to our campuses.

name: Alpha Phi
description:
The objectives of our Fraternity are the promotion of growth in character, unity of feeling, sisterly affection, and social communion among our members. In all that we do, we try to obey principles of justice and right. We seek the highest ideal of womanhood, and we try to gain this ideal by cultivating not only the power and passion for seeking intellectual development but, also, the spirit of love and charity. We have banded ourselves together to improve our minds and hearts, and uphold our High Ideals of Membership, which are character, generosity, sisterhood and innovation. 

Panhellenic Page: http://texaspanhellenic.com/new-page-1 

National Website: https://www.texasalphaphi.com
summary: Founded in 1920, the Omega Chapter at The University of Texas at Austin was the first Alpha Phi chapter in Texas. Our members share a commitment to excellence and a strong desire to help one another and be of service to our community.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
description:
• To stimulate the ambition of its members
  • To prepare them for the greatest usefulness in the causes of humanity, freedom, and dignity of the individual
  • To encourage the highest and noblest form of manhood
  • To aid downtrodden humanity in its efforts to achieve higher social, economic and intellectual status
summary: Our purpose is to promote scholarship, brotherhood, fellowship and love for all mankind in a unified effort and therein render service to our community....

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
The Alpha Rho chapter of Alpha Phi Omega is the premier co-ed service organization on the UT campus. Our fraternity is based on the principles of leadership, friendship, and service. We give our members opportunities to volunteer on campus and in the community, create meaningful friendships, and develop personally and professionally through many leadership opportunities! We can also be seen at home football games and many campus ceremonies with our World's Largest Texas Flag opened proudly.

We are an inclusive organization and welcome all who are interested. If you want to serve your community while making great friends, we would love to meet you!
summary: Texas APO is the premier co-ed service organization on the UT campus. We pride ourselves on the principles of leadership, friendship, and service, and have lots of exciting and meaningful opportunities to help the community and make close friends!

name: Alpha Phi Sigma - Pre Health Honors Society
description: Alpha Phi Sigma is a College of Natural Sciences-recognized pre-health honor society that strives to empower students in their pre-health journeys. APS provides many meetings with physician guest speakers, career mentorship guidance, volunteer opportunities, shadowing opportunities, and community contacts in order to prepare the future members of healthcare professions. Providing more than just a gathering place for a diverse pre-health community, Alpha Phi Sigma offers leadership roles for self-growth and experience, discounts for medical school examination prep courses, and more. This organization seeks to aid all students in their unique journey into the world of healthcare.
summary:
Visit our website, texasaps.org, for more details.
Alpha Phi Sigma is a College of Natural Sciences-recognized pre-health honor society that strives to empower students in their pre-health journeys with many opportunities and contacts.

name: Alpha Sigma Rho Sorority
description: The purpose of our sorority is to recognize the necessity of promoting sisterhood and Asian Awareness within the University campus and the community while in the process of pursuing academic excellence as well as interpersonal growth, moral development, and strength in unity.
summary: We recognize the necessity of promoting sisterhood and Asian Awareness within the University campus and the community while in the process of pursuing academic excellence as well as interpersonal growth, moral development and strength in unity.

name: Board of Advocates - Law School
description: The Board of Advocates promotes development of practical skills in oral and written legal advocacy as well as a wide range of activities designed to provide participants realistic experience in trial and appellate advocacy, negotiation, and client contact. The Niemann Cup established by Stanley P & Claudie P. Wilson in 1984, is awarded to the top advocate of each graduating class.
summary: The Board of Advocates promotes development of practical skills in oral and written legal advocacy as well as a wide range of activities designed to provide participants realistic experience in trial and appellate advocacy, negotiation, and client cont..

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Inc. - Epsilon Beta Chapter
description: The purpose of our sisterhood is to impact communities and transform lives through our commitment to public service. While sisterhood is the core of our sorority, we also strive to attain the highest levels of scholastic excellence. The major programs of the sorority are based upon our organizations Five Point Programmatic Thrust; which consists of Economic Development, Educational Development, International Awareness and Involvement, Physical and Mental Health, Political Awareness and Involvement. Ultimately, we are a sisterhood called to serve.
summary: The purpose of our sisterhood is to impact communities and transform lives through our commitment to public service. While sisterhood is the core of our sorority, we also strive to attain the highest levels of scholastic excellence. The major programs...

name: Delta Tau Delta
description: The Gamma Iota chapter of Delta Tau Delta is a fraternity devoted to brotherhood, growth, and service. It gives members the chance to meet people, share his talents, and form relationships that last a lifetime.
summary: Texas Delt is a fraternity devoted to brotherhood, growth, and service. It gives members the chance to meet people, share his talents, and form relationships that last a lifetime. Delt also provides opportunities to members to make an impact on others.

name: American Marketing Association, UT Collegiate Chapter
description:
Texas AMA is the UT Austin chapter of the national American Marketing Association, from which our paid chapter members may take advantage of our organization’s student resources, events, and development programs, such as our weekly speaker series, professional workshops, FCOM/Agency projects, and the AMA National Conference in New Orleans. All majors are welcome to join AMA!

As a current McCombs student organization, Texas AMA ultimately is the premier space on the UT campus in which interested students may learn about how to market themselves “remarkably” and prepare for a profession in the marketing world. Our general meetings are our “Speaker Series” on Tuesdays from 6-7 pm, in which we host a variety of marketing professionals across industries to share their own experiences and advice about preparing for a marketing-oriented profession. In addition to these networking opportunities, we strive to provide our members with opportunities to obtain real hands-on marketing experience through our FCOM program and Consulting Agency. FCOM is an introductory program for new members, most especially freshmen, to gain an early, thorough introduction to Marketing Consulting. Members apply to the FCOM semesterly program in the fall semester to conduct semester-long marketing consulting projects collaborating with fellow student organizations. Our most popular program is the Consulting Agency - an 8-9 month-long program starting in the fall semester, in which accepted members work in teams assigned to real company clients who provide each of them with a marketing task to solve. For the duration of the program, agency teams prepare a marketing campaign project for their clients, which they present for competitive judging in the spring semester at our own Spring Marketing Showcase Conference.
summary: Texas American Marketing Association strives to provide its members with the resources and opportunities to learn how to market themselves “remarkably."

name: American Medical Student Association - Pre-med
description:
Founded in 1996, AMSA UT represents the undergraduate branch of the American Medical Student Association which has chapters in medical schools all across the country. We serve as a link between students interested in medicine and practicing physicians from various specialties. AMSA maintains and offers to its members an important social network that has experience with applying to medical school, preparing for the MCAT, and taking classes.

At our general meetings, we host professional speakers such as physicians from a range of specialties, admissions personnel from medical schools, and a variety of other health-related professionals.

Our organization also offers several volunteering, social, and development opportunities throughout the year:

We volunteer with local organizations such as Austin Speech Labs, Aids Services of Austin, Central Texas Food Bank, and local churches and shelters. We also partner with large organizations in events like Lights Out for Lymphoma and Austin Marathon. Volunteerism is an integral aspect of community and health advocacy, and AMSA is committed to providing members with these meaningful experiences.

AMSA also acts as a support network and family for college students, and our welcoming community is unmatched. We host casual events such as movie nights, game nights, cooking and craft socials, camping, holiday mixers, and more to foster member interaction, as well as larger celebrations such as banquet and boat party to reward students for all their hard work and accomplishments. Students have the option of joining "FAMSA," our unique mentorship program that offers mentees additional resources and friendships that last a lifetime.

Lastly, we offer planning, interview, and application workshops for our members so that they can effectively plan out their undergraduate years in preparation for medical school, sharpen their interviewing/conversational skills, and work towards achieving their dream as a physician. Our local and national partnerships like Kaplan, the Princeton Review, Jack Westin, and MedSchoolCoach offer all the scholarships and resources needed to help members succeed.

AMSA has a long history of success in its missions of health advocacy, education, and community. Here at UT, we bring those pillars to life in a big, Texas-sized way! Hook 'em!

 

*UT recognized Healthy Student Organization Program (HSOP) participant

*Participant of Pillars of Forty Acres program
summary: Founded in 1996, AMSA at UT represents the national organization that has chapters in college and medical schools across the nation. We are a one-stop shop for all the resources needed for pre-medical students. Check out our website and social media!

name: American Red Cross Club at The University of Texas at Austin
description:
Join our Slack and newsletter for upcoming events!

Welcome Red Crossers and prospective members! As a branch of the American Red Cross of Central Texas, we proudly serve the UT and Austin community with a warm heart and open arms. The American Red Cross Club at UT Austin aims to support and comfort those suffering in our community, both local and abroad, in the face of crisis while promoting the education of our peers to promote their personal well-being and awareness with the ultimate goal of inspiring more people to join us in community service.

 

For more information, check out our website: http://texasarc.weebly.com

Check out our past meetings and Slack:https://linktr.ee/texasredcrossclub

 

Info Session: January 26th, 2026

Meetings every other Monday from 6:00pm - 7:00pm
summary: The American Red Cross prevents and alleviates human suffering in the face of emergencies by mobilizing the power of volunteers and the generosity of donors.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description:
Since 1921, the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) at the University of Texas has been a perfect compliment to and integral part of the educational experience and career preparation for civil engineering students here at the University. ASCE student members have a greater understanding of their career interests and opportunities by the council and advice they receive at our chapter meetings from industry professionals.

Members that get involved gain technical experience from the Concrete Canoe Competition and various case competitions. They gain leadership experience through responsibilities within the society. They create a closer camaraderie to those they are in class with establishing lifelong professional and personal friendships. Many members take the opportunity to serve and volunteer for the community through countless charities and events that UT ASCE provides.

See why we are considered a flagship ASCE student chapter!
summary: Since 1921, the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) at the University of Texas has been a perfect compliment to and integral part of the educational experience and career preparation for civil engineering students here at the University.

name: Engineers for a Sustainable World
description:
Our chapter pursues many activities focused on the promotion of sustainability. Our members run multiple technical projects at any given time, including Aquaponics, Solar Power Smoothie Cart, Stormwater Filtration, Glass Recycling, and Ecobots. We regularly attend various events around campus where we educate others on the benefits of sustainability, and we volunteer for activities like Girl Day and Longhorns Don't Litter's Waller Creek Cleanups. We provide our members with a framework to pursue their sustainable goals!

VISION
A world in which engineering fosters environmental, social, and economic sustainability
​to improve both the quality of life and the condition of our planet.

MISSION
ESW encourages engineers and non-engineers to participate in creative sustainability projects, attend educational trainings, and build collaborative partnerships across universities and within our communities.

 

Sign up for our NEWSLETTER!!:

https://us4.list-manage.com/subscribe?u=dd194091e6b319f87ac759091&id=bacc972e04

For updated information, go here:

http://www.eswut.com/
Instagram @ESWUT

Join the slack channel where we talk and post about upcoming events: 

http://esw-utexas.slack.com (join with your utexas email)

ESW is a proud participant in UT's Healthy Student Organization Program and has been recognized as a gold org for 2017-2018, 2018-2019, and 2021-2022, 2022-2023.
summary:
ESW mobilizes students through education, technical projects, and collaborative action to impact local and global sustainability challenges.

For updated information, go here:

http://www.eswut.com/
Instagram @ESWUT

name: Environmental Law Society
description: The Environmental Law Society is a social and professional student organization at The University of Texas School of Law that coordinates and brings together environmentally-minded students for educational, volunteer, career-oriented and social events. ELS provides UT students with networking events, an environmental job database, and its members' various experiences in order to assist fellow members in pursuing environmental career opportunities both during and after law school. ELS also sponsors hiking and camping trips, happy hours, and speakers in an effort to provide a better Law School experience for its members.
summary: To promote awareness of issues in environmental law.

name: Epic Movement
description:
We are a missional movement that desires to know Jesus and to make His name known. We are committed to providing an environment and community that allows for the exploration, application, communication of the Christian faith, especially in the context of the Asian American culture.

Our vision is to see movements everywhere so that everyone knows someone who truly follows Christ.
summary: We are a missional movement that desires to know Jesus & to make His name known. We're committed to providing an environment and community that allows for the exploration, experience, & commitment to Christianity, especially in the Asian American context

name: Texas Canterbury at The University of Texas at Austin
description:
The Episcopal Student Center of Austin seeks to love and serve Christ in all people, by living into our core values as a community:

  • to be open, inclusive, and affirming

  • to cultivate a sense of home

  • to pray together regularly in the Episcopal tradition

  • to create a safe space to wrestle deeply with the “big questions”

 

Student service Sunday at 6:30 PM in All Saints' Chapel on *27th and University Ave, (free) dinner to follow.

* Just by Kinsolving Hall!

 

Centered small group discussion and (free) dinner, Wednesdays at 6:30 PM in the Student Center.

 

If you are interested, please stop by at any of these times or shoot me an email at a_varghese1@outlook.com. Thanks! God bless, and have a great day!
summary:
Students inviting other students to fellowship within the Episcopal church.

Come as you are, all are welcome.
