name: Graduate Student Executive Committee
description: To foster a community and represent the interests of the graduate students in the department of geological sciences.
summary: To represent the interests of the graduate students in the department of geological sciences.

name: University of Texas Guild of Carillonneurs
description: The Guild maintains a regular ringing schedule of the Kniker Carillon during the academic year, furthers the musical education of its members through performance and instruction, performs at official functions of the university, and encourages general interest in the Carillon at the University of Texas.
summary: We ring the Tower bells!

name: Habitat for Humanity Campus Chapter
description:
Interested in joining? Send a message to uthabitatforhumanity@gmail.com and we can put you on the list to receive our email newsletter! Follow our Instagram @utexas.habitat or check out our website at uthabitat.org for information on meetings and events *instead of Hornslink*!

Habitat For Humanity launched at The University of Texas in 1989. We are a student-led organization dedicated to supporting the mission of Habitat for Humanity International. We do this in four ways:

Service: Through our partnership with Austin Habitat, we participate in regular workdays to build, decent, affordable housing for families that qualify.

Advocacy: We strive to increase awareness of the problem of substandard housing and to call people to action to initiate change.

Fundraising: We consistently work toward sponsorship of a Habitat home here in Austin, and donate funds to Habitat for Humanity International.

Education: By raising awareness on campus and in the Austin community, we strive to promote the Habitat mission of eliminating poverty housing.

 

Our ultimate goal is to eliminate poverty housing nationally and globally. Our group maintains a focus on volunteerism and fundraising, helping to buy the materials needed to build a house and providing the labor necessary to put it together.
summary: Our organization helps provide affordable housing for low-income families while raising awareness of the lack of affordable housing in Central Texas. Our members love to attend builds, work alongside future homeowners, and fundraise for this mission.

name: Japanese Association
description:
Our goal is to create such opportunities for all on campus who are interested in Japanese culture to come together through various social activities in order to teach, spread, and increase awareness of Japanese Culture. We also host annual festivals such as Golden Week to recreate the authentic matsuri vibe in Japan. People of all backgrounds are welcome; you do not need to be Japanese or take any Japanese courses :)

Please checkout our linktree for socials and more information!

Linktree: JA Linktree

Facebook Page: Visit Our Page
summary: To create such opportunities for all on campus who are interested in Japanese culture to come together through various social activities (from tea ceremonies to taiko drumming) in order to teach, spread and increase awareness of Japanese culture.

name: Jester East Residence Hall Council
description: Jester East RHC was established to promote community, embrace diversity, and foster positive experiences for the residents of Jester East through interactive and stimulating social and cultural programs and events. These programs and events cater to the residents’ needs, inform them about various on-campus programs, and strive to make their time in Jester East interactive, educational, and fulfilling.
summary: Jester East RHC was established to promote community, embrace diversity, and foster positive experiences for the residents of Jester East through interactive and stimulating social and cultural programs and events.

name: Jester West Residence Hall Council
description: To enhance the overall experience for every student living in the Jester West Residence Hall.
summary: To enhance the overall experience for every student living in the Jester West Residence Hall.

name: Jewish Legal Society
description: The Jewish Legal Society strives to create a Jewish community within the law school. Engaging students and faculty members, JLS plans a variety of events, including student/faculty dinners, happy hours, and holiday celebrations. In addition, JLS co-sponsors events with other Jewish graduate student groups on campus and Jewish young professional groups in Austin.
summary: The Jewish Legal Society strives to create a Jewish community within the law school. Engaging students and faculty members, JLS plans a variety of events, including student/faculty dinners, happy hours, and holiday celebrations.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc.
description: To inspire service in the public interest, to assist in the aims and purposes of colleges and universities, to promote the spiritual, moral and intellectual welfare of members.
summary: To inspire service in the public interest, to assist in the aims and purposes of colleges and universities, to promote the spiritual, moral and intellectual welfare of members....

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description:
Founded in 1870 at Indiana Asbury (now DePauw University) in Greencastle, Indiana, Kappa Alpha Theta is the first Greek-letter Fraternity known among women. In 1904, Alpha Theta Chapter at the University of Texas became the second Theta chapter in the South. We stand for sisterhood, philanthropy, character and academic excellence.

Since 1989, Kappa Alpha Theta has supported the advocacy work of CASA for Children, our international philanthropy. We share CASA’s belief that all children have the fundamental right to be safe, and to be treated with dignity and respect. Court Appointed Special Advocates—CASA volunteers—work with children who have been removed from the home due to abuse or neglect. A CASA volunteer provides a voice for the child in the courtroom and the community.
summary: In 1904, Alpha Theta Chapter at the University of Texas became the second Theta chapter in the South. We stand for sisterhood, philanthropy, character, and academic excellence.

name: Kappa Delta
description:
My name is Claire Pittman and I am so honored to be the 2024-2025 Kappa Delta President at the University of Texas. I have been so lucky to call KD my home for the past three years of college and the women in our chapter have shaped my life in countless ways.

Here at Sigma Epsilon, we pride ourselves on our sisterhood, philanthropy, leadership and academics. We are currently recognized by National Kappa Delta as a Merit Chapter, which means that we are in the top 10% of all 169 Kappa Delta chapters in the nation. I am proud to say that last year we raised over $50,000 for our philanthropies: Prevent Child Abuse America and the Center for Child Protection in Austin. We also get the unique opportunity to provide hands on volunteer work with local Girl Scout troops, inviting the girls to the Kappa Delta house and helping them earn badges.

We encourage our members not only to be involved in Kappa Delta, but also our community by joining outside organizations and holding leadership positions on campus. Just a few of the many outside organizations that Kappa Deltas are involved in are Texas Orange Jackets, Student Government, Texas 4000, Spark Magazine, and many other pre-professional and service based organizations. All in all, we want our members to pursue their unique passions, and we support them in doing so.

All of our members are encouraged to live by our open motto, “Let us strive for that which is Honorable, Beautiful, and Highest.” Our motto is what makes our sisterhood truly loving and encouraging. We strive to instill confidence in each other and everyone around us through everything we do. This sisterhood has given me endless support, countless memories, and the strength to grow into the person I am today. I am incredibly thankful for the impact Kappa Delta has had on my life and I will never forget it. If you are planning on going through recruitment this fall, be sure to check out our recruitment page for more information!

 

Hook 'em!

-Claire Pittman
summary: Here at Sigma Epsilon, we pride ourselves in sisterhood, philanthropy, leadership, and academics. We are currently recognized by Kappa Delta Nationals as a Merit Chapter, which means that we are in the top 10% of all 162 Kappa Delta chapters nationally.

name: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Inc. is a national sorority that fosters professional development, academics and lifelong learning, and higher education degree attainment; an organization dedicated to community service for institutions of higher education and the community with an emphasis on the Hispanic/Latinx population.

 

Follow us on our social media platforms!

Instagram: @texaskdchi

Twitter: @Texaskdchi

Facebook: @KDChi Pi
summary: The Purpose of Kappa Delta Chi is to promote the values of Unity, Honesty, Integrity and Leadership among women at colleges and universities....

name: Kappa Epsilon - Pharmacy
description: Students in College of Pharmacy. Strives to promote professional as well as individual achievement; establish lines of cooperation and communication between faculty and students; promotes professional consciousness and a bond of lasting fellowship between its members.
summary: Students in College of Pharmacy. KE strives to promote professional as well as individual achievement; establish lines of cooperation and communication between faculty and students; promotes professional consciousness and a bond of lasting fellowship.

name: Kappa Kappa Gamma
description: To promote leadership, scholarship and friendship to women who attend the University.
summary: Kappa Kappa Gamma is an all women's sorority organization located at 2001 University Avenue and founded in 1902. Our goals are to promote leadership, scholarship and friendship to women who attend the University of Texas.

name: Longhorn Wrestling
description: Longhorn Wrestling is a co-ed, student-run sports team on the Forty Acres that focuses on leadership, discipline, and perseverance. We train wrestlers of all skills levels by practicing for two hours Monday through Thursday each week. Our season starts in October and we compete in National Collegiate Wrestling Association (NCWA) tournaments across Texas until March. The NCWA National Championships are hosted at the beginning of March each year. We have both competitive and non-competitive memberships, both of which enjoy access to all of our practices, workouts, & training.
summary: Men's and Women's collegiate Wrestling team at the University of Texas.

name: Alpha Xi Delta
description: To foster the ideas of sisterhood, lifelong friendship, and service making a common bond for a lifetime.
summary: To foster the ideas of sisterhood, lifelong friendship, and service making a common bond for a lifetime.

name: American Association of Drilling Engineers
description: The American Association of Drilling Engineers (AADE) at The University of Texas at Austin is dedicated to advancing education in drilling engineering and fostering technical knowledge related to the petroleum industry.
summary: The dissemination of knowledge of the theory and practice of all aspects of drilling engineering and the furtherance of the professional development of the student member....

name: American Association of Pharmaceutical Scientists at the University of Texas at Austin- Student Chapter
description: To conduct activities that promote the general welfare of graduate students in pharmacy and other scientific disciplines; to encourage and facilitate graduate students communication and interaction amongst themselves and also with various experts in the field; to represent the university community at the national level.
summary: To conduct activities that promote the general welfare of graduate students in pharmacy and other scientific disciplines; to encourage and facilitate graduate students communication and interaction among themselves and also with various experts.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
description:
To further, within its locality, the objectives and programs of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics, Inc., i.e., advancing the arts, sciences, and technology of aeronautics, astronautics and hydronautics.

Interested in joining? The best way to get the most up-to-date information is by joining AIAA's slack!
summary: To further, within its locality, the objectives and programs of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics, Inc., i.e., advancing the arts, sciences, and technology of aeronautics, astronautics and hydronautics....

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
description: Texas AIChE seeks to connect and empower the whole Chemical Engineering community through numerous professional, social, service, and academic events. We aspire to be the hub of Chemical Engineering at UT Austin, providing students with the resources and a community that will allow them to excel during their time at the university and beyond.
summary: Expose Chemical Engineering Students to academic and career opportunities, while also providing a fun social environment for peer networking.

name: American Journal of Criminal Law
description: The American Journal of Criminal Law continues to contribute to over four decades of articles published to promote and encourage improvement in the administration of criminal justice. The Journal has established a respected reputation as one of the nation’s longest running and most prolific legal serial publications, serving as vanguard to top student-edited legal journals devoted to criminal law.
summary: The American Journal of Criminal Law is a student-run legal journal dedicated to publishing articles on criminal law, constitutional law, and related topics.

name: Campus Events + Entertainment
description:
To plan programs that educate, develop, entertain, and enlighten the students, faculty, and staff of The University of Texas at Austin and the surrounding community. To create a sense of community through programming and leadership experiences for the students at UT Austin. To act as a resource for other student organizations. To provide a student voice in the allocation of student fee money for programming and leadership development.

Campus Events and Entertainment consists of several event planning committees and one event funding board. These committees are: Arts & Performances, Films & Media, Mainstage, Special Events, Student Events Funding, Traditions, and Unwind. 

Campus Events + Entertainment hosts over 100 events each year.
summary: To plan programs that educate, develop, entertain, and enlighten the students, faculty, and staff of The University of Texas at Austin and the surrounding community.

name: College of Education Council
description:
The Education Council serves undergraduate students in Applied Learning and Development & Kinesiology and Health Promotion. We make legislative decisions for the College of Education at our bi-weekly meetings. Our members take part in social activities to get to know each other and engage in service projects together. We host other fun events, such as the EdWeek fall festival and movie night, throughout the year.

The mission of the Education Council is to:

1. promote positive student-faculty relations

2. develop a sense of belonging in the College of Education community

3. initiate or assist in the improvement of academic standards through the investigation of problems in curriculum, facilities, teaching excellence and administration

4. foster effective communications with the college administration

5. provide a sounding board for student's suggestions and issues
summary: The Education Council is an official channel for student participation in policy-making and the development of student activities within the college. We connect student orgs with one another and host events to celebrate their work and achievements.

name: EERI (Earthquake Engineering Research Institute)
description: To expose students to state-of-the-art earthquake engineering research and design practices, as well as to provide an additional means of communicating UT research findings to other students and to the industry.
summary: Our goal is to expose students to state-of-the-art earthquake engineering research and design practices, as well as to provide an additional means of communicating UT research findings to other students and to the industry.

name: Electronic Game Developers Society
description:
Love video games? Curious as to how they get made? Then EGaDS is the club for you! We are a group of interdisciplinary game development lovers; we want to give you a peek behind the curtain as to how they are created, and give you the skills and confidence to begin creating your own. Read about the opportunities and events we offer below, then visit the links for more information!

Regular Meetings -- Tune in as numerous gaming professionals stop by the GDC to share their stories of how they made it, and the advice they have for those who are looking to enter into the gaming industry. On top of giving career advice, members are also exposed to various topics concerning the industry. Great chances to learn about diverse aspects of the game development world!

Workshops -- Led by EGaDS officers, students will get hands-on experience in vital tools to game development such as C# scripting for Unity, and Blender. Designed for beginners in mind (but all skill levels can benefit), members will walk away being confident in starting their own projects!

Game Jams -- Throughout the academic year, EGaDS hosts game jams such as the Global Game Jam and the Texas Game Jam where members have the chance to collaborate with their fellow peers and friends to design a game. A fun opportunity to test your skills and gain experience!

Social Events -- Members will have the opportunity to attend socials to meet fellow developers. Professionals such as artists, designers, programmers where you will have the chance to learn about various companies around Austin and the gaming culture here, in addition to networking!

Join our Discord server to stay up to date with EGaDS information and to join our community of student game developers!

https://discord.gg/cqbxwvS

Click this link if you're interested and want more information!

https://linktr.ee/texasegads
summary: To allow students opportunities to network with the gaming industry and to provide a place where they may learn about game development and improve their skills alongside other passionate students.

name: Health Careers Mentorship Program
description:
HCMP is a 100% student-led organization dedicated to teaching mature, motivated pre-medical students about both the clinical and non-clinical aspects of health care. In addition to gaining valuable leadership experiences, selected individuals will develop relationships with health professionals within the Austin community. We provide an environment that fosters intellectual and personal growth, peer support and friendship, and ethical values.

Follow us on Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat and Twitter: @hcmptexas
summary: HCMP is a 100% student-led organization dedicated to teaching mature, motivated pre-medical students about both the clinical and non-clinical aspects of health care.

name: HOSA- Future Health Professionals
description:
Meetings are 7-8pm! The dates are correct on Hornslink, but the time includes set-up and clean-up.

Our mission is to provide a forum for the discussion of health issues, aid students in preparing for professional health school, provide a social environment in which pre-health students can meet other pre-health students, and give our members the opportunity to give back to the community in the form of volunteer activities. TX HOSA offers shadowing, volunteer events, socials, guest speakers from a variety of healthcare professionals, and state and international competitions. 

Join Texas HOSA and get involved in a variety of activities aimed to enable members to attain their fullest potential as a future health professional whilst building meaningful connections! Test your medical knowledge in local, state, and international competitions with the opportunity to win scholarships and awards. Learn about healthcare careers firsthand through in-person and virtual shadowing opportunities. Engage in volunteer opportunities striving to assist the local UT Austin community and global organizations. Build connections with your fellow peers as you attend Boba socials, trips to the Alamo, paddle-boarding adventures, and competitions together! There is truly something for everyone at Texas HOSA.
summary: TX HOSA is a pre-health organization that offers shadowing, volunteer events, socials, guest speakers from a variety of healthcare professionals, and post-secondary competitions at the state and international level.

name: Health Science Honors Council
description:
The University of Texas at Austin Health Science Scholars Program (HSS) recognizes an elite group of students in the College of Natural Sciences who have future goals and aspirations in the healthcare field.

 

HSS provides access to honors courses, enrollment in the nationally recognized Freshman Research Initiative, participation in an extensive array of clinical and research internships, and access to a wide range of merit scholarships specifically designed for health profession students. Over the course of four years, HSS students will participate in faculty-led and health professional-led seminars and workshops designed to explore health-related topics. These opportunities encourage the exploration of different professional schools and career paths. The program also requires students to complete a substantive Capstone Thesis.

 

Since the HSS program is not an academic major, students in the program can be enrolled in any major within the College of Natural Sciences. The program provides dedicated academic advisors to support success in all aspects of the college experience. HSS students are eligible for housing in the Honors Residential Community as well.

 

This website is managed by the Health Science Scholars Council, a peer-elected leadership group within the HSS Program. The council oversees weekly communications, organizes and hosts events, and implements new initiatives.
summary: To represent the interests of and create opportunities for all members of the Health Science Scholars Program at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Her Campus at Texas
description:
Here at Her Campus at Texas, we aim to cultivate a community based on support and empowerment through publishing UT voices, hosting bonding events, and nurturing a collaborative environment of collegiate writers. Staff members will gain valuable life experience in journalism, editing, marketing, event planning, and digital media. Additionally, writers will cover a range of topics that speak to their interests and passions, including (but not limited to) fashion, lifestyle, wellness, and politics. Her Campus at Texas is a gold-level nationally ranked chapter with Her Campus Media (the #1 media site for college women) and aims to showcase Longhorn voices within this international community. To read our content, please visit: https://www.hercampus.com/school/texas
 
Nationally speaking, Her Campus is an online publication for college women by college women predominately. Written entirely by the world's top college journalists – with 10,000+ contributors and counting – HerCampus.com features national Style, Beauty, Health, Love, Life, Career, Entertainment, News, DIY, LGBTQ+, High School, and After College content supplemented by local content from 340+ campus chapters nationwide and in ten countries. In addition, Her Campus offers an email newsletter, The InfluenceHer Collective, Her Conference®, College Fashion Week®, High School Ambassador Program, Her Campus Shop, The Her Campus Guide to College Life, and even more products, programming, tools, and events to fulfill its mission of serving college women across every platform.
 
Her Campus serves as a career launching point for its team of college journalists. Since joining the Her Campus Team, members have been offered jobs and internships with Glamour, Vogue, Buzzfeed, Vanity Fair, Seventeen, Marie Claire, Harper’s Bazaar, People, The Huffington Post, Teen Vogue, InStyle, Esquire, O magazine, MTV, The Washington Post, Digitas, Ogilvy, and many more.
summary: The purpose of Her Campus at Texas is to provide students with an opportunity to work with an online collegiate magazine. We aim to uplift the voices of UT students through community and empowerment.

name: Kappa Kappa Psi
description: To serve and promote the Texas Longhorn Band, to honor outstanding band members, to stimulate campus leadership, to foster a close relationship between college bands, and to provide fellowship for all engaged in college band work.
summary: To serve and promote the Texas Longhorn Band, to honor outstanding bandmembers, to stimulate campus leadership, to foster a close relationship between college bands, and to provide fellowship for all engaged in college band work.

name: Kappa Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc.
description:
We, the Sisters of Kappa Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc., shall create a Sisterhood directed towards the advancement of women. Kappa Phi Gamma aims to instill integrity of character and trust as components of personality. The Sorority shall encourage bonding of women in order to strengthen Sisterhood. We shall render service to the community and the university, thus supporting the betterment and unification of society. The Sisterhood shall develop leadership skills and high academic standards that follow into the professional world. Kappa Phi Gamma shall promote pride of culture, womanhood, and self.​

In 1998, Kappa Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc. became America's first women's Greek-letter organization to promote a South Asian interest. Her roots date back to the Jester basement at the University of Texas at Austin where 27 collegiate women from a myriad of backgrounds collaborated to form what would be the most diverse and strongest sorority of its kind.
summary: In 1998, Kappa Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc. became America's first women's Greek-letter organization to promote a South Asian interest.

name: Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc.
description:
Mission Statement

“We, the sisters of the Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Incorporated, strive to strengthen Sisterhood amongst women by providing Service and promoting Cultural Diversity to our members and our community, thus securing the bond of kinship for posterity. Kappa Phi Lambda exists to further educate individuals about Pan-Asian culture, while keeping our own identities. We aim for academic excellence, keeping in mind the importance of education and learning as the key to strength, awareness, and fulfillment.”

Brief Description

Kappa Phi Lambda was established on March 9, 1995 in Binghamton, NY at Binghamton University. On November 21, 2009, Kappa Phi Lambda was established at The University of Texas at Austin. Kappa Phi Lambda provides an opportunity for young women to be involved on campus through service and cultural events while joining a sisterhood that develops strong bonds and leadership skills. We host events for the benefit of the campus, educating students about the important of cultural diversity and service. 

 

Social Media:

Website: http://www.kpltexas.com/

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/texaskappas/

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/kpltexas/
summary: Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. provides an opportunity for young women to be involved on campus through service and cultural events while joining a sisterhood that develops strong bonds and leadership skills.

name: Kappa Rho Pre-Medical Honors Sorority
description: Kappa Rho is a pre-medical honors sorority focused on providing a close-knit community to those who identify as women and have an interest in medicine. Founded in 2010, our organization offers many opportunities for members to further their interest in medicine, establish important skills as a future health professional, and form lifelong friendships through exciting social events. With unique service projects, opportunities to engage with various physicians, and professional development opportunities, we provide members with diverse avenues to give back to the community and proactively prepare themselves for a career in medicine. If you are interested in joining, be on the lookout for our fall recruitment event details on Instagram: kapparhoaustin and our website: https://kapparhotx.wixsite.com/home
summary: Founded in 2010, Kappa Rho is a pre-medical honors society focused on providing a close-knit community to women who have an interest in medicine.

name: Kappa Sigma Fraternity
description: To promote fellowship, leadership, scholarship, and service among the members.
summary: To promote fellowship, leadership, scholarship, and service among the members.

name: Kinsolving Residence Hall Council
description: To promote interest and involvement in the Kinsolving Residence Hall. Our involvement contributes to a stronger sense of community among residents. KRHC supports RAs, sponsors social and community activities, and coordinates with our parent organization, URHA.
summary: To promote interest and involvement in the Kinsolving Residence Hall. Our involvement contributes to a stronger sense of community among residents. KRHC supports RAs, sponsors social and community activities, and coordinates with our parent organization.

name: Koinonia Christian Fellowship
description:
Koinonia [kȯinəˈnēə] · Greek for "fellowship"

 

Koin fellowship is a group that’s open to all students, that tries to live like the first Christian community in Acts 2, and that goes through college together!


That looks like good food, kayaking on Lake Austin, hanging out, purpose, getting to know Jesus, deep questions about life, supporting one another.
summary: Koinonia is a Christian student organization on campus where students can come to study the Bible and find answers to their questions about God, Jesus, and Christianity.

name: Korean Music Association
description:
We hold meetings throughout the year usually in UTC. There we highlight different genres and topics related to K-Pop and the Korean music & entertainment industry, hold discussions, and play games!

Outside of meetings, we host socials, weekly dance workshops where we teach two different K-Pop dances, and prepare our K-Pop dance team (KM8) for performances on campus!

If you're interested in our org, please don't hesitate to request to join us! We'll love to have you!
summary: Join us to have fun discussing K-Pop/ the Korean music and entertainment industry!

name: Marine Science Club
description:
Ahoy There! We are the Texas Marine Science Club, and we are proud to be the University of Texas at Austin’s premier student organization for all things aquatic. The Marine Science Club is dedicated to introducing and sharing the wonders of the seven seas to all students, regardless of major. Through academic opportunities, professional development workshops, and social events, we aim to provide all aspiring oceanographers with the skills, resources, support, and fish energy needed to further explore and advance themselves in the marine world, whether that be a profession or a passion.

Meetings are biweekly Mondays at 5:00 pm in BIO 12H so come swim on by if you’re not feeling too crabby! We guarantee you’ll have a whale of a good time!

Another key purpose of the club is to inform students about UT's Marine Science Institute in Port Aransas, Texas. To learn more about these unique opportunities, come to a meeting or email the officer board at texasmarinescienceclub@gmail.com!

Check us out on Instagram @txmarinescience !
summary: The Marine Science Club is dedicated to introducing the wonders of the seven seas to all students. We aim to provide aspiring oceanographers with the skills, resources, and support needed to advance themselves in the marine world. Come swim on by!

name: Marine Science Institute Graduate Student Association
description: The University of Texas Marine Science Institute (UTMSI) Graduate Student Association, also known as “The GSA,” is an advocacy group for the graduate students in the Department of Marine Science. The GSA works to improve the graduate school experience of our students through activities, community outreach, professional development, and communication with the administration. The University of Texas at Austin Graduate Student Assembly represents the interests of all UT Austin graduate students at the University level, while we work primarily on concerns specific to Marine Science graduate students with our department's faculty and administration.  For instance, we bring concerns about degree programs and student services to the attention of the administration and have regular meetings to communicate the state-of-the-students to our Director. Furthermore, the GSA is dedicated to incorporating the voice of students into the major decision-making processes at UTMSI including faculty hiring, curriculum design, and strategic planning for long-term goals in this department.
summary: To support and to be of service to all graduate students in residence at the Marine Science Institute at Port Aransas through many varied programs and projects.

name: MBA Soccer Club
summary: MBA Soccer Club

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Board
description: To act as a liaison between ME graduate students and faculty. To aid in professional development of graduate students and enhance the graduate student experience in the Mechanical Engineering Department. For any inquiries, please contact the chair, Isaiah Scott, at iscott@utexas.edu. For more information please visit: http://sites.utexas.edu/megsb/
summary: The Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Board acts as a liaison between ME graduate students and faculty. MEGSB also aids in professional development of graduate students and seeks to enhance the graduate student experience.

name: La Fe (Latino Fellowship)
description:
Latino Fellowship (LaFe) is 1 fellowship of 2 within the InterVarsity Christian Fellowship at UT-Austin. IVUT is a multiethnic community seeking God and responding to him by inviting every corner of the campus to experience His love.

 

InterVarsity LaFe is "A kingdom familia rooted in Christ sending out witnessing communities who declare and demonstrate the Gospel to Latinos on our campus."

Our Website: http://redriver.intervarsity.org/ivut-overview/
National LaFe website: http://www.intervarsity.org/mem/lafe/
summary: InterVarsity LaFe is "A kingdom familia rooted in Christ sending out witnessing communities who declare and demonstrate the Gospel to Latinos on our campus."

name: Lambda Chi Alpha Fraternity
description: To offer the best fraternal experience, focusing on personal character development through leadership, scholarship, community service, and positive social experiences.
summary: To offer the best fraternal experience, focusing on personal character development through leadership, scholarship, community service, and positive social experiences...

name: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority Incorporated
description: The purpose of Lambda Theta Alpha shall be to provide a sisterhood based on unity, love, and respect, in an effort to foster the development of strong leaders who will then provide and practice political, social, and cultural activities. It shall also be the purpose of Lambda Theta Alpha to promote unity through charitable and educational programs, maintain a higher standard of learning, and serve as a voice for all students.
summary: The Gamma Chi Chapter was established on November 20, 2004, at The University of Texas at Austin. Our sisterhood is based on unity, love, and respect. We host and engage in social, cultural, and educational programming.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description:
Make sure to check out our website, utasme.org, for the most updated information! 

Follow us on Instagram: @ut.asme

Check out our photos of our events!

ASME activities include:

  • General Meetings with Company Representatives
  • Lunch n' Learns with Company Representatives
  • Tailgates
  • Intramural Sports - Soccer, Volleyball, Flag Football etc.
  • Wide Variety of Socials from Dancing and Rock Climbing
  • Volunteering Across Austin
  • Fireside Chats with Professors
  • Lab Tours
  • Make-and-takes
  • How-To Code 
  • Professional Networking with ASME National
  • E-WEEK/ Rivals Week/ SEC participation
  • Fall and Spring Banquets
  • Fall Retreat
  • Leadership Opportunities

All ASME activities are FREE with ASME Texas student chapter membership. Most activities (including general meetings, lunch n' learns, and socials) include FREE FOOD! You can get as much out of your membership as you put into it, so come hang out and get to know your fellow MEs! You might even land yourself a job!
summary: The American Society of Mechanical Engineers is committed to aiding students in their personal and professional development, to building unity among students, and to contributing to the surrounding community.

name: Arab Students Association
description:
Mission: To establish a central hub for the youth Arab community at the University of Texas at Austin in which the organization focuses on producing an environment that fosters the Arab culture, history, and identity. The organization will lay its foundation on three primary pillars. The first pillar, education, aims to propagate the presence and awareness of the Arab identity on campus; the second pillar, community, creates a habitat that encourages the Arab community to familiarize themselves with one another and allow for them to aid each in their college journey; the third pillar, philanthropy,  emphasizes giving back to the community through scholarships, fundraising, and volunteering. Through the application of these three pillars, the Arab Student Association will be combating the contemporary Arab stereotypes in America by reframing the context in which our communities function.

Please follow our Instagram to stay updated with our latest events! 

@asa_utaustin
summary: ASA is committed to creating a welcoming community to those who identify as Middle Eastern/North African, but we also openly invite the greater UT population to learn about our unique and diverse culture through potlucks, dabke nights, and more.

name: Architectural Engineering Institute
description:
To enable architectural engineering students to exchange ideas about their future professions. Sociability is developed throughout the semester at meetings and activities allow members to meet and learn to interact effectively. Responsibility is developed through each project and commitment that is undertaken by the members involved. Professionalsim is developed as the student member becomes aware of his or her duties to all around him or her in their social and engineering encounters.

In addition to helping cultivate networks, AEI supplies engineering students with support for academics, opportunities for hands-on experience, and professional development in the engineering industry.

http://aeitexas.wixsite.com/utaei
summary: The Architectural Engineering Institute at UT is a nationally affiliated student organization devoted to providing architectural and civil engineering students with a link of communication to companies.

name: Central Texas Model United Nations
description:
Welcome to our Hornslink page!
 
We invite you to join us on our quest to host the greatest model UN conferences in Texas, and also compete on the most elite MUN team! No Model UN experience is required. While Hornslink is not our primary form of communication, we would love to connect with you another way. You can follow us for updates on Instagram: @texasmun. When we get closer to the beginning of each semester, we publicize more information about information sessions and our application process.
 
 
Thanks and we hope to see you on the Forty Acres!
Hook 'em Horns!
 
 
Central Texas Model United Nations (CTMUN) is a certified 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that hosts multiple events throughout the school year. During the spring, we host the CTMUN conference for around 500 high school and middle school students from across Texas and abroad, and in the fall, we host a large conference for over 900 students. Student "delegates" engage in UN and non-UN (crisis) committees. We also sponsor a traveling MUN team, where UT students attend collegiate-level conferences across the country and the globe.
summary: Central Texas Model UN competes in and hosts Model United Nations Conferences. CTMUN hosts two conferences for high/middle school students, sponsors a traveling MUN team, and fosters a sense of community for all UT students engaged in global affairs.

name: Arnold Air Society John H. Payne Squadron
description:
The organization is aimed at furthering the development of Air Force officers by creating a closer and more efficient relationship within the Air Force officer training program through means of service projects, fundraisers, and social gatherings.

Mission Statement. Detachment 825, AAS Payne Squadron, delivers a rigorous and accelerated training program designed to instill core competencies of the USAF and USSF in AFROTC cadets.

Vision Statement. Develop AAS Actives into disciplined and dedicated subordinate leaders, committed to excellence and the continual development of themselves and their peers across all domains of performance.
summary: The organization is aimed at furthering the development of Air Force officers by creating a closer and more efficient relationship within the Air Force officer training program through means of service projects, fundraisers, and social gatherings.

name: TexASHRAE
shortName: UT Austin Student Chapter of ASHRAE
description: ASHRAE is a building technology society that focuses on building systems, energy efficiency, indoor air quality and sustainability within the industry. ASHRAE's mission is to advance the arts and sciences of heating, ventilating, air conditioning and refrigerating to serve humanity and promote a sustainable world. Student Branch Members have the opportunity to widen their skills and advance their knowledge of the field, attend presentations, go on campus and facility tours, work on real-world projects, and network with area engineering professionals.
summary: ASHRAE is a building technology society that focuses on building systems, energy efficiency, indoor air quality and sustainability within the industry. ASHRAE's mission is to advance the arts and sciences of heating, ventilating, air conditioning and r...

name: Asian American Campus Ministry
description: Asian American Campus Ministry is a Christ-centered community that seeks Godly relationships and lives out an active Gospel.
summary: Asian American Campus Ministry is a community of students who seeks to be a loving community that pursues God curiously, responds actively, and reflects openly.

name: Asian Business Students Association
description:
The Asian Business Students Association shall serve as a center for educational and occupational opportunities within the business community, to foster a mechanism for effectively addressing the needs of Asian business students, and to provide a medium conducive to the desires of Asian students interested in business for higher achievement.

We strive to develop Asian Americans as successful leaders in the professional world. We prepare our members for success in various ways, whether by exposing them to a wide spectrum of industries or by providing resources for becoming effective, well-rounded leaders. Our organization proudly welcomes individuals of many different majors in and outside of McCombs and ethnicities, as our motto is “Not Asian, Not Business, Not a Problem.”
summary: The Asian Business Students Association serves to develop our members into multidimensional leaders in the business world.

name: Asian Pacific American Law Student Association
description:
Asian Pacific American Law Student Association (APALSA) is an academic, professional, and social organization dedicated to promoting the interests, and addressing the concerns, of the Asian Pacific Islander (API) community at the law school. APALSA's goal is to promote greater awareness of API culture and identity both within the legal community, and beyond. APALSA also strives to provide greater opportunities for its student members through mentorship programs, networking events, and sponsored panel discussions. 

APALSA is open to all students of every background.
summary: The Asian Pacific American Law Student Association (APALSA) is an academic, professional, and social organization dedicated to promoting the interests, and addressing the concerns, of the Asian Pacific Islander (API) community.

name: Asian Pharmacy Students Association
description:
Asian Pharmacy Students Association is an organization that provides students with a well-rounded cultural awareness of integrative medicine and the vital role of pharmacists in the community.  Students will learn about complementary and alternative medicine from insightful speakers invited to APSA's meetings.  Membership in APSA means forming lasting friendships as well as having the opportunity to actively serve our community as student pharmacists. As a member of APSA, students, as future pharmacists, will engage in high level interactions with the community in events such as the annual Vietnamese Health Fair, Project Collaboration at UT with other pharmacy organizations, Explore UT, and various Pharmacy Month activities that promote the public’s awareness of how integral pharmacy is in the healthcare field.

Mission Statement: Our mission is to promote Asian cultural awareness, the benefits of complementary and alternative medicine, community involvement and collaboration between students. 

Events: Vietnamese/Chinese/Korean Health Screening, Project Collaborate with other pharmacy organizations, Explore UT, Cultural and Language seminars, Herbal Medication Discussions, and social events
summary: Asian Pharmacy Students Association is an organization that provides students with a well-rounded cultural awareness of integrative medicine and the vital role of pharmacists in the community through volunteering and leadership opportunities.

name: Christian Pharmacists Fellowship International - Student Chapter
description:
Christian Pharmacists Fellowship International (CPFI) is a worldwide ministry of individuals working in all areas of pharmaceutical service and practice. We are an organization that:

  • Provides fellowship among like-minded professionals
  • Challenges and promotes spiritual growth
  • Encourages the advancement of knowledge and ethics in the practice of pharmacy
  • Encourages evangelism and the integration of faith into practice
  • Provides support and opportunities for service in both home and foreign missions
summary: To support each other academically, emotionally and spiritually in our efforts to encourage and help other pharmacy students.

name: Christian Students on Campus
description:
Christian Students on Campus (CSOC UT Austin)
Serving Longhorns since 1973

 

Who We Are

CSOC UT Austin is a Christian campus ministry at the University of Texas at Austin. We love the Lord Jesus and treasure His Word. Our goal is to study the Bible, grow in our faith, and share the gospel on the UT Austin campus.

 

What We Do

A vibrant Christian life starts with a personal relationship with Jesus Christ and being nourished by His Word. Our community exists to help students thrive spiritually throughout their time at UT—and beyond.

 

Weekly & Special Events

  • Bible Studies – small-group fellowship
  • Solid Ground – weekly ministry series
  • Weekend Retreats – time away for fellowship, prayer, and growth

Get Connected

Main site →

Visit CSOC UT Austin online

Instagram →

Follow us on Instagram @csoc_tx

Facebook →

Join the CSOC Facebook community

YouTube →

Watch messages on CSOC’s Youtube channel

X / Twitter →

Find us on X – @csoc_tx

Podcast →

Listen to the CSOC Campus podcast

Mobile app →

Download the CSOC UT Austin app (iOS & Android)

 

Our Mission: To foster a community where students grow their relationship with Christ, deepen their faith, and make an impact for God’s kingdom.

 

Whether you’re seeking answers, fellowship, or a place to serve, Christian Students on Campus invites you to grow in your Christian walk with us during your time at The University of Texas at Austin.
summary: Christian Students on Campus (CSOC UT Austin) is a student organization at the University of Texas at Austin. We have been joyfully serving the UT Austin community since 1973. We love the Lord Jesus and His word and aspire to know the truths of the Bible

name: CHYK West at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
Chinmaya Yuva Kendra (CHYK) is an international intellectual organization focused on developing a philosophical understanding of Hinduism. CHYK consists of fellow Longhorns and has only one requirement for attendees: a genuine curiosity about the applicability of Hinduism in one's daily life. Meetings include meditation time, workshop style activities, and book-guided discussions. Other activities include retreats, seva opportunities, and Balvihar/temple events etc. Attend a meeting to learn more. No commitment or fees required!

Please join us by using the signup form below to be added to our mailing list!

Sign Up Here!
summary:
Chinmaya Yuva Kendra (CHYK) is an international intellectual organization focused on developing a philosophical understanding of Hinduism. Our motto: Harnessing Youth Potential through Dynamic Spirituality

Mailing list: http://eepurl.com/gDgfvD

name: ClockTower Cultural Productions
description:
Our mission is to impact the local and international communities by celebrating the value of cultural and artistic diversity and contributing to the senior community through service and cultural engagement.

Raas Rodeo XIV Playlist
summary: The organization aims to promote and celebrate Indian dance culture throughout the Austin community by organizing competitions to create space for artistic expression and connect individuals through the shared experience of dance.

name: Filipino Students Association
description: Mabuhay! The Filipino Students Association (FSA) is committed to upholding and promoting our four core pillars: culture, sports, dance, and community. We embrace diversity in our membership and hold numerous socials, sports (including e-sports) tournaments, cultural workshops and performances, and modern dance opportunities throughout the academic year. Every spring, our active members compete in the annual GoodPhil competition against other FSAs in Texas, in either sports or performance.
summary: Mabuhay! The Filipino Students Association (FSA) welcomes members of all backgrounds and is committed to upholding and promoting our four core pillars: culture, sports, dance, and community.

name: Fine Arts Council
description: The Fine Arts Council continually seeks to raise the standard of education for College of Fine Arts students at the University of Texas at Austin by facilitating student-faculty interaction, promoting academic excellence, encouraging collaboration between departments, and representing students in matters of college and university-wide concerns.
summary: The Fine Arts Council serves as the official student voice of the College of Fine Arts alongside the Senate of College Councils and Student Government to address academic affairs, student life, and university wide concerns.

name: Hong Kong Students Association
description:
Hong Kong Student Association at the University of Texas at Austin serves to connect students who are interested in Hong Kong/Cantonese culture. Everyone is welcome, and you do not have to be from Hong Kong or Cantonese to join! Every week, we try to host an event for our members, such as general meetings, study socials, meetups on campus, and hangouts around Austin. Make lifelong friends through our Boba Buddies system where we group you up with other members for smaller group hangout sessions. And, at the end of every semester, members gather and eat hot pot together!

For more information about upcoming events, check out our HKSA Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/hksatx/

 

德州大學奧斯汀分校香港學生會是為了所有講廣東話的朋友營造溫暖的學生社區而成立的團體. 我們的活動可以給予一些文化背景類同的學生溝通和促進友誼的平台. 我們亦對香港文化有興趣的朋友表示熱烈的歡迎!!

我們保證依度將會為所有來這裡讀書的粵語朋友提供一個溫馨的家！！
summary: Hong Kong Student Association at the University of Texas at Austin serves the Cantonese-speaking students community as a social group.

name: Honors Business Association
description: The Texas Honors Business Association (HBA) at The University of Texas at Austin serves as the official student organization for the Canfield Business Honors Program at the McCombs School of Business. Since its inception in 1980, HBA has provided academic, professional, social, and philanthropic opportunities for its members. Texas HBA strives to build up the strong sense of community within the Canfield Business Honors Program and to enhance the college experience for all Canfield BHP students.
summary: To promote interaction between honor students as a group, provide a forum for discussion about the honors program and the college in general and represent the honor students in all appropriate functions....

name: Hook 'Em Arts
description:
We bring students together from across UT to nerd out about theatre, music, and the arts! Our mission is to be a point of connection for all on campus arts organizations! In our monthly meetings we will advertise upcoming events from around campus including auditions, socials, and shows. We will also host our own events outside of scheduled meeting times including Trivia Night (with the chance to win tickets to Texas Performing Arts shows), Painting with Bob Ross, and meet and greets with industry professionals! No artistic, music, dance, or theatre experience is required to participate. 

 

We send announcements through Discord, Instagram, and a weekly newsletter. Check out the ways to connect with us here: Linktree

 

Every student who has an interest in Hook 'em Arts may join by regularly attending meetings. No membership fees are required. Active members receive additional perks!

 

Things we do:

  • Meet monthly with other students who love the arts for updates on campus opportunities
  • Connect with other students and arts orgs
  • Receive inside information about student discounts and newly announced shows
  • Plan events for students to get to know the artists better
  • Maintain a space where students can engage with each other within an arts context
  • See and promote performances from across campus
  • Learn about careers in the arts
  • Go backstage
  • Host awesome events
  • Giveaway tickets for TPA events at every meeting
  • Invite students to join the Student Volunteer Usher Program
  • Gain exclusive access to many opportunities within Texas Performing Arts

 

Our goals are to share with UT students the opportunities provided by Texas Performing Arts through marketing, public relations, outreach, and fund raising. We strive to increase attendance, engagement, awareness, and experience in the performing arts.
summary: We bring students together from across UT to nerd out about theatre and enjoy the arts! We are the student voice of Texas Performing Arts, and meet weekly for a variety of fun activities. No artistic or theatre experience required.

name: Hum A Cappella
description:
Hum A Cappella is UT Austin's premier, competitive, South Asian fusion a cappella team. We blend Western and South Asian music styles in unaccompanied arrangements and compete at national, collegiate a cappella competitions. However, we welcome and encourage singers from any background and style of music to join our team!

 

We perform in various events on campus as well as in the broader Austin area. In the spring, we travel across the nation to compete against the best teams in our circuit.

 

Hum has a legacy of being one of the top teams in the nation for our category. Here are some of our accolades from previous years:

 

From our 2024-2025 Competition Season:

2nd Place at Davis Dhwani (Davis, CA)

3rd Place at Awaazein (Dallas, TX)

5th Place at All American Awaaz (Nationals in San Francisco, CA)

 

A Few of our Other Notable Accomplishments:

2nd Place at Davis Dhwani, 2024

1st Place at Sangeet Sagar, 2023

3rd Place at All American Awaaz, 2023

 

Make sure to check out some of our sets from previous years on Youtube, and feel free to reach out to us on Instagram @humacappella !!
summary: Hum A Cappella is UT Austin's premier, competitive, South Asian fusion a cappella team. We blend Western and South Asian music styles in unaccompanied arrangements and compete at national collegiate a cappella competitions.

name: Latin Economics and Business Association
description: The Latin Economics and Business Association is a prominent student organization in the McCombs School of Business. Our mission is to recruit passionate students who are driven and committed to expand their knowledge, foster relationships across Latin American cultures, and develop their professional skills. We do this by offering a range of opportunities of growth through community service, social gatherings, athletic activities, expert led talks, and recruiting events. Our vision is to help shape well-rounded individuals and leaders of tomorrow.
summary: We recruit passionate and driven students who wish to expand their knowledge, develop professional skills and establish life-long relationships among Latin American cultures through talks from leading experts in business, social gatherings & philanthropy

name: The Latino Pan-Hellenic Council
description:
The purpose of the Latino Pan-Hellenic Council is to act as a governing body, which serves to unify the Latino/a/x Fraternal Organizations. In addition to increase awareness, increase interest in and strengthen Latino/a community on campus.

LPHC's Mission: To unify Latina/o/x-based fraternal organizations by increasing awareness, developing interest, and strengthening the Latina/o/x community and culture at The University of Texas at Austin through shared values and common interests.
summary: The purpose of the Latino Pan-Hellenic Council is to act as a governing body, which serves to unify the Latino/a/x Fraternal Organizations. In addition to increase awareness, increase interest in and strengthen Latino/a/x community on campus.

name: Moore-Hill Residence Hall Council
description: As the Moore-Hill RHC we want to foster a welcoming, inclusive, and knowledgeable community, not only in Moore-Hill, but across Waller Creek as well. To achieve this, we work with our RA's and other halls to put on events that allow our residents to make new friends, learn new things, and most of all, have fun.
summary: Moore-Hill RHC focuses on planning events for residents, improving the hall, and advocating for positive changes in housing and dining.

name: Muslim Students' Association
description:
Texas MSA Tenants: 

1) Helping Muslims in Austin carry out Islamic activities in pursuance of Islam as a complete way of life; 2) carrying on religious, social, civic, charitable, literary, athletic, scientific, research and other Islamic activities; 3) strengthening brotherhood and sisterhood among Muslims; and promoting friendly relations and understanding between Muslims and non-Muslims.
summary: Texas MSA provides Muslims at UT an opportunity to participate in Islamic, educational, outreach, service, and social activities while strengthening the sisterhood and brotherhood among Muslims.

name: Phi Chi Theta
description: Phi Chi Theta is a national professional fraternity for students in Business and Economics established in 1924 to promote the cause of higher business education and training for all individuals to foster high ideals for all pursuing careers, to encourage fraternity and cooperation among people preparing for such careers, and to stimulate the spirit of sacrifice and unselfish devotion to the attainment of such ends.
summary: Phi Chi Theta is a national coeducational professional business and economics fraternity founded in 1924 and here at UT we are known as the Delta Mu Chapter. Our three pillars are Professional, Social, and Philanthropy. Learn more at texaspct.com

name: Phi Delta Chi
description: The Lambda Chapter of Phi Delta Chi is a national and professional co-educational fraternity at the University of Texas at Austin College of Pharmacy. Since 1883, Phi Delta Chi has worked to advance the profession of pharmacy and its allied interests, and to foster and promote a fraternal spirit among our Brothers. PDC is a lifelong experience and promotes scholastic, professional, and social growth in its Brothers. Across the nation, Phi Delta Chi has chartered over 60 active chapters and has had over 40,000 cross into our Brotherhood.
summary: The Lambda Chapter of Phi Delta Chi is a national and professional co-educational fraternity at the University of Texas at Austin College of Pharmacy. Since 1883, Phi Delta Chi has worked to advance the profession of pharmacy and its allied interests.

name: Phi Gamma Delta Fraternity
description: To be a fellowship and home away from home for its members. To provide guidance from peers in almost any direction. To provide not only brotherhood on this campus but on a national scale and thereby to broaden members horizons. Performance of community service and charity functions are major activities. GTBO
summary: To be a fellowship and home away from home for its members. To pro- vide guidance from peers in almost any direction. To provide not only brotherhood on this campus but on a national scale and thereby to broaden members horizons. Persistence and GTBO!

name: Phi Kappa Psi Fraternity
description:
The Phi Kappa Psi Creed

I believe that Phi Kappa Psi is a brotherhood of honorable men, courteous and cultured, who pledge throughout their lives to be generous, compassionate, and loyal comrades;
I believe that I am honor bound to strive manfully for intellectual, moral, and spiritual excellence; to help and forgive my Brothers; to discharge promptly all just debts; to give aid and sympathy to all who are less fortunate;

I believe that I am honor bound to strengthen my character and deepen my integrity; to counsel and guide my Brothers who stray from their obligations; to respect and emulate my Brothers who practice moderation in their manners and morals; to be ever mindful that loyalty to my Fraternity should not weaken loyalty to my college, but rather increase devotion to it, to my country, and to my God;

I believe that to all I meet, wherever I go, I represent not only Phi Kappa Psi, but indeed the spirit of all fraternities; thus I must ever conduct myself so as to bring respect and honor not to myself alone, but also to my Fraternity;

To the fulfillment of these beliefs, of these ideals, in the noble perfection of Phi Kappa Psi, I pledge my life and my sacred honor.
summary: The Phi Kappa Psi Fraternity engages men of integrity, further develops their intellect and enhances community involvement.

name: Phi Kappa Sigma
description: The Objects of Phi Kappa Sigma fraternity shall be the promotion of good fellowship and the cultivation of the social virtues among our members; the protection of just rights and the advancement of the best interests, present and future, individual and collective, of all those who shall be associated together as members of this fraternity; the encouragement of good scholarship and the breadth of training for our members; and cooperation in the educational and cultural programs of institutions of higher education in which our chapters are located.
summary: The Objects of Phi Kappa Sigma fraternity shall be the promotion of good fellowship and the cultivation of the social virtues among our members; the protection of just rights and the advancement of the best interests, present and future, individual and...

name: Phi Lambda Sigma - Pharmacy
description: Phi Lambda Sigma is the National Pharmacy Leadership Society. Our chapter, the PSI Chapter, exists to recognize leaders and foster leadership development in the College of Pharmacy and the profession of pharmacy.
summary: Phi Lambda Sigma is the National Pharmacy Leadership Society. Our chapter, the PSI Chapter, exists to recognize leaders and foster leadership development in the College of Pharmacy and the profession of pharmacy.

name: Pre-Veterinary Association
description: We are dedicated to providing members with accurate and current information on the application process, resume-building activities, and general news in veterinary medicine. We also offer resources for personal shadowing and job experience to help you through your journey in getting into veterinary school. Through our organization, you will connect with veterinarians, animal professionals, volunteer organizations, and peers sharing similar long-term goals. Follow our Instagram and join our mailing list for detailed information about events!
summary: We are an organization striving to provide Pre-Veterinary students with information about veterinary school, medicine, and the veterinary career while pursuing volunteer opportunities as an inspired group.

name: Public Interest Law Association
description: The Public Interest Law Association of the University of Texas School of Law aims to create a community of students interested in public interest legal work and support students pursuing public interest legal careers.
summary: Promote public interest law at the University of Texas School of Law.

name: Assault & Flattery
description: A tradition begun in 1953, Assault & Flattery is the musical theater company at the University of Texas School of Law.
summary: A tradition begun in 1953, Assault & Flattery is the musical theater company at the University of Texas School of Law....

name: Association for Computing Machinery
description: The Association for Computing Machinery is a chapter at UT of the national ACM that exists to build a community for technologists of all skill levels and interests. With events such as Corporate Banquet, Gaming Night, and CS101, we cover a variety of topics. ACM aims to cultivate a welcoming community where students develop in their social, academic, and professional lives.
summary: To provide Computer Science students (and students interested in CS) an outlet to make like-minded friends; help students gain a better understanding of CS and excel in CS classes; help students make contacts in industry; and foster a sense of community.

name: Graduate Nursing Student Association
description: The Graduate Nursing Student Association (GNSA) is the official graduate student nursing organization recognized by The University of Texas. All graduate nursing students are automatically members and are encouraged to be active participants. GNSA's stated purpose is to promote the educational and professional development of nurses enrolled in graduate school at The University of Texas at Austin and their welfare to the end to make the educational experience both personally and professionally meaningful. The Association sponsors such activities as research colloquia, publishing seminars, and the Gallman Distinguished Lectureship.
summary: The Official graduate student nursing organization recognized by The University of Texas to promote the educational and professional development of nurses enrolled in graduate school and their welfare for a meaningful personal and professional experience.

name: Collaborative Artists
description:
.•☆. ✎Hello! We are the Collaborative Artists at UT at ATX.˚ ✩ ⋆｡˚

 

✑ WHAT DO WE DO? We hold weekly, in-person, social art meetings at 7 PM CST on Fridays at WEL 2.310 during the school year, online meetings for the summer, and when it's in season, zine work! Yes, we have an official zine every year for members to participate in. Contributors get their physical copies for free and the theme is decided every fall semester.

✑ HOW DO I BECOME A MEMBER? Use this link to join our Discord server:https://discord.gg/waX62FYhpr

This is where all of the members regularly communicate to share art, music recommendations, play video games, and much more! We also post announcements and host our online meetings through this platform. Discord membership is required, in other words. 

There are NO membership fees, major requirements, or attendance count. You are free to come and go whenever at any point in the semester. Though we start at 7 PM, our meetings can last into the deep night so if you feel like you're late, you're probably not! Feel free to drop in :D

✑ WHAT'S YOUR SOCIAL MEDIA? We have an Instagram  but you can best contact us through email or Discord.
summary: We are a group of art enthusiasts from all different majors at UT. Meetings are held weekly and involve fun social art activities like downtown photography tours and gouache painting. Come collaborate with us!

name: College at the Austin Stone
description: To make the supremacy of the name and purpose of Jesus Christ known at the University of Texas.
summary: We exist to make the supremacy of the name and purpose of Jesus Christ known at the University of Texas.

name: College Republicans at Texas
description:
The College Republicans at Texas are the only student org on the 40 Acres directly associated with the Texas GOP. Come check out the best party on campus!

 

Our website can be found at:

https://sites.google.com/view/texascr/home?authuser=0.

Interested in joining? Please fill out the form below:

https://forms.gle/1jQJKNPNVgKqSjFTA.

Twitter and IG: texascr.
summary: To proactively advance principles and values of the Republican Party on campus, to coordinate and provide logistical support to the Republican Party of Texas, and to engage and educate the larger student population on contemporary political issues.

name: Communication Council
description:
Communication Council's main purpose is to address student concerns in the Moody College of Communication by fostering and maintaining a strong relationship with administration. Additionally, we facilitate interactions between students, faculty, and staff in the college, represent the college at University-wide events, and serve the city of Austin through our volunteer and community service outreach.

We accept new members every semester, and the applications go live on the first day of classes. Check our website for more information - commcouncil.utexas.org

The objectives of Communication Council shall be:

  • To meet student needs in the Moody College of Communication through special events, programming, and student services; and,
  • To represent student opinion on college-specific and campus-wide issues; and
  • To act as the official liaison between students and administration/faculty within the Moody College of Communication; and,
  • To promote communication within the Moody College of Communication and throughout the University of Texas at Austin; and,
  • To encourage interaction among all departments within the Moody College of Communication; and,
  • To work with recognized student organizations in the Moody College of  Communication and the University; and,
  • To allocate the Dean's funds to Communication Student Organizations; and
  • To represent Moody students’ needs within the Senate of College Councils
summary: Our council's main purpose is to address student concerns in the College of Communication by fostering and maintaining a strong relationship with our college's administration. Additionally, we facilitate interactions between students, faculty , and sta...

name: Foot in the Door Theatre
description:
Audition info will be posted at the beginning of the semester. Follow our Instagram account @footinthedoortheatre or request to join on Hornslink for updates. 

Foot in the Door is a student-run theatre group at UT Austin. You do not have to be a Theatre major to join our org! We put on 1-3 shows a year, with students involved/leading all aspects of the productions. Whether you're interested in acting, directing, or working behind the scenes, there's a place for you with us! All students are welcome to audition or help with tech!

Feel free to contact us if you have questions about auditions, how to get involved, or about our organization in general.
summary: Foot in the Door is a student-run theatre group at UT Austin. We put on 1-3 shows a year, with students involved/leading all aspects of the productions. All students are welcome to audition!

name: Forever Texas
description:
Forever Texas was established in 2003 as a place for young women to come together and create everlasting college experiences at The University of Texas at Austin. We pride ourselves as being a smaller, tight-knit organization where our members can find their forever friends as they navigate through their college journey. Our goal is to create an environment in which our "Forevers" can get in touch with our university, community, and experience wonderful things they would never have pushed theirselves to do otherwise. 

Spirit

Forever Texas girls love to show school spirit! Besides rooting for our world-class athletic teams, Forever Texas celebrates talent from every corner of our campus. Whether it’s a dance performance, music jam session, or a playoff game, you can count on us to be there bleeding burnt orange! Win or lose, rain or shine, we always stand by our Horns.

Spirit traditions/events include:

  • Tailgates
  • Group football seating
  • Watch parties
  • Intramural Sports

Service

Forever Texas is dedicated to helping others and giving back on the UT campus and to the Austin community. We are especially proud to support our main philanthropy, Texas Advocacy Project! Texas Advocacy Project, established in 1982, helps to end dating and domestic violence, sexual assault, and stalking in Texas and empowers survivors through free legal services and access to the justice system.

Other service opportunities include:

  • Texas THON
  • Relay for Life
  • Austin Pets Alive
  • Inside Books
  • Pease Park Cleanup
  • Blanton Museum of Art
  • Light the Night
  • Marathon Kids
  • City-Wide Hold Up
  • Race for the Cure
  • Austin Marathon
  • Trail of Lights

Sisterhood

We’re a diverse group of women from all sides of campus and all sorts of majors, but we all come together to make a loving family. Forever Texas ladies love having fun and spending time together. Most importantly, Forever Texas sisters love each other! We have many annual events and sisterhood activities that really bring us together to make friendships and memories that last way beyond our college years.

Our past sisterhood activities have included:

  • New Member Induction and Retreat
  • Mixers with Fraternities & Sports Teams
  • Picnics at Zilker
  • Themed Parties
  • Halloween Skate Dash
  • Craft Night
  • Winter Formal
  • Midnight Pancakes
  • Boat Party
  • Please visit our linktree HERE for more information and links to our instagram!
summary: To instill the values of sisterhood, spirit, and service in each and every one of our members through a variety of self and community improvement activities.

name: French and Italian Graduate Student Organization
description: The French and Italian Graduate Student Organization's mission is to represent and support the academic, social and professional growth of our students. We organize cultural and academic enrichment events, as well as social activities to build community within our department. We encourage all graduate students to participate and contribute to the life of our French and Italian programs.
summary: We are a non-profit educational group dedicated to the representation and support for the academic, social and professional growth of graduate students in the department of French and Italian.

name: Freshman Launch
description: Freshman Launch is an organization tailored for first-year business students to grow as young professionals as they begin their time as Longhorns. Throughout the year, members will work to understand their values as a leader, explore how they function in a team, learn from speakers, and develop skills such as communication and ethics. Members will partake in professional development workshops regarding topics such as networking, public speaking, LinkedIn, resume development, and career path exploration. In the second semester, members will put their knowledge into action, taking on a project to solve the business problems of top-tier companies as a team.
summary:
DISCOVER yourself as a leader
BUILD skills, knowledge, and friendships
LAUNCH your career as a young professional

name: Friar Society
description: Since 1911, The Friar Society has recognized students, staff, and faculty members who have made significant contributions to The University of Texas. In addition to being the oldest honor society at UT-Austin, The Friars are a multi-generational philanthropic organization with various endowments housed within The Texas Exes Organization. Active student members work in tandem with The Friar Society Alumni Network to host reunion weekends every spring and fall on campus.
summary: To recognize students for outstanding contribution to The University.

name: IEEE Power and Energy Society
description: For anyone interested in the power and energy industry, we provide opportunities for members to explore career pathways, apply engineering concepts in projects, network with industry professionals, and connect with fellow students who have similar interests.
summary:
IEEE-PES provides networking opportunities and technical project involvement for students interested in power and energy.

http://sites.utexas.edu/ieeepes/

name: IEEE Robotics and Automation Society
description:
The IEEE Robotics & Automation Society at the University of Texas at Austin

Inspires robotics research and aspires to make robotics more accessible to persons of all backgrounds. Since 1997, from our primary residence in UT's Electrical and Computer Engineering department, we have connected undergraduate students from mechanical, electrical, aerospace, computer, and other engineering (and non-engineering) fields. We help students gain practical multi-disciplinary experience in the lab, then demonstrate our abilities at various robotics competitions while promoting robotics via community outreach.

 

Join our DISCORD to get connected!
Check out our LINKTREE for all resources!
See our WEBSITE for more information about RAS!
Stay updated on our MAIN INSTAGRAM for events!
Take a look at our SPAM INSTAGRAM for fun :)
summary: A student org focused on robotics! We accept all majors and all levels of robotics experience. Check out our website at ras.ece.utexas.edu and https://linktr.ee/UT.RAS (our linktree) for updates!

name: Ignite Texas
description:
Ignite Texas is a four-day retreat for incoming freshmen and transfer students who are looking to find Christian community in Austin. With a band and speaker, small group time, and fun camp traditions, the retreat not only provides students with friends and community before arriving to campus, but also gives them the tools they need to seek spiritual growth during their time at UT.
