Our purpose is to glorify the Lord by serving incoming students and showing them their first glimpse of the body of Christ at the University of Texas. At retreat, the Ministry Fair introduces campers to many churches and Christian organizations in Austin, allowing them to see God’s influence both on campus and in the city.

 

Founded in September 2002, Ignite Texas was created as a model of the mission statement for a retreat called "Impact" at Texas A&M University. Every August since the foundation of Ignite, the staff has served by outreaching to incoming students through the unity of the body of Christ. The name "Ignite" represents the organization’s purpose: to ignite the hearts of freshmen to seek God during their college career. [Philippians 1:9-11, 1 Corinthians 12:12, 1 Timothy 4:7-8]
summary: The purpose of Ignite Texas is to provide a weekend of spiritual growth to incoming freshmen through the unity of the body of Christ established on campus at UT Austin. The retreat provides outreach and resources to establish their faith in college.

name: Indian Cultural Association
description: Indian Cultural Association seeks to increase awareness about the South Asian heritage at The University of Texas at Austin. Founded on the three tenets of culture, community, and camaraderie, the Indian Cultural Association serves students as a cultural liaison to spread influence of the Indian ethnic identity. With various social, service, cultural, and professional events, our ultimate mission is to foster a strong appreciation for diversity and the preservation of the Indian culture on the campus and in the community as a whole.
summary: With various events and meetings, our ultimate mission is to foster a strong appreciation for diversity and the preservation of the Indian culture on the campus and in the community as a whole.

name: Liberal Arts Council
description:
We are no longer accepting applications to join the Liberal Arts Council for Spring 2024. We look forward to seeing any potential applicants apply Fall 2024.

 

Liberal Arts Council (LAC) is the paramount student governmental organization within the College of Liberal Arts. A member of UT's Senate of College Councils, LAC provides a voice to COLA students at the university-wide level, while still providing resources at the college level.

The objectives of the Council shall be in the spirit of interdisciplinary education, to devote itself to the furtherance of the liberal arts; and, to represent all students, departments, centers, and programs of the college.
summary: Liberal Arts Council serves as the official voice of the students in the College of Liberal Arts. We are no longer accepting applications to join the Liberal Arts Council for Spring 2024. We look forward to seeing any potential applicants apply Fall 2024

name: Liberal Arts Honors Student Council
description:
Welcome to the Liberal Arts Honors Student Council! We plan social events, champion mentorship, provide academic enrichment, and spearhead service opportunities to engage the LAH community. We serve all Liberal Arts Honors cohort students, and anyone is welcome to attend our general meetings! By building a small and collaborative community in the Liberal Arts, we strive to make a bigger university feel like home for all of our students!

Meetings are held every Monday at 6:00 pm in SZB 4.508!
summary: We plan social events, and provide academic enrichment and service opportunities to engage the LAH community.

name: National Community Pharmacists Association
description:
We are dedicated to the continuing growth and prosperity of independent community pharmacy in the United States.

We are the inaugural student chapter of the National Community Pharmacy Association representing the professional and proprietary interests of independent community pharmacists and will promote and defend those interests.

We are committed to high-quality pharmacist care and to restoring, maintaining, and promoting the health and well-being of the public we serve.
summary: NCPA represents the interests and professional development of UT pharmacy students. We support the growth and innovation of independent community pharmacy through compounding education, community outreach, entrepreneurship, and legislative advocacy.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description:
Foster unanimity of thought and action as far as possible in the conduct of traditional black greek letter collegiate fraternities and sororities and to consider problems of mutual interest to its member organizations.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is NPHC? The National Pan-Hellenic Council is the governing body for the historically African-American sororities and fraternities at The University of Texas at Austin. NPHC emerged in an effort for African-American collegiate fraternities and sororities to participate co-actively in the spirit of unity. Our interest together reflect a duty toward both racial uplift and African-American solidarity upon which the National Pan-Hellenic Council and our organization were founded. Nationally NPHC was founded on May 10, 1930 and The University of Texas NPHC was charted in 1965.

What is membership intake? Membership Intake is the process of selecting, educating and initiating new members into an NPHC chapter. This process is prescribed by the National organization and guided by alumni/ae advisors. For many of our organizations this is a 3-6 week process that is concluded with a new member presentation to reveal its newest members to campus. In order to be considered for membership intake you must have completed the specific organization application, submitted the membership fees along with having 12 hours of completed course work.

I want to join an NPHC chapter. What do I do? NPHC chapters typically have intake during the fall and spring semester and to be eligible for membership intake you have to have a minimum of 12 hours of completed coursework at UT. The specific requirements for each organization vary. Review out their websites, both local and national for more detailed information about the organization you are interested in joining. Interested students are encouraged to have a record of community service and campus involvement.

How else can I get my questions answered about NPHC? Each semester, NPHC hosts their signature event "Meet the Greeks." This informative program introduces students who are interested in learning more about the Divine Nine organizations on campus. Throughout the program students are encouraged to ask questions and engage with the chapters as well. For more information, you should contact the NPHC President at texasnphcpresident@gmail.com or you can reach out to the Sorority and Fraternity Life office at sfl@austin.utexas.edu.
summary: Foster unanimity of thought and action as far as possible in the conduct of traditional black greek letter collegiate fraternities and sororities and to consider problems of mutual interest to its member organizations....

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description:
The mission of the National Society of Black Engineers is to increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

 

We are here to offer you resources, support, and a community through out your college journey!

If you want to participate in academic events, professional development events, mental health events, intramural sports, community service events, Regional and National Conferences, socials, and more, then join the TEXAS NSBE FAMILY! 

 

NSBE FAT Video 2024-2025

 

If you are interested, join our Slack, follow our Instagram, and check out some of our events! Check out our gallery to see some great memories that have been made within the NSBE family.
summary: Our mission is to increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

name: National Student Speech, Language and Hearing Association
description:
1) Encourage professional interest among students in the study of Speech, Language and Hearing Sciences (SLHS)

2) Provide a vehicle for student representation in matters of professional concern

3) Provide continuity to the dissemination of professional information

4) Promote interaction between faculty and students, graduates and undergraduates, and student speech pathologists and audiologists

5) Commitment to service and advocacy to the organization and the professional field
summary: UT Austin chapter of National Student Speech Language Hearing Association (NSSLHA)

name: Native American and Indigenous Peoples Association
description:
The Native American Indigenous Peoples Association engages with scholarship and community work by Indigenous and non-Indigenous faculty, students, and community organizers to amplify Indigenous matters dialogue, support, and action across campus.

NAIPA is both a critical community and a critical forum for inter-tribal, cross-cultural, hemispheric, and global dialoguing on Native/Indigenous lives, in order to learn about, support, and seek solutions to critical matters related to Indigenous communities. These matters include but are not limited to language and cultural revitalization, gender and sexual violence, migration and displacement, political mobilization and land rights, urban and diasporic experiences, and afro-Indigenous experiences.
summary: The Native American Indigenous Peoples Association engages with scholarship and community work by Indigenous and non-Indigenous faculty, students and community organizers to amplify Indigenous matters dialogue, support, and action across campus.

name: Natural Sciences Council
description: Natural Sciences Council was founded in 1972 to serve as a liaison between the students and the faculty in the College of Natural Sciences (CNS). We help direct feedback of programs in the college and work to resolve the problems students face in their daily academic and social communities. Through our events and programs, we connect students to the resources they need to succeed here and beyond, including campus entities, students of similar interests, faculty members, and organizations around UT and Austin. We constantly seek feedback from CNS students regarding functions and programs in the college and focus on how we can improve in order to better enhance the CNS student experience.
summary: Natural Sciences Council was founded in 1972 to serve as a liaison between the students and the faculty in the College of Natural Sciences (CNS). We work with the college and its students to create resources and improve the CNS student experience.

name: Texas Pre-Physical Therapy Society
description: Pre-PT society provides future PT students with an atmosphere for them to get to know their peers with the same goals, receive personal information from PT school representatives about DPT programs, and get access to resources and tips for applying to PT schools.
summary: Make pre-physical therapy students aware of academic and career opportunities as well as job shadowing and observation experience.

name: Pi Beta Phi
description: The mission of Pi Beta Phi Fraternity for Women is to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential and enrich lives through community service.
summary: The mission of Pi Beta Phi Fraternity for Women is to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential and enrich lives through community service.

name: Pi Kappa Alpha Fraternity
description: Pi Kappa Alpha is a brotherhood committed to the courageous pursuit of excellence in every chapter of life. Through the mutual advancement of our members and our Fraternity, we encourage one another to live with integrity and leave the communities we participate in better than when we found them.
summary: To help young men become gentlemen and prepare for life....

name: Redefined Dance Company
description:
Redefined Dance Company is a street styles based dance team based at the University of Texas at Austin with a mission to spread our love for dance with the community and explore our collective passion in all forms and styles together. 

From competing at World of Dance, performing in community showcases, to hosting the Wreck Them Peoples charity convention every year, we seek to share our passion with the community. Established in 2007, the team has been interconnected with the greater Austin street and club dance scene for many years!

We audition new members looking to join our family every semester. If you're interested, be sure to follow us on Instagram (@redefineddanceco) to get notified about our next audition, as well as upcoming performances!
summary: Redefined Dance Company is a dance team based at the University of Texas at Austin with a mission to spread our love for dance with the community and explore our collective passion in all street and club dance styles together.

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description:
RUF stands for Reformed University Fellowship and is the campus ministry of the Presbyterian Church in America. As a Christian ministry at UT, we strive to reach students with the good news of Jesus. We also want to equip our students to serve others.

Jesus said that he did not come to save the righteous, but sinners. At RUF, we do not pretend to have it all together. We are people in need of grace. Consequently, all are welcome to be part of our group, regardless of personal or religious background. RUF exists for the "convinced" and the "unconvinced," the lost, the found, the burned, bored, cynical, or spiritual. We are a community learning to love God, our friends, and our university because we believe that God first loved us. If this sounds interesting to you, please contact us. We would love to get to know you!

To find out more about RUF, check out our website at www.ruf.org/ut

We have large group every Wednesday evening at 7pm at the Texas Federation Of Women's Clubs (2312 San Gabriel St.) in West Campus. Come out for some worship and fun!
summary: RUF stands for Reformed University Fellowship and is the campus ministry of the Presbyterian Church in America. As a Christian ministry at UT, we strive to reach students with the good news of Jesus. We also want to equip our students to serve others.

name: Sigma Lambda Alpha Sorority, Inc.
description:
Sigma Lambda Alpha Sorority, Inc. is dedicated to community service, academic achievement, cultural awareness, and sisterhood. Although SLA was historically founded as a Latina-based sorority, we pride ourselves in our commitment to retain members from all backgrounds.Thanks to our diversity we continue to grow as an organization in service, leadership, unifying the Greek community, and creating a stronger bond as sisters.

        We strive to shape strong and independent women into leaders who excel in their local and national communities. Founded in 1992, our outstanding sisters are agents of change in their fields of study and universities. With a focus on excellence and professionalism, we utilize our academic experiences to make a positive impact and uplift the Latinx community, not only at the University of Texas at Austin but society as a whole. 

        If you are interested in being a Texas Señorita or have any questions, feel free to email us at slapresident.eta@gmail.com and/or visit our socials @Texasseñoritas                                                                                                                              

"Ellas tienen ambición. Ellas tienen dignidad. Ellas tienen determinación. Ellas son Señoritas Latinas En Acción."
summary: SLA is a sisterhood focused on service, academics, culture, and leadership. We empower Latinx women to excel at UT Austin and in society, fostering strong, independent leaders who uplift our community. Together, we shape a better future. #MakeItYourTexas

name: Sigma Lambda Beta-Eta Alpha Chapter
description:
Sigma Lambda Beta International Fraternity, Inc. is based on cultural understanding and wisdom. We believe in the principles of fairness, opportunity, and in the equality of all men no matter their race, culture, or ethnicity. Our biggest goal is to see that all of society can realize the importance of these principles. To achieve this goal we all strive to model these behaviors and present ourselves in a gentlemanly and educated fashion. Year after year, the Eta Alpha chapter of Sigma Lambda Beta at the University of Texas produces countless leaders and brothers who go on to change the world, as well as countless hours committed to scholarship, community service, and cultural awareness as well as proving a home away home.

Membership: Our membership is open to all male students, who are enrolled full-time and are in good standing with the university.

Functions: Activities & Events include: Dia De Los Muertos, Battle of the Betas Sports Competition, Haskell Street Halloween Block Party, Cesar Chavez street clean up through Keep Austin Beautiful, Parents Weekend, CPR Awareness Day, SLB State & National Events, Cultural Awareness Lecture Series, National and Chapter Founders Week, Famous Purple and White Stroll Competition, Mixers with other Greek - Lettered Organizations, Annual Formal.
summary: Sigma Lambda Beta is based on cultural understanding and wisdom. We believe in the principles of fairness, opportunity, and in the equality of all men no matter their race, culture, or ethnicity. Our biggest goal is to see that all of society can reali..

name: Sigma Lambda Gamma National Sorority
description: Sigma Lambda Gamma is a multicultural Greek letter organization with the mission to be the premier organization committed to providing a mechanism of empowerment to all women. Through promoting standards of excellence in morality, ethics and education, we empower young women of color and the greater Austin community. Sigma Lambda Gamma National Sorority, Inc. prides itself on being an organization committed to social justice, fighting for all oppressed groups and educating our members to give them the tools necessary to make changes within their own communities. If you want to learn to lead with distinction, speak with conviction, and flourish unapologetically, then feel free to contact us at slg.xi.recruitment@gmail.com or check out our social media handles @Utexas_Gammas.
summary: Sigma Lambda Gamma, historically a Latina based sorority, shall be primarily one of promoting standards of excellence in morality, ethics and education.

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon Fraternity
description: 1) Uphold, develop and strengthen the character of its members; 2) in- culate those principles which naturally revolve upon an individual as a member of society; 3) impress upon its members the true significance of fraternal relationships; 4) aid and sustain, as far as possible the government and discipline of the institution where it is located; 5) promote scholarship, athletic excellence and social compatibility.
summary: 1) Uphold, develop and strengthen the character of its members; 2) in- culate those principles which naturally revolve upon an individual as a member of society; 3) impress upon its members the true significance of fraternal relationships; 4) aid and s...

name: Sigma Phi Lambda
description:
Sigma Phi Lambda (Phi Lamb) is a Christian sorority that centers on faith, fellowship, and fun with the purpose of glorifying God and making His name great. Through weekly meetings, prayer groups, sisterhood events, date events, and chapter-wide retreats, we provide opportunities to connect and grow within a supportive community. Join Phi Lamb and find, not only your best friends, but your sisters. Sigma Phi Lambda is not presently, nor will it ever be Panhellenic.

Our heart and passion behind our organization can by summed up in our sorority verse, Romans 15:5-6:

"May the God who gives endurance and encouragement give you a spirit of unity among yourselves as you follow Christ Jesus so that with one heart and one mouth you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ."

 

Have a question? Reach out to us over email (texasphilamb@gmail.com) or one of our social media accounts!

Want to see more of Phi Lamb? Check out our Facebook or Instagram!
summary: We are a Christian sorority focused on faith, fellowship, and fun with the purpose of glorifying God and making His name great. Our activities include prayer nights, date events, community outreach, and retreats. Join Phi Lamb and find your best friends!

name: Sigma Phi Omega Sorority, Inc.
description: Est. in 1991 at the University of Texas at Austin, we are the oldest Asian-interest sorority on campus. As sisters of Sigma Phi Omega, our goal is to build unconditional sisterhood and everlasting relationships. Our national philanthropy is to raise awareness of domestic violence and sexual assault in the Asian community and promote the prevention of domestic violence and sexual assault against women.
summary: We are the oldest Asian-interest sorority at the University of Texas at Austin and have helped many young women transition to college life. Our primary goal as a sorority is to promote sisterhood and everlasting relationships.

name: San Antonio Student Pharmacists Association
description: Promote the profession of pharmacy to the San Antonio community and University of Texas Health Science Center at San Antonio (UTHSCSA). Also, to provide a channel of communication for the University of Texas College of Pharmacy students between San Antonio and other affiliated campuses.
summary: Promote the profession of pharmacy to the San Antonio community and University of Texas Health Science Center at San Antonio (UTHSCSA). Also, to provide a channel of communication for the University of Texas College of Pharmacy students between campuses.

name: San Jacinto Residence Hall Council
description: The purpose of our council is to represent the residents living in San Jacinto Residence Hall. We will strive to build a community within the hall through sponsoring various programs and events that reflect the interests of the residents. Our council will ensure San Jacinto remains a great place to live on campus. The council will continue to work closely with the University Residence Hall Association (URHA), the Division of Housing and Food Service (DHFS), and the University of Texas at Austin.
summary: The purpose of our council is to represent the residents living in San Jacinto Residence Hall. We will strive to build a community within the hall through sponsoring various programs and events that reflect the interests of the residents. Our council w..

name: Senate of College Councils
description:
**Requesting membership to Senate through HornsLink does NOT mean you have applied to Senate! If you would like to stay connected, please fill follow us on social media (@utsenate) to stay up to date on recruitment events and applications!**

 

The purpose of the Senate of College Councils is to serve as the official voice of students in academic affairs through representation by the College Councils. The members of Senate value the opportunities the University of Texas at Austin has to offer, and strives to publicize, expand, and improve them for the students on the 40 acres. Senate deals with issues like plus/minus grading, teacher evaluations, study abroad, core curriculum streams, advising, and tuition regulation, all at a hands-on level.  

The Senate of College Councils is divided up into eight committees: Academic Enrichment,  Academic Policy, Engagement and Outreach, Community Advocacy, Faculty Affairs, Fundraising, Recruitment and Retention, and Undergraduate Research.  These committees focus on various aspects of academic affairs and work with college councils to write legislation, host events, and meet with faculty and staff to enact visions.
summary: To serve as the official voice of students in academic affairs through representation by the College Councils.

name: Rho Chi Pharmaceutical Honor Society
description: National Honor Society in Pharmacy. Its official purpose is to promote scholarship, friendship and to recognize high attainments in the Pharmaceutical Sciences.
summary: National Honor Society in Pharmacy. Its official purpose is to promote scholarship, friendship, and to recognize high attainments in the Pharmaceutical Sciences.

name: Rifle and Pistol Club
description:
The Rifle and Pistol Club gives students the opportunity to learn about firearms and develop their shooting skills. No prior experience or personal firearm ownership is needed to join.

We do not have any requirements for members, so feel free to come to any of our events, which will be posted in our Discord below. This means even if you don't know how to shoot or if you don't have a gun, you can still come out to the range and we'll equip you!

You take what you will from the club, be it just hanging out and shooting guns for the sake of making controlled explosions or actively training to compete in the various types of competitions.

Our club operates online through Discord, so make sure to join to keep up with events and communication, or just to chat and meet new folks!

https://discord.gg/r8kRZtjtPD
summary: The Rifle and Pistol Club gives students the opportunity to learn about firearms and develop their shooting techniques through range trips and open matches. No prior experience or personal firearm ownership is needed to join.

name: Texas Rotaract
description:
The Rotaract Club is the university chapter of Rotary International. We pride ourselves in “service above self,” service at home and overseas, and the creation of leaders. Our sponsoring Rotary club is the Austin University Area Rotary Club.

We are a Rotary-sponsored service organization, both local and international, for young adults who work together to develop leadership and professional skills. We interact and collaborate with Rotarians in the Austin area. We plan and carry out service projects and activities, such as fundraising events, helping local charities, and international development projects. We volunteer in the UT area and Austin Community to make a difference in the lives of those around us. Last year we had over 200 collective service hours!

E-mail texasrotaract@gmail.com with any questions.
summary: Texas Rotaract is the university chapter of Rotary International. We pride ourselves in “service above self,” service at home and overseas, and the creation of leaders.

name: Saaya Dance Company
description:
We are Saaya,! UT's premiere co-ed fusion dance team! We have been combining Bollywood, Bhangra, Classical, Contemporary & Hip-Hop since 2008, with plans to add even more styles of dance this upcoming year! 

Saaya is just coming back from one of our most successful seasons. We are so excited to get started on our 2025-2026 season! 


Here are some of our past accomplishments and we can't wait to see this list to grow.

 

2024-2025

3rd place at ATL Tamasha

1st place at Norman Naachle

2nd place at Motor City Dhamal

1st place at Jazba

4th place at Legends

 

2023-24

1st place at ATL Tamasha

2nd place at UIUC Udaan

2nd place at Maryland Minza

1st place at Jazba

3rd place at Legends

 

2022-23

1st place at ATL Tamasha

1st place at Aaja Nache

1st place at Jazba

1st place at A-Town Showdown
