name: Social Work Council
description: The University of Texas at Austin Social Work Council attempts to further the education of social work students by providing a forum for communication with faculty and staff to improve the School of Social Work. The council also provides educational, service and social action activities to enhance student involvement and cohesion.
summary: Social Work Council is intended to advocate for social work students within the Steve Hicks School of Social Work (SHSSW), within the broader UT community, and in the state of Texas.

name: American Library Association & Society of American Archivists UT Student Chapter
description: To serve as a means of introducing and integrating new librarians and archivists into the profession; to enhance the educational function of the society by providing focus for students to discuss library and archival issues, identify with the profession, and engage in professional activities to promote communication among student members; to develop the leaders of tomorrow's library and archival professions; and to attract new members into the American Library Association and Society of American Archivists.
summary: To serve as a means of introducing and integrating new librarians and archivists into the profession.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
description:
Our purpose is to prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world, to celebrate diversity and participate socially on campus, and to provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local communities. At core, we are an equally social and professional organization for students to be involved on campus.

Our chapter website is here, and please follow our Instagram @sasetexas as we post announcements for all our meetings and events there!

Email us at sasetexas@gmail.com to receive our newsletters!
summary: Our purpose is to prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world, to celebrate diversity and participate socially on campus, and to provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local community

name: Longhorn Racing
description:
Longhorn Racing provides students with the opportunity to explore different engineering fields and grow their tangible skills through a collaborative and innovative environment.  Each year we design, build, and test two formula-style vehicles (electric and internal combustion) from scratch.  Every 2 years, we design, build, and test a solar-powered vehicle as well.  All of our vehicles compete every summer in locations across the nation. 

 

For more information, or to contact us, please visit us at longhornracing.org
summary: UT Austin Chapter of Formula SAE and Solar Vehicle Teams

name: Texas Women's Club Lacrosse
description:
Lacrosse tournaments, scrimmages, practices and social related events

Please visit our website for more info: http://texaswomenslax.weebly.com/
summary:
The nationally competitive, women's lacrosse club at The University of Texas at Austin. Texas Fight, y'all!  
Travel within TX and out of state
Scrimmages & a tournament in fall + league games & tournaments in spring
Practices: 7-9 MTuW in spring

name: Texas Women's Rugby
description: Our goal is to promote rugby among our campus community while creating an inclusive and supportive environment for our players. Everyone is welcome to join us at practice and learn about the sport! We are a no-experience needed club; we teach you everything you need to know from the ground up. We practice T/Th 6-8 PM at UT's intramural fields, Whitaker Fields.
summary: Promote rugby among university students, faculty, and staff. Anyone, regardless of experience, is welcome to join us at practice and learn about rugby!

name: Texas Darlins
description: Established in 2003, Texas Darlins is an organization bound by sisterhood, driven by service, and unparalleled in spirit. As the official spirit group of the Texas Men's and Women's basketball programs, we unite every kind of woman with the aim of changing our community and altering ourselves through service and spirit. Embedded in each of us are the qualities that make a strong leader, devotion, kindness, loyalty, determination, and grace. We are a group of individuals move as one toward a common goal: unity in service, sisterhood, and spirit.
summary: Texas Darlins is an organization bound by sisterhood, driven by service, and unparalleled in spirit. We unite every kind of woman with the aim of changing our community and altering ourselves through service and spirit.

name: Texas Wranglers
description:
The Texas Wranglers serve as a Male Spirit Group at the University of Texas at Austin. Texas Wranglers affords students an opportunity to make lifelong friends from all walks of life; to become a student leader through community involvement; to broaden one's network throughout the student body; and to gain the strength of character, mind, and body to overcome any adversity.

Since 1996 we have raised $475,000 for Easter Seals Central Texas, our main philanthropy which serves to promote independence and create opportunities for people with disabilities to pursue their hopes and dreams.

Our three pillars, scholarship, leadership, and athletic ability embody our values as an organization. Defined by a culture of academic success, many of our members and leaders excel in challenging majors and honors programs. Leadership is the most cherished attribute of the Texas Wranglers. Members take initiative as leaders in the community and work to empower those around them to fulfill their utmost potential. Wranglers are united in their passion for playing sports, improving their fitness, and most importantly taking on a challenge.

The Wranglers do not limit their membership by race, creed, or financial ability; rather, we strive to bring out the best in anyone who accepts the challenge to become a Texas Wrangler.

Texas Wranglers Website

Texas Wranglers Instagram
summary:
The Texas Wranglers is a brotherhood dedicated to serving the Austin community, acting as the primary spirit group for the Men's Basketball Team, and striving for excellence in scholarship, leadership, and athletic ability.

www.texaswranglers.org

name: Texas Wushu
description: Jiayou! Here at Texas Wushu, we train, learn, and grow as a community while engaging in Wushu, or Chinese martial arts (Wu means martial, and shu means the art of). Wushu is a highly structured performance art that draws from many traditional Chinese martial art styles and is practiced by millions around the world! We welcome everyone, regardless of experience level! There are weekly practices to teach a variety of forms, weapons, and other kung fu fundamentals. We also perform demos for several on- or off-campus events throughout the semester. We host frequent social events in addition to practices to maintain our friendly social atmosphere. If you want to stay current, follow us on Instagram at @texaswushu or visit our website.
summary: Texas Wushu is a performance martial arts organization led by UT students that perform, demonstrate, and compete at a national level. Come learn more about a contemporary sport with ancient history!

name: Thai Association of Austin
description:
1. Exhibit Thai cultural display
2. Organize and set up meetings between Thai students
3. Advise incoming students (first year, transfer, exchange)
summary: Thai Association of Austin was founded since 1973 with the mission to foster the Thai community and Thai culture in Austin. We organize social and cultural events for our members and provide any needs for new members.

name: Campus Environmental Center
description:
Find your place in environmentalism.

With so many ways to get involved and make an impact, it's hard to know where to start. Let us help:

The Campus Environmental Center strives to create a diverse and inclusive community - that provides opportunities to explore environmentalism and sustainability to all interested students. The community is open to all and can look different for each student involved. A community member is any student who is involved with the CEC in any way, whether that be a single event or every event. We have three project teams that focus on different niches within sustainability. Trash to Treasure (@ut_trash2treasure) focuses on sustainable fashion through thrift sales, clothing swaps, and mending workshops. Green Events (@utgreenevents) focuses on diverting landfill waste through zero-waste consultations and campus compost drop-offs. Microfarm (@utmicrofarm) is an organic farm that donates produce to UT Outpost, provides resources for student farming, and educates students about regenerative agriculture. 

Here’s the best way to stay involved in our community:

#1 – Sign up for our newsletter: There is no application or requirements to be a Community Member. Get involved how you want to and what works with your life.

#2 – Follow us on social media: We post about our project teams, campfire speaker series, and upcoming events! We’re active on Instagram!

#3 - Join our Slack! Join our Slack community and get updates on CEC events, professional opportunities, and educational events in the Austin area!

#4 – Get involved (and bring your friends!): Again, there are no requirements to be considered a Community Member. You are free to get involved, whether that’s attending or helping plan, and bring someone along:

  • Speaker Series (i.e. Climate Change Science, Careers in Sustainability)
  • Hands-on Workshops/Volunteering
  • Project Team Events (i.e. weekly Microfarm Volunteer Days) 
  • Service events (i.e. Waller Creek Clean Ups)
  • Socials (i.e. Turtle Pond Picnics)
  • Wellness Events (i.e. Yoga)
  • See all of these ways to get involved on our calendar!

# 5 - Leadership Positions: Each team of the CEC has compensated Team Leaders and voluntary-basis Team Members with a variety of responsibilities. Applications for Team Members open at the beginning of each semester and at the end of the school year for Team Leaders. 

Linktree: To find all our current information and opportunities to stay involved, check out our Linktree.

About the CEC

The Campus Environmental Center is a community of students who explore, learn about, and act on contemporary environmental issues on campus and in the Austin area. We welcome diverse interests, ideas, and perspectives as we grow together to become effective environmental change-makers now and in the future.

We are proud to be the oldest and largest student organization at The University of Texas Austin for those interested in the environment, and we are sponsored by the UT Office of Sustainability.

Our Principles

Our mission: The CEC provides diverse opportunities to catalyze a regenerative culture that is accessible and intersectional for the UT community.
 
Our vision: To build a center rooted in solidarity and empathy where all are empowered to protect

people and the planet.

We have five paths to carrying out our mission:

  • Student Leadership: We develop leadership and professional skills through team-based projects, programs, and initiatives.
  • Innovation: We promote circular communities through cross-collaboration and dynamic, experiential learning opportunities.
  • Community Building: We foster a home for all students navigating the integration of sustainability in their lives. We strive to cultivate a hub of backgrounds to enrich one another’s experiences.
  • Accountability: We provide a platform for students to hold each other and the university accountable regarding sustainability through open and productive feedback systems.
  • Culture & History- We root our initiatives in historical context to promote interdisciplinary practices and approaches to the nuanced world of sustainability.
summary: Through student leadership, the CEC provides diverse opportunities to catalyze a sustainable culture that is regenerative and accessible to the UT community. Come find your niche within environmentalism!

name: Texas Mahjong Club
description:
We host general meetings where members can play mahjong, socialize, and make new friends every week! Not a member yet? No problem! Feel free to join us and try out Mahjong without worrying about dues! We also hold special events for holidays like Lunar New Year, Halloween, and Christmas. We occasionally partner with other organizations as well to share our love of mahjong with their members!
 
Please join our discord for our updates on meeting times and other special events: https://discord.gg/9xk2drbZCV
summary: We host weekly mahjong game nights, and occasionally other social events. No experience needed, we're happy to teach new players!

name: The Project
description:
The Project is a student-led day of service partners with Austin communities to create meaningful impact through service and collaboration, while providing a way for UT students to practice socially responsible leadership and engage as active citizens beyond campus borders.

 

By coming together as unified student body, participants share their time, energy, and passion for service with the broader community, creating tangible impact both across Austin and in the relationships built along the way. Since its founding in 1999, The Project has grown into the largest day of service at UT Austin and a valued campus tradition.

 

Each year, our student planning team builds year-long partnerships with local Austin neighborhoods to learn directly from community members, identify needs, and support ongoing initiatives. Volunteers engage in a wide range of service projects, such as gardening and tree planting in public green spaces, mural painting, playground building, neighborhood cleanups, and more

 

To learn more, visit The Project or email us at theproject@austin.utexas.edu. Students interested in continuing their involvement in service or community engagement are encouraged to explore additional opportunities through our larger organization, the Community Engagement Collective.
summary: The Project, UT Austin's largest day of service, is an annual event that connects UT students with Austin communities. Each year, the Project Planning Team partners with a local neighborhood to do service projects that support the community.

name: The Review of Litigation
description: The Review of Litigation at Texas Law publishes three annual issues to national and international readership. The Review balances the interests of academia with pragmatic issues important to practicing attorneys and judges. We publish on topics related to procedure, evidence, trial and appellate advocacy, alternative dispute resolution, and often-litigated substantive law.
summary: Publication of 4 issues of The Review of Litigation at UT Law to national and international readership.

name: Theta Chi Fraternity
description: The purpose of Theta Chi is to give men at The University of Texas the opportunity to be a part of a truly life-changing experience by providing them with the tools to grow as a student, leader, and man. Theta Chi is not just another club, but an organization of men with the desire to build lifetime friendships and devote time to develop values. Theta Chi teaches, inspires, and empowers any member to be successful both inside and outside of the classroom and throughout their lives.
summary: The Delta Mu Chapter of Theta Chi fraternity is comprised of gentleman who are not only leaders in the classroom but leaders in their communities and student organizations as well. Theta Chi strives to remain active on campus and the Austin community.

name: Theta Tau - Professional Engineering Fraternity
description:
Hi there! We are UT Austin's gender-inclusive professional engineering fraternity! The Purpose of Theta Tau is to develop and maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship. For more information (including FAQs), please see our website!

RUSH INFO: Please see the in the Instagram @texasthetatau or GroupMe for more information!

Interested in rushing Theta Tau? To register for rush, fill out our interest form, and join our rush. Find more about rush here.

 

Check out our website for the most up-to-date information about Rush!

 

Rush Calendar: Please see the in the Instagram or GroupMe for more information!
summary: The Purpose of Theta Tau is to develop and maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship. Come check out our website at https://texasthetatau.org!

name: Thurgood Marshall Legal Society
description: The Thurgood Marshall Legal Society exists to support and uplift Black students seeking a legal education at the University of Texas, to improve the academic and social climate in Austin for Black law students, to aprise the Black community of the need for more Black attorneys, and to collaborate with organizations who are fundamentally committed to diversifying the legal profession.
summary: The Thurgood Marshall Legal Society exists to support Black law students in their academic, social, and professional endeavors.

name: Turing Scholars Student Association
description: The Turing Scholars Student Association is an association for students in the Turing Scholars Honors Program. The Turing Scholars Honors Program is an honors program for computer science (CS) majors and is housed in the Department of Computer Science. Our goals as an organization are to develop better community within the Turing Scholars and provide support for theses.
summary: The Turing Scholars Student Association is an association for students in the Turing Scholars Honors Program of the Computer Science Department.

name: UT Archery
description:
Our goal is to further archery as a competitive intercollegiate sport and to provide students, staff, and faculty, regardless of experience, an opportunity to obtain and improve their archery skills.

Find us on our website!

UT Archery Club is sponsored by the Division of Recreational Sports:

www.utrecsports.org
summary: Our goal is to further archery as a competitive intercollegiate sport and to provide students, staff, and faculty, regardless of experience, an opportunity to obtain and improve their archery skills.

name: UT Badminton Club
description:
Whether you're a seasoned player or just picking up a racket for the first time, the Badminton Club welcomes you to join our vibrant community. Experience the thrill of badminton, make new friends, and embark on a journey of skill development and personal growth. 

 

Check out our website for more information about our club!

Join our Slack for announcements! The link to the salck can be found on our instagram page @utaustin_badmintonclub :)
summary: To promote interest and encourage participation in the game of badminton. We play Tuesdays and Thursdays from 7:30 PM - 10:30 PM and hol trainings on Sundays from 4:00 PM - 7:00 PM

name: UT Fencing Club
description:
**PLEASE CONTACT US THROUGH OUR INSTAGRAM @TEXASFENCING FOR ALL QUESTIONS AND INTEREST**

UTFC aims to provide members of the University community with the opportunity to develop their fencing skills and increase their enjoyment of the sport through participation in organized drills, bouting, and tournaments!

We are a club open to all UT students, faculty, and staff. If you are interested in fencing but have no prior fencing experience, please come by one of our practices and we will teach you! In our club, we practice all three fencing weapons: foil, epee, and sabre.

UTFC competes in four SWIFA (South Western Intercollegiate Fencing Association) tournaments throughout the year. Additionally, UTFC hosts a USFA tournament in the fall or spring semester. Attendance at these tournaments is not mandatory; however, SWIFAs are a lot of fun and provide UTFC members the chance to bond and meet other fencers across Texas!

Outside of practices and tournaments, UTFC are often seen chowing down at Pluckers, meeting up together for movie nights, or going out for Dim Sum.
 
If you would like to join a club to make friends, learn about fencing, and get an AWESOME workout, then UTFC is right for you! Please feel free to contact us or swing by a practice in BEL 608B :).
 
Practice Times:
Monday - 8:00pm to 11:00pm
Tuesday - 8:00pm to 11:00pm
Wednesday - 8:00pm to 11:00pm
Thursday - 8:00pm to 11:00pm
Saturday - 10:00am to 1:00pm (SPRING)
Sunday - 10:00 am to 1:00pm (FALL)
 
*Note: Not all practices are mandatory. Regular UTFC members may attend anywhere from 1 to 4 practices a week, depending on their schedules.

For more information, visit utexasfencing.weebly.com/
summary: UTFC aims to provide members of the University community with the opportunity to develop their fencing skills and increase their enjoyment of the sport through participation in organized drills, bouting and tournaments!

name: Texas Judo
description: We teach Judo to members of all skill levels ranging from beginner to expert and we hope to teach you too! We improve our Judo skills with our amazing coaches while also making new friends and bonding as a team. Practices are held every Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from 6PM-8PM and Sunday from 11AM-1PM in RSC 1.138. If you are interested, just show up to practice! We regularly have socials and tournaments, so whether you're joining our club to learn a new skill, make friends, or compete, we've got you covered!
summary: We provide Judo instruction to members of all skill levels while also having fun and making friends!

name: UT Men's Soccer Team
description: Participation in intercollegiate athletic soccer programs.
summary:
The University of Texas Men's Soccer team is the elite Men's Soccer Program on UT's campus. 
3x National Champions: '90, '95, '96
3x National Finalists: '97, '17, '21
2x National Semi-Finalists: '10, '22, '24

name: UT Men's Ultimate Club
description:
Ultimate Frisbee, or ultimate for short, is a vastly growing sport nationwide. Our club has also been following this trend as we see more and more people at tryouts and membership numbers increase. For this reason, we have been able to expand our program to involve two teams: 

TUFF: A team

Graze: B team

TUFF consistently finishes in the top 10 teams in the nation, Graze is one of the best B teams in the nation and is a developmental team that helps players learn the ins and outs of the sport so that they can reach TUFF's roster.
summary: We represent the University of Texas in the frisbee disc sport by playing in the top tournaments nationwide. Tuff, the A team, often finishes in the top 10 teams in the nation while Graze, the B team is consistently one of the best B teams in the nation.

name: UT Racquetball Club
description:
UT Racquetball is all about promoting growth, camaraderie, and competition within the racquetball community on campus.

-The meeting times for the Fall and Spring semesters will be every Monday and Thursday from 6:00PM to 9:00PM at the Rec Sports Center (RSC), located next to the stadium on San Jacinto.
-The racquetball club has courts 1-3 and 5-7 at these times. 


Officers:

Gerardo G., President
Clayton P., Vice President
Rishi C., Authorized Rep
summary: To promote competitive racquetball within The University and to promote and develop an intercollegiate team to represent The University at nationwide tournaments while focusing on training and skill building

name: UT Sailing Team
description: The University of Texas Sailing Team is a nationally competitive sport club.  We compete with other college teams as part of the Intercollegiate Sailing Association (ICSA).  We strive to compete at high levels while expanding the appreciation and love for sailboat racing throughout the University, our district, and the greater national sailing community.
summary: The University of Texas Sailing Team is a nationally competitive sport club. Our mission is to share the knowledge and love of competitive sailing with the University community.

name: Texas Chapter of Student Society of Health-System Pharmacists
description: The Texas Chapter of Student Society of Health-System Pharmacists has over 100 members who are all pharmacy students enrolled in the University of Texas College of Pharmacy. We are an affiliated chapter of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists (ASHP) a ~50,000 member national organization of pharmacists from diverse workplaces in the profession of pharmacy. We are also affiliated with the state level organization, the Texas Society of Health-System Pharmacists (TSHP) and the local Austin Society of Health-System Pharmacists (AASHP).
summary: Membership for pharmacy students interested in health-system pharmacy. Organization opportunities geared towards pharmacy residencies and specialty pharmacy practice.

name: UT Water Ski Team
description:
Texas Water Ski was established to represent The University of Texas at Austin at sanctioned tournaments in the South Central Region of the National Collegiate Water Ski Association (NCWSA). We are a RecSports sponsored organization and ski on a private ski boat at a private ski lake. All necessary equipment is provided by the team. We welcome skiers of all levels, even those who have never skied. Contact the team at utexaswaterski@gmail.com

 

President: Ethan Carrillo

Vice President: Austin Ellis

Treasurer: John Cooper

Captain: Michaela Jenkins

Secretary: Jon Stevenson

Social: Thea DeBellis

Lake Coordinators:Elizabeth Hillman, Charles Goranson

 

https://youtu.be/q6fCxnUd4OA
summary: To represent The University at sanctioned tournaments of the South Central Conference of the American Ski Association....

name: UT Women's Club Soccer
description: The University of Texas Women’s Club Soccer Team is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering personal growth, skill development, and leadership among student-athletes. We provide a competitive yet supportive environment that empowers our members to excel both on the field and in the classroom. Through high-level training, competition, and team-building experiences, we create opportunities for our players to develop meaningful relationships and enhance their college experience. Our success is driven by the strength of our community and the lasting impact of the experiences we share throughout the season.
summary: Promote personal growth and skill development among student athletes.

name: UT Women's Ultimate Frisbee
description: To render and perpetuate the love of ultimate frisbee in Austin and, specifically, at UT, through athleticism and sportsmanship.
summary: To render and perpetuate the love of ultimate frisbee in Austin and, specifically, at UT, through athleticism and sportsmanship.

name: UT Women's Volleyball Club
description:
Texas Women’s Club Volleyball is a great way for women on campus to continue their love for the fast-growing sport. The UT RecSports affiliated organization is comprised of two teams. Both teams practice twice a week, for 3 hours each practice and actively participate in various fundraising, volunteer, and social events. As an organization, we pride ourselves on our highly competitive nature. Teams will travel to different cities and states to compete against the best teams in the nation. Every year UTWVC teams are national title-contending teams. Our mission is to promote the development of the sport of volleyball, while providing a fun, competitive environment.

Please visit our website texaswvc.org or instagram @texaswvc for more information on tryouts and open gyms. 

This is preferred than requesting to join our org via Hornslink!!!
summary: Texas Women's Club Volleyball allows dedicated women with an interest in the game to continue playing at a competitive level. UTWVC has consistently been ranked as one of the best clubs in the region and nation.

name: UT Women's Water Polo
description: To teach water polo and compete in nationals.
summary: To teach and compete in water polo nation wide and state wide.

name: Undergraduate Business Council
description: The Undergraduate Business Council serves as a vital link between the students of the McCombs School of Business administration, faculty, and the professional world. Through student-led programs and activities, UBC educates and informs the McCombs student body about academic issues, professional topics, and general interests. By serving as the umbrella organization for McCombs, UBC's mission is to help organize and unite the McCombs Affiliated Student Organizations to make a strong student body that supports its school. Please check our Instagram (@texasubc) below for our recruitment timeline and procedures!
summary: By serving as the umbrella organization for the McCombs School of Business, the Undergraduate Business Council's mission is to help organize and unite the McCombs Affiliated Student Organizations to make a strong student body that supports its school.

name: Undergraduate Mock Trial Association
description:
Interested in law school, becoming a lawyer, or working on your public speaking skills for a future in fields like business or government?

Texas Mock Trial is a competitive team where members travel to competitions across the country and compete as attorneys and witnesses using a new criminal or civil case each year. Members are coached by real trial attorneys, learn the federal rules of evidence, and how to write and deliver materials like opening and closing statements. Competing on Texas Mock Trial provides UT students with an opportunity to experience at an undergraduate level what being a trial attorney is like!

We want YOU! After finishing in the top three in the nation last season and being the only mock trial team in Texas to compete at the National Championship tournament in three consecutive years, we want to recruit talented individuals who have the potential to become strong advocates! Don't worry if you have no prior mock trial, debate, or theatre experience—we would love to have you! See you at tryouts!

Texas Mock Trial holds tryouts every September.
summary: We compete in mock trial against the best universities in the country. Learn from real trial attorneys, strengthen your understanding of the law, and hone critical thinking and speaking skills as part of our traveling team.

name: Undergraduate Exploratory Council
description:
Weekly meetings will be announced here on HornsLink and through our Instagram (@utexasuec)! 

These meetings are open to any student who is exploratory or interested in changing majors! Free food/snacks will be provided at every meeting. 

Meeting topics have included: internal transfer student panels, resume and personal statement workshops, and more! In addition, guest speakers and presenters may join us to speak about campus resources and their career/major exploration journeys.

If you are interested, please fill out our interest form: (to be added) 

 

Join our 2025-2026 GroupMe for further updates: GroupMe

 

Questions or Inquiries? Message us on Instagram @utexasuec

 

Fill out this form to stay up to date with upcoming events from the UEC - https://forms.gle/fuL6LyVSS7chkdFg9

 

Fall 2025 Meetings:

- Tues, Oct 21, 5:30-6:30pm FAC 2.116 Internal Transfer Student Panel

- Tues, Nov. 4, 5:30-6:30pm FAC 2.116 Resume and Personal Statement Workshop

- Tues, Nov. 18, 5:30-6:30pm FAC 2.116 Internal Transfer Info Session / Q&A

 

Our 25-26 Executive Board!

President: Sarah Smith 

Vice President: Leighton Jones

Public Relations Officer: Ashley Abimni
summary: The Undergraduate Exploratory Council (UEC) is the official student representative body for undeclared, transfer-curious, and/or exploratory students. We support, educate, and guide students through the internal application process year-round.

name: Labyrinth Progressive Student Ministry
description:
Labyrinth is as an ecumenical campus ministry that is open to students of all races, genders, sexualities, and those seeking a greater understanding about ancient Christianity through a progressive lens. Whether you’re a long-time believer, or hopeful skeptic, spirituality in college is a journey with twists, turns, and dead ends. You have questions and beliefs, doubts and dreams, and school work to go along with it. Here at Labyrinth, we welcome and encourage you on your journey by providing a safe place to ask questions about God, explore and grow in your spirituality, and connect with other students. 

Worship: Sundays at 5:30 PM in the chapel, located on 22nd St, between Guadalupe St. and San Antonio St.
summary:
Labyrinth is a progressive Christian community that is LGBTQ-friendly, racially inclusive, and open to all students, whether atheist, questioning, or devout.
Labyrinth members are an open, diverse group and a true community.

name: University Democrats
description:
To increase campus civic engagement, support local Democrats, and fight for the progressive reforms needed to build a more fair, just, and equitable world—and to have a blast while doing it. Meetings are Wednesdays at 8 in Budine Hall.

Not only is UDems an outlet to interact with politics in a very casual, social settings, there are several opportunities for individuals who wish to participate in politics professionally. UDems is frequently visited by an array of candidates and elected officials always looking to hire passionate students. Members have interned for several elected officials including Austin's represented in Congress, the  state legislature, district judges, county commissioners, and more.
summary: To increase campus civic engagement, support local canidates, and to fight for the progressive reforms needed to build a more fair just and equitable world - and to have a blast while doing it. Meetings are Wednesdays at 8PM

name: University Fashion Group
description:
Founded in 1978, University Fashion Group (UFG) is UT Austin’s longest-standing and premier professional fashion organization, open to students of all majors who are passionate about the fashion industry. UFG offers hands-on experience in various committees, including creative/media, PR, modeling, and business, to empower members to explore their interests.

All members have the opportunity to gain real-world experience in the fashion industry by engaging with professional speakers and leaders, planning and executing our annual UT Capstone Collection Fashion Show, attending New York Fashion Week, creating a UFG Coffee Table Book, and building a local UT community with a shared love for fashion. 

Our mission is to build student confidence and meaningful connections to the fashion industry, while continuing to shape UFG into a professional-driven organization that evolves alongside the future of UT’s Textiles & Apparel program.

To become an official Spring 2026 UFG member, check out our application here.

Check our last Spring's HornRaiser 2025 Capstone Collection UFG video.
 
For the latest information, visit our website and follow us on Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and LinkedIn.
summary: Founded in 1978, University Fashion Group (UFG) is UT Austin’s premier professional fashion organization, helping students of all majors explore the industry and gain real-world experience through NYFW participation, our Capstone Fashion Show, and more.

name: University Management & Business Research Association
description:
The University Management and Business Research Association (UMBRA) is a pro bono consulting organization that uses Design Thinking to empower students to create real-world solutions for our business clients. We are hypothesis-driven and emphasize prototyping and testing to determine effective solutions. Members gain valuable, hands-on experience in ideation, market research, and business analysis by working closely with our clients to create tangible impact.

Visit our website and Instagram to learn more!
summary: The University Management and Business Research Association is a pro bono consulting organization that uses design thinking to empower students to create real-world solutions for both large and local business clients.

name: Amateur Radio Club at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
The experimental station 5XU was established on the University of Texas campus on October 1, 1921. Since then, our goal is to further the enjoyment of Amateur Radio (or Ham Radio) by providing aid in obtaining a license, by operating an HF and VHF radio station, and through contact with other operators and radio enthusiasts.

Everyone affiliated to UT is welcome to join us at our meetings, and we will be happy to add you as a member to the club. We don’t charge any dues and any level of experience is welcome, so anyone can enjoy the broad spectrum of Ham radio activities with us!

Please visit our website at https://n5xu.ece.utexas.edu/ to learn about our meetings, club activities, and about amateur radio.
summary: UTARC/N5XU is the official Amateur Radio club of the University of Texas at Austin. We are students, faculty and staff who enjoy amateur radio operations with all modes on the main ham radio bands. Our club welcomes everyone and joining is 100% free.

name: Texas Leadership Education and Development
description: Texas Leadership Education and Development in the Office of the Dean of Students wants each student at UT Austin to realize their potential as a leader to create positive change. Through participation in our leadership programs, students will learn to live and practice leadership each day to serve their campus and community. We believe every UT student has the power to lead. Through our varied and creative programming, there is a place in Texas LEAD for everyone.
summary: Texas LEAD in the Office of the Dean of Students is an office that supports every student at UT through leadership education and development and works to ensure that each student has the potential to create positive change.

name: University Panhellenic Council
description:
We, as Undergraduate Members of women’s fraternities, stand for good scholarship, for guarding of good health, for maintenance of fine standards, and for serving, to the best of our ability, our college community. Cooperation for furthering fraternity life, in harmony with its best possibilities, is the ideal that shall guide our fraternity activities.

We, as Fraternity Women, stand for service through the development of character, inspired by the close contact and deep friendship of individual fraternity and Panhellenic life. The opportunity for wide and wise human service, through mutual respect and helpfulness, is the tenet by which we strive to live.
summary: The University Panhellenic Council at UT Austin governs the 13 National Panhellenic sororities. Our mission is to foster a vibrant and inclusive Greek community dedicated to empowering women through scholarship, service, and sisterhood.

name: University Residence Hall Association
description:
Welcome

The University Residence Hall Association (URHA) was started at The University of Texas at Austin in 1981 and is a student organization committed to improving the quality of living on campus for on-campus students. We perform a variety of functions to serve the best interests of our residents.

We work very closely with University Housing and Dining (UHD), our parent organization, to constantly relay resident input we receive. We serve as the official voice of the residents, and use this to review and change policy as suggested by the residents.

URHA's voting body is comprised of 16 Senators, representing each hall on campus. These senators also serve as Residence Hall Council (RHC) officers in their individual halls. Both URHA and the RHCs implement programming to foster a better sense of community in the residence halls and increase overall development of a community atmosphere. By getting involved, you could help out with organizing fun activities, building community in your residence hall, and gaining transferable leadership skills.

Everyone who resides in a University residence hall is automatically a member of URHA and their hall's RHC. A small portion of every resident's housing fee goes to funding these organizations. You can attend RHC meetings to find out what is going on in your area or URHA meetings to find out what is going on with the entire on-campus population.

If you have any questions/comments/concerns, please feel free to contact us on our website anytime!

Mission Statement

The University Residence Hall Association (URHA) empowers, motivates, and advocates for on-campus residents by providing them with skills and resources for them to excel and positively impact the campus community.

Vision Statement

The University Residence Hall Association seeks to create a network of engaged campus residents sharing common experiences through leadership and development opportunities that enhance the community.

Conferences

URHA attends 3 conferences each year. Below is some information about those conferences. The Executive Board Members will attend each conference as well as some members of the Senate with Advisors. After that, the remaining spots will be filled by residents within the halls.

NACURH Annual Conference

The NACURH Annual Conference is an annual conference that is hosted by one of the schools in the National Association of College and University Residence Halls (NACURH). At this conference, representatives from all of the NACURH member schools from all over the world come together to represent their respective school’s RHA and take care of important national business, and also meet and learn from each other while having a great time. During this conference, attendees attend some of the top programs from around the country, meet many new people, and discuss policy changes that may affect the corporation.

SWACURH Regional Leadership Conference

The Southwest Affiliate of College and University Residence Halls (SWACURH) Regional Leadership Conference (RLC) is an annual conference that occurs in the fall and is hosted by one of the schools in the SWACURH Region. The RLC is a cohesive effort from all of the schools in our region to use the collective experience of our region's residence hall associations to promote further growth of the on campus residents at every college and university within our region.

SWACURH Regional Business Conference

The SWACURH Regional Business Conference (RBC) is a conference mainly for URHA Executives to attend and discuss policy changes in the region.

Sponsorship and Advertising Requests

URHA works with many organizations on campus and can provide funds to Residence Life and non-Residence Life student organizations. In addition to providing funds for events and programs on campus, we also provide advertising space on Table Tents to student organizations so they can reach as many people as possible.

Useful Links

University Residence Hall Association

University Housing and Dining

The University of Texas at Austin
summary: The University Residence Hall Association empowers, motivates, and advocates for on-campus residents by providing them with skills and resources in order for them to excel and positively impact the campus community.

name: University Securities Investment Team
description:
In 2010, our founders sought to create an interactive investing experience for students that was both engaging and challenging, without being exclusive to just a small number of select students. USIT was created to make an investing education open and available to all students who wish to learn how to invest. By giving any student the opportunity to pitch stocks and participate in engaging market discussions to support our real-money portfolio, members learn to become investors and business leaders.

Website: www.texasusit.org

Instagram: @texasusit

Facebook: University Securities Investment Team
summary: USIT is the premier organization for investing. Our members manage a ~$60,000 real-money portfolio. All grades and majors are welcome to join.

name: University Theatre Guild
description:
The University Theatre Guild is the oldest student-run theatre organization at UT Austin. We do plays and musicals for all to enjoy!

We put on two full-length productions and one children's theatrical production each semester, with members participating in every aspect of creation and implementation. This includes performing, directing, technical theatre, publicity, etc. We also have numerous social activities and gatherings throughout the year that allow our members to get to know one another and find their family here at UT Austin!

Every level of theatre experience is welcome, from newbies to professionals! 

UTG serves as a creative outlet for theatre lovers of all kinds who want to surround themselves with like-minded and fun individuals. UTG is completely autonomous in its funding, management, and production.

- - - - - 

The University Theatre Guild meets on Wednesdays at 7pm. Feel free to stop by and check us out!


Our UTG Discord Server is where we primarily communicate. https://discord.gg/msdkn5mJUS

Follow us on Instagram @TexasUTG to see more of what we do!
summary: University Theatre Guild serves as a creative outlet for theatre lovers of all experience levels who want to surround themselves with like-minded individuals. UTG is completely autonomous in its funding, management, and production.

name: UT Dance Team
description:
The University of Texas Dance Team is the official competitive dance team at the University of Texas at Austin. It is a spirit and athletic organization under the Texas Recreational Sports Program (RecSports). Focusing on jazz and hip-hop, the team typically hosts a seasonal showcase, appears in multiple events on the Forty Acres as representatives of UT Austin, and attends local and national
competitions, including NDA Camp, ADTS College Classic, and NDA Nationals.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=senGYej7oHY
summary: As the official competitive dance team for the University of Texas at Austin, we work to represent our university with pride and grace at various public performances around Austin, as well as multiple dance competitions nationwide.

name: ISPOR
description:
ISPOR—The Professional Society for Health Economics and Outcomes Research (HEOR)—is an international, multistakeholder, nonprofit organization. The Society is the leading source for scientific conferences, peer-reviewed and MEDLINE®-indexed publications, good practices guidance, education, collaboration, and tools/resources in the HEOR field. This student chapter of ISPOR is composed of students who share a passion for the study of pharmacoeconomics and outcomes research. 

ISPOR is dedicated to improving healthcare decision-making. We believe that every healthcare decision should be informed by the best scientific research. Toward that goal, the Society’s mission is to advance health economics and outcomes research excellence to improve decision-making for health globally.

ISPOR has annual meetings where researchers/students can present research and participate in forums/sessions learning the latest in health economics, statistics, regulatory, patient health outcomes, population science, and much more. The organization is also very active in setting guidelines for research design and resource for public health policy. If you are interested in health outcomes and/or pharmacoeconomic research, consider joining us!
summary: We provide a platform to participate and develop skills in Health Economics, Health Services and Outcomes Research.

name: Austin Texas Nursing Students' Association
description:
The ultimate purpose of Austin Texas Nursing Students' Association is to bring Texas nursing students closer together while providing as many opportunities to give back to our community as possible. We provide a variety of service opportunities, a platform for meaningful social connections, as well as numerous networking opportunities.

 


UTNSA also represents nursing students in the Senate and Student Government and serves as the official liaison between students and the legislative authority. Similarly, we send students to state and national conventions each year. We also reach out to encourage and inspire the youth of Austin ISD (Elementary, Middle, and High School) to the healthcare related field. No matter what you are seeking, there are individualized opportunities for you within our organization. 

 

Want more info?  Interested in joining? Please email utnsaofficial@gmail.com and/or check us out on Facebook!
summary: The ultimate purpose of Austin Texas Nursing Students' Association is to bring Texas nursing students closer together while providing as many opportunities to give back to our community as possible.

name: Speak Easy
description: Speakeasy (formerly Toastmasters) provides a welcoming and friendly environment for people of all backgrounds, years, and majors to improve their public speaking and communication skills. We meet every Friday from 6-7:15pm at RLP 1.102. Check out our Linktree come visit any weekly meeting to learn more!
summary: Speakeasy (formerly Toastmasters) helps people improve their public speaking and communication skills. The club hosts weekly meetings featuring speaking practice. This club is a chapter of Toastmasters International and is open to all students.

name: TX Votes
description:
TX Votes is a nonpartisan, non-advocacy student organization dedicated to increasing civic engagement across UT's campus. We accomplish this goal through tabling, classroom presentations, social media outreach, hosting civic events, distributing registration and election information directly to UT professors, and encouraging other student groups to involve their members in the civic process through our Civic Engagement Alliance. Just this past fall semester (2024), as one of the leading nonpartisan voter engagement organizations on campus, we've visited over 221 classrooms (both in-person and virtually), registered over three thousand UT students to vote, and hosted several fun celebrations for major civic holidays including; National Voter Registration Day and National Voter Education Week. TX Votes is made up of students majoring in a wide array of degrees from Biology, English, Computer Science, Government, and Political Communication to Studio Art. We are nonpartisan, so we will never tell you who or what to vote for. Just that you should vote!

Students who participate in TX Votes have the opportunity to run for elected positions their first semester, apply to represent UT Austin at the Harvard IOP National Campaign Conference, train to be Volunteer Deputy Registrars (VDRs), and meet local elections officials. TX Votes graduates have gone on to attend work for Representative Hinojosa, Senator Watson, Round Rock Independent School District, Peace Corps, TriNet, Campus Vote Project, political fundraising companies, Indeed, Google, Texas Exes, and even create their own consulting companies. They have also gone on to attend law school/graduate school at William and Mary University, UC Berkley, Princeton University, Harvard University, Texas A&M University, and the New York School of Interior Design. 

We love what we do and want to welcome you into our voting family. We hope you share a passion for civic engagement on the Forty Acres! If you have any questions please do not hesitate to reach out. You can find us at:

Email: txvotes@gmail.com

Instagram: instagram.com/txvotes_cea/
summary: TX Votes is a nonpartisan student organization dedicated to increasing civic engagement across UT's campus through voter registration, tabling, partnering with other student orgs, hosting fun civic events, and so much more!

name: Vietnamese Students Association
description: The Vietnamese Students Association was established at the University of Texas on October 12, 1972. Today, we are one of the largest student-run organizations on campus. We welcome people of all backgrounds to join VSA and to see what we're all about. As a social and cultural student organization, VSA is a great way to meet new people and to make lifelong friends. Being a student-run organization, VSA puts great emphasis on the community, the Vietnamese culture, sports, and the Texas VSA family.
summary: VSA's purpose is to facilitate a friendly environment between Vietnamese students, other international students, and the University community. Aiming to exhibit Vietnamese culture whenever possible and to advance growth in individuals for the future.

name: Women in Aerospace for Leadership and Development
description:
WIALD (pronounced “wild”) is a student-run organization at the University of Texas at Austin. The percentage of women applying for the undergraduate Aerospace Engineering program at The University of Texas at Austin is historically low. In an effort to alleviate this problem, a group of women in engineering came together during the fall semester of 2009 and, with the help of faculty members and the Women in Engineering Program (WEP), formed Women in Aerospace for Leadership Development (WIALD). WIALD aims to facilitate the development of leadership skills for women in all undergraduate engineering majors. We strive to attract and retain more women in engineering by making group activities fun, exciting, and ultimately valuable in achieving long-term career goals. Each year we focus on a different hands-on project. In the past, we have launched a payload into space, sent a balloon into the upper atmosphere, flown on NASA’s “Vomit Comet,” and modified a drone to carry a glider which autonomously navigated to pre-determined waypoints.

COME JOIN OUR MEETINGS! Meetings will be announced bi-weekly on our social media!

For more information check out our website at http://sites.utexas.edu/wiald/
summary: Organization providing aerospace industry project-based leadership and development opportunities for undergraduate engineering students. Our vision is for more women to graduate with confidence and experience in the field of aerospace engineering.

name: Women in Business Association
description:
Women in Business Association (WBA) strives to increase members’ knowledge of and involvement in the world of business in numerous ways. First, we invite educated and experienced professionals from a variety of different fields, including finance, accounting, management information systems, supply chain, technology, and more. Members are encouraged to interact with our invited speakers to promote rapport within our organization as well as gain an increased understanding of what it is like to have a business career. We also place a heavy emphasis on professional development through networking opportunities, technical workshops, and informative events that can help guide undergraduates through the recruiting process. In addition, we focus on building personal connections between our members through unique social, fundraising, and community service events, so members can build a circle of friends and mentors. As a note, we are open to ALL majors, generally anybody who is interested in pursuing a career in business and are gender-inclusive. 

Please email membership.texaswba@gmail.com for any questions regarding membership.

For more information, visit our site: texaswba.org and check us out on Instagram (@texaswba).
summary: Women in Business Association (WBA) strives to increase our members' knowledge of and involvement in the world of business by inviting educated and experienced professionals to speak and share advice with our organization.

name: Women in Computer Science
description: To provide opportunities for professional and personal growth through networking and events on technical and career-oriented topics.
summary: To provide a community for Women in Computer Science to meet other women, network with top technology companies, and encourage more women to join the technology field.

name: Women in Mechanical Engineering
description:
Women in Mechanical Engineering (WME) is a tight-knit community dedicated to helping Mechanical Engineers at UT Austin expand their networks and create community, develop improved professional and academic skills, and ultimately uplift the next generation of female leaders in STEM.

 

Interested in more information? Email texaswme@gmail.com

Interested in becoming a partner? Email texaswme.corporate@gmail.com
summary: WME exists to provide professional, academic, and personal resources to its members. We create opportunities for women in the Mechanical Engineering Department to build community and network with other undergraduate, graduate, and faculty ME women.

name: Women in Medicine
description:
Women in Medicine supports the professional interests of women at UT pursuing careers in health-related fields. This organization unites future doctors, physician's assistants, pharmacists, nurses, dentists, veterinarians, physical therapists, and researchers. Socials, volunteering, trips to medical schools, charity events, and opportunities to converse with health professionals are just a few of WIM's many benefits. 

Come join Women in Medicine at our meetings that start at ***6:00 PM!!!*** It says the start time is 5 pm because we booked the room an hour early to set up, but feel free to stop by and hang out with us!

For information on how to join, point requirements, and events, please visit our website. This is where we keep the most up-to-date information! https://wimutaustin.wixsite.com/website
summary: Women in Medicine supports the professional interests of women at UT pursuing careers in health-related fields.

name: Women in Psychology
description:
Women in Psychology is built on the principles of career exploration in psychology and related fields, connection with motivated individuals, and discovery of UT resources. All degrees and genders are welcome, with ongoing membership enrollment throughout the semester. Come check us out!

Women in Psychology strives to:

  • Create a social and friendly community that supports and encourages the professional development and advancement of students in psychology.
  • Help students learn about the diverse professional options in psychology and help them in preparation for graduate school by inviting speakers from the psychology community including professors, graduate students, and other professionals.
  • Create networks between members and professionals at the university and in the community
  • Offer opportunities to interact with each other and the community through volunteer activities and socials
  • Promote discussion of relevant professional and academic topics
  • Provide assistance in the discovery of research opportunities and positions in research labs

Through the following activities: guest speakers, WIP families, professional development workshops, volunteer activities, social events, advice on classes, and group discussion.

Instagram: @wip_utaustin

Groupme link: Join Here

Sign up for our weekly newsletter: Join Here
summary: We are built on the principles of career exploration in psychology and related fields, connection with motivated individuals, and discovery of UT resources. All degrees and genders are welcome, with ongoing membership enrollment throughout the semester.

name: Women's Law Caucus
description:
Women’s Law Caucus seeks to propel women into successful roles as both students at Texas Law and as future lawyers. Our mission is to create an environment of support for women in the law school and enhance the role of women in the legal profession. We value professionalism, community service, compassion, and inclusion.

Women’s Law Caucus provides opportunities for members to meet classmates, faculty, and legal professionals through mentorship programs, monthly breakfasts, roundtable lunches, and networking events. In addition, Women’s Law Caucus provides academic support through its outline bank and strives to serve the local community through pro bono opportunities and community service.
summary: Women’s Law Caucus seeks to propel women into successful roles as both students at Texas Law and as future lawyers. Our mission is to create an environment of support for women in the law school and enhance the role of women in the legal profession.

name: Zeta Beta Tau
description: To foster and perpetuate ties of brotherhood, to advance the ideals and purposes of Zeta Beta Tau fraternity, to encourage and promote scholarship, and to strengthen and develop leadership.
summary: To foster and perpetuate ties of brotherhood, to advance the ideals and purposes of Zeta Beta Tau fraternity, to encourage and promote scholarship, and to strengthen and develop leadership....

name: Zeta Psi Fraternity
description:
Zetes come together to have fun, enjoy life, get the most out of college, better ourselves and form lifelong bonds of brotherhood. Despite all the fun that college brings, the main reason you came to school is to get an education with the hopes of that translating to success in life. However, a great many of the things you need in life—to get by, have fun, and ultimately succeed — aren’t taught in class. All our brothers can attest that you get more than the necessary knowledge, experience, and skill from Zeta Psi. Our goal, and the reason we exist, is to help you succeed by graduating with that edge you need in life, and have a really good time doing it. The result: better and more successful men with great memories, lifelong friends and the best experience you can have in college or beyond.

 

Zeta Psi gives our brothers a competitive edge for life. Being active in the local chapter gives you an opportunity to learn many management skills which can be applied to the business world, to grow as leaders, to and find your strengths. All the while, you and your chapter are being supported by our central office and staff, and local and regional alumni.
summary: Zeta Psi prides itself on being an elite social fraternity on the UT campus and on the national stage. Our goals are to help men mature academically, socially, and professionally and to help them develop the skills necessary for success in life.

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
description:
The object of this organization shall be to promote the purpose and programs of the fraternity; to function as a constructive, vital force in the lives of its members; to cooperate with the local alumnae; and to serve as a cooperative, active part of the campus community.

Zeta strives to increase breast cancer education and awareness through it's philanthropic efforts. Our motto is "Seek the Noblest."
summary: The object of this organization shall be to promote the purpose and programs of the fraternity; to function as a constructive, vital force in the lives of its members; to cooperate with the local alumnae; and to serve as a cooperative, active part of t..

name: Austin Chapter of SIAM
description:
To promote interaction between members of the applied mathematics community at UT Austin, across departments, institutes, and professional marks. To provide a forum for the discussion of applied and computational mathematics and pedagogy; To help members prepare for future careers in applied and computational mathematics; To promote publications, conferences, prizes and other opportunities offered by SIAM.

 

Join our mailing list to stay updated on events: https://utlists.utexas.edu/sympa/info/siam
summary: To promote interaction between members of the applied mathematics community at UT Austin, across departments, institutes, and professional marks. To provide a forum for the discussion of applied and computational mathematics and pedagogy; To help membe..

name: AWAKE (Anointed Worship Awakes Koreans and Everyone)
description:
AWAKE is a Christian organization leads campus worships for Korean Students, faculty, staff and all . The worship service is open to the public. AWAKE functions as a bridge among Korean Churches and ministries. It provides an opportunity to unite in Jesus Christ and extend the roof from local churches to the city of Austin.

Contact
Email: awake.texas@gmail.com

Facebook Group Website
https://www.facebook.com/groups/257972327652980/

Facebook LIKE Page
https://www.facebook.com/pages/AWAKE/682719001849839

Instagram @awake_utaustin
summary: AWAKE is a Christian organization leads campus worships for Korean Students, faculty, staff and all . The worship service is open to the public. AWAKE functions as a bridge among Korean Churches and ministries.

name: Break Theory
description:
Please contact us on Instagram @BbreakTheory

Greater Austin Breaking Community: https://www.facebook.com/pages/The-B-Boy-Session

TEXAS UNION PRACTICE SCHEDULE
Texas Union, 2247 Guadalupe St
UNB 3.312, T-House Suite - 7:30PM to 9:30PM
summary: To promote the art of bboying (Breakdancing) to the UT and Austin community at large. Anyone who is interested in the dance is welcomed to join, as we have no membership fee or requirement. Members consist of students and faculty of all skill levels.

name: Seva Charities
description:
Seva Charities is an organization started by a group of passionate and driven students at The University of Texas at Austin. It was created to make a large impact not only in the local Austin community, but also throughout the world. We find charities whose values and missions line up with ours and put on events to raise money for these causes, both local and global. We strive to create tangible, sustainable change in this world also through volunteering in our local Austin community. We truly do believe in the power of giving and that college students, if passionate enough, can make a difference!

check out our link tree: https://linktr.ee/sevacharities
summary: Seva Charities finds non-profit organizations/charities whose values and missions line up with ours and put on events to raise money for these causes, both local and global here at UT.

name: Orthodox Christian Campus Ministries at UT Austin
description: Our mission as the Orthodox Christian Campus Ministries at UT Austin is to develop and maintain a relationship with our Lord Jesus Christ through prayer and worship, to reach out to the community through service and evangelism, and to grow in the bond of love through fellowship with each other in the Holy Spirit.
summary: An organization for Oriental Orthodox and any other students to pursue their love for Jesus Christ and serve others.

name: Global Medical Brigades
description:
Global Brigades is the world's largest student-led movement for global health and holistic development. Global Medical Brigades aims to provide pre-professional students with the opportunity to gain real life experience in the medical field through the study and application of hands-on training in rural Latin America, Greece, and Ghana while offering their services to the community at large. GMB is the leading volunteer-based international medicine organization, with a dedication to both providing care to underserved populations, and sustainable public health development at UT Austin. Students work in pop-up medical clinics, provide public health education workshops, while also serving the Austin community as well.

We pride ourselves in hosting one of the most diverse groups of people at UT, made up of not only pre-health professions students, but also liberal arts, business, engineering and other majors. No previous training is required. In addition to all the volunteer opportunities, the organization offers ample resources to help prepare for crucial exams such as the MCAT; as well as guidance and support through upper classman and professional guest speakers in the healthcare field.

For more information and membership inquires, please visit us at https://texasgb.wixsite.com/texasbrigades and contact us through texasglobalmedicalbrigades@gmail.com. 

Meetings are bi-weekly every other Monday- 7pm.

*Note: Requesting membership through HornsLink will not make you an official member. Please contact us directly via email or Instagram (@texas.gmb) to join.
summary: Global Medical Brigades provides students with hands-on clinical and public health experience through service in rural communities across Latin America, Europe, and Africa, fostering sustainable healthcare development and long-term community partnerships

name: Global Medical Training
description:
We offer healthcare students the opportunity to obtain first-hand medical experience while providing free basic medical services to communities in Central America. Our workshops teach students basic clinical skills and basic Medical/Dental knowledge in preparation for and during clinics leading up to the trip. We also provide students with a first-hand experience of cultural, social, historical, and political life in these regions. In addition, we serve our local community through volunteering, hosting social events, and helping members with professional development. 

Check out our website: www.gmtlonghorn.com 

Check out our Instagram @gmtlonghorn

Check out one of our trip videos here: Here!
summary:
GMT UT is a non-profit organization that empowers students with the knowledge and awareness of the humanitarian need of medical assistance in underserved areas abroad.  Meetings are from 6-7pm every other Wednesday.
Webpage: www.gmtlonghorn.com

name: Indian Students Association
description: Established in 1954, the Indian Students Association (ISA) at The University of Texas at Austin is an umbrella organization that aims to connect all South Asian organizations on campus and continues to spread awareness of our beautiful culture through philanthropic ventures and large-scale events. Over the past years, we have donated more than $13,000 to charities all over the globe. Each the end of each semester, we hold Food For Thought, an event where we pass out free Indian food to UT students as they prepare for finals. In addition, we host Jeena, a national South Asian A Cappella competition, and TAAL, a dance competition for the South Asian dance teams at UT to showcase their talent to a crowd of over 1,500 people, on an annual basis.
summary: Established in 1954, the Indian Students Association (ISA) is currently one of the largest student organizations. We are an umbrella organization that aims to connect all South Asian organizations on campus.

name: Indonesian Student Association (PERMIAS)
description: The aim of PERMIAS Austin is to promote Indonesian culture in Austin and also to build, maintain, and reinforce the cultural identity and relationships between Indonesian students across the city of Austin.
summary: The aim of PERMIAS Austin is to promote Indonesian culture in Austin and also to build, maintain and reinforce the cultural identity and relationships between Indonesian students across the city of Austin.

name: Institute of Electrical & Electronics Engineers
description: IEEE UT is a student branch of the international IEEE organization. We are a professional, academic, and social development organization that organizes corporate-sponsored talks, informational workshops, and recreational events aimed to enrich members’ experience at UT. IEEE members benefit from personal and professional support through mentorship from upperclassmen and alumni, opportunities for involvement within Cockrell and the greater Austin community, and relationships with classmates and peers.
summary: Our mission is to foster the growth of a community of electrical engineering students within the Cockrell school through social, educational, and informational events that provide opportunities for academic, personal, and professional development.

name: Institute of Latin American Studies Stu. Assn.
description: The Institute of Latin American Studies Student Association is an association of students in the Institute and all University of Texas students who are interested in and have a broad range of views on Latin America. While the Primary purpose of this association is to respond to the concerns and the educational needs of LLILAS students, events are open to the entire UT community. The nature of ILASSA activities is established by its members and includes educational forums, service projects, fund raisers, and social events.
summary: The Institute of Latin American Studies Student Association is an association of students in the Institute and all University of Texas students who are interested in and have a broad range of views on Latin America.

name: Intellectual Property Law Society
description: To create a forum for law students interested in intellectual property to meet and share ideas and goals.

name: Lions Club at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
The Lions Club at the University of Texas at Austin is more than just a service organization – we’re a family united by a shared passion for giving back and making a positive impact. We offer a wide range of service opportunities, organizing events every Saturday and Sunday where members can engage in hands-on projects to help those in need. Beyond our service work, we host fundraisers to support both local and global causes.

As part of the world’s largest service organization with over 1.3 million members worldwide, we are proud to contribute to a global network dedicated to improving lives!
summary: We are Lions. We are a group of like-minded men and women aimed towards the betterment of our community, our campus, and our world.

name: Nritya Sangam Indian Dance Troupe
description:
Nritya Sangam Indian Dance Troupe is a not-for-profit organization at The University of Texas committed to serving its community through its passion for Indian classical dance and its philanthropic spirit. NS was founded in 2002 by two students who shared a love for dance. The phrase "Nritya Sangam" means "unity of dance," which is exemplary of the type of students selected to join Nritya Sangam; our members represent a diverse array of schools and styles of Indian dance. In addition, each member is an experienced dancer, carrying years of extensive training under the instruction of their respective teachers. We dance to keep up with an art form and talent we have spent so many years cultivating, and to preserve our age-old culture and traditions. 

The ancient Indian dance styles that we primarily derive inspiration from are Bharatanatyam and Kuchipudi. We hope to expose the mainstream community to the richness and depth of an art little-known in America through performances on the UT campus, as well as around Austin and other parts of the state and nation. The members of Nritya Sangam serve as ambassadors of traditional Indian fine arts on-campus, as the troupe is the only competitive Indian classical dance group at the university.
summary: Nritya Sangam Indian Dance Troupe is a not-for-profit organization at The University of Texas committed to serving its community through its passion for Indian classical dance and its philanthropic spirit.

name: Omega Chi Epsilon - Chemical Engineering
description:
The objective of Omega Chi Epsilon (OXE) is to recognize and promote high scholarship, original investigation, camaraderie, and professional service in the Department of Chemical Engineering at the University of Texas at Austin. The Society serves both undergraduate and graduate chemical engineering students nationwide.

To fulfill the goals of the Society, the Epsilon Chapter at the University of Texas at Austin invites students who are in the top 20 percent of their junior or senior class. Graduate students with a 3.5 GPA are also eligible for membership. New members go through a semester-long candidacy process to understand the values promoted by Omega Chi Epsilon. The Chapter also encourages meaningful student-faculty dialogue within the department.
summary: The objective of Omega Chi Epsilon (OXE) is to recognize and promote high scholarship, original investigation, camaraderie, and professional service in the McKetta Department of Chemical Engineering at the University of Texas at Austin.
