name: Minority Women Pursuing Law
description:
Welcome!

The purpose of UT's Minority Women Pursuing Law (MWPL) is to unite and motivate minority women who are interested in law, justice, and obtaining a law degree. Our organization aims to help prepare members not only for the admissions process but also for the types of experiences to expect during and after law school. We aspire to create long-lasting networks of minority women that will benefit everybody at each step of the way.

Make sure to log in to see any of our upcoming events!                                                                                                                                                                

ENGAGEMENTS

As a dedicated pre-law organization, we provide our members with valuable information and networking opportunities at each Engagement. Our Engagements are what we consider our general meetings. We begin with announcements related to MWPL, including updates & upcoming events. We then host a variety of speakers at every Engagement where members get to hear the stories and advice of admissions representatives, attorneys, and various other respected individuals in the field of law. Past speakers have included: 

  • Nicole Simmons - Director of Pipeline Outreach and Development & UT Law
  • Maria Amelia Calaf - Senior Partner at Wittliff | Cutter PLLC
  • Jackie Borcherding - Assistant District Attorney for Williamson County
  • Rhonda Evans - Former Assistant Prosecutor for the State of Ohio and current UT Professor
  • Heather Creed - Student Relations Director, Baylor School of Law 
  • The Honorable Aurora Martinez Jones - Travis County Civil Courts Judge and founder of MWPL
  • Diana Medina - Travis County Assistant District Attorney
  • The Honorable Darlene Byrne - Travis County Civil Courts Judge
  • Maria Rivera - Assistant Dean for Admissions and Financial Aid
  • Shavonne Henderson - Assistant Director of Policy Research at the Institute of Urban Policy Research & Analysis
  • Jamila Frone - Vice President of Litigation Practice at EarthJustice
  • Karintha Fenley- UT Austin Pre-law Advisor​

 

LAW SCHOOL TOURS

Minority Women Pursuing Law provides members with opportunities to tour different law schools. Law school students conduct these tours so that our members will get to see each school from a student’s perspective. MWPL members get to hear wisdom from law student panels and law school admission panels to receive insight into the application process at each law school. Members also sit in on various law classes to gain a firsthand experience of law school and envision themselves in a law school setting. MWPL members have toured: 

  • The University of Texas School of Law 
  • The University of Houston Law Center 
  • Cornell Law School
  • University of Michigan Law School
  • The University of California, Irvine School of Law
  • South Texas College of Law 
  • St. Mary’s University School of Law 
  • Southern Methodist University Dedman School of Law
  • Baylor Law School
  • Thurgood Marshall School of Law
  • The University of Tulsa College of Law
  • ​University of Pennsylvania Carey Law School

 

LAW FIRM TOURS​

MWPL provides law firm tours throughout the semester to give students an understanding of what life as a lawyer is like. Members explore the behind-the-scenes work, what each firm does under their specializations, and the differences between small, mid-range, and large law firms. We often enjoy a delicious spread, a tour of the law firm’s layout, and a panel of the firm’s most experienced attorneys. MWPL has had the privilege of touring:

  • Jackson Walker LLP
  • Bracewell LLP
  • Husch Blackwell LLP
  • Wittliff Cutter PLLC
  • Perkins Coie LLP

 

COURTHOUSE TOURS

MWPL tours courthouses to give members a peek into the inner workings of several courts in Austin. We have toured courthouses such as the Blackwell-Thurman Criminal Justice Center and Heman Marion Sweatt Travis County Courthouse. We have sat in on cases and met the presiding judges who then share their powerful, personal stories with our members. Past presiding judges include:

  • The Honorable Judge Orlinda Naranjo 
  • The Honorable Judge Brenda Kennedy 
  • The Honorable Judge Eric Shepperd​

 

LSAT PREP

By working with a variety of LSAT (Law School Admission Test) Prep companies, MWPL also hosts events that cover different topics relevant to pre-law students such as the law school application process, tips on creating strong applications, taking the LSAT, career skills, and more. Members get to hear from LSAT experts firsthand on the importance of an LSAT score to the overall application, how to best prepare for the exam, and when the best time to start studying is. MWPL offers Mock LSAT Exams, workshops focusing on each section of the LSAT, and Q&A events to answer all your questions.

 

MWPL also helps members study by offering discounts on preparation courses through several LSAT Prep Partners. For members who know that a course is for them, these are great deals to take advantage of. Additionally, MWPL offers scholarships that cover the full or partial costs of LSAT preparation courses, as well as vouchers that cover any costs in the law school application process as a whole, including but not limited to application fees, CAS report fees, transcript fees, LSAT registration fees, etc. MWPL hopes to alleviate the financial burdens on members in their LSAT journey. All details on available discounts and scholarships can be found here.

 

SOCIALS & VOLUNTEERING

MWPL offers members the chance to network and have fun with other talented minority women through socials and volunteer work. MWPL hosts various social events like movie nights, hiking trips, bowling, Galentine's Day celebrations, study night socials, end-of-semester banquets, and more. While we are a pre-law organization, we think it is important to take a break from our studies and enjoy our time as college students. Volunteering events are also an important outlet that allows our members to become involved in the local community with organizations like Clean Up Austin, Inside Books Project, and Letters Against Isolation. MWPL also strives to support other cultural and minority organizations on and off-campus by collaborating on projects like our Blood and Menstrual Products Drive in partnership with We Are Blood, Texas Women of Excellence, and the Austin Diaper Bank. 

 

MERCH & FUNDRAISERS

MWPL creates new merchandise to make sure our members can represent our organization with pride. Each semester, a new complementary shirt is designed for all official members to enjoy. Additionally, we provide several fundraising opportunities where people can purchase MWPL shirts from previous years and order unique merchandise designed each semester, including but not limited to sweatshirts, water bottles, tote bags, masks, and caps. MWPL also hosts fun fundraising events, such as our plant potting fundraiser where we potted several plants to sell on campus, or profit shares where we enjoy some food and relax. All profits go back to the organization to make the MWPL experience brighter and better. 

 

MENTORSHIP

As of 2018, MWPL has offered a free mentorship program that connects members with each other through a professional and social setting. This gives members a better chance to get to know others with the same interests, while also giving them the opportunity to learn from, grow with, and encourage one another. We aim to develop greater relationships with each other that we can take with us through our careers and beyond. Mentors are able to grow their leadership skills and commitment to our community while mentees are able to learn more about the pre-law path and seek wisdom from experienced MWPL members. We host exciting events exclusive for Mentorship Program members to attend together. MWPL also allows pairs to compete for an end-of-the-semester prize, which has included luxurious gift baskets, spa days, and sit-down lunches with prominent legal professionals.

 

PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT

MWPL provides members with opportunities to develop themselves professionally. Not only do our speakers share advice on how to navigate the professional world, but we offer more opportunities to network with legal professionals around Austin. For instance, we have hosted events where members can interact one-on-one with experienced legal professionals like at our Lunch With A Lawyer Event in partnership with the Travis County Women Lawyers’ Association as well as our Networking Event with the Women's Law Caucus at Texas Law. This has led to some wonderful community connections and MWPL is proud to say that all types of people in the legal community have reached out to MWPL looking for motivated members to work in their law firms, courthouses, law organizations, nonprofits, etc. Additionally, MWPL hosts professional clothing drives where our generous network of attorneys donate new or gently used pieces of professional clothing for anyone in need. We take great efforts to support our members through any interviews, applications, and networking events in hopes of setting the next generation of empowered attorneys up for success.
summary: To unite and motivate minority women at the University of Texas at Austin who are interested in law and going to law school....

name: Texas Orange Jackets
description:
Texas Orange Jackets is one of UT's oldest honorary service and spirit organization that uplifts nonbinary, transgender, gender-diverse, and female-identitying communities who seek to better The University and the City of Austin, to empower everyone to be leaders in their respective communities, and to promote a lifelong pursuit of the four core tenets: service, leadership, scholarship, and community. Members of Texas Orange Jackets serve as official hosts for The University of Texas at Austin and are committed to four core values:

  • Service that is consistent, meaningful, and impactful,
  • Leadership that exhibits selflessness, passion, and dedication,
  • Scholarship that encourages critical thought and diversity of perspective regarding societal issues, and 
  • Community that, as the foundational tenet, builds unwavering solidarity alongside all movements committed to change for the common good

Orange Jackets accepts new members in the fall semester only, as the new member initiation program is a full year (two semesters) commitment. There are two Orange Jacket application processes, and you can access them at our website, ojackets.org (this is necessary before requesting membership via Hornslink!) or follow us on all forms of social media @txorangejackets.

Our advisor is Dean Henderson.

name: Society of Physics Students
description:
We are the University of Texas at Austin Society of Physics Students! We aim to advance our love and knowledge of physics through weekly meetings that feature UT Physics professors, showcase undergraduate physics research, and connect physics students together. Additionally, we aim to cultivate an interest in physics in our broader academic and local communities by participating in a multitude of departmental, social, and volunteer events like Gone to CNS, Welcome Week, Girl Day, and Prospective Students Day.

 

SPS is open to ANYONE with a general interest in Physics, and our weekly meetings will be on Mondays at 5:30-6:30. Feel free to contact us with any questions at spsofficers@gmail.com. Additionally, the best way to keep up with SPS (weekly meeting information, volunteer events, and much more!) is to join our mailing list at https://utlists.utexas.edu/sympa/subscribe/sps?previous_action=info.
summary: We are a student-led organization open to anyone with a love of physics. We feature year-round guest lectures & talks from professors and others in the industry, information sessions, socials, and competitions.

name: Society of Women Engineers
description:
At the core of everything UT SWE does is our mission to create a welcoming community that allows women engineering students to develop as leaders.  With our membership of students spanning all engineering disciplines, we hope that each member can find in SWE a community of friends, a professional network, and a wealth of resources to help them achieve their full potential as engineers and leaders.

UT SWE hopes to create an environment focused on inclusivity and diversity and to inspire women engineers by pushing them to empower themselves and others. SWE will focus on fostering relationships with minority engineering student organizations, developing more opportunities for member leadership development, and building lasting relationships within and outside our section. We're looking forward to co-hosting events with other minority in engineering organizations, expanding a leadership development program called Project SWE, and building lasting relationships with high schools and SWE alumni in the Austin community. 

We hold a variety of weekly events that can be broken down into four categories: advancement, outreach, professional development, and socials. Some of our signature events include Ice Cream Social, Networking Night, Leadership Retreat, and our K-12 Camps. Our general meetings occur every other Tuesday from 7:00 PM - 8:00 PM.

Stay connected with UT SWE by following us on Instagram and visiting our website to learn more about our organization and upcoming events.
summary: To strengthen and empower women engineering students by: * creating a sense of identity and community, * providing resources for women engineers, *encouraging females to engage in opportunities and * enhancing leadership and professional skills

name: STEEL Dance Company
description: Steel Dance Company is an entirely student led organization at The University of Texas at Austin. Our main goal is to offer dancers at UT an opportunity to dance and perform throughout their college experience. Every Fall semester, we host Project Linking the Arts, an event which showcases various art groups around UT. In the Spring, we host our Spring Show to present our self-choreographed dances.
summary: Steel Dance Company's main goal is to offer dancers at UT an opportunity to dance and perform throughout their college experience.

name: Student Bar Association
description: The student body of the law school is organized as a Student Bar Association, the membership of which includes all students in the school. The officers of the association are the president, vice president of operations, vice president of programming, vice president of communications, financial director, and Board of Governors. The organization coordinates the intellectual, cultural, social, and community service activities of the student body. Its goals are to unify the students and direct them toward worthy ends and to enhance cooperation and understanding among students.
summary: The student body of the law school is organized as a Student Bar Association, the membership of which includes all students in the school. The Board of Governors is in charge of the association and acts as the student government of the law school.

name: Student Council for Exceptional Children
description:
Student Council for Exceptional Children (SCEC) is a student organization at UT Austin that advocates for adults and children with special needs. As a group we promote awareness and inclusion by participating in events dedicated to these individuals, such as Rosedale Ride, Mary Lee Foundation, DSACT Buddy Walk, Growing Roots and many more. 

General meetings are held every other week in the Sanchez Building (SZB) and various volunteer events occur throughout the semester. 

Our organization is open to students in all majors, so come to our next meeting to see what we’ll be up to this year!
summary: Our purpose is to advance the education of individuals with disabilities and bring awareness to the inequalities that these individuals face on a daily basis.

name: Student Government
description:
Please Do **NOT** Request Membership through HornsLink! If you would like to stay connected, please follow us on instagram (@ut_sg). 

The University of Texas at Austin Student Government (UTSG/SG) is the official association for the expression of student opinion. It is our privilege to support responsible student participation in university policy and decision-making processes in order to enhance the quality and scope of the student experience. We are responsible for the transparent and effective use of student fees to promote student welfare, and for providing leadership development opportunities for UT students.

 

The Executive Branch:

The Executive Branch is comprised of the Executive Board and staff, and policy directors. Together, this branch works together to carry out the Executive Alliance platform and execute programming related to their advocacy areas of interest to serve the student body. Learn more about the executive branch here.

 

The Legislative Branch:

The Legislative Branch consists of a legislative body known as the Assembly. The Assembly is made up of elected student representatives from each college within the university that pass legislation to represent the official voice of the student body and define official Student Government policies, positions, and stances in matters directly affecting students per the UTSG Constitution and governing documents. Learn more about the legislative branch here.

 

The Judicial Branch:

The Judicial Branch is charged with interpreting the Student Government Constitution, the Student Government Code of Rules and Procedures, and substantive rules, controlling authorities, regulations, or bylaws passed by the Student Government Assembly. This branch fulfills the vital role of ensuring that Student Government complies with these governing documents. Learn more about the judicial branch here.
summary: UT Austin Student Government (UTSG) is the official association for the expression of student opinion.  We advocate for student interests to high level administration, and work to improve campus life through initiatives, events, and policy advocacy.

name: Student Health Advisory Committee
description:
Since 1997, the Student Health Advisory Committee (SHAC) has provided an essential link between University Health Services (UHS) and the entire UT student body. We advocate for prevalent student health concerns on campus, design and implement creative initiatives to promote UHS, and actively work to empower the student body on issues regarding their health and wellness.

Why choose SHAC?

As a member of the Student Health Advisory Committee, you will have the opportunity to:

  • Develop deeper insight into health care in a university setting
  • Serve on UHS committees and work directly with UHS leadership
  • Represent the entire UT student body at regional and national levels
  • Participate in interview processes for potential UHS health care providers and staff
  • Enhance leadership skills
  • Strengthen professional development
  • Build connections and lasting friendships with fellow students and the UHS staff
summary: The Student Health Advisory Committee provides an essential link between University Health Services and the entire UT student body.

name: Student National Pharmaceutical Association
description: The Student National Pharmaceutical Association is an education and service association of student pharmacists and pre-pharmacy students concerned about pharmacy and other health professions with a focus on minority students and communities. The purpose of SNPhA is to plan, organize, coordinate, and execute programs geared toward improvement of the health, social, and educational environment of the community; while providing opportunities for professional development in pharmacy.
summary: The Student National Pharmaceutical Association is an education and service association of pharmacy students and high school affiliate members, concerned about pharmacy and other health professions.

name: GSC Student Leadership Committee
description: The Gender and Sexuality Collective strives to create a safe place on campus for LGBTQ+ students. We have weekly social hours, field trips, and advocacy events. There are no requirements for membership, so all that we ask is that you participate whenever you can. Join our GroupMe to have access to meeting discussions and more direct announcements! Please email gscstudentleadershipcommittee@gmail.com or DM @gscatut on instagram with any questions or if you're interested in being on our officer board!!
summary: The Gender and Sexuality Collective strives to create a safe place on campus for LGBTQ+ students.

name: Students in Medical Laboratory Science
description:
Join our GroupMe and Discord!

SMLS is an organization of students from the University of Texas at Austin who are interested in becoming Medical Laboratory Scientist/Technologist. We strive to bring awareness to MLS by providing information about the various hospital programs and what it entails to be a Medical Scientist/Technologist. 

Visit our website for more information about the MLS degree at UT Austin and MLS as a career!

Join our GroupMe, Discord, and/or Instagram to stay the most up-to-date on all of our events. We have exciting events planned for the 2025-26 school year and can't wait to see you there!
summary: To increase awareness about CLS/MT as a profession. To promote the ideas of scholarship, leadership, service and fellowship. - to provide students the opportunity to realize their academic and leadership potentials, to provide opportunities to particip...

name: Supply Chain Management Student Organization
description:
SCMSO exists to promote the Supply Chain Management major, inform students about career opportunities related to supply chain management, logistics, and operations, and provide a welcoming network of fellow students passionate about supply chain management.

Through various professional development opportunities and weekly general meetings, SCMSO educates its members in the recruiting process for supply chain internships and full-time
positions, and connects its members with corporate leaders and talent acquisition specialists.

SCMSO Contact Information (Follow Insta/Slack for up to date info!):

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/scmso_ut/
 
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/scmso
 
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/230001493715467/
 
Website: https://txscmso.org/
summary: An organization for students studying or just interested in Supply Chain, Logistics, and Operations to connect with fellow peers and career opportunities. Come to our Wednesday meetings to meet new friends with similar interests!

name: Synapse
description:
Synapse is the all-encompassing undergraduate neuroscience organization at UT, focused on providing neuroscience students and enthusiasts a place to deepen their knowledge of the brain, build community, and prepare for a career in neuroscience (whether that’s in medicine, research, industry, or beyond)! We have a mix of general meetings with talks from neuroscience faculty around UT and other cool learning opportunities, student-driven journal clubs, and social events for our members.

General meetings are every other Monday from 5:30 - 7pm (speaker comes at 6pm) in BUR 108 - be ready for free pizza! We have no membership dues or fees, and students from all majors are welcome to join!

If you'd like to connect with us, join us here on Hornslink! Then, check out our website, join our email list, and join our Discord server to stay in touch and get the latest on meetings, socials, and everything else! Everything Synapse is linked at https://linktr.ee/synapsetexas.
summary: We aim to cultivate a welcoming space in which students—brought together by a shared appreciation for the brain—find social belonging and academic guidance at a large university. In short, we are all about neuroscience and community.

name: Taiwanese American Students Association
description: TASA is a social and cultural student organization that celebrates Taiwanese culture while providing a fun atmosphere to make long-lasting connections and friendships. We hold general meetings every other week, as well as a mix of social, cultural, and philanthropic events. If you join, you’ll be sorted into a family, providing a close-knit community within the organization.
summary: To provide cultural identity to Taiwanese Americans, Asian Americans, and those interested in Taiwanese culture by offering social, cultural, and philanthropic events and facilitating a close-knit student community.

name: Tau Beta Pi - Engineering
description:
Tau Beta Pi is an interdisciplinary engineering honor society that recognizes students with high academic achievement as well as a commitment to personal and professional integrity. Established in 1885, TBP is the second oldest collegiate honor society in America.

Founded in 1916, our Texas Alpha chapter promotes community service and fosters a spirit of liberal culture in the Cockrell School of Engineering. We also provide members with various speakers from the engineering field throughout each semester. The Texas Alpha chapter invites the top 1/8 of juniors and 1/5 of seniors by credit hours every fall and spring semester.

We do not use HornsLink for communication, registration, or admission. Please contact us through our email.
summary: Tau Beta Pi is an interdisciplinary engineering honor society that recognizes students with distinguished scholarship and exemplary character. Established in 1885, TBP is the second-oldest collegiate honor society.

name: Tau Beta Sigma - Beta Gamma Chapter
description:
Tau Beta Sigma (TBS) is the National Honorary Band Sorority for college bands. We are a co-educational organization, focusing on service to collegiate bands and encouraging the advancement of women in music. TBS promotes an appreciation of band music through service, recognition, and leadership development.

The UT chapter achieves this purpose through activities such as serving water at games and carrying med kits for the Longhorn Band. Our chapter is also active in the Austin community, volunteering at events like the Austin Marathon or playing alongside high school band members at their football games.

 

Is Tau Beta Sigma strictly service?

While service is our main priority, we make time for social activities too! We have a holiday party, family brunch, and various other social events.

How large is Tau Beta Sigma?

The Sorority was founded in 1946 and has grown to roughly 150 chapters nationwide! Our chapter - Beta Gamma - is consistently one of the largest chapters.
summary: We are a co-educational service sorority who serves the University of Texas Longhorn Band. We promote equality and diversity, such as empowering women in music!

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon Fraternity
description: To form a brotherhood which is conducive to the betterment of its members in the educational, fraternal, and social aspects of their lives.
summary: To form a brotherhood which is conducive to the betterment of its members in the educational, fraternal, and social aspects of their lives....

name: Teachers of Tomorrow
description:
Teachers of Tomorrow is an organization designed to provide resources, opportunities, and mentors for aspiring educators within the university. As an organization, we work to serve our community and assist other schools or educational programs around us in the area.

Our purpose: 

  • to develop a community of College of Education students who share similar professional goals
  • foster the relationships developed  through discussions, mentoring and volunteer work
  • supplement the education of elementary future teachers with workshops, speakers, and social events
  • encourage the habit of professional development among future educators and builds student and faculty relations within the College of Education
  • work through the Education Council to assist in the improvement of academic standards 
  • introduce members to classroom teachers and administrators working in the field of education
TOT is open to all majors. Meetings and socials are held bi-weekly in the Sanchez Building (SZB)!
summary: Teachers of Tomorrow is a community of College of Education students who hope to one day change the world. Our time together is spent developing relationships and professional experiences through panels, discussions, and volunteer work in schools.

name: The Tejas Club
description: One of the oldest student organizations at the University of Texas, the Tejas Club was founded in 1925 on the principles of friendship, leadership, and scholarship.
summary: One of the oldest organizations at the University of Texas, the Tejas Club was founded in 1925 on the principles of friendship, leadership, and scholarship.

name: Terry Scholar Student Association
description: The Terry Foundation is a Houston-based scholarship program founded to provide full scholarships to outstanding Texas high school graduates and transfer students for attendance at public colleges and universities within the state. As the Terry Scholar Student Association, our mission is to unify Terry Scholars, promote social and academic health of freshmen and continuing scholars, and honor the foundation by giving back to the community.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote leadership, service, and academic success while serving as a unifying center of engagement and information for Terry scholars on the University of Texas at Austin campus.

name: Texas 4000 for Cancer
description: Texas 4000 for Cancer at the University of Texas at Austin empowers students to take a role in the fight against cancer through fitness, philanthropy, and community engagement. The organization will function through the academic term to cultivate student leaders in conjunction with the world’s longest annual charity bike, which will take place in the May to July time frame. The student organization and the summer ride team are separate entities.
summary: Texas 4000 for Cancer at the University of Texas at Austin empowers students to take a role in the fight against cancer through fitness, philanthropy, and community engagement through a student organization and summer ride team.

name: Texas Advertising Group
description:
TAG's mission is to help students stand out by providing them with a network of peers and professionals who will help them pave a path to a successful career in advertising.

 

TAG dues have closed for the semester, but we’re just getting started! Follow us on Instagram or join our GroupMe to stay up to date on next semester’s recruitment and events.
summary: TAG's missions is to help students stand out by providing them with a network of peers and professionals who will help them pave a path to a successful career in advertising.

name: Texas Asian Pan-Hellenic Council
description:
The Texas Asian Pan-Hellenic Council governs the 10 Asian-interest Greek-letter organizations on campus, promoting the values of leadership, scholarship, and service within the Asian American community.

Notable events include an information session (Greek Sneak Peek) and an talent showcase (Greek Show Out) during semesterly recruitment, as well as Asian Cultural Expo in the spring. These events connect the broader campus community with Asian Greek life to celebrate Asian American culture and promote interest in these organizations.

As of Spring 2026, TAPC comprises of ten Asian-interest Greek letter organizations:

Sororities:​

  • alpha Kappa Delta Phi (aKDPhi)

  • Alpha Sigma Rho (ASR)

  • Delta Phi Omega (DPO)

  • Kappa Phi Gamma (KPhiG)

  • Kappa Phi Lambda (KPL)

  • Sigma Phi Omega (SPO)

Fraternities:

  • Delta Epsilon Psi (DEPsi)

  • Gamma Beta (GB)

  • Lambda Phi Epsilon (LPhiE)

  • Omega Phi Gamma (OPhiG)
summary: The Texas Asian Pan-Hellenic Council is the governing body for Asian-interest Greek letter organizations at the University of Texas at Austin. The council is dedicated to promoting leadership, scholarship, service, and Asian awareness.

name: Texas Ballroom
description:
When: 

  • Mon: 8-10 PM Standard Basic
  • Tues: 6-8 PM Country Basic, 8-10 PM Competition Country
  • Wed: 8-10 PM Social
  • Thurs: 6-8 PM Competition Latin Ballroom, 8-10 PM Latin Basic

Where: Bellmont Hall room 402 (or 628 for special events)

 

To Join: General membership dues are $65 for the semester, with an additional $50 fee if you plan to compete at ballroom and country competitions. These dues get you access to ALL of our classes, socials, events, etc. You can join at any point throughout the semester - just show up to any class and speak with an officer, and we will help you get set up!

We host a Free Week at the start of every semester - this is a full week of free classes before any formal joining process, so keep an eye out for us on Speedway, or on our socials for information about that each semester! 

 

Our mission:

Texas Ballroom strives to foster an appreciation for partner dancing at every skill level, further the art and sport of ballroom dance, and maintain an engaging social atmosphere in which any individual can learn and grow as a dancer. Competitive ballroom, Latin, and country dance are completely new and foreign to most undergraduate students in the United States; the most common exposure to any partner dance being Dancing with the Stars or the occasional cotillion. Our mission is to introduce this rich and vast world to the University of Texas community. We teach dancers from the ground up, starting with beginner classes that focus on teaching partner dance as a social activity, accompanied with social events and team building activities. Our beginner classes give new members a broad introduction to many different styles of ballroom dance, and serve as a foundation for a long term social hobby and skill. The social events and friendly atmosphere provide a community of supportive peers that is crucial for any student during their time at university. Many members are satisfied at this level, and our organization is honored to have helped instill in them some of the valuable lessons that come from dance. Other members decide to pursue competitive partner dance. For these dancers, we provide advanced technique and performance coaching, as well as a team atmosphere that inspires confidence and perseverance. Texas Ballroom attempts to make the barrier of entry to competitive ballroom dance as low as possible. We organize travel, lodging, registration, and partner matching, and we keep a collection of donated costumes and shoes for our members. Through our club, competitive dancers also get coaching from some of the best professional dance coaches in the Austin area. Whether casual or competitive, our members develop an appreciation for dance that lasts a lifetime, and form social groups to support them in college and beyond.
summary: Texas Ballroom is a UT Sport Club which fosters appreciation for the art and sport of ballroom dance. Our members range from beginners to experienced competitors. We balance elite training with a social atmosphere, promote teamwork and companionship.

name: Texas Baseball Diamonds
description:
The purpose of Texas Baseball Diamonds is to support the UT Baseball team while upholding our 3 pillars of Spirit, Social, and Service. Our organization creates lasting friendships while supporting the baseball team and serving out Austin community.

Spirit: We are the official women's spirit organization for the UT Baseball team. We support the UT Baseball team by attending games, greeting and assisting with Kids Run the Bases at home games, and any other activities that the baseball staff needs.

Social: We are a smaller organization compared to many UT spirit organizations, but we believe this is what makes us unique! We really get to know each of our members which allows for strong friendships and a family of Diamonds. We have social events within our organization, we also have mixers with other organizations on campus, and host large social events during the year.

Service: Philanthropy is a large part of what we do! Our primary philanthropy is RBI Austin. RBI Austin exists to engage and develop inner-city Austin youth athletically, academically, and interpersonally, empowering them to lead the transformation of their communities. Diamonds has a service credit requirement for each member in order for members to learn how to serve our community while strengthening the relationships in the organization because we do the volunteering together! Here are a few events we have done in the past: our annual RBI Trivia Night Fundraiser, RBI Day at the Disch, RBI Field Day, Meals on Wheels lunch bag making, park clean-ups, UT Microfarm, profit shares, 5K walks, bake sales, and many more.
summary: Texas Baseball Diamonds is the official women's spirit organization of Texas Baseball. Founded in 1988 on three pillars of spirit, social, and service, our vision is to support the baseball team, create lasting friendships and serve our Austin community.

name: Texas Belles Event Planning Organization
description:
Texas Belles is a social, event planning organization at the University of Texas, where we plan events for…well, ourselves! We are a diverse group of students who have the desire to build community, foster lifelong friendships, explore Austin, and develop professionally. 

Whether or not you are interested in event planning, Belles is a great, low-stress way to get connected socially. We host outings, mixers, and provide philanthropic and professional opportunities. Our meetings are every Wednesday from 6:30-7:30 PM–come check us out! We are affiliated with the Moody College of Communication, but are open to members of all majors! Please visit our main website for more info: texasbellesepo.com
summary: Texas Belles is a pre-professional event planning organization for students interested in the hospitality industry. We are affiliated with the Moody College of Communication but do not restrict membership to those majors.

name: Texas Crew
description:
Texas Crew is a co-ed club sports team under the Division of Rec Sports at UT.

Texas Crew competes against other club teams both locally and nationally. Competitions are hosted across the country, including places like Boston, Tennessee, Louisiana, Texas, Georgia and Austin.

Rowers for Texas Crew practice 6 days a week in both spring and fall semesters. The major championships are held in April and May, though there are other national competitions throughout the season.

No prior rowing experience is necessary to join Texas Crew.
summary: A competitive club rowing team representing The University of Texas around the nation.

name: Texas Dragon/Lion Dance Team
description:
The Texas Dragon/Lion Dance Team (TDDT) is a performance-driven dragon and lion dance group based in Austin, Texas. Our goal is to share and, in many cases, introduce the art of dragon and lion dancing to the community. We are one of the few teams in Central Texas that performs year-round, for a wide variety of events. In the past we've performed for elementary schools, cultural events, festivals, restaurants, and weddings. Our prime performance season is during the Lunar New Year (early February or March) where we perform the gauntlet of back-to-back performances for businesses and organizations all over Austin.

For more information about our organization, or to see our practice & performance calendar, please visit our web site.
summary: Our goal is to share and introduce the art of lion and dragon dance throughout the community.

name: Texas Economics Association
description:
The purpose of the Texas Economics Association, as the oldest non-national economics student association at the University of Texas at Austin, is to provide professional, academic, social, and philanthropic opportunities to its members. Through case studies, internship connections, networking, social events, community involvement, and academic lectureship, TEA is committed to the advancement of its members.

TEA is open to ALL undergraduate students.
summary: The purpose of the Texas Economics Association, as the oldest non-national economics student association at the University of Texas at Austin, is to provide professional, academic, social and philanthropic opportunities to its members.

name: Texas Engineering World Health
description: Meet the biggest student organization dedicated to student innovation and entrepreneurship in healthcare.
summary: Our goal is to provide students the technical skillset, financial backing, and organizational mentorship to design medical devices and bring their ideas into the commercial market.

name: Texas Environmental Law Journal
description:
What Does TELJ Publish?
TELJ is a biannual publication that provides practitioners with current legal articles, developments, and information pertaining to environmental and natural resources law, as well as news of activities and other relevant events.
summary: The Texas Environmental Law Journal is produced for publication by a select group of students of the University of Texas School of Law, in association with the Environmental and Natural Resources Law Section of the State Bar of Texas.

name: Texas Federalist Society
description: The Texas Federalist Society is part of a national network of judges, practitioners, public policy experts, government officials, and students who share a conservative/libertarian perspective on the law. Our goal is to expose the student body to those perspectives, which we believe are often not well or not fairly represented in the average classroom. To that end, we host debates, panels, and speeches to fairly and seriously engage with the most pressing issues of the day. We also forge conservative and libertarian community through frequent networking events.
summary: The Texas Federalist Society is part of a national network of judges, practitioners, public policy experts, government officials, and students who share a conservative/libertarian perspective on the law.

name: Texas Gymnastics
description:
Texas Gymnastics is a competitive club that offers an opportunity for members to improve skills in artistic gymnastics and provides an opportunity for men and women to compete in gymnastics at the national club level. Our competitions begin in the Spring, so don't forget to visit our website, YouTube, Instagram, and Tik Tok in order to stay up to date with our meet schedule.

Tryouts for the 2024-2025 team will begin the third week of the fall semester. If you are interested in joining and want more information, please contact either the women's captain, Sarah Bush, or the men's captain, Jonathan Harvey. Their information can be found via the roster.

Current practice times are Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays from 8:00-10:15pm, and Sundays 11am-2pm. 

Please remember that you must be logged in to view the roster emails.
summary: Texas Gymnastics is a competitive club that offers an opportunity for members to improve skills in artistic gymnastics, and to provide an opportunity for men and women to compete in gymnastics at the national club level.

name: Texas Handball
description:
Texas Handball is a low-commitment sports team with the goals of:

1) Promote handball at The University of Texas and beyond

2) Sponsor a competitive handball team and assist its members financially at competitions (e.g., entry fees, lodging, etc.)

3) Continually increase participation in the three tournaments we host each year
summary: 1) Promote handball at The University of Texas and beyond; 2) sponsor a competitive handball team and assist its members financially at competitions (e.g., entry fees, lodging, etc.); 3) when possible, provide team members with equipment and uniforms

name: Texas Hillel
description:
Founded in 1927, Texas Hillel at The University of Texas at Austin’s mission is to enrich the lives of Jewish undergraduate and graduate students so that they may enrich the Jewish people and the world. Texas Hillel students, professionals and volunteer leaders are dedicated to creating a pluralistic, welcoming and inclusive environment for Jewish college students, where they are encouraged to grow intellectually, spiritually and socially. Texas Hillel helps students find a balance in being distinctively Jewish and universally human by encouraging them to pursue tzedek (social justice), tikkun olam (repairing the world) and Jewish learning, and to support Israel and global Jewish peoplehood. Texas Hillel is committed to excellence, innovation, accountability and results.

The University of Texas at Austin is a vibrant campus community with more than 4,000 Jewish students, making Texas Hillel host to the largest population of Jewish students in the region. Texas Hillel plans more than one hundred different activities each year, and programs under its sponsorship have been recognized by the University as Best Campus Service Organization (for social action and community volunteerism), Best Campus Political Organization (for Texans for Israel advocacy and education work and for the White Rose Society's anti-genocide campaign), and Most Outstanding Campus Religious Organization. Texas Hillel has also been recognized by Hillel: The Foundation for Jewish Campus Life as an Exemplar of Excellence and with the Haber Award in 2005 for the White Rose Society (a Holocaust Remembrance and Genocide Awareness group).
summary: Texas Hillel creates opportunities for all Jewish students at The University of Texas to develop their own Jewish interests and participate in a welcoming campus-based community that respects the value of pluralism and promotes ongoing involvement.

name: Texas Hispanic Journal of Law and Policy
description: The Texas Hispanic Journal of Law & Policy (THJ) provides an academic forum for discussing novel, significant, or emerging legal and public policy issues affecting Latino/a/e and Hispanic communities. THJ publishes materials from within and outside the legal community, including scholarly articles, book reviews, and student notes. THJ welcomes individuals from all backgrounds and strives to provide members with a second home.
summary: The Texas Hispanic Journal of Law & Policy is a student-run law journal at the University of Texas School of Law dedicated to serving as a global academic forum for legal and policy issues affecting Latino/a/e and Hispanic communities everywhere.

name: Texas Ice Hockey
description: The University of Texas Ice Hockey Team is part of the TCHC in Division 2 of the American Collegiate Hockey Association. We represent the University of Texas as we play other schools around the state and country. If you are interested in joining our program feel free to contact us with the information below.
summary: To provide the students and faculty of the University of Texas the opportunity to watch or play competitive Ice Hockey and to improve the ability and skills of those who play. We strive for success off and on the ice.

name: Texas Intellectual Property Law Journal
description:
Our primary focus centers on providing significant and innovative contributions to U.S. and international intellectual property law. We are sponsored by the IP Section of the State Bar of Texas. We have the largest subscription base of all law journals published through The University of Texas, and we provide positive revenue to UT through publication royalties.

Articles appearing in the Journal focus on cutting edge, substantive legal issues and recent developments of interest to our practitioner and academic subscribers in the areas of Patent, Copyright, Trademark, Unfair Competition, and Trade Secret law, as well as other areas of law in the context of IP (e.g., constitutional, contracts, antitrust, criminal, torts, international, M&A, corporate, administrative law). TIPLJ also publishes articles concerning issues arising in IP litigation and practice before the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office, state, and federal courts. Articles submitted for publication in the Journal are written by academicians, practitioners, and students from around the world.
summary: Our primary focus centers on providing information on significant and innovative contributions to U.S. and international intellectual property law by publishing a regular journal.

name: Texas International Law Journal
description: The Texas International Law Journal (TILJ) is a student-run organization that delivers leading scholarship related to international legal, theoretical, and policy analysis. TILJ publishes academic articles, essays, and student notes in the areas of public and private international law, international legal theory, the law of international organizations, comparative and foreign law, and domestic laws with significant international implications. TILJ is among the oldest and best-established student-published international law journals in the United States. Over the years, TILJ staff has made the most of its established heritage. We have developed international repute by forging close ties with numerous scholars and authors worldwide. This position has helped us develop one of the largest student-published subscription circulations of any international law journal in the United States. With an editorial board and staff made up of full-time J.D. and LL.M. students from the University of Texas School of Law, the Journal maintains a refined and well-organized editing process. As economic integration accelerates and nations forge closer ties in the new millennium, we are confident the Journal will continue to provide a significant contribution to the burgeoning field of international law.
summary: The Texas International Law Journal (TILJ) is a student-run organization that delivers leading scholarship related to international legal, theoretical, and policy analysis.

name: Texas Iron Spikes
description: The official purpose of the Iron Spikes is to promote service throughout the University of Texas and the Austin Community. We also promote spirit for the University of Texas Baseball Team.
summary: The official purpose of the Texas Iron Spikes is to promote service throughout the University of Texas and the Austin Community. We also promote spirit for the UT baseball team.

name: Texas Journal of Oil, Gas & Energy Law
description: The Texas Journal of Oil, Gas, and Energy Law's (TJOGEL) vision is to be a leading energy law journal. We want to be the forum of choice for energy law practitioners, professors, and students around the globe. Our mission is to provide energy professionals with relevant information on emerging issues in the dynamic field of oil, gas, and energy law, while cultivating the industry's future leaders.
summary: The Texas Journal of Oil, Gas, and Energy Law's (TJOGEL) vision is to be a leading energy law journal. We want to be the forum of choice for energy law practitioners, professors, and students around the globe. Our focus centers on providing significant..

name: Texas Journal on Civil Liberties & Civil Rights
description: The purpose of the Journal is to publish scholarly writings on, encourage dialogue, and foster awareness of various civil liberties and civil rights issues. The Journal publishes two issues per academic year.
summary: The purpose of the Journal is to publish scholarly writings on various civil liberties and civil rights issues. The Journal publishes two issues per academic year.

name: Texas Lassos
description:
Texas Lassos strives to promote spirit, service, and social activities to all members while fostering friendship, leadership, and cooperation within our community. Ultimately, we hope that by being a safe, fulfilling, and fun space for our members, we make our wider community a better place. Founded in 1998 with only seven members, today Texas Lassos is home to over 150 active members and an accomplished group of alumni. As a whole, we are a group of diverse young people intent on contributing to the University and the Austin Community.

We're not the oldest, we're not the biggest, we're simply the best!

 

Service:

Lassos work hard to improve the UT campus and the city of Austin. We participate in service projects and volunteering for numerous charitable organizations and causes, including Austin Animal Center, Texas THON, Inside Books, Austin Marathon, SUREwalk, Central Texas Food Bank, Best Buddies, and many more! Active members are required to complete 12 hours of service a semester. We have numerous service opportunities available throughout the semester, including service mixers with other organizations on campus!

Spirit:

Texas Lassos are proud to be Longhorns and we support all UT athletics! Each semester, active members are required to attend six games of their choosing. We have tailgates with other organizations for football and baseball. As Lassos, we play IM sports including volleyball and soccer, as well as participate in events like OU Weekend. We also love supporting our fellow Lassos in their individual interests, by attending events like Acapella performances and dance competitions.

Social:

Lassos love to have fun! We have plenty of social events throughout the year that foster sisterhood among our fellow members, friendship with other organizations, and date events as well! Our events include Date Dash, Crush, Destination Unknown, Mixers, Boat Party, Lasso Thanksgiving, Crawfish Boil, Formals, Semi-formals, Mugdown and Shootout.

 

Philanthropy:

In the Fall of 2012, Texas Lassos adopted the Down Syndrome Association of Central Texas (DSACT) as our organization's official philanthropy. Lassos works with DSACT through fundraising, events, workshops, and more. We host Dance Parties with DSACT members, Barnyard Bash as an annual fundraiser, and volunteer with DSACT at Buddy Walk and any events where they need our support in implementing their programming. All Lassos are required to complete a mandatory Disability Awareness Training. 

 

Longhorns Against Drunk Driving: 

LADD (Longhorns Against Drunk Driving) was founded by members of Texas Lassos after the loss of member Vanessa Whitford’s life. LADD was initially created to both spread awareness and provide resources to stop drinking and driving in honor of Vanessa. Today LADD works not only to prevent drinking and driving, but also to provide mental health resources, alcohol/drug safety initiatives, sober events, and recovery ally trainings. LADD has many official partners including LYFT, The Austin Sobering Center, The Steve Hicks School of Social Work, and Texas Athletics. LADD’s mission is to educate, advocate, and stand in solidarity with those who have lost lives to drinking and driving incidents, drug/alcohol use, addiction, or mental health. We strive to provide a safer environment for our students, peers, and community.


Visit our website at www.txlassos.com to learn more about Texas Lassos.
summary: Texas Lassos is a service organization at the University of Texas in Austin. The organization shall strive to promote spirit, service, and social activities to all members while fostering friendship and leadership.

name: Texas Law Fellowships
description:
Texas Law Fellowships (TLF) is a student-run 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that sponsors public interest fellowships for students at the University of Texas School of Law. Every year since 1988, TLF has raised money from the UT community, from Austin businesses, and from the legal field to fund public-interest fellowships for UT law students. We raise most of our funds from the Fall Auction.  TLF also receives some funding from law firms and private benefactors.

Normally, summer internships in public interest and government organizations are completely unpaid. For most students, public-interest internships would not be possible without fellowships to provide financial support. Through TLF, public interest students receive a stipend in the amount of $2,000 to help with the outsized financial need they face.
summary: Texas Law Fellowships (TLF) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that sponsors public interest fellowships for students at the University of Texas School of Law.

name: Texas Lonestars
description: Texas Lonestars is a group of women and woman-aligned or non-binary people at UT dedicated to the pillars of service, spirit, and sisterhood. Each year, the Lonestars contribute hundreds of hours to community service events. The Lonestars truly bleed burnt orange! Whether it be cheering on the Horns from their group seats at football games, marching in the OU Parade, or playing intramural sports, Lonestars have spirit in every sense of the word. Lonestars is more than just an extra curricular activity- its a family. This close-knit organization provides a home away from home for its members.
summary: Texas Lonestars is a group of women and woman-aligned or non-binary people at UT dedicated to the pillars of service, spirit, and sisterhood. Lonestars share a special bond through their love of the university and serving their community.

name: Texas Men's Lacrosse
description: To promote lacrosse through sportsmanship and skill; to teach new players and improve old ones; to provide an outlet for physical creativity and activity; to provide social functions and interaction for the team members.
summary: To promote lacrosse through sportsmanship and skill; to teach new players and improve old ones; to provide an outlet for physical creativity and activity; to provide social functions and interaction for the team members.

name: Texas Men's Volleyball
description: Texas Men's Volleyball fosters a competitive and collaborative community, enabling players to compete and succeed at the highest level of collegiate club volleyball. We are part of the Southern Intercollegiate Volleyball Association, a conference within the National Collegiate Volleyball Federation, and participate in various tournaments across Texas throughout the fall and spring. Each spring, we also compete at Nationals, showcasing our team on a national stage.
summary: Texas Men's Volleyball fosters a competitive and collaborative community, enabling players to compete and succeed at the highest level of collegiate club volleyball.

name: Texas Men's Water Polo
description:
The University of Texas Men's Water Polo Team is a competitive recreational team that competes in tournaments throughout the region of Texas in the Fall semester. The team has won the majority of the division's championships and regularly travels to nationals.

 

The team is open to players of all levels and practices are held Monday through Thursday at the Lee and Joe Jamail Texas Swimming Center from 8 to 10 pm. 

 

Please feel free to reach out to utmenswaterpolo@gmail.com if you are interested in joining the program or have any questions.
summary:
To practice and participate in water polo events and to spark and hold an interest in water polo on campus.

Instagram: @texasmwaterpolo

name: Texas Nutrition at the University of Texas at Austin
description: The goal of Texas Nutrition is to provide the students with a knowledge of opportunities and developments in nutrition and dietetics fields. Texas Nutrition primarily focuses on enhancing the students' exposure to professionals in nutrition-related fields, local and national dietetic associations, and information regarding nutritional degrees at UT. Because Texas Nutrition is not limited solely to nutrition majors, it offers collaboration between students majoring in nutrition and other academic tracks such as exercise science, kinesiology, pre-health professions, business, etc. The organization also offers a variety of additional opportunities including volunteering, social engagements, and intramural sports.
summary: To provide students with a knowledge of opportunities and developments in nutrition and dietetics fields, and to promote healthful eating and behavioral habits within the community.

name: Kappa Alpha Order
description: Omicron was founded in 1883 and has operated out of the Manor on Leon since 1949. With over 3,000 alumni including Clyde Littlefield, Tommy Nobis, and Ben Crenshaw, the Omicron network offers UT students life-long access to an unmatched community.
summary: The oldest fraternity at UT Austin.

name: Texas Pakistani Students Association
description:
Since its initiation over 30 years ago, PSA has served as a great base to promote Pakistani culture on campus. Executive committee members come together every year to host a variety of events throughout the year including, Mock Mehndi and Basant (our signature events), for the enjoyment of the general public. The association has grown from a small group of Pakistani students to a University recognized Student Association around campus. We not only provide different cultural activities for students on campus, but also accommodate Pakistani students coming into a new city or country.

Goals: 

1. To educate the Austin community about what Pakistan and its people have to offer to the great world.

2. To establish a forum to discuss political and social issues which directly involve Pakistan, Pakistanis, and Pakistani-Americans.

3. To create a social fabric to connect students of all majors and interests through the experience and celebration of Pakistani culture.
summary: A solid platform for UT students to experience the unique and traditional Pakistani culture and heritage. It also enables them to gather and share their unique experiences and build lifelong friendships.

name: Texas Pre-Dental Society
description:
Texas Pre-Dental Society is a professional organization dedicated to ensuring the educational and professional development of undergraduate students who have committed themselves to the field of dentistry. TPS facilitates events where students have the opportunity to interact with dental school deans, admissions board members, and established dentists so that they can better understand the expectations of competitive dental school candidates. Membership provides access to academic resources, leadership positions, award opportunities, on-going community service, and social events. The Texas-Predental Society is committed to the health and wellness of its members, and is an active member of HSOP.

 

Do you want to be a member of TPS? Check out our website below to find our meeting schedule, how to join, and all the opportunities we offer!

Please visit our website for more details. 
http://texaspredental.org/
summary: Texas Pre-Dental Society prepares pre-dental students to be confident in their dental school applications. TPS provides support in all areas of dental disciplines: volunteering, shadowing, and more. Visit our website 'texaspredental.org' to learn more.

name: Texas Public Health
description:
Texas Public Health is a student-led organization representing students interested in health education, disease prevention, and the development of a strong public health initiative at the University of Texas at Austin. Our mission is to increase campus-wide awareness of public health & introduce public health professionals to undergraduate students.

​

Texas Public Health is open to all majors interested in public health. We provide information about graduate school, research opportunities around campus, and a vibrant community to explore public health. We hope to raise awareness on public health issues around the globe and foster strong ties of responsibility and dependability to our local Austin community.

If you are interested in joining our organization for the 2025-2026 academic year, please fill out our interest form to join our newsletter!
summary: Texas Public Health is open to students of all majors who are interested in public health.

name: Texas Quadball
description: Quadball is a relatively new sport that is based on the sport from the Harry Potter series. The sport is a fast paced and physical game that involves bits and pieces from multiple sports including rugby, basketball, and dodgeball. Here at the University of Texas we have a traveling team, Texas Quadball, that travels around the state and country competing in tournaments against other university's teams.
summary: An organization dedicated to competing in and promoting the sport of quadball in the UT Austin community, in Austin, and around the country.

name: Texas Review of Law and Politics
description: The Texas Review of Law and Politics is a premier conservative and libertarian law journal. Our scholarship is at the forefront of conservative and libertarian legal thought and is routinely cited in opinions, scholarship, and the popular press. Our authors include federal and state judges, federal and state senators, governors, professors, policy experts, practicing attorneys, and leading law students. Our network includes the leading conservative legal minds in America at every level of professional success.
summary: The Unfettered Pursuit of Conservative Legal Scholarship

name: Texas Rock Climbing
description:
Texas Rock Climbing is the official climbing team at UT Austin, representing UT in the Collegiate Climbing Series, the national governing body for competitive collegiate climbing. We offer opportunities for people both new to climbing and to seasoned climbers to meet new people and to become a part of Austin's vibrant and growing climbing community. TRC hosts numerous social events throughout the year as well as volunteer opportunities that are open to every member!

 

For Spring 2025, we are holding in-person practices every weekday 7:00pm-9:30pm at Austin Bouldering Project - Springdale! For recreational members, practice attendance is optional and members are free to participate in training with the competitive team.

 

Anyone can join Texas Rock Climbing at any point throughout the semester! We will be holding tryouts for out competitive team TBA.

 

To stay updated on upcoming events and to learn more about how to join TRC, join our Discord! This is our primary form of communication:

Current Discord Link: https://discord.com/invite/33eACD7GFM

 

Awards and Accomplishments:

Regional Champions: 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2022

National Champions: 2012, 2013, 2014
summary:
Texas Rock Climbing is the official climbing team at UT Austin, representing UT in the Collegiate Climbing Series, the national governing body for competitive collegiate climbing. 

The team also offers a non-competitive climbing club.

name: Texas Roundtable
description: Texas Roundtable is composed of the presidents and selected representatives of various social, spirit, and honorary organizations at the University of Texas at Austin. Texas Roundtable seeks to unite student leaders in an effort to serve the community, advocate for student interests, and develop leadership potential.
summary: Texas Roundtable seeks to unite student leaders in an effort to serve the community, advocate for student interests, and develop leadership potential.

name: Texas Royals
description:
Texas Royals is spirit group for women and non-binary students at The University of Texas at Austin dedicated to serving our campus and community. Christina Woodard founded Texas Royals in 2013 to help further research and awareness for Alzheimer’s Disease, and to promote sisterhood on campus. 

The four pillars of Texas Royals are service, sisterhood, spirit, and social. Texas Royals seeks to create lasting friendships and strengthen the individual’s tie to The University of Texas at Austin, through both academic success and campus engagement.
summary: Texas Royals is a spirit organization for women and non-binary students on campus. Our mission is to enhance the college experience of our members through involvement in philanthropic, spirit, and social  activities, along with leadership opportunities.

name: Texas Rugby
description:
The University of Texas at Austin Men's Rugby team competes in the Red River Rugby Conference, playing in both 7s and 15s. We participate in the top division of college rugby, the CRAA and we are sanctioned by the Texas Rugby Union. We are members of the UT Sports Club Association and are sanctioned by, and in good standing, with The University of Texas at Austin.

 

We practice on the southeast corner of Whitaker Intramural fields on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 6-8. Prospective members are invited to come out to training. No experience necessary. We aim to offer everyone a chance to complete and enjoy the sport, while selecting the best rugby players for to compete at the highest level on our Varsity A Side.
summary:
The University of Texas Mens Rugby Team - Facebook.com/UTRugby -instagram.com/utexasrugby/  -
 Hook 'Em

name: Texas Running Club
description: Texas Running Club is a UT Recreational Sports teams with a dynamic, competitive, and social atmosphere. We participate in a multitude of races from the Longhorn Run to the Texas Independence Relay to the Austin Marathon. We strive to create a friendly and supportive environment to promote recreational running at UT and help our members reach their running goals. We welcome and invite students of all running levels to come join us for one of our group runs, and see what we are all about! Texas Running Club meets in front of Gregory Gym Mondays thru Thursdays at 6pm and Tuesdays/Thursdays at 7am. At each run, you will have the option to choose between one of our 3, 5, or 7-mile routes. For more information check out our website!
summary: Come see what we are all about at one of our evening group runs (M-Th) at 6pm outside Gregory Gym! Students of all running levels and experience are welcome.

name: Texas Trap and Skeet
description:
Texas Trap and Skeet is a student-run Sport Club at the University of Texas at Austin. Our mission is to educate, support, and bring together both casual and competitive shotgun athletes. Through careful instruction and steady coaching, we produce safe and successful shooters. Our traveling team competes at the local, state, and national levels, consistently achieving high scores (and placing) across four shotgun disciplines (American Trap, American Skeet, Sporting Clays, and Super Sporting Clays). 

We are a diverse yet cohesive group of students who give it our all both in the classroom and on the field. We encourage Longhorns of all skill levels (including complete novices) to come out, see us at practice, and join in on the fun!
summary: Our mission is to educate, support, and bring together both casual and competitive shotgun athletes. We encourage Longhorns of all skill levels (including complete novices) to come out, see us at practice, and join in on the fun!

name: Texas Spirits
description: Texas Spirits is an honorary social, spirit, and service organization for women and non-binary individuals at the University of Texas at Austin. The purpose of Texas Spirits is to promote spirit and serve the University and the Austin community in the best way possible. Our four pillars of spirit, love, service, and friendship represent the core values Texas Spirits strive to uphold.
summary: Texas Spirits is an honorary social, spirit, and service organization for women and non-binary individuals at the University of Texas at Austin. Our four pillars of spirit, love, service, and friendship are core values Texas Spirits strive to uphold.

name: Texas Stock Team
description:
Texas Stock Team (TST) is an organization of The University of Texas at Austin that was started during Spring 2013.

Since 2016, TST has shifted to focus on fundamental analysis. Novice members with little to no experience are welcome to apply to the organization and become familiar with the market, corporate finance, stock pitches, valuation methodologies, and financial modeling. They are encouraged to formulate investment theses in their own time, and execute on those ideas with real or virtual money. They are challenged to think like a financial analyst and research different markets and industries.

Texas Stock Team serves as a close-knit environment for ambitious students to delve deeper into finance and learn in a tight community of like-minded peers. Whether you have extensive experience or simply want to learn, Texas Stock Team welcomes you to apply and join the family.
summary: Texas Stock Team (TST) is an organization of The University of Texas at Austin that was created during Spring 2013 semester. TST teaches members financial concepts in valuation methodologies, accounting, and corporate finance.

name: Texas Student Psychological Association
description:
TSPA is a student-run organization targeted towards any students interested in psychology! We attract guest speakers affiliated with UT, the community, and private practices to talk to us about their experience in the field of psychology, related careers, and various pertinent topics. TSPA is first, and foremost, a club that fosters learning and emphasizes building connections! Visit our website to learn more! (https://bit.ly/tspa-ut)

Come out (anytime during the semester) and see what we are all about!!
 
General Information:
Meetings are held every other Monday @ SEA 4.244 from 6pm - 7pm 
https://linktr.ee/tspa 
 
President: Aliana Taylor
Vice President: Srushti Bhakta
Treasurer: Daniel Ortiz
Secretary: Cassidy Cole
Volunteer Coordinator: Jose Mejenes
Social Coordinator: Carolina Maldonado
Community and Outreach Director: Kyra Arora
Resource Coordinator: Miguel Almanza
summary: Our mission is to provide students with opportunities to explore the world of psychology through guest speakers, volunteer projects, workshops, and socials.

name: Texas Sweethearts
description: The Texas Sweethearts are a diverse group of young women dedicated to serving the Austin community and supporting University of Texas athletics. We strive to promote scholastic achievement, integrity, and leadership while fostering lifelong friendships among our Sweetheart sisters.
summary: The Texas Sweethearts is an honorary service, spirit, and social student organization at the University of Texas at Austin. Our members serve both the Longhorn and greater Austin community while promoting spirit throughout campus.

name: Texas Table Tennis
description:
Texas Table Tennis is a club for table tennis enthusiasts of all levels to enjoy the more competitive side of collegiate table tennis. We love to introduce UT students to the sport of table tennis and guide them to improve their game and have fun doing so! We expect our members to come to practice regularly and have the desire to improve. As a team, we travel to different cities throughout Texas to participate in both team and individual tournaments. Our aim is to help members succeed in whatever goals they have set for themselves both in and out of the sport. In short, we are a fun-loving group of people with a competitive spirit. We welcome players of any skill level and would love to see you at our practice!

Instagram: @texas_tabletennis

Discord:      discord.gg/G6ah6fRu2R
summary: Texas Table Tennis is a club for table tennis enthusiasts of all levels to enjoy the more competitive side of collegiate table tennis. We love to introduce UT students to the sport of table tennis and guide them to improve and have fun doing so!

name: Texas Taekwondo
description:
We train in Olympic-style Taekwondo sparring and compete at regional, state, and national tournaments in both the collegiate and open categories, including the National Collegiate Taekwondo Association's annual Championships, a USA Olympic Taekwondo endorsed event (in which we are 6x back-to-back national champions!).

Practice is open to anyone regardless of martial arts experience; beginners are not only welcome but encouraged to join - historically, over half of the incoming club members have never done Taekwondo before. Feel free to drop by anytime in workout clothes!

Those interested should look for us at the Party on the Plaza student organization fair (for Open House, announced on our website and Facebook page or just drop in anytime during the semester for a physically and mentally rewarding experience.

After the first three free practices for the fall and spring semesters, we will hold regular practices for members in RSC 1.138 at these times:

  • Tuesday and Thursday, 7:00 - 9:30 pm
  • Saturday, 12:00 - 3:00 pm 

And in Belmont 904 at these times:

  • Monday and Wednesday, 7:00 - 9:30 pm

Visit our website at http://utexastaekwondo.com/

Like us on Instagram at http://www.Instagram.com/TexasTaekwondo.

Contact us at texastaekwondo@gmail.com

Gold Award holder of the Healthy Student Organization Program.

 

Texas Fight!
summary:
All skill levels welcome! Practices are:

Sparring: RSC 1.138  
T, Th: 7:00-9:30 PM

Poomsae: BEL 904
M, W: 8:00-10:00 PM

Strength and Conditioning
M,W: 7:00-8:00 PM

Visit our website to see our full practice schedule!

name: Texas Theme Park Engineering and Design
description:
Texas Theme Park Engineering and Designis focused on introducing students to the themed entertainment industry and engaging them in activities which involve hands-on educational experiences.

We host meetings with speakers from themed entertainment companies, participate in design competitions, and go on behind-the-scenes tours, and build full-scale guest experiences on campus.

​Our goal is to help students from all majors explore options within the ​themed entertainment industry and discover atypical career option within their degrees.
summary: Our purpose is to expose engineering students to the industry of Theme Park and Amusement entertainment by conducting hands on activities that allow them to have design experience. We also hope to expose them to other TPED groups in the country.

name: Texas THON
description: Texas THON is a student-run philanthropy that raises awareness and funds for Dell Children’s Medical Center – our local Children’s Miracle Network Hospital. Texas THON unites the Austin community to help provide children with necessary medical treatment and serve all families, regardless of their ability to pay. We seek to help promote not only health but confidence in the lives of our Miracle Kids and their families. Our year-round fundraising efforts culminate in a 8-hour spring event where our Miracle Makers dance to change kids' health.
summary: Texas THON at The University of Texas at Austin is a student-run philanthropy that raises funds and awareness for Dell Children's Medical Center of Central Texas - our local Children's Miracle Network Hospital, right here in Austin.

name: Texas Triathlon & Cycling
description: Texas Triathlon and Cycling is a club that provides consistent group workouts, training, and support for triathlon and endurance races. We are a group of fun, health-conscious athletes with various interests and backgrounds and enjoy working out together. We also have a wide range of skill levels, and we are beginner friendly. If you have any interest in joining our ridiculously good-looking group of fun people, don't hesitate to contact Elan, our President, at president@texastriathlon.org!
summary: Texas Triathlon and Cycling is a club that provides consistent group workouts, training, and support for triathlon, cycling, and endurance races.

name: Texas Horizons
description: Texas Horizons is a coed social-service organization that seeks to provide an environment that inspires individuality and creative expression amongst all its members. In addition to providing a unique college experience, Texas Horizons aspires to make an impact on UT and the city of Austin through community involvement.
