Through all of this, Texas Horizons seeks to cultivate close-knit, authentic relationships amongst its members.
summary: Texas Horizons is a coed social-service organization that seeks to provide an environment that encourages individuality and expression. In addition to providing a unique college experience, we aspire to make an impact on UT and the city of Austin.

name: Texas Undergraduate Investment Team
description:
TUIT is the first, premier investment team at The University of Texas at Austin. Founded in 2007 by Eric Chang and James Peng, the organization offers members a tangible finance-oriented education, a culture of mentorship, and a network for career development. Members learn about investing and financial modeling through the qualitative and quantitative analysis, and we focus on developing critical thinking skills that are transferable to any career. TUIT also prides itself on its investment returns and strategy. Our small team dynamic and strong culture of mentorship helps each member pursue their own personal and career goals and ultimately Pay it Forward—our motto.

Please check our website texasuit.com for more information and recruiting timelines. We typically accept applicants at the beginning of the fall and spring semester.


Website: www.texasuit.com
summary: 28 members. $35-40k portfolio. Founded in 2007. The first, premier investment team at UT Austin.

name: Texas Undergraduate Law Journal
description:
About Us

 

The Texas Undergraduate Law Journal is a law journal edited, published, and distributed entirely by undergraduate students at the University of Texas at Austin. We seek to enrich undergraduate students' interests in school by providing a forum to develop skills essential to legal scholarship. While raising awareness of current legal issues, encouraging discourse, and upholding the university's academic values, we give those students an opportunity to publish their works in a nationally reviewed academic journal.

 

Prospective members of TULJ can become either writers or editors for both online and print content. TULJ manages a blog where members and non-members can publish shorter, more concise articles, so everyone has an opportunity to contribute. Throughout the semester, writers and editors work together to publish online content, refine print content, raise funds, and host socials and panels. We also host a podcast called "Law Law Land" where our members get together to discuss different legal and contemporary issues. 

 

We encourage students from all backgrounds to apply. Law continues to become increasingly interdisciplinary, and insights from members around campus help develop more holistic, competent, and logically sound writing.

 

Texas Undergraduate Law Journal is a sponsored student organization at the University of Texas at Austin. The organization is run entirely by undergraduate students and is in no way affiliated with the Texas Law Review or the University of Texas at Austin School of Law. None of the views expressed through Texas Undergraduate Law Journal reflect those of its sponsors, the University, Texas Law Review, or the School of Law.

 

Visit our website to apply or submit an article.
summary: A law journal written, edited and published entirely by undergraduate students.

name: Texas Wake
description:
Texas Wake is the official Wakeboarding team of the University of Texas. It is a club in which UT wakeboarders and wakesurfers can connect and ride.

We teach beginners as well as help experienced riders progress their skills. Texas Wake goes out on different boats multiple times a week for 4 hours each day, during which members can wakeboard or wakesurf. We have partnered with QuestATX and Texas Ski Ranch, cable parks near Austin, where every Texas Wake member has access to unlimited passes to the park, enabling them to wakeboard to their heart's content. We also compete (optional but highly encouraged) at the collegiate level in various competitions nationwide. No experience necessary! There is free gear available through the team.

The club uses Instagram for sharing recruitment updates, photos and videos of our riders. Follow Texas Wake's account "@texas_wake" to keep updated with the club's content. 

Texas Wake Instagram
summary: Texas Wake is the official Wakeboarding Team of UT. Texas Wake gives UT students the chance to learn/further develop their wakeboarding/surfing skills and connect with some amazing people. We compete in collegiate level competitions nationwide!

name: Texas Wesley UMCM
description:
We’re a Christian org at UT that values YOU! 

We believe that a healthy spiritual life leads to a healthy identity and that God’s love is compassionate, life-giving, and includes everyone. We believe that faith in God embraces hard questions and that Scripture must be understood in its original context. We use both our minds and our hearts to search for God and to follow Christ together. Our key values are: Inclusive Love, Exploring Faith, and Real Friendship. 

Come join us! The Wesley is a United Methodist campus ministry but all are invited and welcome!  Weekly meetings Thursdays at 7 pm.
summary: We’re a Christian org at UT that values YOU! Our key values are: Inclusive Love, Exploring Faith, and Real Friendship. Weekly meetings Thurs @7 PM

name: Disability and Access
description:
Disability & Access works with students with disabilities to ensure they have equal access to their academic experience, often through the provision of classroom and testing accommodations.

D&A is located on the fourth floor of the Student Services Building.

 Information on how to register and downloadable forms, including guidelines for documentation, accommodation letter requests, and releases of information are available online. Please do not hesitate to contact us if you have any questions. We look forward to making your experience at the university more accessible!

If you would like to join the D&A Hornslink page, please email access@austin.utexas.edu with the subject line of "Hornslink". We look forward to staying connected with you!
summary: Disability and Access works with faculty and staff to ensure students with disabilities have equal access and opportunity in all parts of campus life. D&A also connects students with groups, student organizations, and scholarships.

name: Equal Opportunity in Engineering Program
description:
The Engineering Student Success Center (ESSC) provides a welcoming space for students to develop the skills and confidence needed to achieve their academic and personal goals. ESSC encourages growth and achievement while enriching students’ academic experiences and personal development through focused programming facilitated by the Equal Opportunity in Engineering Program (EOE), the Women in Engineering Program (WEP) and the programs and student organizations coordinated by Engineering Student Life (ESL). 

Established in 1970 by Texas Engineering, the Equal Opportunity in Engineering Program (EOE) promotes the recruitment and academic development of students within Cockrell. The EOE mission is to promote inclusivity and support Texas Engineering students from all backgrounds by providing equal access to quality education and fostering an environment in which every student can thrive. Participants in EOE can expect to build their leadership, communication and team-building skills through real-world experiences and connections to leaders in industry and academia. All Texas Engineering students are invited to join EOE’s vibrant community and peer support network.
summary: EOE invites students to become part of an exciting community that focuses on academic success and personal growth.

name: Longhorn Legislative Aide Agency of Student Government
description:
Please Do **NOT** Request Membership through HornsLink! If you would like to stay connected, please follow us on instagram (@utsg_lla)!

 

Current Directors: 

Jet Tran, jetvtran3@gmail.com  

Kayla Pearl, kaylapearl14@icloud.com

 

 

LLA's Mission: 

Longhorn Legislative Aide is a first-year leadership agency within student government designed to develop the University of Texas's future leaders. LLAs are an active group of twenty interns that provide feedback and help author resolutions to issues across campus while working closely with the Student Government Executive Board, Assembly, and other agencies. LLAs will understand what it means to be a longhorn by focusing on the UT core values of Learning, Leadership, and Responsibility.

LLA strives to create an environment for students to grow by learning from not only hands on experience but also one another. LLA aims to fully equip students for leadership in the future. LLA becomes a community for the members to support each other and those around them in any endeavor. Join the famiLLAy as soon as you get to UT!
summary: LLA is an internship within Student Government that works with the Executive Board and Assembly in order to deal with issues on campus. Mentors, Friends, and memories come with being an LLA. LLA will mature each intern in the professional world.

name: Texas Performing Arts
description:
One of the largest and most active live arts organizations in the U.S., TPA operates venues on the UT campus including Austin’s largest theatre, Bass Concert Hall.

Our presenting programs include Broadway in Austin, the Texas Welcomes series of national touring artists, and a curated Performing Arts Season of international theatre, music, dance, and more. TPA is also a leading producer of new performance, offering creative opportunities to both local artists and national productions.

TPA is part of UT’s College of Fine Arts and fulfills the university’s public service, research, and educational missions. More than twenty thousand students throughout Central Texas and across the university experience the arts through TPA’s extensive educational programs.
summary: Texas Performing Arts serves The University of Texas at Austin campus and the Austin community at large through a diverse season of international performances, campus and community engagement activities, and collaborative partnerships.

name: Women in Engineering Program
description:
The Women in Engineering Program (WEP) helps women in the Cockrell School get connected, develop as engineering leaders, and graduate with skills for life.

Established in 1991, WEP strives to:

  • recruit and retain female engineering students,
  • increase the percentage of female engineering graduates, and
  • provide a supportive structure that encourages the success of women in the Cockrell School of Engineering.

The concept of WEP was originally proposed by women on the engineering faculty and enthusiastically received and supported by the Dean. WEP now is a college-wide effort with a full-time director, a recruitment program coordinator, a retention program coordinator, an administrative associate and faculty advisors, in addition to student staff and volunteers. Each year, WEP initiatives reach more than 1,500 currently enrolled students and some 1,000 future students.

Women account for approximately 22 percent of the current Cockrell School student body. WEP programs focus on pre-college outreach and recruitment, academic enrichment and retention, community building, leadership enhancement and career development.
summary: The Women in Engineering Program (WEP) helps women in the Cockrell School get connected, develop as engineering leaders, and graduate with skills for life. WEP strives to recruit, retain and graduate more women from engineering.

name: Hindu Students Association
description: Since its establishment in 2009, UT’s Hindu Students Association (HSA) has been providing opportunities to explore and celebrate Hinduism. The organization’s three main pillars are education, service, and culture. Our primary focus is to educate and raise awareness about Hindu philosophy by creating a space where people can share their views and ask questions to deepen their understanding. We hold weekly discussion-based meetings over diverse, relevant topics to foster conversations allowing participants to consider Hindu ideologies in an inclusive environment. People of all backgrounds attend, encouraging us to critically analyze Hinduism through different lenses. In addition to educational efforts, HSA engages in service within our community. In the past, we have crafted therapy dolls for patients at Dell Children’s Hospital and assembled activity kits for residents at a local retirement home. We regularly volunteer at the Concho Community Garden and the Austin Hindu Temple as well. Most notably, we collaborate with the UT Green Events team to ensure our events are sustainable. HSA's third pillar is to embrace Hindu culture and find its relevance in our contemporary lives. Adhering to this, we organize events that highlight five major celebrations and help individuals clarify misconceptions to understand Hindu philosophy. Through these events, HSA offers a vibrant introduction to a rich culture that many individuals might not otherwise have the chance to experience. While we primarily focus on Hindu culture, we also foster understanding and appreciation of all faiths.Through commitment to education, service, and cultural celebration, HSA not only enriches the UT community but also cultivates inclusivity and understanding that bridges gaps between diverse beliefs.
summary: The Hindu Students Association aims to cultivate and foster cross-cultural dialogue and interaction on issues that pertain to Hindus domestically and globally, empower young Hindu adults through education, & improve their representation at UT Austin

name: Brackenridge, Roberts, & Prather Residence Hall Council
description: Residence Hall Student involvement for BRP Residence Halls
summary: Residence Hall Student involvement for BRP Residence Halls

name: Office of the Dean of Students
description: The Office of the Dean of Students is committed to helping all UT students – undergraduate, graduate, and professional – reach their fullest potential. Our office provides robust student support services along with opportunities for leadership experience, a variety of student work environments, engaging programming, and specialized resources.
summary: The Office of the Dean of Students provides opportunities and experiences that support student learning and personal development.

name: University Leadership Network
description:
The University Leadership Network (ULN) is a nationally recognized incentive-based scholarship program for students with demonstrated financial need. Each year, 500 freshmen are selected to begin the program. ULN includes a comprehensive four-year plan that involves leadership training, experiential learning opportunities, and university service. The ULN program helps students to develop academic and leadership skills consistent with graduating in four years.

ULN Mission

To encourage and support students to graduate in four years and become leaders through professional and experiential learning opportunities that advance their education, communities, and lives.

 

Scholarship

ULN students receive up to a $20,000 scholarship ($5,000/year) based on participation in the program's comprehensive four-year components. Students receive the scholarship money in monthly installments during the fall and spring semesters. This scholarship is in addition to any other grants or scholarships a student might receive.

Experiential Learning

Developing marketable skills through experiential learning is a key element of the ULN program. The ULN model ensures that all second- through fourth-year students complete 10 hours of experiential learning per week as a part of their scholarship requirement.

ULN Events are open only to ULN Students, Unless otherwise specified
summary: The University Leadership Network (ULN) is a nationally recognized incentive-based scholarship program for students with demonstrated financial need. Each year, 500 freshmen are selected to begin the program.

name: Texas Interdisciplinary Plan Mentor Academy
description:
The TIP Mentor Academy consists of over 80 trained undergraduate students from the Colleges of Natural Sciences and Liberal Arts. Their goal is to enhance academic excellence and to encourage a successful transition to the University of Texas at Austin for our TIP Scholars.

Through weekly contact with their mentees, highly trained peer mentors help students learn about campus resources, adjust to college life, pursue extracurricular opportunities, and develop educational and career aspirations.  Mentors also assist Scholars with a group research project for their UGS 303 signature course.

The Mentor Academy experience offers:

• A chance to provide excellent academic and social support for our incoming TIP Scholars

• Opportunity to further develop leadership skills

• Valuable professional development training and international certification as a mentor and/or tutor

 

Mentor Academy Facts

•Since 2008, the  Academy’s curriculum and training has been  adapted  for training College of Natural Sciences  student employees in peer leadership roles.
 
•Frequent peer mentoring conference presentations  and research collaborations  have connected the Academy with other UT academic departments and universities nation-wide.
 
•Chapters  on TIP’s mentor model and select trainings are included in the Handbook For Training Peer Tutors and Mentors, a  publication of the College Reading and Learning Association (CRLA) and in Undergraduate Curricular Peer Mentoring Programs:  Perspectives on Innovation by Faculty, Staff, and Students, edited by Tania S. Smith

 

What Mentors are Saying:

“Being a TIP mentor has given me a better perspective on the diversity of the University.  I’ve also learned to communicate better and how to explain concepts in multiple manners.  I’ve also gotten to know a great bunch of students I never would have met otherwise.”

“Being a TIP Mentor is a great way to develop leadership skills.  What we learn now in mentor trainings will continue to help later in life, and being a mentor is awesome because you get to help others.  It’s a lot of work and commitment to be responsible for 4-6 people, but it’s been a great experience.”

“The biggest thing I have gained from TIP Mentoring is a greater sense of self-motivation and initiative. I find myself taking the first step to get things done, and I’m not afraid to interact with people I need to work with. Being a tutor has also given me an even greater understanding of a subject as well as how to convey information more effectivel
summary: The TIP Mentor Academy consists of over 85 trained undergraduate students from the Colleges of Natural Sciences and Liberal Arts. Their goal is to enhance academic excellence and encourage successful transition to the University of Texas for TIP Scholars

name: College of Natural Sciences Peer Leader Academy
description:
The Peer Leader Academy (PLA) provides a foundation of professional development training and certification for peer leaders (tutors, mentors, undergraduate teaching assistants, etc.) who are employed in the College of Natural Sciences. Training involves the development of leadership and transferable skills that can be utilized in their current roles, in the classroom, and in their futures.  Peer Leaders serve in meaningful roles and are a vital component of creating community within the college.

Benefits:  What will I gain through being a part of the Peer Leader Academy?

  • International certification as a mentor or tutor through CRLA or certification from the College of Natural Sciences Peer Leader Academy
  • Transferrable Skills, Leadership Development, & interaction with UT Students, Staff and Faculty
  • Opportunity for valuable experiential learning

Training

Peer Leaders attain certification based on guidelines provided by the College Reading and Learning Association (CRLA) through their training and documented student contact hours.

In order to attain Level I (Basic) certification, CNS Peer Leaders complete:

  • 15 hours of Peer Leader training within their first 2 long semesters of employment or prior to employment.
  • Attend a CNS PLA Training conference to earn 8 of their 15 hours of training (August or January)
  • 50 hours of student contact

Options for earning CNS PLA training hours*

  • Conference Attendance (8hrs) + Supplemental Training (3 hrs) + Program Specific Training (4hrs) + 50 hrs of Student Contact = Level I Certification
  • CNS PLA Conference Attendance + a NSC 109 or CH372C course + 50 Hrs of Student Contact = Level I Certification
  • Level II and Level III Certification are earned with an additional 10 hrs of training and 25 hrs of student contact per level

*Students may not earn double credit for attending the same training more than once, excluding cultural competency & campus/community resources.

 

Training Topics

Training topics are based on the CRLA certification guidelines for peer tutors and mentors which include, but are not limited to:

  • Role of the Peer Leader
  • Professional Conduct
  • Problem-solving
  • Campus & Community Resources
  • Cultural Competency
  • Tutoring in Math & Science
  • Compliance with University & College Policies
  • Time management & goal-setting
  • Situation assessment & student referrals
  • Building rapport & motivating peers
  • Setting b
summary: The Peer Leader Academy (PLA) provides a foundation of professional development training and certification for peer leaders (tutors, mentors, undergraduate teaching assistants, etc.) who are employed in the College of Natural Sciences.

name: DoS Graduate Assistants
summary: Group created to allow DoS Graduate Assistants to connect with each other and stay updated on all the latest in the department.

name: Chemical Engineering Graduate Leadership Council
description: The Chemical Engineering Graduate Leadership Council (GLC) aims to serve the graduate students of the McKetta Department of Chemical Engineering by hosting fulfilling events which enrich life beyond research and by advocating for students in departmental affairs.
summary: This council is responsible for the management of professional enrichment, social, financial, and recruitment activities as it pertains to chemical engineering graduate students at UT.

name: Lambda Phi Epsilon Fraternity, Inc.
description: Lambda Phi Epsilon's vision is to become the preeminent international Asian interest fraternal organization, providing outstanding leadership, philanthropy, and advocacy in the community.
summary: Lambda Phi Epsilon's vision is to become the preeminent international Asian interest fraternal organization, providing outstanding leadership, philanthropy, and advocacy in the community.

name: Diversity in Advertising & PR
description:
Diversity in Advertising and Public Relations was founded as Minorities in Advertising in 2013 at The University of Texas at Austin. A small group of multicultural woman looked around and realized that the industry lacked diversity and decided to try and change that. MIA would then go on to become Diversity in Advertising and PR (DA&PR), in order to become a more inclusive organization and to create an empowering space for like-minded individuals of any gender or ethnicity. We stand on the three pillars of diversity, networking, and professionalism. We have weekly meetings/workshops, agency guest speakers, and local agency tours.

SIGN UP for our newsletter & JOIN our GroupMe:groupme.com/join_group/62070926/XoON3MPl
summary: DAPR is a student-run organization that focuses and celebrates diversity in the Ad/PR sphere to empower underrepresented talent. DAPR was created to connect members of all backgrounds with professionals, strengthen portfolios, and build community at UT.

name: Neuroscience Graduate Students' Association
description: The Neuroscience Graduate Students' Association (NGSA) is the Interdisciplinary Neuroscience Program's student organization. Working both individually and collaboratively, the officers on the board perform a variety of tasks to serve INP graduate students. For example, officers represent student interests to faculty and to UT’s Graduate Student Assembly, organize outreach and mentoring opportunities, build and maintain resources for students, plan social events throughout the year, and work with faculty and staff to organize program events such as recruitment weekend and the monthly INP Dialogues talks. The guiding goal of the board is to improve students’ experiences, whether that be by scheduling a group outing to the Austin Bouldering Project or by advocating for changes to the Graduate School’s health insurance policies. In addition to bettering the training environment for current and future students, NGSA helps build a stronger sense of community among INP students, facilitating the growth of support networks that bolster students’ professional success and personal wellbeing.
summary: NGSA is the Interdisciplinary Neuroscience Program's graduate student organization. We organize social and outreach events for INP students and bring together a community for all who are interested in neuroscience

name: Heartfulness Meditation Group
description:
Stressed out with classes? Looking to try something new? Join the Heartfulness Meditation Club to add more peace and Zen to your life.

Whatever experience with meditation you may have, all are welcome to join us online for group meditations at 8:30 PM every Tuesday. Please reach out to us if you are interested, and we can email you the link!
summary:
Stressed out with classes? Looking to try something new? Join the Heartfulness Meditation Club to add more peace and Zen to your life.

Whatever experience with meditation you may have, all are welcome to join us online for group meditations.

name: Longhorn Gaming
description:
Welcome to Longhorn Gaming, the central hub for casual gaming & competitive esports at UT!

We bring people together on campus to create a community at UT for gamers from all different backgrounds and skill levels. Whether you play casually or competitively, multiplayer or single player, Longhorn Gaming is the place for you!

We host LANs, watch parties, compete in collegiate esports leagues, and provide opportunities for UT gamers to socialize across campus.

We communicate all club announcements through our organization Discord server, linked here 

Discord is completely free to sign up and use. More information on membership perks can be found by clicking the #membership channel on the left sidebar of the server.

If you are a current student interested in growing LG, please reach out to one of our Officers or email us at longhorngaming@utexas.edu.

 

Interested in getting involved with LG?

Join our discord and be on the lookout for our announcement about committees!


LG is a Sponsored Student Organization (SSO) through University Unions, a Division of Student Affairs.

Please follow us on our other socials linked below!
summary: Longhorn Gaming is the central hub for casual gaming at UT. No matter your background, what game you play, or your skill level, LG is the organization for you. We host LAN parties, compete in tournaments, and play video games together every day.

name: Chi Kappa Phi Service Society
description:
Chi Kappa Phi Service Society is a Co-Ed organization established to serve our campus and the surrounding Austin community. We hope to inspire a lifelong dedication to philanthropic service by fostering friendships strengthened through a tradition of scholarship and leadership. By doing so, we seek to contribute to our community by facilitating each member’s journey to discover and develop their own philanthropic interests. We will do this by providing numerous service opportunities throughout the school year, especially emphasizing one of our main philanthropies: Oak Springs Elementary School.

 

Working closely with Oak Springs since Chi Kappa Phi's inception has been rewarding in many ways. Through collaborative efforts, we work to provide the necessary support and assistance for the school to develop the minds of young leaders. Understanding the importance of education and community involvement, our goal is to establish a healthy working relationship with the school and be able to coordinate a mentor program, as well.

 

Each year, our new class (called the Peep Class) organizes a creative event to benefit an organization or cause of their choosing. This class project is designed to allow each new member to directly impact our community through philanthropy while learning valuable management, communication, research, and creative skills. Along with uniting the new class, this project will raise awareness for the chosen organization or cause as well as increase our service society’s presence on the campus and in the city community.

 

If you are interested in joining our organization, please reference our Instagram where we will be uploading our recruitment calendar/events! We look forward to meeting you soon!

RECRUITMENT CALENDER:
Info Session #1: August 27th, 7:00-8:00pm at Union Sinclair Suite (3.128)

Meet & Greet #1: August 28th, 7:00-8:00pm at Union Sinclair Suite (3.128)

Info Session #2: September 2nd, 8:00-9:00pm at WCP Meeting Room (1.106)

Meet & Greet #2: September 3rd, 6:30-7:30pm at Turtle Pond
summary: The mission of Chi Kappa Phi is to inspire a lifelong dedication to philanthropic service by fostering friendships strengthened through a tradition of scholarship and leadership.

name: Texas Equestrian Team
description: The primary purpose of the Equestrian Team is to compete in intercollegiate horse show competitions. Through these competitions, the equestrian team provides eligible students from beginning through advanced levels with the opportunity to develop their riding skills, sportsmanship, and enrich their educational experience. The club also allows members to develop leadership skills and encourages involvement by helping to improve the University and the local community. Find our feature in Austin Fit Magazine here.
summary: The primary purpose of the Equestrian Team is to compete in intercollegiate horse show competitions. Through these competitions, the equestrian team provides eligible students from beginning through advanced levels with the opportunity to develop thei...

name: Sikh Students Association
description: To promote the values and to spread awareness of Sikhi through events involving the university community and the Austin community. You can join the GroupMe for 2025-26 here.
summary: To promote the values and to spread awareness of Sikhi through events involving the university community and the Austin community.

name: International Graduate Student Ministry - at Austin
description:
International Graduate Student Ministry (IGSM) is a Christian fellowship that welcomes international graduate students from all walks of life and backgrounds. We know the adjustment to a new country can be difficult, so we want to be a home-away-from-home for you here at UT! We host biweekly bible studies and community building events throughout the school year. Please fill out this Google form if you're interested in getting connected! https://forms.gle/cAnDt7qg3nj4qgxD6

 

We meet as a group for weekly Bible studies, usually on Fridays, to learn what the Bible says about our lives. Along the way, we eat good food, have fun together, and have times to answer timeless questions while building up a sense of family in our group. So, whether you’re looking to have fun, make new friends, or learn about the Bible, you’re at the right place. Come join us!

 

https://youtu.be/9yee5ZSqWao
summary: International Graduate Student Ministry (IGSM) is a Christian fellowship that welcomes international graduate students from all walks of life and backgrounds. We seek to be a home-away-from-home for our students at UT!

name: Texas Business Healthcare Association
description: Texas Business Healthcare Association is an organization within the McCombs School of Business at The University of Texas at Austin. TXBHA is focused on filling in the gap between business and healthcare by exposing students to the financial, managerial, legal, and consulting aspects of the healthcare industry. Each semester, TXBHA curates a series of speakers to provide insight on these topics, in addition to providing community service opportunities and organizing social events. Membership is open to all UT students interested in medicine, dentistry, pharmacy or business.
summary: The Texas Business Healthcare Association at The University of Texas at Austin is a student organization dealing with business, healthcare, and student involvement. TXBHA is open to all students.

name: Election Supervisory Board
description:
The Election Supervisory Board (ESB) is tasked with conducting and overseeing all campus-wide and first-year student elections. The entities that defer to the ESB for election processes are: 

  • Graduate Student Assembly
  • Student Government
  • Texas Student Media
  • University Co-op
  • University Unions

The ESB conducts candidate seminars, adjudicates complaints, holds hearings, and issues advisory opinions on all election activities. These and all other powers granted to the ESB fall strictly within the purview of the Campus-Wide Election Code, which is adopted by each entity mentioned above, revised annually, and approved by the Office of the Dean of Students.

For more information about Campus-Wide and First-Year Elections, please visit www.utexasvote.org. 

If you have questions regarding Campus-Wide Elections, contact the Election Supervisory Board at sgoadvisors@austin.utexas.edu.
summary: The Election Supervisory Board (ESB) is tasked with conducting and overseeing all campus-wide student elections.

name: Austin Chinese Campus Christian Fellowship
description: We offer a place for Chinese Christian students and those who are interested in Christianity to meet up, and organize Bible studies, prayer meetings, in addition to some other organizational activities. We’re  actively looking for Christian students who are faithful and passionate about helping catechumen and seekers learn about Christianity. We study the Bible in Mandarin, so it’s better if you’re familiar in Mandarin😉
summary: Gather Chinese Christian Students; Austin Chinese Church outreach for UT Students; Provide Bible studies and Christian activities...

name: Longhorn Catholic Council
description: The Longhorn Catholic Council is a community of students who represent Catholics on campus and those interested in the Catholic faith. The LCC serves as the student council and extension of the University Catholic Center's (UCC) mission and vision of bringing the Truth, Beauty, and Goodness of Jesus Christ to UT Austin. The LCC seeks to promote Catholic activities and provide events and opportunities for spiritual growth for any interested member of the UT community. We are a way for students to be part of the Catholic community and make the UCC their spiritual home while in college.
summary: The LCC is the organization of Catholic students on campus and those interested in the Catholic faith. The LCC seeks to promote Catholic activities on campus and provide opportunities for spiritual growth for any interested member of the UT community.

name: Omega Phi Gamma Fraternity
description:
Omega Phi Gamma is an Asian interest fraternity established in 1995 at the University of Texas at Austin with established chapters all over Texas. We strive to promote brotherhood, leadership, and service within the Asian-American community, and continue to maintain the highest levels of excellence while encouraging the growth of strong and successful men. Omega Phi Gamma stands as the sole fraternity at the University of Texas at Austin that offers an endowment scholarship program to provide financial assistance to incoming students in their educational pursuits. Every year, we host the annual Unity Talent Show to celebrate and showcase Asian-American talent at the University of Texas at Austin. UNITY celebrates these heritages, promoting cultural diversity, and recognizing the similarities and differences of all Asian-American cultures in hopes of bringing people closer together.

UNITY FALL 2024: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pOll4YF5_Ys&pp=ygUVb21lZ2EgcGhpIGdhbW1hIHVuaXR5
summary: Omega Phi Gamma is an Asian-interest fraternity established in the Spring of 1995 at the University of Texas at Austin. We strive to promote brotherhood, leadership, academics and service within the Asian-American community.

name: American Association of Psychiatric Pharmacists, Austin Texas Collegiate Chapter
description:
The mission of the American Association of Psychiatric Pharmacists, Austin Texas Collegiate Chapter is to provide resources to help fight the stigma against substance use disorders, mental health conditions, co-occurring disorders, and recovery. 

It was designed by conscientious students, faculty, and staff in the College of Pharmacy. AAPP Austin, Texas Collegiate Chapter's students, faculty, and staff members are able to refer others to the appropriate University and Austin resources that best address their particular needs.
summary: We aim to educate our students about career paths within psychiatric and neurologic pharmacy, and to engage with our peers and community to lessen stigma related to substance use disorders, mental health conditions, co-occurring disorders, and recovery.

name: Student Veterans Association
description: The SVA is dedicated to creating a welcoming social environment for the veteran communities on campus through comradery, social functions, guest speakers, and offering a space to become a better student.
summary: "The SVA is dedicated to creating a culture for veterans on campus to further their social, academic, and professional skills while advocating for their needs."

name: Student Emergency Services
description:
Student Emergency Services assists students with different types of emergencies, including student deaths, death of a relative, family emergencies, attempted suicide, student illness/injury, interpersonal violence or stalking, sexual assault,  missing students, apartment or house fire, natural disaster, financial problems, and concerns about academic adjustment or performance.

Services can include student advocacy and absence notifications, coordination with relevant campus departments, limited emergency funds, outreach and support for students, short-term emergency shelter (when available), flag-lowering ceremony (for current students only), and general consultation.
summary: Student Emergency Services, in the Office of the Dean of Students, works with campus and community organizations to provide a number of services to assist students, and in some cases their families and friends, during an emergency or crisis situation.

name: Discovery Scholars Program
description: The mission of the Discovery Scholars Program is to provide support for college transitions, connect students to campus resources, and encourage responsibility for students’ individual educational journeys. During their time in the program, Discovery Scholars will acquire skills to help them to succeed in both their majors and in life after UT Austin.
summary: The two-year Discovery Scholars Program (DSP) invites students admitted to the School of Undergraduate Studies to work with DSP advisors as they transition from high school to college and navigate their unique path at UT Austin.

name: University Unions
description: Representing five centers for student life, the University Unions is an organization committed to enhancing the campus community through a variety of facilities, programs and services. Comprised of Hogg Memorial Auditorium, the Student Activity Center, Student Services Building and the Texas Union, as well as housing Campus Events + Entertainment, the Unions provide a space for for leisure, recreation, cultural, educational and social opportunities.
summary: Representing five centers for student life, the University Unions is an organization committed to enhancing the campus community through a variety of facilities (HMA, SAC, SSB, TXUN), programs and services (E+E).

name: Study Abroad
shortName: Take the World by the Horns!
description: Our mission is to provide outreach, service, and support to students considering or committed to studying abroad. We accomplish this by partnering with faculty and staff to facilitate participation through integration into the curriculum, and by continuously seeking ways to improve our own practices to better serve students and the University.  Our vision is that all UT students who are interested in and eligible to study abroad will have the opportunity to do so.
summary: Study abroad changes lives, and the work of Study Abroad at the University of Texas is guided by our commitment to promoting access to study abroad opportunities for our diverse population of students.

name: Texas Student Media
description:
Texas Student Media (TSM) is the main source for news on campus. TSM is composed of The Daily Texan newspaper, Texas Travesty, the Cactus Yearbook, KVRX 91.7 FM, TSTV-Texas Student Television, BurntX and the Advertising Department. All of these entities are run by students for students. It is a great place for students to get hands on, professional experience in a variety of communications fields that will help them succeed upon graduating from UT. 

Interested in getting involved? Complete the form at www.participateTSM.com. Indicate the area(s) that you’re interested in and the manager from that entity will respond with next steps. Each department handles applications differently. More info available at www.texasstudentmedia.com.
summary: TSM is comprised of several student-produced media properties: The Daily Texan newspaper; the humor magazine Texas Travesty; the Cactus yearbook; KVRX 91.7 FM; Texas Student Television (TSTV), and BurntX.

name: Residence Hall Program Team
description: RHPT is a staff of on-campus students who plans entertainment events for on-campus residents. We host 8-10 events per semester and coordinate comedians, bands, movies, tournaments and other events for students who live in DHFS residence halls.
summary: The Residence Hall Program Team (RHPT) is a unit within the Division of Housing and Food Service and is comprised of a staff of undergraduate students hired to plan and coordinate entertainment events for on-campus residents.

name: Student Governance Organizations
description: Student Governance Organizations is an area within The Office of the Dean of Students. We assist with administrative, financial and programatic support of Student Government and the Senate of College Councils at the University of Texas at Austin.
summary: Student Governance Organizations is an area within The Office of the Dean of Students.  We assist with administrative, financial and programatic support of Student Government and the Senate of College Councils.

name: Healthyhorns
description:
Healthyhorns is the umbrella brand for University Health Services, Counseling Mental Health Center and the Longhorn Wellness Center.

University Health Services provides healthcare and public health leadership to support students in reaching their optimal health so they can achieve their academic and personal potential. Visit healthyhorns.utexas.edu for more information.

Counseling and Mental Health Center serves UT’s diverse campus community by providing high-quality, innovative and culturally informed mental health programs and services that enhance and support students’ well-being, academic and life goals. Visit cmhc.utexas.edu for more information.

Longhorn Wellness Center (LWC) supports the missions of University Health Services and the Counseling and Mental Health Center by addressing priority college health issues. We do this using multi-level, evidence-based prevention strategies such as peer education, social marketing, social norm campaigns, environmental changes, and strategic campus partnerships. Visit healthyhorns.utexas.edu/healthpromotion.html for more information.
summary: UT's healthy campus coalition: University Health Services (UHS), located in the Student Services Building, is an on-campus healthcare facility just like your family doctor and more.

name: Texas Global
description: Connect with us for Education Abroad, intercultural activities, global research opportunities, international student and scholar services, English language programs, and more.
summary: In an increasingly interconnected world, international education is more important than ever, and UT is committed to educating global citizens and carrying out research that changes lives and addresses the world’s greatest challenges.

name: The Center for Students in Recovery
description:
The Center for Students in Recovery (CSR) supports students recovering from addictions at the University of Texas at Austin. We offer programming, resources, and activities that provide a pathway to both recovery and academic achievement. Since its founding in 2004, hundreds of students have turned to us for support. Our goal is to help students in recovery stay in recovery while enrolled here. We also welcome students curious about sobriety to explore, experience, and understand recovery at UT.

We provide students in all stages of recovery comprehensive, on-going opportunities for personal and academic success, creating a campus that celebrates recovery while enriching lives.
summary: The Center for Students in Recovery (CSR) supports students recovering from addictions at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Longhorn Scholars Program
description:
Mission Statement

Longhorn Scholars Program provides support for the successful transition from high school to college and encourages participation in programs that enrich students’ educational experiences and promote lifelong learning.

Program Components

  • Strategic academic advising
  • Mentoring
  • Leadership development
  • Rigorous academic curriculum options
  • Creating community
  • Unique academic opportunities
  • On-going program evaluation and assessment
summary: Longhorn Scholars Program is a four-year program that serves top students from selected Texas high schools whose graduates have historically been underrepresented at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Longhorn Orientation
description: Longhorn Orientation welcomes thousands of incoming students each year, ensuring their successful transition to the University. We provide programs and resources that empower students to achieve academic and co-curricular success.
summary: Longhorn Orientation welcomes thousands of incoming students each year, ensuring their successful transition, both academically and socially, to the University.

name: Texas Parents
summary: Texas Parents is the official parents’ association of the University of Texas at Austin with a fully-staffed campus office to serve the needs of all UT parents and families.

name: Community Engagement and Advocacy Agency of Student Government
description:
Please Do **NOT** Request Membership through HornsLink! If you would like to stay connected, please follow us on instagram (@utsgcea)!

Current Directors: 

Tabitha Verdugo, Tabithaverdugo@gmail.com 

Ezinne Osemene, osemene.ezinne@gmail.com  

 

CEA’s Mission:

The Community Engagement and Advocacy Agency (CEA) enhances interaction between Student Government and the student body through organizing various activities, events, and social initiatives! We lead outreach efforts to increase Student Government’s presence and engagement across campus life. Additionally, we aim to educate Student Government, campus, and student communities about the value of belonging and to promote advocacy initiatives.
summary: The Community Engagement and Advocacy Agency (CEA) enhances interaction between Student Government and the student body through organizing various activities, events, and social initiatives!

name: Club Tennis
description:
Texas Club Tennis at the University of Texas at Austin is a licensed recreational sports organization that promotes the sport of tennis throughout the UT community and brings together all levels of players to practice and compete against other University club teams around the state of Texas and across the nation.

We fall under the umbrella of the United States' Tennis Association's Tennis on Campus collegiate program. This division sprung from the need for a bridge between junior and adult tennis for those not playing varsity college tennis and has grown immensely since its inception over ten years ago. For more information visit http://www.tennisoncampus.com/

The Texas Club Tennis team has a rich history of participation and strong results at the sectional and national levels. Some of our accolades include:

  • 2008 National Champions
  • 2012 Spring Invitationals Runner-up
  • 2013 Sectional Champions
  • 2018 Sectional Runner-up
  • 2018 Spring Invitationals Champions
  • 2019 Sectional Champions
  • 2020 Sectional Champions
  • 2022 Sectional Champions


And various other wins in Tennis on Campus regional events.

We participate in many regional events across the state, a sectionals championship, and send teams to the National Championship and Invitational Tournaments every year.

With each year we grow as an organization and are adding new and exciting things to our club. We are looking to expand and make a name as the largest and most respected sports club on the UT campus. 

In addition, there are numerous social opportunities afforded by Texas Club Tennis. We hold socials, volunteer events, and various other events that serve to foster a sense of camaraderie. People make life-long friendships through club tennis, and we really strive to create a casual, friendly environment while still making tennis a big priority.

If you are interested in Texas Club Tennis we will be tabling various places around campus and at Party on the Plaza during the fall semester. In addition, you can check out our website at texasclubtennis.com. If you have any questions, feel free to email us at uttennisclub@gmail.com and we will try to respond as soon as possible.

Thanks and Hook'em!
summary: The goal of club tennis is to bring together players of different levels in order to practice, play tournaments, and make friends. We hope to support and spread the game of tennis throughout the UT campus.

name: Opportunities for Undergraduate Research at UT Austin
description: Each year, a select group of DoS Scholars is chosen to participate in OUR UT (Opportunities for Undergraduate Research at The University of Texas at Austin) program. Led by Associate Dean of Students for Research, Dr. Audrey Sorrells, these undergraduate students are matched with research being conducted by the DoS Research Institute. In an effort to connect research with the practice of student affairs, projects focus on co-curricular programming affecting student performance, engagement, retention and graduation. Current topics being explored include culturally responsive practices, mentoring, judicial services and student veterans. DoS Scholars are chosen through a competitive process.
summary: OUR UT is an undergraduate research program for incoming freshmen students. Students take part in higher education research projects working with a student mentor, graduate students, faculty and student affairs professionals.

name: School of Human Ecology
description:
We seek to improve the quality of life for all people through research and education on basic and enduring human experiences across time, place and life circumstances. We are particularly interested in: the foods we eat, the relationships we nurture and the textiles that protect and clothe us.

The world we live in is complex. We draw on the disciplines of science, history, business, art and health to advance our understanding. We motivate our students to be leaders in their communities; we prepare our students to improve the quality of life for all people. The School of Human Ecology is science based and human focused.
summary: We are human development and family sciences, nutritional sciences and textiles and apparel. We are science based and human focused.

name: Ronald E. McNair Scholars Program
description:
The Ronald E. McNair Post-baccalaureate Achievement Program recognizes that the diversity of researchers is an integral component of scholastic excellence. It seeks to produce a new generation of scholars and researchers that will more accurately reflect the growing diversity in intellectual perspectives, life experiences, and cultures represented in academia.

The McNair Scholars Program at The University of Texas at Austin is a federally funded program (one of the TRIO Programs funded by the Department of Education) mandated by Congress. The goal of the program is to increase the number of students in doctoral degree programs who are low-income and first-generation undergraduates or students who come from groups underrepresented in graduate education, particularly African American, Hispanic/Latino, American Indians, Alaskan Native, Native Hawaiians, and Native American Pacific Islanders. The McNair Scholars Program will serve 25 students each year. An integral component of the program is the Summer Research Institute (SRI) Internship at UT Austin. Each summer, up to 13 students will be selected for this ten-week paid internship. Program participants must be committed to enrolling in graduate programs. The program will track progress towards the successful completion of a doctoral degree.

 

Program Services

  • Research opportunities for participants who have completed their sophomore year of college (Opportunities for research or other scholarly activities at the grantee institution or at graduate centers are designed to provide participants with effective preparation for doctoral study).
  • Exposure to cultural events and academic programs not usually available to other undergraduates.
  • Mentoring (Mentoring programs involving faculty members or students at institutions of higher education, or any combination of faculty members and students).
  • Seminars and other scholarly activities designed to prepare students for doctoral studies
  • Summer internships
  • Travel to research conferences and graduate school visits
  • Tutoring
  • Academic counseling
  • Assistance in obtaining student financial aid
  • Assistance in securing admission and financial aid for enrollment in graduate programs
  • Guidance on teaching methods and strategies via our McNair Teaching Academy

Workshops

  • The Nuts and Bolts of Graduate School
  • The Faculty Mentor Relationship
  • Public Speaking and Presentations
  • GRE Prep
  • Writing Personal Statements
  • Creating a CV
  • Choosing the right school and program for you
  • How to fund your graduate education

Eligibility

A student is eligible to participate in the McNair Scholars Program if the student meets all the following requirements:

(a)    Is a citizen or national of the United States; or

Is a permanent resident of the United States; or

Is in the United States for other than a temporary purpose and provides evidence from the Immigration and Naturalization Service of his or her intent to become a permanent resident; or

Is a permanent resident of Guam, the Northern Mariana Islands, or the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands; or 

Is a resident of one of the Freely Associated States.

(b) Is currently enrolled in a degree program at The University of Texas at Austin.

(c) Is-

  1. A low-income individual who is a first-generation college student; or
  2. A member of a group that is underrepresented in graduate education (Black, Hispanic, American Indian, Alaskan Native, Native Hawaiian, Native American Pacific Islanders); or
  3. A member of a group that is underrepresented in certain academic disciplines as documented by standard statistical references or other national survey data submitted to and accepted by the Secretary on a case-by-case basis.

(d) Has not enrolled in ANY post-baccalaureate programs at an institution of higher education.

 

Contact Us

mcnairprogram@austin.utexas.edu  512.471.1205
summary: The Ronald E. McNair Post-baccalaureate Achievement Program recognizes that the diversity of researchers is an integral component of scholastic excellence. It seeks to produce a new generation of scholars and researchers that will more accurately reflect

name: Opportunities for Undergraduate Research at UT Austin Student Mentors
description: The OUR UT Mentors is a new program that uses sophomore students that previously participated in the OUR UT program to act as mentors to incoming freshmen. Their responsibilities include assisting new OUR UT participants with acclimatizing to the university both academically and socially. Mentors meet with their mentees at least bi-weekly throughout the school year. Their activities are tied to the five themes of 360 Connections. In addition, mentors also continue their involvement with research projects being run through the DoS Research Institute (DRI).
summary: The OUR UT Student Mentors are former students in the OUR UT program. They act as mentors to the incoming freshmen who participate in OUR UT. They also take part in continuing research out of the DoS Research Institute.

name: Jackson School of Geosciences
summary: The JSG includes the University’s Department of Geological Sciences - one of the country's oldest geoscience departments - and two world-renowned research units -- the Institute for Geophysics and the Bureau of Economic Geology.

name: Healthyhorns Peer Educators
summary: Students who lead health and wellness initiatives on campus to student groups, organizations and classes.

name: Department of Biomedical Engineering
summary: The Department of Biomedical Engineering at The University of Texas at Austin

name: College of Natural Sciences Student Life
description: The CNS Student Life team provides staff support and resources to science-based organizations whose focus and activities benefit CNS students through leadership experience, outreach and networking.
summary: The College of Natural Sciences is associated with over 60 student organizations, all of which provide students with a way to meet people, get involved, develop leadership skills and learn more about a specific field.

name: Department of Civil, Architectural and Environmental Engineering
description:
We are home to six research areas of study: Building Energy and Environments, Construction Engineering and Project Management, Environmental and Water Resources Engineering, Geotechnical Engineering, Structural Engineering, Mechanics, and Materials, and Transportation Engineering.

The mission of the Department of Civil, Architectural and Environmental Engineering encompasses excellence in undergraduate and graduate education, research, and public service. We will do the following:

  • Educate the next generation of engineering leaders to formulate and solve complex problems of importance to society, to collaborate as productive team members, to engage in life-long learning, and to act professionally and ethically

 

  • Extend engineering knowledge across the breadth of the discipline and beyond through creative, innovative research ranging from the fundamental to the applied

 

  • Facilitate the understanding and use of new ideas, technologies, and practices for the betterment of society through service and leadership in local, state, national, and international communities
summary: The Department of Civil, Architectural and Environmental Engineering at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Center for Asian American Studies
description: Founded in 2000, the Center for Asian American Studies (CAAS) at The University of Texas at Austin is an interdisciplinary academic program promoting understanding and awareness of Asian Pacific American (APA) issues and communities. We house undergraduate major, minor, and honors programs and organize lectures, films, conferences, speakers' series, discussion forums, and collaborate partnerships with community and campus organizations. CAAS seeks to develop and highlight transnational and hemispheric perspectives and narratives of Asians in the Americas and the American South.
summary: The Center for Asian American Studies (CAAS) at The University of Texas at Austin is an interdisciplinary academic program promoting understanding and awareness of Asian Pacific American (APA) issues and communities.

name: DoS Graduate Assistant Supervisors
summary: A group of professional staff members who supervise graduate assistants.

name: SNAFU Improv
description: SNAFU Improv is UT's ONLY long-form improvised comedy troupe. We create never before seen, never before practiced, and completely made up on the spot shows based on audience suggestions! Follow us on Instagram @snafu_prov.
summary: SNAFU Improv is UT's ONLY long-form improvised comedy troupe. We create never before seen, never before practiced, and completely made up on the spot shows based on audience suggestions!

name: ATX Science Olympiad
description: ATX Science Olympiad is a non-profit student organization dedicated to inspiring creative problem solving, STEM literacy, and a love for learning through its outreach activities and biannual Science Olympiad tournaments at the invitational and regional level. We seek to increase student engagement across Texas by providing resources and support to new and upcoming teams of middle and highschoolers. We want all teams, regardless of background, to be able to experience a challenging yet fun team-based Science Olympiad experience that motivates their students to learn outside the classroom.
summary: We are a STEM outreach organization that runs Science Olympiad competitions for 6th - 12th graders and seeks to aid in the establishment of SciOly teams in middle and high schools across Texas, increasing the accessibility of team-based STEM learning.

name: School of Human Ecology Ambassadors
description:
Mission Statement 
To serve the School of Human Ecology by building community within the student population and supporting the school’s outreach efforts. We provide a voice for the students by acting as liaisons between faculty and the student population. Ambassadors provide leadership and engagement opportunities for Human Ecology students.

Description
The School of Human Ecology Ambassadors is a sponsored student organization of up to 35 of the best students that Human Ecology has to offer. In order to become a member, students must be nominated by faculty, staff or current member. Ambassadors act as representatives of the SoHE disciplines (Public Health, Nutrition, Human Development & Family Science, and Textiles & Apparel) within the UT community, the Austin area, and to prospective students.

Structure
The organization is structured around the three committees: Mentoring, Social, and Events/Outreach.
summary: The purpose of the organization is to promote a sense of community within the School of Human Ecology and act as a liaison between the students and the faculty at the school.

name: Women's Community Center
description:
The WCC functions as a central hub, providing personalized guidance to individual students and student organizations. It serves as a welcoming space for students to connect, fostering a sense of community on campus. The center’s staff offers support and advice, addressing students’ concerns effectively. In collaboration with various campus and community organizations, the WCC ensures that all UT students can access essential resources and support services. This comprehensive approach aims to empower students, enabling them to excel academically while navigating the challenges of a large campus. The center’s goal is to foster a strong sense of connection among Longhorns and with the university at large.

The road to establishing the WCC goes back decades. After students separately established the Women’s Resource Center in 1997 and the GLBTA Agency in 2001, the two groups decided to pursue a joint center in the spring of 2002 to demonstrate greater student demand and establish an organization that would have greater long-term sustainability. The groups benefited from a new Dean of Students, Teresa Graham Brett, who was sensitive to women’s and LGBTQIA+ issues, and a new Student Government President, Brian Haley, who campaigned on a promise to establish a Gender and Sexuality Center. A proposal for a Gender and Sexuality Center was approved in March 2004 and its doors officially opened in August of that year. In January of 2023, because of Senate Bill 17, the Gender and Sexuality Center became the Women’s Community Center, but the center’s commitment to making sure that all Longhorns feel like they belong at UT Austin is still in place.

Check out our Linktree.
summary: The mission of the Women’s Community Center (WCC) is to be a place for Longhorns of all genders to connect, find resources, and get support around experiences of intersectionality, community, and gender solidarity.

name: Bhakti Yoga Club
description:
Link to join UT Bhakti Yoga GroupMe: https://app.groupme.com/join_group/48268664/Ms8srJLE

*

Our Vision  

" To present the science of bhakti-yoga to the UT  community and help individuals experience and achieve a higher level of consciousness, knowledge, well being, and happiness."
     

Who are we?

We are a student club at UT Austin campus. Our club has been instrumental in bringing to the UT community, the Science of the holistic Yoga process. Yoga in the western world is typically known as a physical workout leading to flexibility and muscle tone. However, Bhakti yoga is the highest and true form of yoga that caters to not just the body but also to mind, intellect, and soul.  The word 'Bhakti' itself is a word that means 'loving devotional service' and our club is devoted to helping students connect with the divine within and experience the true happiness and bliss on a spiritual platform.

We also invite experienced yogis, traveling monks, and distinguished speakers to discuss various aspects of our existence such as Relationships, Satisfaction, what is happiness, Karma, mind, laws of mind, developing spiritual qualities, etc. We hold vegetarian cooking classes especially considering student needs and conditions. Our focus is to bring about a transformation, inside-out, and working on the ecology of the heart. Thus this holistic process of Bhakti yoga helps not just to integrate your body, mind, and soul but also to integrate your existence!

 

Our Activities:

We have virtual meetings on Fridays from 6:30 pm to 8:00 pm. Link to the virtual meeting is provided in the GroupMe (link to join UT Bhakti yoga GroupMe: https://app.groupme.com/join_group/48268664/Ms8srJLE).

*

Each of our sessions will comprise of the following activities

  • Yoga for Body or Hatha yoga can help you recognize your hidden physical and mental potentials. Through the continued practice of asanas, you will gain flexibility, strength, and derive health benefits.

  • Mantra or Kirtan Yoga: Mantra is a Sanskrit derivation of two words - "man", meaning the mind, and "tra", meaning deliverance. mantra means delivering the mind. From what you might ask? From the attachments of the mind. Only when the mind is not distracted can one remain steady in the yoga (linking) process.

  • Philosophy of Yoga helps us understand our identity as distinct from the body, mind, and intellect. It helps us understand the goal and reason for existence.

  • Yoga of the Soul involves operating from the level of spiritual consciousness.  The yoga of the soul permeates into activities involving the body, mind, and intellect, giving them spiritual substance. More importantly, this process helps develop a feeling of Ananda i.e. happiness, contentment, or satisfaction.

  • Our Motto: A holistic yoga system would involve considerations of all aspects of existence without discounting anyone so that the net effect produced is towards the intended goal of the process.  So speaking about us, our motto is to help 'Integrate body, mind, and soul. Integrate your existence' We are passionate about the integrated approach to the practice of this ancient science of yoga and look forward to sharing it with the UT community.
summary: To present the science of Bhakti-yoga to the UT community and help individuals experience and achieve a higher level of consciousness, knowledge, well being, and happiness. REGISTER AT:  https://www.tinyurl.com/BYCform

name: Student Employee Excellence Development Program
description: SEED is a professional development program for students employed by UT Austin. The program offers free, one-hour workshops on 16 different topics, all designed to help you succeed in your current job and your future career. We also focus on work/life balance. Each workshop is presented twice a semester by an expert from one of the SEED partner orgranizations across campus. Student employees who attend at least 6 workshops and write a short reflection on what they learned can earn a Student Employee Excellence Certification. An Advanced Certification opton is availabe for student employees who attend at least 10 workshops and complete a project that furthers student employee training. The program is open both to undergraduates and graduate students.
summary: If you are a student employed by UT Austin, the SEED Program is for you!  SEED offers free, one-hour workshops to help you succeed in your student job and future career. You can learn valuable skills and earn a certification to list on your resume.

name: Longhorn Link Program
description:
The Longhorn Link Program – Student Support Services – is a federally funded TRiO program. This program is funded under Title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965. The University of Texas at Austin was awarded initial funding in the Fall of 2001.

The program provides opportunities for academic development, assists students with basic college requirements, and serves to motivate students towards the successful completion of their post secondary education. The goal of the Longhorn Link Program is to increase the college retention and graduation rates of its participants and facilitate the process of transition from one level of higher education to the next.
summary: The Longhorn Link Program provides students with learning support, leadership development, and enrichment opportunities to ensure academic excellence and graduation.

name: BeVocal: The Bystander Intervention Initiative of The University of Texas at Austin
description:
BeVocal seeks to address multiple types of harm and enhance individual level self-efficacy to intervene as well as enhance collective responsibility and peer norms surrounding the importance of bystander intervention.  We seek to shift the culture of campus with the promotion of intervention as a norm of our community, beginning at orientation through graduation. BeVocal - that's what Longhorns do.  

To accomplish this, BeVocal builds on the existing expertise of campus entities and unifies these with consistent messaging and content. This means that while the issue or type of harm may vary in context, the promotion of intervention, action steps, potential barriers and many resources are consistent.
summary: BeVocal is a university-wide bystander intervention initiative to promote the idea that individual Longhorns have the power to prevent high-risk behavior and harm.

name: UTeach-Liberal Arts
description: UTeach-Liberal Arts is a professional middle and high school teacher preparation program for The University of Texas at Austin undergraduate and post-baccalaureate students planning to teach English, Languages Other Than English, or social studies.
summary: UTeach-Liberal Arts is a professional teacher preparation program for undergraduate and post-baccalaureate students planning to teach middle school or high school English, Languages Other Than English, or social studies.

name: Longhorn Center for Academic Excellence
description:
Longhorn Center for Academic Excellence creates avenues facilitating the capacity of all students to take charge of their learning and their lives. Through programs and services students become engaged members of the University community, exemplifying academic and personal excellence from their first semester at UT to graduation.

As you progress through your years with us, know that the University of Texas at Austin is committed to working with you through any personal or academic challenges that may arise along with all your achievements. The Longhorn Center for Academic Excellence is here for you!
summary: Longhorn Center for Academic Excellence creates avenues facilitating the capacity of all students to take charge of their learning and their lives.

name: Undergraduate Computational Finance Team
summary: Texas Undergraduate Computational Finance is a student run trading organization that educates UT undergraduates about quantitative methods in finance.

name: Blanton Museum of Art
description:
The Blanton Museum of Art at The University of Texas at Austin is one of the foremost university art museums in the country, and has the largest and most comprehensive collection of art in Central Texas. The Blanton's permanent collection of more than 17,000 works is recognized for its European paintings, an encyclopedic collection of prints and drawings, and modern and contemporary American and Latin American art.

The Blanton is considered one of the university's many Gems along with The Harry Ransom Center, the Briscoe Center for American History, The University of Texas Press, UT Libraries, the Graduate School, and the Michener Center for Writers. 

Located at the intersection of Martin Luther King Jr. Boulevard and Congress Avenue, the museum is across the street from the Bob Bullock Texas State History Museum and is adjacent to downtown Austin.
summary: We're one of the foremost university art museums in the U.S. and have the largest collection of art in Central Texas. Current UT faculty/students/staff always receive free admission.  Curiosity welcome!

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated - Omicron Theta Chapter
description:
Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated is a community-conscious, action-oriented organization. Founded on January 16, 1920, Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. has embodied our tenants of  scholarship,  service,  sisterhood,  and finer womanhood  for over a century. Enjoy a documental video in honor of our Centennial Founders' Day here. 

 

Included in the rich history of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc., Omicron Theta chapter was chartered on February 18, 1978, in Austin, TX. Ever upholding the motto and tenants of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc., Omicron Theta has been awarded for commitment to social consciousness on campus, and holds the highest G.P.A. of the National Pan Hellenic Council at the University of Texas at Austin.
summary: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated is a community-conscious, action-oriented organization, founded on the tenants of scholarship,  service,  sisterhood, and  finer womanhood.

name: Substance Abuse and the College Student
summary: Thank you for attending the Substance Abuse and the College Student Symposium on Friday, October 18. This Hornslink group will facilitate the dissemination of additional information on the topic.

name: Texas Army ROTC
description:
The Army ROTC program at The University of Texas at Austin is both challenging and rewarding. If you are attending Concordia University, Huston-Tillotson, St. Edwards University and Austin Community College you can join ROTC. There's no commitment required to join the Army ROTC program; you can try it out during your Freshmen and Sophomore years and gain some valuable leadership skills at the same time. But if you're ready to commit to a challenging career, then an Army ROTC scholarship might be available for you!

 
Military science offers opportunities to develop confidence, self-esteem, and leadership and life skills to succeed in college and beyond. It is an academic curriculum that supplements a student's major and is designed to prepare qualified, high-potential students for service as commissioned officers in the U.S. Army and its Reserve components (the Army Reserve and Army National Guard). Upon completion of the requirements for the baccalaureate or master's degree and military science training requirements, students are commissioned as second lieutenants.
summary: Texas Army ROTC selects, educates, trains, and commissions college students to be officers and leaders of character in the Total Army; instills the values of citizenship, national and community service, and personal responsibility.

name: Student Community of Asian Nurses
description: Student Community of Asian Nurses (SCAN) promotes community and support for those interested in the nursing field. Members are provided a sense of unity and guidance through group discussions and participation in volunteering events. SCAN also aims to promote and preserve asian culture, and raise awareness of how it affects healthcare.
summary: The purpose of the Student Community of Asian Nurses is to provide an environment that promotes learning, discovery, freedom, leadership, individual opportunity, and responsibility.

name: Polymathic Scholars Student Leadership Panel
description: The Polymathic Scholars Student Leadership Panel is the student-run organization affiliated with the Polymathic Scholars Honors Program in the College of Natural Sciences. The panel is responsible for planning all social, scholarly, outreach, and recruitment events for the program.
summary: The Polymathic Scholars Student Leadership Panel is the student organization of the Polymathic Scholars Honors Program in the College of Natural Sciences.

name: Adventist Christian Fellowship at UT Austin
description:
“Come to Me, all you who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.” (Matt 11:28).

Come and finish your week with us as we share our accomplishments, experiences and (sometimes) frustrations of college life. We may feel tired after a week of classes, homework, and exams. That’s why Jesus calls us to rest!

We are a group of Seventh-day Adventist students that like to share the good news of the gospel. We also kayak, hike, bowl, camp and all sorts of other fun things! 

We make most of our announcements on Instagram and Facebook so make sure to follow us.
summary: “Come to Me, all you who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.”(Matt 11:28). Come experience rest and community with refreshing Bible study and fellowship every Friday at 7 pm.

name: Dream Corps at the University of Texas at Austin
description: Dream Corps at UT works closely with students, educators, parents, and communities to raise awareness for children in rural China. We aim to improve the learning environment and long-term development of children in rural China. Although they were born into a poor economic condition without sufficient resources, we hope to encourage them to realize their full potential. We also hope to involve privileged youth in projects and programs designed to cultivate their sense of social responsibility. Indicated by its slogan “Let Reading Inspire”, Dream Corps at UT is dedicated to promoting reading in rural areas of China.
