summary: Dream Corps at UT works closely with students, educators, and communities to raise awareness for children in rural China. Indicated by its slogan “Let Reading Inspire”, Dream Corps at UT is dedicated to promoting reading among children.

name: Saudi Longhorn
summary: An Organization that will serve as a facilitator for Saudi students at UT to get involved in UT culture and for UT students to get involved in Saudi Culture.

name: Texas Bluebonnets
description:
Texas Bluebonnets is a spirit organization on campus. Founded with character as our core pillar, we hope to improve ourselves and our community through our philanthropic efforts with Sunday Lunchbox and other service projects in the Austin community area. In addition, Bluebonnets are dedicated to academia and strive to be excellent students and mentors. We are also proud of attending the prestigious institution of UT Austin and wholeheartedly support our university with our Longhorn spirit. Most importantly, we support our members by incorporating a sisterhood of strong, long-lasting friendships. 

Things to know: 

  1. We recruit at the beginning of each semester (Fall and Spring)
  2. We have an application process that we elaborate on during our info sessions- follow @txbluebonnets on Instagram to keep up with our recruitment schedule!
  3. General members spend about 2-3 hours a week on our activities, but that’s SUPER flexible. New members spend a little bit more time, but we are very understanding of everyone’s schedules.
  4. We do collect dues semesterly, with payment plans available. More information on cost and frequency will be given at our info sessions!
summary: The purpose of the Texas Bluebonnets is to unite diverse UT students through service, social, and spirit events. The Texas Bluebonnets' main focus is on leadership and character development.

name: Texas Table Top
description:
We meet every Friday, 6:00 - 9:00 in UNB 2.404 (Pearce Hall). You do not need to be a member on Hornslink to attend!

We are experimenting with new locations, check our Discord for the latest! https://discord.gg/GCrXNfRCZU

 

Texas Table Top is a student organization with a simple goal: provide an environment to introduce, teach, and play board games of various genres and complexities to the UT student body. 

 

Never played a board game in your life? We can teach! Don't own any games? We have 70+ games in our collection! Have games you want to play with other people? Bring them!

 

Games in our library include:

  • Settlers of Catan
  • Bannanagrams
  • Codenames
  • The Resistance/Avalon
  • Cards Against Humanity
  • Splendor
  • Secret Hitler
  • Terraforming Mars
  • Dominion
  • Blood on the Clocktower
  • Diplomacy
  • ... And more!

 

Most importantly, we do not collect dues and we do not expect you to attend every meeting. All you need to be a part of the club is just to show up! 

 

If you're interested, feel free to come by and check us out during game nights. Additionally, check out our discord server by following this link: https://discord.gg/GCrXNfRCZU
summary:
To provide a variety of tabletop/board games for students to learn and enjoy.  
We have many games both popular and niche for our members to enjoy and play!

name: Student Academy of Audiology chapter at The University of Texas at Austin
description: The SAA Chapter at UT is an organization that exists to introduce students to lifelong involvement in activities that promote and advance the profession of audiology, and provides services, information, education, representation and advocacy for the profession and the public we serve.
summary: To introduce students to lifelong involvement in activities that promote and advance the profession of audiology, and provides services, information, education, representation and advocacy for the profession and the public we serve.

name: Party on the Plaza
description:
WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 30th 2023: 11AM-4PM

Party on the Plaza (POP) is a welcome-back-to-campus party for the entire UT community. All proceeds collected the day of the event benefit the UT Student Emergency Fund. The Office of the Dean of Students-Student Emergency Services maintains this support fund primarily to help students in emergencies or dire financial straits due to loss of income from tragic or catastrophic events. Since 1999, the Party on the Plaza has donated over $100,000 to the fund and other charitable organizations. Longhorns helping Longhorns. Is there a better cause?!

 
POP Goals
  • Offer a large number of activities/games to create a "something for everyone" atmosphere
  • Donate money to a worthy organization
  • Promote RecSports to new and returning students
  • Provide the opportunity for student organizations to promote their activities
  • Provide an opportunity for Student Affairs departments and campus programs to promote their services
Campus Sponsors
  • Recreational Sports
  • Office of the Dean of Students: Student Activities
Activities
  • Student Organization Fair - over 300 student orgs!
  • Food
  • Campus Swag Giveaways
Scholarship Shootout
  • 1 male and 1 female will win a $2,500 scholarship
  • Qualify between 11:00am and 3:00pm on the Gregory Gym Plaza
  • Shootout finals start at 3:00pm
Student Organization Fair

Want to be a part of one of the largest student organization fairs on campus? Join over 300 student organizations at our organization fair. Registration for the fair begins at 10:00am on Monday - August 7, 2023. Student organizations must be currently registered and in good standing with the Student Activities office to participate. As a part of the registration process, each organization will pay a $20 fee that will go to support the UT Student Emergency Fund. Register early - spaces fill quickly!
summary: The purpose of the Party on the Plaza student organization is to promote the largest campus wide organization fair at the University of Texas. Party on the Plaza provides support for the Student Emergency Fund.

name: Texas State Employees Union
summary: EDUCATION OF AND ADVOCACY FOR EMPLOYEES (GRADUATE WORKERS, FACULTY, AND STAFF) OF THE UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS AT AUSTIN

name: Club Golf
description: Club Golf aims to bring golfers at UT together. This is through meeting people to play golf with, playing Club Golf tournaments hosted by the NCCGA, and also by hosting our own events.
summary: Longhorn Golf Club unites UT student golfers not pursuing collegiate golf for both recreational enjoyment and competition. We foster a supportive, inclusive community, enabling members to continue their passion for golf.

name: Schoenstatt University Men at UT
description:
Schoenstatt University Men is a Catholic organization comprised of small discussion circles that meet weekly to explore faith-based topics and cultivate meaningful connections grounded in shared spiritual values. A core focus of our gatherings is fostering a sense of belonging and spiritual growth. We organize events, including retreats, field-days, community service projects, and various service activities. With the help of Jesus Christ and prayers of our Blessed Mother we aim to positively impact the broader university community and encourage personal and collective progress toward becoming compassionate leaders and changemakers.

Here's a link to our movement:

https://schoenstatt-austin.us/sum
summary: Social Men's Fraternity that meets once a week to discuss Catholic Schoenstatt Spirituality.

name: Armenian Students Association
description: Promote awareness of the Armenian community, culture, and causes among the UT student body. Create a community that celebrates Armenian culture, food, and history. Give Armenians a space to network and socialize with each other while building long-lasting friendships. Lastly, the aim is to foster connections with the broader Austin Armenian community and with other Armenian student groups across the country.
summary: Promote community and campus awareness of the Armenian culture and support fellowship among Armenians and their friends.

name: Information and Systems Security Society (ISSS)
description:
Please use our Discord server or our website (isss.io) for information and updates about our organization.

We do not manage our members through HornsLink.

ISSS teaches students about security and privacy through talks, workshops, and Capture the Flag competitions (CTFs). More information can be found on our website, isss.io.
summary: ISSS teaches students about security and privacy through talks, workshops, and Capture the Flag competitions (CTFs). More information can be found on our website, isss.io.

name: Crossroads
description: We are a student-led campus ministry at the University of Texas at Austin. Our vision is to minister to our classmates at UT and also the surrounding Austin community through campus events, volunteering opportunities, and our many college Life Groups.
summary: We are a student-led campus ministry at the University of Texas at Austin. Our vision is to minister to our classmates at UT and also the surrounding Austin community through campus events, volunteering opportunities, and our many college Life Groups.

name: Petroleum Graduate Student Association
description:
We are the Petroleum Graduate Students Association (PGSA) at the University of Texas at Austin. We represent the graduate student organization for the Hildebrand Department of Petroleum Engineering. We are a student run organization that brings together students from all backgrounds interested in the oil and gas industry. We strive to provide a support system and social outlet for the graduate students and act as a liaison between the students and PGE department. 

 

We host technical seminars which aim to bridge the gap between academic research and industry application. Industry professionals typically highlight current technical trends in the oil and gas industry. These talks provide students and industry professionals an opportunity to network and collaborate on ideas.

 

     Our social events include bowling, coffee breaks, and a welcome-back social. These events provide us with the perfect opportunity to socialize with other graduate students, faculty and staff.

 

     We typically participate in two major outreach events each year: UT's Girl day and Explore UT. As a part of our outreach, we demonstrate technical concepts in petroleum engineering using simple household items. In the past, we demonstrated hydraulic fracturing using jello and frosting. Some interesting ideas can be found here - showing the annual Ben Caudle's "Simple Energy Concepts Contest" (the winners are showcased during Energy Week).

 

 

      Our meetings include a FREE lunch or dinner in thanks to our generous sponsors: Schlumberger, Chevron, QRI, and the Hildebrand Department of Petroleum Engineering.

 

     Our regular meetings involve technical presentations from industry representatives, professors and students talking about their research, or organizing our future activities. We do not have a membership fee; all PGE graduate students are members by default. Please feel free to reach out to us with any ideas or questions.
summary: Our goals are to facilitate interaction among PGE graduate students, to gather and disseminate pertinent information, to conduct activities that promote the general welfare of PGE graduate students and to represent their views to the university community

name: United Mission for Relief & Development Texas Chapter
description: UMR Texas utilizes a unique approach and relief, allowing young people to be involved in the transformative process of serving other from start to finish. We are a diverse group of students and volunteers who strive on a daily basis to implement targeted, cost-effective relief and development programs that improve lives. Our motto, "Development Through Service" bears a message of changing lives at both ends: those who need our help and those who want help from others.
summary: United Mission for Relief & Development - Austin aims to provide relief to those in the local community with a focus on building the future leaders of our community while improving ourselves in the service of others.

name: Society for Advancement of Chicano/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science
description:
SACNAS, the Society for the Advancement of Chicanos and Native Americans in Science, is a national organization whose mission is to promote the recruitment, retention and career development of all students interested in the sciences. SACNAS is particularly focused on expanding the opportunities available to underrepresented minorities and empowering them to become leaders capable of having a positive impact in our society and in the scientific community. 

 

SACNAS holds a national conference every year and provides travel scholarships for students to present their research in front of academic professionals. Members of the chapter will be encouraged to attend the national conference. 

https://www.sacnas.org/
summary: Our group is intended to encourage the success of Chicano/Hispanic and Native American students in all stages of their scientific careers. Our goal is to make practicing science at any level more equitable and accessible for underrepresented students.

name: Texas Cru
description:
Texas Cru, a campus ministry of Cru, is an interdenominational Christian ministry at UT. Our heart is to see every student at the University of Texas hear the Gospel of Jesus Christ in an ongoing way through a relationship with someone they know who truly follows Him. We engage with students through small groups, monthly meetings, and family-style events to do so. 

 

Here’s the best way to get involved in our community:

#1 – Follow us on social media: We post about our weekly events, bible studies, and retreats. We’re active on Instagram!

#2 - Join our GroupMe or Slack! Join our GroupMe/Slack and get updates on events throughout the week!

#3 – Get involved (and bring your friends!): Again, there are no requirements to be a part of Cru! You are free to come to an event and get involved!

If you're looking for a next step, join one of our Outreach Teams:

  • Welcome Team
  • Community Team
  • Prayer Team
  • Go Team
  • Social Media Team
summary: TEXAS CRU is a Christian community of students and staff seeking to love God and others at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: International Undergraduate Student Ministry-Austin
description:
欢迎来到德州大学奥斯汀分校！

International Undergraduate Student Ministry 是得克萨斯大学奥斯汀分校 (UT Austin)为国际本科生打造的社团——一个让你结交朋友、享受美食、畅玩互动、探讨有意义话题的 "异乡家园"。

我们组织的活动包括：
�� 奥斯汀城市探索
🛒 亚洲超市采购行
�� 家常美食晚餐会
⚽ 运动局
�� 趣味短途旅行
📚 学习自习局
🤝 社区志愿服务
�� 节日庆祝活动
�� 夜市（Night Market）
……还有更多！

Welcome to UT Austin!
 
International Undergraduate Student Ministry is a UT Austin student club and community for international undergraduate students —a home away from home to make friends, enjoy good food, have fun, and talk about meaningful topics. 

We host events like Welcome to Texas BBQs, Austin tours, Asian grocery trips, host family dinners, sports tournaments, fun trips, study times, community service, holiday celebrations, Night Market (夜市), and more. 

The group is hosted by Christian UT students and alumni, but our student group has members of all religious backgrounds, and is open to students of any spiritual/religious/ethnic background. Whether you’re looking to have fun, make new friends, or talk about meaningful topics, you’re at the right place. 


To get connected, fill out a "Get Connected" form and/or join the Hornslink roster!  

 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4rCtkDDqzHA
summary: IUSM is a group for international undergraduate students who are looking for a home away from home where international students can come, relax, eat good food, go on trips, talk about meaningful topics, and go through the ups and downs of life together!

name: Future Doctors of America
description:
Future Doctors of America is an organization focused on aiding UT students on their pre-med journey. We encourage a family atmosphere with other students on similar paths through family nights, off-campus socials, and other group activities. We also focus on professional resources and services, this includes but not limited to: physician speakers, volunteering, scholarships, and mentorship. 

Find a fun way to get to know people on the premedical track and gain lasting social and professional experiences!

 

Step 1: Join this Hornslink page, which will also add you to our newsletter mailing list.

Join our GroupMe!

Check out our website!

Follow our Instagram!

General Meetings: Tuesdays 6pm-7pm in RLP 1.108
summary: To provide premedical UT students with opportunities and resources including but not limited to: physician speakers, volunteering, a social environment, scholarships, and a place to meet and make friends.

name: Women of Excellence
description:
Texas Women of Excellence (WOE) is a student organization focused on empowering female and non-binary students on campus. We empower members through our pillars of mentorship, leadership, empowerment, service, professionalism, and diversity. Our core principle is to increase the visibility of women and non-binary individuals in leadership around campus by building confidence in their ability to achieve their goals at the University of Texas and Beyond.

 

If you are interested in becoming a member of WOE, please follow us on Instagram and join our Recruitment GroupMe to stay updated on Recruitment Events: Link to Fall 2025 Recruitment GroupMe

 

The WOE Membership Application is due Friday, September 12th at 11:59pm. The link can be find here: Link to WOE Membership Application
summary: Women of Excellence connects well-established UT leaders with incoming first and second-year women and nonbinary individuals. Texas WOE was founded on six pillars: mentorship, leadership, empowerment, service, diversity, and professionalism.

name: In Between the Lines
description:
In Between the Lines is a place to read and discuss books with others! We meet once a week on Wednesdays at 6:30 in Parlin 101, and every other meeting is social-oriented.

Anyone can recommend a book and we all vote on them. We read books over a two week or four week period along with two short stories.

We accept everyone and love every level of commitment to the club! Join at any time of the semester. Dues are ten dollars but cover parties and merch, so anyone can come to the weekly meetings for free.

For more information, email us at utinbetweenthelines@gmail.com.

We have a Slack where we post updates and talk about the group! This is the best way to get information and stay connected with other members and the officers: Join here! 

You can also find us on Instagram!
summary: Read books! Make friends!

name: American Concrete Institute
summary: The mission of the American Concrete Institute (ACI) Student Chapter at UT Austin is to promote student interest in concrete and to develop an awareness of the mission and the work of the American Concrete Institute through its committees, publications,

name: Black Student Alliance's Freshman Action Team
summary: The Freshman Action Team, also referred to as F.A.T., was established through the Black Student Alliance to encourage campus and community involvement, to create unity between freshman and the campus as a whole, and to develop essential leadership skills

name: College of Fine Arts
description: The College of Fine Arts at The University of Texas at Austin prepares students for the creation, practice, study, criticism and teaching of the arts, in a context that emphasizes cultural diversity, community engagement and technical innovation. It includes the Butler School of Music, Department of Art and Art History, and Department of Theatre and Dance.
summary: College of Fine Arts

name: American Society for Microbiology at The University of Texas at Austin
description:
Our organization promotes interest in microbiology among UT students and the wider Austin community. Through chapter meetings and social events, we provide valuable resources to help our members grow professionally and expand their networks by connecting with academic researchers, faculty members, and industry professionals. We also engage in primary and secondary school outreach to promote healthy sanitary practices and educate children on the role of microbiology in everyday life. By sparking curiosity at a young age, we aim to inspire the next generation of scientists and highlight potential career paths in the field.

Contact us!

austinasmoncampus@gmail.com

Join our Email List!

https://utlists.utexas.edu/sympa/subscribe/ut-asm?previous_action=info

Join our GroupMe!

https://groupme.com/join_group/66578633/THarhAY8

Follow our Instagram page!

https://www.instagram.com/texasasm/

Follow our LinkedIn page!

https://www.linkedin.com/company/american-society-for-microbiology-at-ut-austin/
summary: The American Society for Microbiology at UT Austin connects our members with the brilliant and diverse field of microbiology—the study of how microorganisms shape ecology, medicine, and industry.

name: Office of Admissions
summary: The Office of Admissions believes in the value of meeting with and speaking to prospective students in person. Admissions counselors conduct daily information sessions for prospective freshman students and their families, sharing details about UT Austin

name: Horns for Life
description:
Horns for Life is a University of Texas at Austin non-sectarian student organization that advocates against abortion because we believe in the fundamental dignity and equal value of all human lives. We seek to serve the university community by tabling, volunteering, advocating for policy change, fundraising for student-parents, and fostering a pro-life attitude among the talented and bright student body.

Our meetings occur every Tuesday from 7:00pm-8:00pm in Garrison 0.128. Throughout this semester, we will discuss various consistent life topics, volunteer at pregnancy resource centers, fundraise for the Student Pregnant or Parenting Scholarship and build community. Check our website at https://texashornsforlife.weebly.com/about.html to learn more about us!

Insta: Texashornsforlife

*updated for Spring 2026*
summary: We are a consistent life organization working to spread a culture of life on campus through outreach, volunteering, and awareness.

name: Hill House Students
description:
Our Purpose

Hill House exists to equip students and faculty to integrate their faith in academia, the workplace, and the community. We do this by:

  1. Nurturing Christian identity, wisdom, and character
  2. Engaging contemporary questions and culture with the riches of the Bible and the Christian intellectual tradition
  3. Exploring the call to follow Jesus in every area of life and society

 

Our Beliefs

Hill House affirms and holds fast to the fundamental Christian beliefs found in Scripture and articulated succinctly in the Apostles’ and Nicene Creeds. We are tethered to historic, traditional, orthodox doctrine and teaching, as well as to the heritage of the Protestant Reformation.

 

At the same time, Hill House is a place of diverse Christian thought. In our community, you’ll find Presbyterians, Anglicans, Roman Catholics, Baptists, Methodists, non-denominational Protestants, and more. We are, however, committed to the shared pursuit of Christian truth found and embodied in Jesus Christ.

 

In keeping with its Christian commitment, Hill House strives to enlarge and extend conversations about God, faith, morality, meaning, and truth rather than narrow or end them. We gladly listen to and learn from those who may not share our convictions. While we have no official affiliation with the University of Texas, Hill House supports higher education, affirms critical thinking, strives to work collaboratively with UT, and seeks to promote and contribute to the intellectual life of the university community.
summary: Hill House is a Christian study center serving UT Austin that supports students and faculty who are asking questions about God, discerning their sense of calling, and discovering how faith informs every area of life.

name: Council of Graduate Chemists
description:
The Council of Graduate Chemists will serve to take ownership of what shapes our experience as graduate students in the Department of Chemistry at the University of Texas-Austin.  This group seeks to benefit all graduate students in chemistry by fostering professional and community relationships, informing about school-wide policies, and advocating for the needs of our peers. 

 

Goals:

  • Peer support and socialization
  • Student advocacy and representation at the departmental and university level
  • Professional development and networking
  • Consolidate information pertinent to chemistry graduate students
  • Volunteering and community outreach
summary: The Council of Graduate Chemists serves on behalf of graduate students in the Department of Chemistry by means of fostering professional and community relationships, informing about school-wide policies, and advocating for our peers needs.

name: Academic Counselors Association
summary: Staff organization. Our goal is to build recognition of the importance of effective academic advising and enhance the professional development of advisors.

name: Radio-Television-Film Department
description: One of the top most desired majors at UT, RTF is one of a handful of film programs in the country that is ranked in the top ten in both film production and in the academic study of media. Read more.
summary: One of the top most desired majors at UT, RTF is one of a handful of film programs in the country that's ranked in the top ten in both film production and in the academic study of media.

name: Noteworthy A Cappella
description: Noteworthy is a co-ed A Cappella group at The University of Texas at Austin, founded in 2014. Noteworthy is a competitive group; they most recently competed in the ICCA 2023 semifinals and the SingStrong NYC A Cappella Competition where they won Audience Choice & Best Soloist. The group loves to perform for the community and at campus-affiliated events. Noteworthy presents an eclectic sound, bridging genres with their original arrangements. Members range from those with over 15 years of musical experience to those who have never read sheet music. As a diverse group, where every voice is heard, each member is valued for what they bring to the table.
summary: Noteworthy is a co-ed A Cappella group at The University of Texas at Austin founded in 2014. Noteworthy presents an eclectic sound, bridging genres with their original arrangements.

name: UT STAFF TOASTMASTERS
description: The mission of UT Staff Toastmasters Club is to provide a mutually supportive, positive and continuous learning environment in which staff members have the opportunity to develop and practice communication and leadership skills that enrich their lives and lead to greater self-confidence and professional growth.
summary: National and State Affiliations: Toastmasters International, Toastmasters Texas (District 55)

name: College of Education Career Service
summary: Career Services at the College of Education

name: SPARK Magazine
description: Since 2014, SPARK has come into its own as a leading print, online, and multimedia publication for emerging creatives in Austin and beyond. We’re a diverse network of student talent, passionate about telling compelling stories, producing evocative visuals, and pushing the boundaries of art, culture, and fashion. SPARK Magazine upholds the SPARK Standard to enable young creatives towards confidence and a robust portfolio for future creative careers.
summary: Student-run multimedia publication pushing the boundaries of art, culture, and fashion. | www.sparkmagazinetx.com

name: IFORTY Administrative Professional Network
summary: A network that supports and promotes innovative administrative professionals on the University of Texas at Austin campus who demonstrate the power of the individual in providing daily support to the mission of the University and the Forty Acres.

name: Association of Professionals in Student Affairs
summary: APSA focuses on Student Affairs Professionals at The University of Texas at Austin and creating an environment where information, best practices, current trends and resources are shared with colleagues across campus.

name: Longhorn Quiz Bowl
description:
The Longhorn Quiz Bowl team competes in quiz bowl tournaments across the nation. Competitions involve teams of students answering primarily academic questions across all disciplines, including history, literature, science, and fine arts. Competitors play in teams of four students, answering tossup questions individually to give the team the opportunity to work together on bonuses.

All experience levels and majors are welcome to participate, and the club is open to both undergraduates and graduate students. Longhorn Quiz Bowl encourages members to learn new subjects outside of their discipline to become well-rounded and knowledgeable people.

To learn more about quiz bowl, check out this page: http://www.qbwiki.com/wiki/How_Quizbowl_Works

Practices are held from 5:00-7:30 p.m. on Mondays and from 6:00-8:30 p.m. on Thursdays in Room 4.104 of the E.P. Schoch Building (EPS). Newcomers are always welcome at practices! Previous experience isn't necessary and members don't need to attend every practice to attend tournaments. The club communicates via Discord. For questions or a link to our Discord server, email Owen Gilroy at owengilroy@utexas.edu
summary: Longhorn Quiz Bowl competes in academic tournaments locally and across the nation. In the 2021-2022 season, Longhorn Quiz Bowl came third nationally at the Intercollegiate Championship Tournament. Both undergraduate and graduate students are welcome.

name: Safety Education Program
description:
In order to complete the 2-step Safety Education Program, organization leadership must complete the online module course and present the Safety Education Organization Training to their membership.

While the deadline for the 2025-2026 academic year is October 21, 2025 it is recommended that organizations complete this program as early as possible in the fall semester.

  1. Safety Education Officer Program
    The president/primary contact must complete the Officer Safety Education online module course. Presidents/primary contacts may self-enroll into the course on behalf of more than one organization in which they serve as president or primary contact for. This is a self-paced course and must be completed each academic year. All organizations that do not complete the course by the deadline of October 21, 2025, will be frozen.

To complete the course, presidents/primary contacts can self-enroll into the Safety Education module course. The course will become available on Aug 25, 2025 at midnight.


Any students in need of accommodations (e.g. closed captioning, etc.) to best engage can reach out directly to our team at safetyeducation@austin.utexas.edu.

  1. Safety Education Organization Training
    After completing the Safety Education Officer online module course, the President, or Primary Contact must provide the Safety Education Organization Training to the entire membership of each organization they represent. You may present this as part of a scheduled meeting that falls before the annual deadline or create an opportunity to present it to your organization. The attendee will receive the presentation via email following completion of the online module course, but if you do not receive it within a week, please contact our team at safetyeducation@austin.utexas.edu. After presenting the Safety Education Organization Training to your membership, please notify our team within one week by completing the Safety Education Organization Training Completion Form.

Are you an advisor of a student organization? Stay up to date on policy changes, safety education, prevention and response strategies, and resources by completing the 25-26 Org Advisor Safety Education module course.

 To complete the course, organization advisors can self-enroll into the Org Advisor Safety Education module course. The course will become available on Aug 25, 2025 at midnight.

 

FALL 2025 DATES & DEADLINES

August 25 – October 21:  Safety Education Officer online module course will be open during this time. 

 

Organizations that do not complete the Safety Education Program by October 21st, 2025 will be frozen.

 

MY ORGANIZATION IS NOT RECEIVING COMMUNICATIONS OR LISTED ONLINE

Please contact us if you are not receiving communications regarding the program, do not see your organization listed on the Program Status Page, or any other issue regarding Safety Education.

Additionally, check and see if roster and officer contact information is up to date on Hornslink.

We work in collaboration with Student Activities, but do not manage student organizations. If your organization is having issues other than Safety Education logistics, including Hornslink, registration, etc., please contact Student Activities. 

 

MORE QUESTIONS?

Check out our FAQ page, contact us at SafetyEducation@austin.utexas.edu.
summary: In order to continue to create a safer UT Community, all registered & sponsored student organizations complete the Safety Education Program annually. Safety Education connects student organizations to risk management, prevention, and emergency resources.

name: Fuse A Cappella
description: Founded in the spring of 2014, Fuse A Cappella is an official a cappella group on campus that competes, serves its community, and performs on and off campus. It was founded to unite a focused group of students across all majors, hometowns, and backgrounds through their love of singing. If you would like to continue making music during college, contribute a new cultural perspective, or make a close group of friends, Fuse A Cappella is for you!
summary: Fuse A Cappella is a co-ed a cappella group at UT that performs on campus, in the community, and in competition. Join Fuse, and join a family of close friends that loves singing as much as you do!

name: HISPANIC FACULTY STAFF ASSOCIATION (HFSA)
summary: Create a welcoming environment for all members, improve the universities work environment, involve members is leadership roles at UT, & promote a better understanding and appreciation of Latino cultures

name: Texas Business of Sports Student Organization (BOSSO)
description:
The Business of Sports Student Organization (BOSSO) seeks to leverage advanced data analytics and research methodologies to provide actionable insights and strategic recommendations within the business of sports with our key pillars: Consulting, Research, Media, Education, and Philanthropy.

  • Consulting: BOSSO provides pro-bono consulting services to sports organizations to enhance decision-making, optimize performance, and drive innovation.
  • Research: BOSSO conducts research and produces written findings reports to advance the field of sports analytics through collaboration with industry experts and professors.
  • Media: BOSSO develops and maintains multiple media channels, including a newsletter, social media platforms, and a podcast, to share insights, highlight research findings, and engage with the broader sports and analytics communities.
  • Education: BOSSO promotes education and skill development by advancing members’ understanding of sports data analytics and business operations through weekly technical workshops covering industry tools and techniques. Additionally, the organization hosts an exclusive speaker series featuring professionals, faculty, and coaches, offering valuable opportunities for learning and networking.
  • Philanthropy: BOSSO supports the sports community through charitable initiatives, volunteering, and outreach programs, including youth sports development, accessible athletic opportunities, and community-based sports events.
summary: The Texas Business of Sports Student Organization (BOSSO) seeks to leverage advanced data analytics and research methodologies to provide actionable insights and strategic recommendations within the business of sports.

name: Gamma Rho Lambda
description:
Gamma Rho Lambda is an all-gender-inclusive Greek organization, a social safe space, and a family. We welcome everyone regardless of identity and would love to have you join us! We hold a series of rush events at the beginning of each semester, reach out to us and find us on Facebook or Instagram for more info!

Rushing Gamma Rho Lambda has three parts, but first you need to come to an information session and recruitment event to be eligible to rush! The schedule will be out on our Instagram page (@grltexas) starting at the beginning of every semester, and we hold rush at the beginning of each semester.

The second part of rushing Gamma Rho Lambda involves attending an interview session with us and the third is a new member process focused on education in LGBTQ+ topics and our organization!

We strive to be a safe and fun space for anyone interested in being a part of our family and learning more about themselves and the LGBTQ+ community.
summary: We are UT's only all-gender-inclusive Greek organization for queer & allied students! If you are in need of an accepting and fun family on the 40 acres, check us out, we would love to have you!

name: Texas Tai Chi
description: Texas Tai Chi is no longer active
summary: no longer active

name: Beekeeping Society
description:
Through student leadership, the Beevo Beekeeping Society aims to spread a love of beekeeping and native pollinators at the University of Texas at Austin. Committed to pollinator conservation and sustainable practices, Beevo fosters awareness through hands-on education, community outreach, and sustainability-focused social events. Our objective is to cultivate an eco-conscious campus and inspire others to support the vital role of pollinators in our environment. We have partnered with the Xerces Society to award UT Austin with BeeCampus USA Certification.

The Beekeeping Society meets bi-weekly during the school year and offers a variety of social, educational, and sustainable events that are open to both members and the general UT public. Hive checks are offered weekly when weather permits and are open to society members and a limited number of general UT public members.

Follow our Instagram @beevobeekeepers to stay up to date on recruitment and upcoming events!
summary: The Beekeeping Society is a student-led organization that brings beekeeping to the UT community. We offer hive checks, socials, sustainability events, and more! Through our work, UT has achieved Bee Campus USA Certification from the Xerces Society.

name: Campus Cat Coalition
description: Affiliated with Austin/Travis County Humane Society, Austin Animal Center, Alley Cat Allies, and the SPCA.
summary: Spay/neuter and management program for feral campus cats.

name: UNIVERSITY POLICE ORGANIZATION
summary: To Promote the professionalism and training of UTPD Staff. To promote police and public trust and interaction.

name: Pride and Equity Faculty Staff Association
description:
PEFSA is a University Resource Group established in the Fall of 2006 to promote the interests of LGBTQ and allied faculty and staff at UT Austin. All UT faculty and staff are welcome to join. Help us to make a difference!

Purposes:

  • To promote the welfare of GLBT faculty and staff and their allies at the University of Texas at Austin

  • To provide a mechanism for faculty and staff to network and support each other's work efforts

  • To offer opportunities for faculty and staff to meet in culturally and socially supportive gatherings

  • To organize informational workshops and training about GLBT issues

  • To share university and community resources related to GLBT faculty and staff

  • To provide a forum to exchange ideas and information with administration and the broader University community about GLBT issues on campus.

  • To focus on strengthening recruitment and retention of faculty and initial employment and career growth of staff.
summary: PEFSA is a University Resource Group established in the Fall of 2006 to promote the interests of LGBTQ and allied faculty and staff at UT Austin. All UT faculty and staff are welcome to join. Help us to make a difference!

name: Texas Music Producers
description:
Join our Discord for our main communications here!

Texas Music Producers won’t turn anyone into a superstar overnight. However, what it will do is provide musicians with a collaborative environment to build and apply their technical skills.

TMP finds value in exploring the depth of modern software, and helping members gain authority in their own creative space by understanding their tools. Learning fundamental principles of sound design and mixing allows artists to effectively translate an idea from their minds into a real song. At TMP, the learning happens in real time with others who not only have the exact same interest in production, but who also contribute different musical backgrounds and tastes.

We believe that students learn best from well-rounded, relatable young mentors, and we host several collaborative challenges throughout the year to help bring people together. 

We encourage any UT student interested in music production and sound design to attend our events; they are free and open to all majors!

To stay up to date with meeting times, locations, and events, follow our instagram @texasproducers join our discord here

We meet once a week. Check the discord for weekly meeting times and locations.

For any questions or concerns contact us at texasmusicproducers@gmail.com.
summary: Texas Music Producers is a community of young producers who are beginning and continuing their journeys in music. Join us to meet like-minded people, make next level music, and learn more about the industry.

name: Emancipet on Campus
description:
Welcome to Emancipet on Campus, a student organization at The University of Texas at Austin! Our mission is to provide an outlet for members to actively serve the animal-welfare oriented community of Austin and to continuously represent the interests of and raise awareness for the non-profit veterinary cause of the Emancipet clinic of Austin.

Meetings are every other Monday from 6-7pm. Everybody is welcome!

Our meeting days for Fall 2024 will be announced soon as well as the room number. 

Join our GroupMe for notifications about upcoming meetings and volunteer opportunities: GroupMe

Visit our website for more info: emanicpetoncampus.weebly.com

Sign-up here to get weekly newsletters: Newsletter Sign-Up
summary: We provide animal-related volunteer opportunities and activities for the betterment of animal welfare in the city of Austin.

name: Students Expanding Austin Literacy
description:
Students Expanding Austin Literacy (SEAL) is a University of Texas student service organization that helps kids dive into reading. SEAL reading buddies are matched with their own elementary school student, and serve as semester-long reading buddies and mentors. Our goal is simple: to help our kids fall in love with reading. If you are a UT student, love kids, and enjoy children's books, then SEAL is for you!

Since consistency is crucial to developing a strong relationship, all SEAL members commit to volunteering one hour each week. Members sign up for a weekly (Monday - Friday) one-hour time slot that they are available for consistently. Many times are offered to accommodate your busy schedules, and carpool groups are arranged for transportation.

 

Instagram: @seal_ut
summary: Students Expanding Austin Literacy (SEAL) is a University of Texas student service organization that helps kids dive into reading!

name: Texas Real Estate Law Society
description: The mission of the Texas Real Estate Law Society is to provide educational, networking, professional development, and leadership opportunities for individuals interested in real estate, land use, zoning, housing, and construction law.
summary: The mission of the Texas Real Estate Law Society is to provide networking, educational, professional development, and leadership opportunities for law students interested in Real Estate and related areas of the law.

name: Healing with Harmonies
description:
Healing with Harmonies is an organization of passionate students who are inspired to touch the world through music. We perform music from many genres at a variety of venues to connect with our audiences. All instrumentalists and vocalists are welcome to perform with us!

Contact us at healingwithharmonies@gmail.com to receive more information and sign up for our newsletter. We'd love to meet you!
summary: Healing with Harmonies is an organization of socially dedicated students who are inspired to touch the world through the power of music. We perform music from many genres at a variety of venues to connect with our audiences.

name: GRADUATE COORDINATOR NETWORK
summary: Forum for graduate coordinators & university administrators to exchange information, develop & implement new ideas, learn new skills.

name: Student College of Clinical Pharmacy – Austin Chapter
description:
The American College of Clinical Pharmacy (ACCP) is a national pharmacy professional organization that provides leadership, education, advocacy, and resources to enable clinical pharmacists to achieve excellence in practice and research.

ACCP Student Chapters provide students with a local connection to the national organization. Student chapters exist for the following reasons:

1. To familiarize students with the field of clinical pharmacy by providing information about careers and opportunities within the field of clinical pharmacy;

2. To promote dedication to excellence in patient care, research, and education; and encouraging the professional development necessary to function within an interdisciplinary team;

3. To advocate for the role of clinical pharmacists in providing direct patient care, including evaluation of the patient and his or her pharmacotherapeutic needs; initiation, modification, or discontinuation of patient-specific pharmacotherapy; and ongoing pharmacotherapeutic monitoring and follow-up.

4. To encourage participation in the American College of Clinical Pharmacy (ACCP) at the national level and in local/regional chapters.
summary: A student chapter of the American College of Clinical Pharmacy (ACCP) that orients students to the practice of clinical pharmacy by providing opportunities in research, information on careers, and skill development in clinical practice.

name: Brazilian Jiu Jitsu at the University of Texas
description:
UTBJJ is the official RecSports sponsored Brazilian Jiu Jitsu organization at UT Austin. We are both a recreational and competitive club and welcome those wanting to learn self defense and those who wish to compete locally. Beginners are always welcome, no experience is needed! Everyone gets 4 free practices so if you have any interest, make sure to check us out. We hope to see you on the mats!

 

Practice Schedule:

-Tuesday 5-7 PM (No-Gi)

-Thursday 5-7 PM (Gi)

- Friday 4-6 PM (No-Gi)

- Sunday 1-3 PM (No-Gi)

 

At the RecCenter in room RSC 1.138

(After entering, walk straight until you hit the back wall; look left, and the room will be there)

 

Instagram: @heelhookem

Website: utbjj.org

Please check out our Guide to UTBJJ for more info about the club and how to join.
summary:
UT BJJ is a great place to learn and compete in a growing and emerging self-defense sport while being part of a great community. Beginners are always welcome. We hope to see you on the mats!!

Check out our Instagram for the latest updates! @heelhookem

name: African Students Organization
description: ASO is dedicated to form links between African Students on the UT campus and the Austin community at large. Although we strive to create a medium for African students to socialize and interact, ASO is open to students of all backgrounds. We aim to encourage and enhance awareness about African issues, culture and values. We are committed to advocate the rights of African students on the UT campus. We are eager to form ties with similar organizations here on UT.
summary: ASO is a social organization committed to promoting African culture on the UT campus and surrounding community through dance, music, poetry and more. At meetings, we actively focus on educating everyone on issues that affect Africans and Africa.

name: International Business Student Association
description:
The purpose of International Business Student Association (IBSA) at The University of Texas at Austin is to expand member’s knowledge of global issues that affect the way we do business. We are an organization committed to learning about foreign business practices, culture, and customs in ways that will provide us with better global understanding of our international economy. For this reason, we advocate international and cultural awareness because we believe that as business professionals, global leaders, and members of society, it is important to understand international issues that affect business practices. We promote interaction with international and exchange students while also advocating professional and social development because we believe it is a gateway to building a network of exceptional connections.

We are focused on the following four areas:

  • International and cultural awareness
  • Professional development
  • Social development
  • Leadership
summary: The purpose of International Business Student Association is to expand member’s knowledge of global issues that affect the way we do business. We are committed to international and cultural awareness, professional and social development, and leadership.

name: If/When/How at the University of Texas at Austin
description: If/When/How at the University of Texas at Austin is a national network of law students and lawyers who are committed to reproductive justice. We believe all people should have the ability to decide if, when, and how to create and sustain a family. Our chapter engages in activism, advocacy, and volunteer projects around reproductive health, rights, and justice.
summary: If/When/How at the University of Texas at Austin is a national network of law students and lawyers who are committed to reproductive justice. We believe all people should have the ability to decide if, when, and how to create and sustain a family.

name: Texas Athletics
description:
Spirit and pride run deep at The University of Texas, and never is that more evident than game days. Find some burnt orange and show your true colors at one of our many Texas Athletics home sporting events. 

The Big Ticket was created for students to enjoy the more than 150 athletic events on campus throughout the school year. Once purchased, just bring your valid UT student ID to the event, scan and you're in. It's that simple. For more information, visit TexasSports.com/bigticket
summary: Texas Athletics exists to develop student-athletes through their connection to master coaches, elite performance centers and focused academic nurturing.

name: Structural Engineering Association of Texas - Student Chapter
description: The Structural Engineers Association of Texas (SEAoT) Student Chapter at The University of Texas at Austin seeks to enhance students’ understanding of the structural engineering field through technical discussions with professionals and professors.
summary: The Structural Engineers Association of Texas (SEAoT) Student Chapter at The University of Texas at Austin seeks to enhance students’ understanding of the structural engineering field through technical discussions with professionals and professors.

name: Lang Stuttering Institute Student Leadership Organization
description:
The Lang Stuttering Institute Student Leadership Organization strives to unite university students from diverse backgrounds, interests, and majors in order to educate, advocate, and spread awareness about stuttering. Stuttering is a stigmatized communication difference and is often misrepresented in society. The Blank Center, is a place on campus where individuals of all ages can receive services for stuttering, free of cost. Our organization holds events on and off campus to support the individuals in the Center through fundraising and advocacy. Through our fundraising and volunteer efforts, we strive to make the world a better place for people who stutter. 

Click here to subscribe to our mailing list.

Click here to be added to our Flare!

Instagram: @txlsislo
summary: The Lang Stuttering Institute Student Leadership Organization seeks to unite university students from diverse backgrounds and majors in order to educate, advocate, and spread awareness about stuttering.

name: Texas Guadaloop
description:
Texas Guadaloop is a diverse group of men and women from UT Austin disrupting modern transportation with hyperloop technology. Similar to a bullet train, hyperloop is a fully electric transportation system that reaches blistering speeds by reducing air resistance and friction. Winning two SpaceX Innovation Awards in the process, Texas Guadaloop has designed, built, tested, and showcased hyperloop pods that levitate utilizing state-of-the-art air-bearing technology. Through engineering innovations, public advocacy, and international collaboration, Texas Guadaloop seeks to advance transportation towards a faster, cheaper, and more sustainable future. 

 

This year, Texas Guadaloop is taking hyperloop technology to new heights. We are completely transforming our old pod by incorporating cutting-edge innovations like linear induction motors, magnetic levitation, and regenerative braking. We will be competing in the Netherlands in the Summer of 2022 to showcase our technology to the world. No university team in the world has ever demonstrated a pod with our planned features. We’re ready to burst onto the world stage, guns blazing, Texas-style.

 

But we aren’t just an engineering team, we’re a movement. That means we need talent from all fields! We need business people to make tactical decisions to propel our team even farther onto the world stage. We need writers to communicate our vision with the world and to help build relationships with university and corporate partners. We need artists, designers, and filmmakers to transform societal conceptions about hyperloop. Wherever you come from, and whatever you do, we need you.

 

We build the future. Welcome to Texas Guadaloop.
summary: We are revolutionizing the transportation industry with sustainable hyperloop technology. We bring together students from all walks of life to change the world as one team.

name: Consult Your Community- Texas Chapter
description:
Texas CYC is a non-profit student-led organization providing pro-bono consulting services to local small businesses around Austin. 

 

CYC empowers undergraduate students to leverage their knowledge of business with their passion for service. Students engage with low-income and minority-owned small businesses over the course of a semester through an innovative program in which they are trained to provide business owners with comprehensive, pro bono consulting services. Through this process, students gain hands-on business experience and develop skills that prepare them for their future careers, while business owners become empowered to foster their own economic independence and secure their future prosperity, both of which contribute directly to our nation’s economic development and long-term sustainability.
summary: Texas CYC is a student-led organization that provides pro-bono consulting services to Austin's small, minority-owned businesses and nonprofits.

name: Design for America at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
About Design For America 
Design for America (DFA) is a multidisciplinary student organization that uses human-centered design thinking to create social impact. DFA aims at creating a community of social innovators that inspire change and help to solve problems in their local community.
 
About DFA@UT 

Design for America at UT Austin (DFA@UT) is a student-led design studio that uses human-centered design to address real challenges in our campus and Austin communities. Relaunched in Fall 2025, DFA@UT brings together designers and non-designers to collaborate on projects focused on belonging, access, and social impact.

Our teams have worked on initiatives such as the Dead Fountain Project, Campus Study Spaces, and the Kinsolving Courtyard Redesign, reimagining shared spaces to better support student life and community connection. This year, DFA@UT is partnering with the Austin nonprofit Keep Austin Fed to design solutions around food access, volunteer empowerment, and community distribution systems.

Through our partnership with DFA National, members also contribute to nationwide projects like Lifeguard Retention and Changemakers with the YMCA, gaining experience tackling large-scale social challenges.

DFA@UT empowers students to turn empathy into action by building thoughtful, inclusive solutions while growing as designers, leaders, and community advocates.
summary: Design for America at the University of Texas at Austin is an organization that uses human-centered design to create social impact. We are committed to working with members of the community to tackle issues in Austin and on campus.

name: MTNA - UT Austin
summary: Bring together music students who are studio teachers for networking and collaborating to advance the teaching field.

name: Working Moms Group
summary: We build community and a support network for working moms at UT Austin

name: Beta Theta Pi
description:
Mutual Assistance – Betas believe that men are mutually obligated to help others in the honorable labors and aspirations of life.

Intellectual Growth – Betas are devoted to continually cultivating their minds, including high standards of academic achievement.

Trust – Betas develop absolute faith and confidence in one another by being true to themselves and others.

Responsible Conduct – Betas choose to act responsibly, weighing the consequences of their actions on themselves and those around them.

Integrity – Betas preserve their character by doing what is morally right and demanding the same from their brothers.
summary: Men's social fraternity with a mission to develop men of principle for a principled life.

name: Alternative Breaks
description:
Alternative Breaks (AB) sends small groups of UT students to outside communities to grow as leaders and learn through immersive service experiences. AB is designed for passionate students who are looking to spend their academic breaks on productive and interactive service-learning opportunities. Alternative Breaks pushes students to participate in transformative learning, consistent reflection, and meaningful community service.

Alternative Breaks revolves around five key tenets: Service, Learning, Community, Cultural Humility, and Active Citizenship. By becoming Active Citizens, AB participants promise to carry what they learn back to Austin and back on campus.
summary: Alternative Breaks (AB) sends small groups of UT students to outside communities to grow as leaders and learn through immersive service experiences.

name: INFORMS Student Chapter at The University of Texas at Austin
description: Faculty Advisor: Professor Eric Bickel
summary: Encourage interest in Operations Research (OR) and the Management Sciences (MS); provide an informal forum for people interested in the educational programs, opportunities, methods, and techniques of ORMS; sponsor colloquia whose speakers address ORMS.

name: Longhorn Swim Club
description:
Longhorn Swim Club, or LSC, is a student organization that swimmers and hydrophilic humans at UT can embrace. Founded to provide an active swim group on campus with land-based opportunities abound, Longhorn Swim Club offers group swims, socials, swim meets, and more! We welcome swimmers of all experience levels. Come check us out, and dive into a program that is anything but dry. 

 

Follow us on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/LonghornSwimClub/

Donate to our club: https://utdirect.utexas.edu/apps/utgiving/online/nlogon/?menu=VSLS
summary: For all swimmers (old and new) who want to train with a group of people and engage in the sport of swimming

name: Longhorn Dance Club
description: Longhorn Dance Company (formerly known as Club) is a low-commitment organization that meets once a week for a class. We embrace all styles of dance as well as all levels. We do not hold auditions, and membership fees are required. Meetings are held every Wednesday from 7-8:30 pm, room announced in Slack channel weekly. Feel free to come out and join us! If you have any questions feel free to contact any officer or visit our Instagram where we have all information posted (@longhorndanceco).
summary: LDC is an inclusive organization that embraces all genres of dance. All skill levels and majors are welcome to attend! Our organization provides a great opportunity to relieve stress and have fun with dance in a low commitment environment.

name: Haven Student Fellowship
description:
HAVEN Student Fellowship started at the University of Texas at Austin in September of 2014. Our vision is to introduce young people to Jesus Christ so they can find safety from sin, self, and satan. Our values are prayer, the Word of God, transparency, and generosity. Our mission is to Reflect What it Means to be Young & Christian in the Contemporary World​​ through the way we live, the way we love, and the way we lead.

​

​​

 

​
summary: Haven Student Fellowship is a Christian org dedicated to bringing people to Christ. We want to create an atmosphere where students can be transparent about their daily struggles as young Christians. Haven meets on Fridays at 6:30 PM.

name: Freetail Hackers
description: An organization dedicated to hosting 2 hackathons per year, inviting students to collaborate internally among 6 teams to design, implement, market, and host the events. The hackathons are a space for 200 - 1000 students to come together and innovate new product solutions addressing the latest challenges of the community, economy, and society. Both of our hackathons have historically been free to collegiate students, with mentors, judges, organizers, and sponsors also attending the event. Our legacy event is HackTX.
summary: An org focused on fostering a community of students who like to build, program, design, and experiment with technology regardless of major.

name: Texas Pearls
description: The purpose of Texas Pearls is: to volunteer for various community events; to raise money for various charities; to introduce members to social activities; to develop mature, lasting friendships and leadership skills among its members; and to promote the University of Texas and the Austin community.
summary: Texas Pearls is a service organization for students looking to build leaders and foster friendships through serving the University of Texas campus and Austin community.

name: UT Austin Student Organization Advisors
description:
The Student Activities area of the Office of the Dean of Students has created several resources in order to help advisors in their work with registered and sponsored student organizations.

This group is only available to individuals who have been listed as a "University advisor" in a student organization HornsLink roster.  If you are aware of other advisors who are not listed in this group, please direct them to work with their perspective student organization to ensure that they are properly listed in their HornsLink roster or have them contact Student Organizations at studentorganizations@austin.utexas.edu

About Student Activities

Student Organizations in the Office of the Dean of Students provides several services to support to the over 1,100 registered and sponsored student organizations on campus. At the beginning of the spring term, Student Organizations registers all student organizations and maintains a student organization database that lists contact information, organization purpose, membership requirements and functions. Student Organizations offers many services to student organizations, providing information through its publications, training workshops, one-on-one consultations and event planning support.

One of the most important benefits of being a registered and sponsored student organization is access to Student Organizations staff and graduate student team who can assist with program planning, risk management assessment, conflict resolution, fundraising and other issues related to student organizations.

Other services we provide include:

  • Student Organization Center (SOC), Located in Student Services Building, Suite 4.400 or accessible by e-mail at SOC@austin.utexas.edu
  • General Purpose Classroom Room Reservations
  • Indoor and Outdoor Space Reservations
  • Event Planning
  • Student Organization Travel: Approval, Insurance, Safety Resources
  • Interpretation of UT Austin's Institutional Rules

Student Organizations staff take great pride in our efforts to support The University of Texas at Austin's educational mission. The incredibly wide range of student organizations offer many exciting opportunities to enrich student's educational experiences and support the develop a variety of leadership skills.
summary: The purpose of this group is to provide training, resources, and networking opportunities for UT Austin Registered and Sponsored Student Organizations.

name: Campus Elections
description:
The Campus Elections group is a temporary organization available for use by all candidates in elections during the First-Year Elections and Campus-Wide Elections campaign seasons. Membership is only available to eligible candidates who have filed with the Office of the Dean of Students to run in the respect election. Currently active for the Spring 2026 Campus Wide Elections. 


To be added to the Campus Elections organization, each candidate must:

  1. File to be a candidate for the Campus Wide Elections by 12 p.m. on Monday, February 9, 2026.
  2. Create a HornsLink profile (candidate representative must create a profile as well.
  3. Complete the Campaign Organization and Protected Marks Agreement (candidate only).
  4. Request to join the "Campus Elections" student organization. Candidates must request to join as a "candidate" and the Candidates Authorized Agent must request to join as "campaign staff".
  5. Attend the Candidate Seminar as indicated in the election timeline for policy training (Monday, February 9th at 6:30 p.m. in WCP 2.302
  6. Removal: Candidates who do not attend the candidate seminar for policy training, drop out of the elections or are disqualified from the elections will be removed from the Campus Elections student organization.
summary: Temporary organization available for use by candidates participating in first-year and campus-wide elections during the election campaign season.

name: Journal of Law and Technology at Texas
description:
The Journal of Law and Technology at Texas (JOLTT) is committed to exploring the vast impact technology has on the policies and practice of law. Our goal is to gather articles, host events, and bring speakers which serve to illuminate the changing legal landscape.

Examples of intersecting topics include: access to justice (legalzoom, efiling), civil procedure (spoliation, ediscovery), privacy (NSA, wiretapping), torts (self-driving cars, digital trespass), criminal law (hacking, dangerous manipulation of technology), medical law (prosthetics attached to computers, nanotechnology for attacking diseases), digital rights, contracts, etcetera. Nothing is untouched.

Membership is restricted to students, faculty, and staff of the University of Texas at Austin, per registered student organization guidelines.

The Journal of Law and Technology at Texas is a registered student organization. This organization is not sponsored by the University of Texas at Austin. The views expressed are by the student organization and do not represent the views of the University or its officers.
summary: An organization dedicated to scholarly research and publication on technology law and policy.

name: University of Texas Weightlifting Team
description: The club operates under the national governing body of USA Weightlifting and competes at local, regional, and national competitions. The mission of the team is to provide the growth of the sport on the University of Texas campus and to provide recreational and competitive opportunities to all students interested in the Olympic sport.
summary: The University of Texas Weightlifting Team is a competitive Olympic weightlifting team club that seeks to teach, train, and develop student-athletes of all levels in the sport of Olympic weightlifting.

name: Austin Student Chapter of the Acoustical Society of America
description: The student chapter of the Acoustical Society of America at The University of Texas at Austin is comprised of students who are studying towards a degree with a specialization in acoustics, as well as students who simply have an interest in acoustics.
summary: The student chapter of the Acoustical Society of America at The University of Texas at Austin is comprised of students who are studying towards a degree with a specialization in acoustics, as well as students who simply have an interest in acoustics.

name: Texas Latinos in Engineering and Sciences
description:
Our mission is to ignite a passion for STEM while providing opportunities for and support for under-served students to grow into leaders of their communities. Texas MAES provides a wide variety of academic, outreach, and professional development opportunities for students such as resume reviews, leadership development conferences, networking events, finals study nights, bowling, game nights, potluck-night socials, and much more!

We are always looking for interested students to join! Help make a difference and build a support network here at UT with Texas MAES! Feel free to join our Discord:

https://discord.gg/6EAAakpEKR
summary: Texas Latinos in Science and Engineering, or Texas MAES, is focused on promoting diversity in STEM through outreach events while providing support and resources for UT students to be successful.

name: ATX KDC
description:
Hi, we are ATX KDC! We are a dance crew founded to promote K-pop culture and modern dance styles at UT Austin. You can catch us performing at on and off-campus events, filming covers for our YouTube channel, and sharing our love of K-pop and modern dance with Austin and beyond.

Check out our YouTube channel to see more of what we do! 

youtube.com/c/ATXKDC

Follow us on our social media linked below!
summary: Organization that showcases the mixture of Korean pop culture and American hip hop culture through dance.

name: Machine Learning and Data Science
description:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=H17lf1Okd48

 

We promote data science and machine learning through our general meetings every Tuesday at 7 pm. These meetings include technical workshops, career advice, industry speakers, and socials. We also host the Deep Learning Workshop Series, Long Competition, and Datahack.

 

Events are free and open to all UT students. Newcomers welcome!

 

Important Links:

  • Website: Learn more about what we offer

  • Discord: This is our main communication channel

  • Instagram: Follow us to get notified about events
summary: We host workshops and events on topics in machine learning and data science - students of all levels and majors are welcome!

name: Harmony Longhorn Association
description: Our primary goal is to help freshman feel at home in the University. There are certainly other organizations like ours out there. The key difference is that we link Harmony alumni. These students graduated in class sizes that rarely exceed 80 students. As such, they are used to a much smaller community. This org hopes to smooth out the transition.
summary: Focused on connecting students from Harmony ISD who are currently attending the University of Texas at Austin.

name: The Space - ATX
description:
The Space - ATX is a community that creates an open space for dancers to grow and express themselves through freestyle or choreography. By offering free classes and sessions every week, dancers will have the resources needed to flourish. The Space strives to facilitate each member's experience by connecting them to the dance scene in Austin. 

Classes are free and open to dancers of all levels. Classes generally focus on choreography influenced by popping, locking, hip hop, waacking, and vogue. We post our weekly schedules @atxthespace on Instagram and Facebook, so come by and say hi!
summary: The Space - ATX is a community that creates an open space for dancers to grow and express themselves through freestyle and/or choreography. We offer free dance classes every week that teach choreography influenced by hip hop, locking, popping, and more!

name: Women in Civil, Architectural, and Environmental Engineering
description:
Goals:
• Create a professional network for civil, architectural, and environmental engineering students, graduates, and faculty members.
• Build an inclusive social community for undergraduates interested in CAEE (civil, architectural, and environmental engineering).
• Equip members with peer mentorship in their studies.

**membership not limited to a single gender or major

 

Here is our linktree: 

It provides info about WCAE, how to sign up for WCAE newsletter, WCAE Groupme, and more!

https://linktr.ee/texas.wcae?utm_source=linktree_profile_share<sid=f357cb5e-d418-4f33-93b7-f4076fdd4b50
summary: WCAE is dedicated to creating an inclusive community for undergraduate women, supporting women with mentorship throughout their studies, and engaging alumni and faculty role models to advance leadership and career development in engineering.

name: Science Under the Stars
description:
Science Under the Stars is a free, monthly public outreach lecture series founded and organized by graduate students in the Department of Integrative Biology at University of Texas at Austin. Since 2009 we have hosted over 100 speakers at our public events!

Events are held outdoors at dusk at Brackenridge Field Laboratory, 2907 Lake Austin Blvd, Austin, Texas 78703 during the fall and spring academic semesters. Talks are also livestreamed and can be watched at any time on our YouTube channel.
summary: Science Under the Stars is a graduate student run public lecture series. Events start at dusk outdoors at Brackenridge Field Laboratory monthly. Please only request to join if you are an Integrative Biology associated graduate student.

name: Austin Korean Presbyterian Church Psalm Young Adults
description: Ko PSALM young adult community is a group for those Korean-speaking young adults. We seek to restore the faith community in The New Testament through house church ministry. Through the ministry, we want to be faithful disciples of Jesus Christ, love our neighbor, and reach out to the world with the Gospel, following the Great Commandment and the Great Commission.
summary: We want to be faithful disciples of Jesus Christ, love our neighbors, and reach out to the world with the Gospel, following the Great Commandment and the Great Commission.

description:

New Dungeon Master Form

⤷ https://forms.gle/eDwj7KP4nytaAMuWA

 

   Hello, curious adventurers! (ᵔ ᵕ ᵔ )⊃━☆.*+.

☆ Texas DnD is a gaming club open to all levels of experience! If you’re interested in Tabletop RPGs such as DnD and Pathfinder, you’ve found the right destination! 

☆ We provide a safe space and a very inclusive community for all DMs and players to plan and run games for anyone to join! 

☆ You and your group can always play at one of our meetings! No reservations are required. If you need supplies like battle mats, dice, etc., let an officer know beforehand and we'll help you get set up!

 

｡･:*:･ﾟ★,｡･:*:･ﾟ☆

So… what is a Tabletop RPG?

   We just slung around many acronyms and weird words, so let’s define some of them! 

☆ Tabletop RPG (TTRPG) stands for “Tabletop Role Playing Games,” or simply RPGs for short. Any game where you can act out your character in a first-person way is an RPG. At the end of the day, it’s just you and some friends sitting around a table solving a problem — or causing one!

 

Okay, now, what is DnD?

   “DnD” is just a shortening of Dungeons & Dragons!

☆ You might recognize this game as a game of math, percentile dice, and taverns full of adventuring parties ready to rack up some gold or save a kingdom. 

☆ What you might not realize, however, is that this game is much more than slaying dragons.

 

Fine then. So what does DnD usually involve?

   A chance to be creative! 

☆ Whether you’re making a character backstory or worldbuilding, you create your own DnD experience! Only you can decide how you interact with the world. 

☆ There are some rules to the game but it's okay if you don't remember them all; however, most DMs use 'the rule of cool' to guide gameplay rather than hard and fast rules.

 

   Problem-Solving! 

☆ Work with your group to solve any problems your DM can think of to throw at you, and you’ll (probably) survive all of them!

 

   Expanding comfort zones!

☆ Step out of your bubble and meet new people here; everyone’s open to talking about just about anything, and chances are there’s someone interested in whatever you’re interested in. Take charge of your ideas and make them happen!

 

Awesome! How do I get started?

   Join Our Discord for announcements about meetings, DMing, events, and socials! 

☆ https://discord.gg/NGDkDbPUj2

☆ This is the main way of communication for the club, so if you’re serious about joining, do consider joining the club Discord!

 

   New Members, please fill out this membership form and introduce yourself on Discord.

☆ https://forms.gle/UQLoMLofuj4FkjdQ8

 

   Try your hand at DMing! 

☆ https://forms.gle/eDwj7KP4nytaAMuWA

☆ Fill out this form to post your campaign to our open campaigns board!

 

｡･:*:･ﾟ★,｡･:*:･ﾟ☆
