summary: Texas DnD is a tabletop gaming club open to all levels of experience. Our groups generally play TTRPGs such as DnD and Pathfinder; we provide a community for like-minded individuals interested in Tabletop Role-Play Games and meeting new people.

name: Asian/Asian American Faculty and Staff Association
description:
The mission of the Asian/Asian American Faculty and Staff Association (AAAFSA) at The University of Texas at Austin is to support, celebrate, and empower Asian and Asian American faculty and staff by fostering a community that provides opportunities for networking, communication, and growth -- both personal and professional.

AAAFSA also serves as a voice that represents and advocates for its members, while increasing awareness of Asian and Asian American issues within the University community.
summary: The Asian/Asian American Faculty and Staff Association supports, celebrates, and empowers Asian and Asian American faculty and staff by fostering a community that provides opportunities for networking, communication, and personal & professional growth.

name: Delta Upsilon
summary: Men's Fraternity - Building Better Men by focusing on our non-secret heritage and Four Founding Principles: The Promotion of Friendship, Diffusion of Liberal Culture, The Development of Character, and The Advancement of Justice.

name: Apricity Magazine
description:
Apricity Magazine (www.apricitymagazine.com) is the official literary and arts journal of The University of Texas at Austin. We are a nonprofit, international, multimedia literary and arts magazine run by undergraduate students. We also feature interviews of people who have significantly championed the humanities. Our submissions come from an all encompassing international and national base. We welcome poetry/prose submissions and visual/performing art submissions! 

We compete at the Columbia Scholastic Press Association's (CSPA) Collegiate Crown Awards and the Associated Collegiate Press Pacemaker Awards. UT's last CSPA crown award for magazines was in 1989 and gold crown for magazines in 1987. In 2017, we were awarded a CSPA Third Place Gold Circle for Design of a Single Spread. In 2019, we received a CSPA Gold Crown and 2nd Place Cover Design Apple Award for Volume II. In 2024, we were awarded two CSPA Columbia Gold Circle Awards for Design Portfolios and Best Artistic Organization from the UT Office of the Dean of Students.
summary: Apricity Magazine (www.apricitymagazine.com) is UT Austin's official literary and arts magazine. Founded in 2016 by Margaret Siu, we accept all forms of art and feature these pieces online and in a print edition every spring.

name: The Law Veterans Association at the University of Texas School of Law
description: The Law Veterans Association at the University of Texas School of Law is composed of all current and former military and military-affiliated members of the Texas Law School community. In addition to providing support and mentoring to members, TLVA works with other student groups to promote and share military and veterans related legal issues to Texas’s campus.
summary: The Law Veterans Association at the University of Texas School of Law is composed of all current and former military and military-affiliated members of the Texas Law School community.

name: Muslim Legal Society
description: The Muslim Law Students' Association is a student-run organization that serves Muslim law students at Texas Law through community-building, event-organizing and raising awareness around pertinent issues affecting Muslims at Texas Law and in Austin, TX.
summary: The Muslim Legal Society (MLS) brings together students of diverse sects, backgrounds, and cultures to foster a sense of community.

name: Student Chapter of the Society of Petrophysicists and Well Log Analysts at The University of Texas at Austin
description: The Student Chapter of SPWLA at UT Austin is dedicated to advancing the science of petrophysics and formation evaluation among students and young professionals at The University of Texas at Austin. The chapter host monthly technical seminars that are presented by industry leading petrophysicists, formation evaluation specialists, and petroleum engineers. The chapter also hosts an annual student paper contest where winners are invited to present their work at the SPWLA Annual Symposium. The Student Chapter of SPWLA at UT Austin is an officially recognized student chapter of the Society of Petrophysicists and Well Log Analysts.
summary: The Student Chapter of SPWLA at UT Austin is dedicated to advancing the science of petrophysics and formation evaluation among students and young professionals at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Texas Convergent
description: Texas Convergent was established to promote collaboration across the University of Texas through entrepreneurship. Historically, it has been the combination of students from varying backgrounds with the common goal of innovation that has driven the most creative ideas and products. Convergent aims to bridge the gap between the Computer Science department and students across the university by providing hands-on experience through collaborative projects and a community for students to launch their ideas. The three cornerstones of Convergent's multidisciplinary methodology are Technology, Business, and Design.
summary: Texas Convergent was established to promote collaboration across the University of Texas through entrepreneurship. Convergent aims to bridge the gap between the Computer Science department and students across the university.

name: Lebanese Cultural Organization
description:
Lebanese Cultural Organization at UT-Austin is a non-religious, non-political Lebanese social group for students. We are subscribed to the Lebanese Collegiate Network - a national organization that binds Lebanese student groups throughout the United States - that foster networking in local and national areas. Our group organizes a variety of social events in the Austin area to connect fellow people of Lebanese background while having a fun time. We hope to create a welcoming atmosphere for our members that embraces their backgrounds while celebrating Lebanese culture through art (i.e. dabke/dancing), food (i.e. potlucks) and music (i.e. parties)! Persons of non-Lebanese background with a passion for the country of Lebanon are invited to join

 

*To stay tuned for our upcoming events, please follow our Instagram page @lcoaustin

 

*To follow general updates and be connected to the Lebanese of the greater Austin area, please request to join our Facebook group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/140517269799661/?ref=bookmarks
summary: A social group that strives to develop a network for Lebanese students at the University of Texas at Austin  as well as introduce the student body at UT to the Lebanese culture.

name: Association of Research Administrators
summary: Forum for research administrators to network, exchange information, learn new skills, and explore strategies to improve research support at The University.

name: Texas Undergraduate Research Journal
description: The Texas Undergraduate Research Journal (URJ) is responsible for publishing an annual academic journal with articles by students who have engaged in independent research projects across all colleges. Moreover, the URJ organizes various events throughout the academic year to promote student engagement with research on campus. These include the Capitol of Texas Undergraduate Research Forum, Networking Nights, and Research Panels.
summary: The Texas URJ produces an annual journal that publishes research done by undergraduates at UT Austin. Additionally, the URJ hosts panels and networking events throughout the year to introduce students to broad topics in research and in research writing.

name: Pi Lambda Chi Latina Sorority, Inc. - Eta Chapter
description: An important aspect of our Sorority is that women of all ethnicities are welcome to join our Sisterhood and share the experience of being a Sister. We don’t turn any woman away because of race, ethnicity, sexual orientation, or socioeconomic status. We love the diversity our Sorority has. We have Sisters represented worldwide; from California to Jersey, Nepal to Peru. We don’t choose our Sisters, our Sisters choose us.
summary: The mission of Pi Lambda Chi is to create a strong Sisterhood and an educational support network for women on college campuses. Pi Lambda Chi seeks to preserve Latina/Latino culture and history as well as promote education and community service.

name: e-NABLE at UT Austin
description: e-NABLE at UT Austin encourages students to improve their technical and clinical skills while utilizing the 3D printing resources on campus. The members will first learn how to print the prosthetics. Then, members will obtain measurements from patients and work on an individualized design. Each recipient gets their prosthetic/tool for free! Hopefully this organization will reduce the financial and logistic burden getting a prosthetic places on families. For more experienced members, we have R&D teams working on upscaling prosthetic designs and finding ways to make them cheaper and more accessible.
summary: e-NABLE is an international non-profit dedicated to making prosthetics more accessible. Our chapter focuses on 3D printing prosthetics and other assistive tech then distributing them to North/Central Texas amputees, as well as worldwide

name: Texas Ballet Club
description:
Texas Ballet Club is intended to provide a space for students of all experience levels to practice ballet in an environment that is accessible and inviting. The organization focuses on ballet, but we welcome any opportunity for collaboration with other dance groups on campus. Texas Ballet Club welcomes students with any level of ballet experience to join us in weekly student-led ballet classes. 

 

FAQs:

Where and when do you meet? 

Meeting times:

Beginner classes Wednesdays from 5:30- 7:00 PM in WIN 1.172

Intermediate classes Thursdays from 5:30- 7:00 PM in WCP 2.310

Advanced classes Sundays from 11:00 AM -1:00 PM in RSC 1.104

 

Do I need to have ballet experience?

Nope! We welcome all UT students interested in ballet. Our student teachers try to accommodate all experience levels. 

 

What do I wear to your meetings/classes?

You are welcome to wear whatever you are comfortable dancing in. Some people wear socks and athletic clothing; others wear leotards, tights, and pointe shoes. It’s up to you.

 

Where can I get more information? 

Join our GroupMe for updates! https://groupme.com/join_group/60920740/PXCbAg1E
summary: The purpose of Texas Ballet Club is to create an opportunity for ballet students of all skill levels to work on their technique and artistry through weekly technique classes and performances.

name: UTalent Records
description:
UTalent Records is a student-run record label based at the University of Texas at Austin that works to provide a launch pad for independent student artists, educate those interested in the music industry, and provide community for all music-lovers.

Whether you're looking to try out music business, practice specific skills like production or photography, make music yourself, or just meet like-minded people, UTalent will always be here with open arms. If you're curious about how specifically you could be involved in the org, how we operate internally, or would like to talk to an exec member, feel free to reach out via our email, Instagram, website, or Linktree!
summary: UTalent Records is a student-run record label based at the University of Texas at Austin that works to provide a launch pad for independent student artists, educate those interested in the music industry, and provide community for all music-lovers.

name: The Association of Black Psychologists
description:
Hello!

We are the Texas Student Circle of The Association of Black Psychologists (ABPsi). We are an academic and professional development organization designed to support and assist those interested in mental health and other social science fields. We host events, workshops, and have general meetings every other Thursday. Follow us on our social media platforms for upcoming events!
summary: Texas Student Circle of Association of Black Psychologists (ABPsi) mission is to promote professional development, Black Psychology and mental health awareness at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Re:Sound A Cappella
description: Hello! We are an a cappella group made up of students who love Jesus and love to sing. We aim to use the unique talents we are given to bring God the greatest glory through performances, service, gospel sharing, and loving others.
summary: Re:Sound A Cappella is a Christian a cappella group whose vision is to worship God and make Him known through serving others and sharing music.

name: Texas Sunshines
description: Texas Sunshines is an established spirit group at The University of Texas at Austin founded in Spring 2017. Our organization consists of a diverse group of individuals dedicated to service, spirit, and friendship. We are partnered with SVPA, the Sexual Violence Prevention Association, to raise awareness and education regarding sexual violence. Our organization is open to any women or non-binary individuals at UT Austin, and we recruit both in fall and spring semesters. To learn more about our organization, visit our website (www.texassunshines.com) or check out our Instagram (@texassunshines). Stay sunny!
summary: Texas Sunshines is a spirit group for women and non-binary folks at UT Austin founded in the Spring of 2017. Our organization and members are dedicated to service, spirit, and friendship. To learn more, visit our website at https://www.texassunshines.com

name: Animators Club
description:
Have you always been interested in 2D or 3D animation? Or maybe working on concept designs, storyboards and the like? Do you love animated films and TV shows? Wanna share that passion and meet others interested in all of this? Then you're in the right place!

We are Animators Club, an organization working to provide resources to students interested in animation as a career or hobby. We meet on a weekly basis to hold workshops, industry guest speaker sessions, and socials! Please come check us out!

Meetings are currently every Thursday from 7PM-8PM at RLP 0.106. Join our official Animators Club Discord for more info and find our other socials here: https://animatorsclubut.carrd.co/
summary: Interested in 2D or 3D animation? Concept designs or storyboards? Do you enjoy animated films/TV? Then this is the club for you. The Animators Club will provide resources to all students interested in pursuing animation as a career or hobby.

name: Socks for the Sole
description: Socks for the Sole is a student organization and registered 501(c)(3) charitable organization intended to fundraise and distribute socks and other necessary items to people experiencing homelessness in the greater Austin, TX community. We organize service events throughout the year, such as donation drives and fundraising events in order to accrue the proper funds and needed items to fulfill our purpose. In doing so, we hope to connect students at the University of Texas at Austin with opportunities to serve people experiencing homelessness and to understand the larger social justice issues that contribute to homelessness.
summary: Socks for the Sole is a student organization and registered 501(c)3 charitable organization intended to fundraise and distribute socks and other necessary items to people experiencing homelessness in the greater Austin, TX community.

name: Arrows of Christ
description:
ARROWS OF CHRIST is a Biblically-based, eternity-minded Christian student organization. 

We recognize that Christian students today face many challenges and temptations. And in a world growing increasingly corrupt, our response must be stronger, rooted deeply in the Word of God. Our mission at Arrows of Christ is to gather like-minded Christians, provide a godly context for fellowship, and share monumental Biblical and apologetic perspectives that change the way we face the world.
 
Our mission fellowship has three goals: 

  • STEADILY: Maintain a consistent campus presence, sharing Scriptures openly and offering clear, compassionate Bible answers for curious souls.

  • WEEKLY: Host regularly scheduled on-campus Bible studies designed to nurture faith, deepen fellowship, and build spiritual strength.

  • MONTHLY: Organize “Campus Conversations” events, held beneath our distinctive red tents and tablecloths, offering impactful biblical perspectives on life’s crucial questions.

Our recognizable year-round presence on campus includes vibrant BIBLE GIVEAWAYS, RED TENTS, and RED TABLECLOTHS, often visible along Speedway.

What motivates our members to serve with passion and joy? The eager anticipation of the glorious return of our beloved Lord Jesus Christ! Inspired by the faithful legacy of the "Philadelphia Church" (1500-1890 AD), known for steadfastly keeping His Word and not denying His name (Revelation 3:8), we strive to echo this blameless and lovely Biblical precedent. Living holy, uncompromising, and fully consecrated lives fills our hearts with “joy unspeakable and full of glory”—like a bride eagerly awaiting the marriage supper of the Lamb!

We warmly invite you to visit us anytime—look for our welcoming red tents and tables on campus! Whether you have questions or simply need encouragement, we’re here to listen and share Biblical answers. Stop by for a free Bible and let’s start a conversation—one that could impact your eternity.
summary: Arrows of Christ is a Biblically-based, eternity-minded Christian student organization. We love Biblical holiness and do not make any compromises with the Word of God.

name: Association for Women in Mathematics - Texas Chapter
description:
The purpose of the Association for Women in Mathematics (AWM) - Texas Chapter is to encourage women and girls to study and have active careers in the mathematical sciences. We strive to accomplish this goal by promoting equal opportunity and treatment of women and girls in the mathematical sciences, and by fostering community and cooperation in these fields. We meet once a week with a rotating variety of socials, panels, and workshops. All genders are welcome!

 

For discussion with active members and updates for upcoming events, join the club discord: https://discord.gg/Vqkj22gS5d. We post regularly to Instagram as well. More information can be found on our website, https://web.ma.utexas.edu/awm/index.html.
summary: The Association for Women in Mathematics - Texas Chapter aims to promote equal opportunity and the equal treatment of women and girls in the mathematical sciences. All genders are welcome.

name: Social Anime Club
description:
Social Anime Club is a student-led org dedicated to bringing like-minded people together to share their love for anime and manga! We have weekly meetings, where we discuss anime, play games, go out for dinner, and socialize. We also hold social events outside of regular club meeting times.


Follow our social media because that's where we post info about events and meeting times:

Discord

Instagram
summary: A social club at UT. Let's eat food, talk about/watch anime, and get caught up in wacky shenanigans.

name: TEDxUTAustin
description: TEDxUTAustin at the University of Texas at Austin strives to allow distinguished professors, alumni, entrepreneurs, and students to offer informative, insightful, and motivational speeches to the UT community. The goal of TEDxUTAustin at UT is to help organize events at different locations on campus; this includes searching for potential speakers, holding auditions to grant the UT community a voice, and fundraising or marketing to expand the size and duration of events.
summary: TEDxUTAustin hosts the largest TEDx conference in Austin, offering new perspectives and granting students, professors, community members, and distinguished individuals a platform to share thoughts and unite the Austin community.

name: Hispanic Association of Computer Scientists
description:
Hispanic Association of Computer Scientists (HACS) seeks to empower Hispanic students by providing opportunities to engage in leadership, networking and mentoring with peers, faculty and professionals in the area of computer science.

But you do not need to be Hispanic or even in CS to join, as long as you have a passion for diversity and are interested in tech, you are more than welcome in our org.

Follow us on Instagram and Twitter for updates @texashacs !!!!
summary: Hispanic Association of Computer Scientists (HACS) seeks to empower Hispanic students by providing opportunities to engage in leadership, networking and mentoring with peers, faculty and professionals in the area of computer science.

name: The Texas Orator
description: The Texas Orator is a multi-partisan, peer-reviewed political publication founded and maintained by University of Texas students. We value thorough analysis in our publications and strive to protect the ideals of free speech and unhindered access to information on campus and nationwide. We seek to keep the student body informed of the implications of ever-changing political climates, both domestically and abroad, from a range of voices and perspectives. To join TXO, apply here: https://thetexasorator.com/join/
summary: The Texas Orator is a multi-partisan political review founded and maintained by University of Texas students. We value thorough analysis in our publications and strive to protect free speech and unhindered access to information on campus.

name: Texas Blockchain
description: We are an organization that focuses on education and execution with a focus on blockchain technologies. We support students who are business-oriented or software engineering-oriented to gain the necessary skills to land internships and full-time roles in the crypto industry. We foster this growth through market analysis, pitches, research papers, hackathons, conferences, personal projects, and much more. We also  proudly sponsor students to travel to the many crypto conferences that happen across the globe each year.
summary: UT Austin's leading blockchain, web3, and crypto student organization

name: Math Club
description:
The Math Club is a community for anyone interested in mathematics at UT, not limited to math majors. Every Tuesday we host a guest talk or a social event. In the talks, a professor or graduate student talks about an area of math that interests them. These topics range from knot theory to machine learning to quantum computing, depending on the speaker. Social events include board game nights, trivia, and special events like Mathnic, Estimathon, Mathnic, a Pi Day celebration, and more!

Linktree: https://linktr.ee/utaustinmathclub

Longhorn Math: https://discord.gg/gmpKVzpCBt

our website
summary: Math Club is a social and academic organization meant for anyone interested in mathematics.

name: American Academy of Environmental Engineers and Scientists at the University of Texas
description: We are a professional student organization focused on environmental engineering and environmental science but are open to all majors! We seek to provide our members with opportunities to gain exposure to professionals in the field, learn about technical innovation and practice, as well as participate in service projects to serve our community. We provide support for students in pursuing their professional goals as well as fostering a sense of community amongst environmental engineering and science undergraduate students. We host bi-weekly meetings on Tuesdays from 6-7pm at ECJ 1.222 where we discuss current environmental events, learn about career paths and opportunities through guest speakers, and provide food! Please follow our instagram @texasaaees for accurate meeting times!
summary: A professional association for Environmental Engineers and Scientists. The organization will have opportunities for mentorship, speakers, and hands-on environmental engineering and science student projects.

name: Absolute Pitch
description: We are Absolute Pitch! Established in the Fall of 2018, we are one of UT's treble acappella groups. Our mission is to uplift individuals around us with our voices and presence on campus. If you have any questions about our organization, don't hesitate to contact us via email, Instagram, Twitter, or Facebook!
summary: Absolute Pitch is a treble acappella group that sings and performs a wide range of genres and music styles.

name: Catholic Law Student Society
summary: Organization to connect Catholic Law Students with the larger Catholic legal community and provide faith-based resources.

name: Texas Black Lens
description:
What We Create Changes the World!

Black Lens is the home for Black creatives at UT and a hub for all who believe in the power of diverse storytelling. Our mission is to cultivate a safe, empowering, and innovative community where Black students- and those committed to uplifting underrepresented voices- can connect, create and thrive as they pursue their artistic vision. Open to ALL majors!

General body meetings every other Friday, 5:30-7pm

Production meetings immediately follow, 5:45-6:30pm [Only during Spring Production season]

Follow us on social media for updates! Instagram/Twitter: @texasblacklens, TikTok: @texas.black.lens
summary: Black Lens is THE space for Black creatives at UT. Our mission is to establish a safe, empowering, and innovative space for Black creatives on campus to explore their artistic passions and pursuits.

name: The Triple Helix at UT Austin
description: Our organization's goal is to produce an ePublication each year that discusses relevant scientific and ethical issues using a research-based perspective. We emphasize the intersection between science, society, and law through the critical analysis of new scientific findings both in the global scientific community as well as the free exchange of ideas at The University of Texas at Austin. It is not simply a rehashing of old research, but rather a forum where undergraduate authors can disseminate original and creative thoughts about previously done and ongoing work and research. Our team consists of writers, editors, and designers.
summary: The Texas TTH chapter strives to discuss relevant scientific and ethical issues using a research-based perspective.

name: New Wave Longhorns
description:
New Wave Longhorns is a supportive student community that provides social activities, academic growth, and professional development for students on a nontraditional path to their degree. We welcome older students, veterans, parents, full-time workers, or anyone who identifies as nontraditional. Our organization is intended to be a community leader on campus and a voice for nontraditional students. So if you are not on the traditional path, join our organization and help build our growing community at UT and have some fun!

Updated October 2024
summary: New Wave Longhorns is a supportive student community that provides social activities, academic growth, and professional development for students on a nontraditional path to their degree!

name: Gamma Iota Sigma @ UT Austin
description: Gamma Iota Sigma - Gamma Zeta Chapter is a co-ed business fraternity which provides academic, professional, and social opportunities for members. Our focuses are in data science, risk management, insurance, and actuarial science, but we have members of all majors and areas of study. We have frequent guest speakers from the insurance industry as well as workshops in which we teach important skills such as resume creation, how to make a LinkedIn profile, and technical skills such as excel and R.
summary: Gamma Iota Sigma (GIS) - Gamma Zeta Chapter is a national co-ed business fraternity with focus in Data Science, Risk Management, Insurance, and Actuarial Science. GIS accepts students from all academic disciplines.

name: Student Parent Organization
description:
The mission of Student Parent Organization is to create community, provide access to resources, and advocate for parenting students at UT. Student parents must balance family life and academic responsibilities which can lead to feelings of loneliness. SPO is here to remind student parents that you are not alone, and you deserve your place in the Longhorn community. Everyone's journey is unique; SPO is here to support you on yours!

 

Show your support for student parents at UT by signing our petition and visiting our HornRaiser fundraising campaign.
summary: Student Parents Organization is dedicated to creating a community to offer support to student parents who are pursuing academia and family life.

name: Origami Circle
description:
The Origami Circle is dedicated to teaching UT students the art of paper folding. Folders of all experience levels are welcome to our weekly meetings. Our meetings feature fun projects and the opportunity to meet fellow origami lovers! MEETINGS ARE FRIDAYS 6-7 PM AT RLP 0.104!!!

NO MEETING AUGUST 29TH!!

Hop into our Discord for more information: https://discord.gg/rzrD2fy

In partnership with the St. David's Medical Center, the Origami Circle is collecting origami models to give to patients. Please contact us for more information on how to contribute.

Please email us at origamicir@gmail.com with any questions or comments.
summary: Origami Circle is dedicated to teaching UT students the art of paper folding.

name: Friends of MSF at UT Austin
description: Friends of MSF at UT Austin is the student chapter under Médecins Sans Frontières, formerly known as Doctors Without Borders. We seek to open doors for students to get involved in medicine and community service while fundraising for and supporting the efforts of Médecins Sans Frontières both here in Austin and abroad. Friends of MSF at UT Austin focuses on healthcare-related community service, refugee outreach, and global humanitarianism involving medical service.
summary: Friends of MSF at UT Austin seeks to open doors for students to get involved in medicine and public service while fundraising for and supporting the efforts of Médecins Sans Frontières both here in Austin and abroad.

name: Silk Club
description: Silk Club is an organization centered on Austin-based self-identified Asian women and/or queer people to collaborate on projects that celebrate our experience. It is a space committed to validating and empowering intersectional identities, building creative opportunities, and fostering connections between different Asian communities.
summary: Silk Club is an Austin-based organization for self-identified Asian women and/or queer folks to collaborate on creative projects that celebrate our experience.

name: Present Your PhD to a 12-Year-Old
description:
Present your PhD (PYP) is a science outreach group made up of graduate students at the University of Texas at Austin. We want to make a difference in K-12 science education by using our original research to show relatable examples of scientific discovery and impact. 

. 

Scientists often struggle to communicate their findings to the general public. PYP allows graduate students to present their original research to younger students in the community. We present in classrooms, recreation centers, summer camps, and our own events. Our goal is to help develop our members' science communication and presentation skills for any audience, setting us up for success in our future careers. 

. 

If you are interested in joining PYP and participating in science outreach, please email us at: presentyourphd@gmail.com

. 

Cheers!
PYP Team
summary: Present Your PhD (PYP) is a science outreach organization where graduate students present their research to K-12 students.

name: K-Beat
description:
K-Beat is an open workshop, cover, and performance based dance group open to all levels hoping to help others improve on their individual dance skills and build community for a diverse group of kpop fans.
Check out our Social Medias!

Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpXPZKBwoN1eLR3pnRBPtqw 
Instagram: Kbeat_ut

Discord: https://discord.gg/ybW43hTX3
summary: K-Beat is an open workshop and cover based dance group open to all levels hoping to help others improve on their individual dance skills and build community for a diverse group of kpop fans.

name: Wealth Management Student Organization
description:
The Wealth Management Student Organization is a McCombs Affiliated Student Organization (MASO) at the  McCombs School of Business. We support the development and career prospects of students interested in wealth management so they may empower themselves and others to take control of their finances and lead fulfilling lives. 
 
"Spread the wealth" is a slogan we live by, and through a friendly and welcoming environment, our events develop members professionally, educate them in financial planning, financial literacy, and wealth stewardship. We do our part to develop the next generation of wealth managers and prepare students for life after college by covering investment management, retirement planning, estate planning, tax planning, life insurance, gifting, and legal services.
 
Our events are typically each Thursday from 6-7 PM and consist of company nights, case studies, workshops, and socials. At the beginning of each semester, we hold information sessions where we share further details to prospective members. The dates and locations of these information sessions will be shared on our newsletter and Instagram, @wmso.tx.
 
We are open to all majors and grades. To join our newsletter and stay updated on our upcoming events, please fill out this form. If you have a question, feel free to reach out to wmso.tx@gmail.com.
summary: The Wealth Management Student Organization brings together individuals interested in wealth management. We teach CFP (certified financial planning) concepts and support the careers of members interested in wealth management. All majors welcome

name: Association of Computational Engineers
description:
The Association of Computational Engineers (ACE) is an organization for Computational Engineering students to come together and participate in social events, networking, professional development, academic projects, and skill building.

ACE is not restricted to COE majors and is open to any student interested in scientific computing, software, and computer simulation.
summary: The Association of Computational Engineers (ACE) is an organization dedicated to supporting students interested in Computational Engineering/Science, Aerospace Engineering, or related disciplines through technical, networking, and social opportunities.

name: Special Olympics Club at The University of Texas
summary: Special Olympics Club intends to bring together college students that wish to enhance the lives of people with intellectual disabilities through sports, friendship, advocacy and other activities.

name: First Generation Law Students
description: First Generation Law Students (FGLS) is intended to provide a community for those who are the first in their family to attend law school by creating opportunities to learn, network, and socialize within the University of Texas School of Law and the broader legal community.
summary: Creating opportunities to learn, network, and socialize within the University of Texas School of Law and the broader legal community for those who are the first in their family to attend law school.

name: SCORE Athletics
description:
The Problem

Austin, Texas has the fastest growing poverty concentration of any of the top 100 metro cities in America. In Travis County, 1 in 4 children live below the poverty line, and under-resourced, title 1 schools lack sufficient funding and support from the Texas government. Kids that live in these environments grow up without the same opportunities that other kids have to participate in after-school sports programs and be influenced by positive role models.

Our Mission

Team sports and mentorship play a vital role in building character, determination, and leadership. SCORE Athletics provides kids with the opportunity to play team sports and someone who invests in them on and off the field. These kids can be our future doctors, lawyers, business owners, engineers, and athletes; all they need is one person at the right time who goes the extra yard and invests in them. At SCORE Athletics, we believe in these kids. At SCORE Athletics, we believe in champions.

What's the Solution?

Our UT student volunteers travel to our partner schools once a week for a 10-week period to coach the kids in three sports: soccer, flag football, and basketball. Roughly 8-10 volunteers are in charge of about 15-20 elementary kids per school. During each session, these coaches engage the kids in drills and scrimmages in order to both teach them valuable skills and provide them with an opportunity to play team sports.

As stated in our mission statement, our program is designed to impact kids in the classroom as well as the field. Team sports have been proven to teach skills such as communication, leadership, and teamwork; skills that are essential to successful performance in the classroom. SCORE Athletics seeks to build those skills in order to directly improve their classroom experience. SCORE also seeks to provide students with a mentor who is consistent throughout the 10-week program, constantly encouraging and pushing them to achieve more, and caring about them and their well-being.

 

Find us on Instagram • Fill out interest form to join!
summary: SCORE Athletics is an affiliated student organization with SCORE Athletics, an established 501(c)(3) non-profit based in the Austin area that provides kids with the opportunity to play team sports in a safe, competitive environment.

name: Texas Pre-Law Honor Society
description: Texas Pre-Law Honor Society functions as an organization that creates both a social and professional network for pre-law students. Members will meet and hear from practicing attorneys and administrators of law schools regarding the navigation of both law school and the vast legal field. In addition, we host social and volunteering events to extend our activities outside the classroom.
summary: TX-PLHS works to help pre-law students pave their path to law school by inviting guest speakers, preparing for the LSAT, and hosting networking events so that students can learn about life in law school and as an attorney.

name: Afterglow ATX
description: Afterglow is the premier digital and print music publication of the University of Texas at Austin. Produced entirely by students, our mission is to publish innovative work with a special focus on topical stories that push boundaries on how we view different artists, genres, and trends. We foster a distinct creative community with our robust daily digital content, long-form print magazine publishing, new media production, and public engagement through immersive concerts and events.
summary: We are an online music publication with a mission to publish innovative work and spotlight underrepresented artists' work.

name: The Aggie Society at Texas Law
description: The Aggie Society at Texas Law was revitalized in 2018 as a network dedicated to bringing graduates of Texas A&M University and Texas Law together. Our community seeks to connect current students and alumni in order to enhance the learning environment and professional legal community. The Aggie Society at Texas Law offers students a 1L mentorship program, access to a network of legal professionals from across the state, and the life-long support of fellow Aggies and Longhorns. The organization is also deeply committed to giving back to the community through its annual pro bono project, The Big Event.
summary: The Aggie Society at Texas Law seeks to strengthen ties between students, Texas A&M/Texas Law alumni, and the Austin community.

name: Longhorn Smash
description:
Longhorn Smash aims to provide a place for competitive smash bros players at UT to thrive while learning to balance their gaming with academic success. It hosts tournaments or get-togethers weekly, allowing for players to test their skill in fighting games like Super Smash Bros. Ultimate, Street Fighter V, and many others. Longhorn Smash is free to join and encourages participation from all levels of skill. Be sure to contact one of our members on Facebook for more information, or join our active Discord group at https://discord.gg/DMcrFVa

Our Discord channel is where most discussion takes place. It’s how the club members can exchange information and advice. These extensive resources can provide players with the tools necessary to go beyond the collegiate level to professional gaming.
summary: Longhorn Smash is an organization aimed at providing a place for competitive gamers at UT to grow and thrive while learning how to balance their gaming habits with academic success. Super Smash Bros Ultimate is our primary competitive game at the moment.

name: Nurses Christian Fellowship - UT Austin
description:
Nurses Christian Fellowship invites you to be a part of God’s work in your nursing school. Through the NCF community, you will:

  • Be encouraged to grow in your faith in the context of nursing school.
  • Be equipped to integrate your faith with nursing through Bible study and spiritual care resources.
  • Be empowered to share your faith with other nursing students.
summary: Nurses Christian Fellowship invites you to be a part of God’s work in your nursing school through Christ-like community, Bible study, and service events!

name: Texas Institute for Discovery Education in Science
description: The Texas Institute for Discovery Education in Science (TIDES) in the College of Natural Sciences aims to catalyze, support, and showcase innovative, evidence-based undergraduate science education. TIDES was proposed in the CNS 2013 Strategic Plan (as the Texas Center for Science Discovery) as a way to continue and enhance the college’s leading role in STEM education.
summary: The Texas Institute for Discovery Education in Science (TIDES) in the College of Natural Sciences aims to catalyze, support, and showcase innovative, evidence-based undergraduate science education.

name: Texas Dhoom
description: Texas Dhoom is a co-ed, noncompetitive Bollywood fusion dance team! Established in 2018, this organization provides the rewarding dance team experience with only moderate time commitment. We perform at cultural and charity events around campus throughout the school year. Check out our Instagram (@texasdhoom) for more information!
summary: Texas Dhoom is a co-ed, noncompetitive Bollywood fusion dance team!

name: University Ombuds Offices
description: The Ombuds offices can help, whether it’s listening, directing you to the appropriate university office, identifying your options, or coaching you to prepare for a difficult conversation. UT has separate Ombuds offices dedicated to students, staff, and faculty.
summary: The Ombuds offices can help, whether it’s listening, directing you to the appropriate university office, identifying your options, or coaching you to prepare for a difficult conversation.

name: Turning Point USA at The University of Texas at Austin
description: We educate students about the importance of free market and other conservative values through well-planned, effective activism initiatives, unite like-minded individuals and organizations on campus to increase impact and activism , empower and train students to become activists on their campuses and within their communities.
summary: TPUSA UT’s mission is to identify, educate, and organize students to promote the principles of freedom, free markets, and limited government. Our organization is the largest and fastest growing, entirely student led pro America organization at UT Austin!

name: Platypus Austin
description:
The Platypus Affiliated Society organizes reading groups and events focused on problems and tasks inherited from the “Old” (1920s-30s), “New” (1960s-70s) and post-political (1980s-90s) Left for the possibilities of emancipatory politics today. Platypus Austin is open to anyone with an interest in socialism, Marxism, communism, history, philosophy, and/or politics.

The Platypus chapter at UT Austin will be holding a weekly reading group on the history of the Left and Marxism, weekly social events, and host panels on Leftist issues.

Platypus Austin Discord
summary: The Platypus Affiliated Society organizes reading groups and events focused on problems and tasks inherited from the “Old” (1920s-30s), “New” (1960s-70s) and post-political (1980s-90s) Left for the possibilities of emancipatory politics today.

name: Tau Sigma at The University of Texas at Austin
description: Tau Sigma at The University of Texas at Austin strives to recognize and promote the academic excellence and involvement of transfer students on the Forty Acres. Tau Sigma at The University of Texas at Austin is a National Honor Society that provides its members with opportunities to earn scholarships, attend leadership conferences, take leadership positions, and serve others at UT and in the Austin community, all while developing friendships and becoming a more integral part of the Longhorn family!
summary: Tau Sigma at the University of Texas at Austin is a National Honor Society designed exclusively for transfer students on the Forty Acres.

name: Switch Energy Club Longhorn Chapter
description: Switch Energy Club is a student organization at UT-Austin dedicated to holistically and objectively educating motivated students of all majors about the energy industry through meetings, activities, outreach events, and projects. We offer students the opportunity to learn about the policy, engineering, and business sides behind various types of energy, such as biofuels, upstream and downstream oil and gas, solar, carbon capture technology, and many more.
summary: We offer the opportunity to learn about policy, engineering, & business sides behind various types of energy - biofuels, oil & gas, solar, batteries, carbon capture, & many more - via biweekly meetings, events, & our annual energy case competition.

name: Disability and Neurodivergent Law Student Alliance at The University of Texas at Austin
description: The mission of Disability and Neurodivergent Law Student Alliance at The University of Texas at Austin is to build a community and provide support for law students with disabilities and law student who are neurodivergent, have family members or close friends with disabilities, or are just interested in disability rights and advocacy overall.  TLDNA is an affinity group defined broadly in that it welcomes anyone who identifies as a member, advocate, or ally.  TLDNA hopes to increase accessibility within the legal profession; enhance cultural competency with regard to disabilities and neurodivergences; and help our members navigate both law school and the legal profession.
summary: Disability and Neurodivergent Law Student Alliance at The University of Texas at Austin aims to build community and provide support for law students with disabilities or neurodivergence, have family members or close friends with disabilities, or are just

name: The Annette Strauss Institute For Civic Life
summary: The Annette Strauss Institute for Civic Life exists to cultivate informed voters and active citizens. We do so through research, education, and outreach programs focused on three key pillars: civic discovery, young people, and civil dialogue.

name: Moody College Honors Student Council
description:
Add our Events Google Calendar to stay up to date! 

 

Video: About the Moody College Honors Program
summary: We are a student led organization elected by members of the Moody College Honors Program. We create a forum for students to have input in the program's efforts. This includes but is not limited to academic, service, and social event opportunities.

name: Austin Life Church Students Chapter
summary: We are a group of students seeking to grow in or learn more about Christian faith; many of us attend Austin Life Church. Our role is to unite the members of the church outside of Sunday services, and to form a Christian community on this college campus.

name: University Orchestra Student Association
description: We are a group of students in the UT University Orchestra that lead our orchestra by hosting socials, concerts, and outreach events for our musicians and community.
summary: We are a group of students in the UT University Orchestra that lead our orchestra by hosting socials, concerts, and outreach events for our musicians and community.

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority Inc. Mu Upsilon
description: It is the mission of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. to enhance the quality of life for women and their families in the U.S. and globally through community service, civil, and social action. Our goal is to achieve greater progress in the areas of education, health awareness, and leadership development.Our members, affiliates, staff and community partners work to create and support initiatives that align with our vision.
summary: To enhance the quality of life for women and their families through community service, civil and social action. Our goal is to achieve greater progress in the areas of education, health awareness,& leadership development.

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity Incorporated - Mu Rho Chapter
summary: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity was founded at Howard University in Washington, D.C., January 9, 1914. The founders wanted to organize a Greek letter fraternity that would truly exemplify the ideals of brotherhood, scholarship and service.

name: Texas Energy Trading
description:
Texas Energy Trading provides a platform for students to learn and engage in stock and commodities trading. We seek to create an environment where students can share ideas, network with each other, and gain knowledge and skills related to trading in the stock and commodities markets.

Through regular meetings and events, we aim to provide members with opportunities to learn about different trading strategies, market trends, and financial analysis. We also strive to connect members with industry professionals and provide them with resources to help them pursue careers in finance, trading, and related fields.
summary: Texas Energy Trading is a student organization focused on providing useful and meaningful education to other students about commodity trading and stock options through hands-on experience and group discussions

name: Hearts for the Homeless Austin
description:
Hearts for the Homeless (H4H) Austin serves to provide The University of Texas at Austin students with a unique volunteering experience. Its mission aims to enlighten and impassion students that volunteer their help and skills for a greater cause. Furthermore, it works to help the homeless community in Austin, TX by providing free blood pressure screenings and creating caring interactions between volunteers, individuals experiencing homelessness, and all of those in the community. Hearts for the Homeless Austin plans to work with other non-profit organizations, veterans’ organizations, health care clinics, and medical professionals to enhance the effectiveness of this registered student organization. This organization aspires to help shape and mold the minds of our members into knowledgeable, compassionate leaders.

Please check out our website for more information!
summary: Hearts for the Homeless Austin offers to contribute to the health and well-being of one of the most vulnerable populations in our nation through free blood pressure & blood sugar screenings, education, and outreach opportunities.

name: StudentsCare at University of Texas at Austin
summary: StudentsCare is an organization that will set up a hospital buddy system between undergraduate students at UT Austin and long-term pediatric patients at Dell Children's and Parson's House. We strive to bring companionship to patients and their families

name: Texas Consulting
description: Texas Consulting is a student-run management consulting organization focused on advising and working on pro-bono projects for Fortune 500s and global non-profits. We focus on providing our members with hands-on experiences and providing strategic solutions to vital business challenges encountered by our clients.
summary: Texas Consulting is a student-run management consulting organization focused on advising and working on pro-bono projects for Fortune 500s, global non-profits, and venture backed startups. We focus on providing our members with hands-on experiences.

name: Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy at UT Austin
description: AMCP is a national professional society dedicated to the practice of pharmacy in managed care. Its goal is to lead the way in helping patients get the medication they need at a cost they can afford while promoting the development and application of pharmaceutical care.
summary: AMCP is a national professional society dedicated to the practice of pharmacy in managed health environment and to promote the development and application of pharmaceutical care.

name: Texas Talaash
description: Texas Talaash aims to display a mixture of South Asian and Contemporary culture through dance performances and at UT events. Talaash 7.0 hopes to continue growing through learning new styles from multiple cultures, and having fun while competing to perform a thrilling and exhilarating set.
summary: Texas Talaash is UT Austin's premier Bollywood fusion dance team. Founded in 2019, Talaash aims to display a mixture of South Asian and Contemporary culture through dance performances across the nation and at UT events.

name: Association of Black Computer Scientists
description: A student-led organization that provides community, mentorship, networking, and opportunities for underrepresented students seeking educational and professional success in Computer Science.
summary: A student-led organization that provides community, mentorship, networking, and opportunities for underrepresented students seeking educational and professional success in Computer Science.

name: Texas Undergraduate Moot Court Team
description:
Texas Undergraduate Moot Court Team gives students a way to develop and practice moot court skills that can be used in all facets of life. Moot Court Team membership provides students of the University of Texas at Austin community exposure to the workings of appellate courts. Please contact DJ Wu (djwu@utexas.edu) or reach out through email if interested (texasmoot@gmail.com)!

 

What is Moot Court? -> read this article

 

Competition season for the team is in the Fall semester. Recruiting for traveling team members and general members is done in the Spring semester.

 

To learn more about the team and our current activities, check out our Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/TexasUMCT

Or our website: https://texasmootcourt.wixsite.com/tumct-1
summary: The purpose of TUMCT is to give students a way to develop and practice moot court skills that can be used in all facets of life in addition to providing members of the University of Texas at Austin community exposure to the appellate courts.

name: Texas Folklorico Dance Company
summary: Texas Folklorico Dance Company is a student founded organization at the University of Texas at Austin that seeks to educate, preserve and promote Mexican traditions/culture through dance. As well as encourage the community to delve into new experiences.

name: Texas Women in Economics
description:
Texas Women in Economics is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community that unites undergraduate women and allies within the Department of Economics. Our mission is to enhance their academic journey, establish a supportive network for women pursuing economics studies, and offer valuable opportunities for professional development.

Join our Slack!
summary: Texas Women in Economics is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community that unites undergraduate women and allies within the Department of Economics.

name: Health Innovation Fellows
description:
The purpose of the Health Innovation Fellows is to promote the business school as a place that produces leaders with the capability to impact the health care industry through innovation and leadership. When people think of innovation and healthcare, we want them to think innovation + healthcare = McCombs.

The Health Innovation Fellows provide an avenue for students to experience in-depth health care programming to dissect and debate the many complexities of the U.S. health care system beyond the classroom.
summary: The purpose of the Health Innovation Fellows is to promote the McCombs School of Business as a place that produces leaders with the capability to impact the health care industry through innovation and leadership.

name: Students Fighting Climate Change
description:
• Students Fighting Climate Change strives to create an inclusive, positive community for climate action to give people hope and humanize one another as we face the climate crisis. We aim to embrace the intersectionality of other movements to make the world a more equitable and just world for all.

  • Our organization provides a platform for members to learn about, politically engage in, and become activists for climate justice.

    We demand that UT (1) publicly acknowledge the climate crisis, (2) accelerate the renewable energy transition on University Lands, (3) strategically divest from fossil fuels, (4) incrementally reinvest in non-fossil fuel-related investments, (5) reenvision the Carl J. Eckhardt Natural Gas Power Plant on campus, (6) set a goal to achieve carbon neutrality by 2040, and (7) form a democratic climate action committee to oversee the proper carrying out of these demands.

    Goals

    1. Educate and inform UT Austin stakeholders about the university’s contribution to climate change
    2. Mobilize UT Austin stakeholders to challenge the university’s contribution to climate change
    3. Foster an inclusive community of people who are concerned with human and planetary health
    4. Build connections with other groups and organizations that have similar goals

If you want to get involved, please follow us on Instagram, fill out our Interest Form in the Linktree in our bio, and join our GroupMe.
summary: SFCC strives to raise awareness in our community on the climate crisis in an interdisciplinary way, and build relationships with other organizations on campus and in Austin to uplift each other and empower all of us to be better climate activists.

name: Journalism Graduate Student Council
summary: Journalism Graduate Student Council serves as a liaison between students and faculty within the School of Journalism and Media. We represent student voices and offer a space to share concerns as well as to foster connection and professional development.

name: Longhorn Investment Team
summary:
We build more than resumes. Longhorn Investment Team is redefining the way students learn about business with an integrative approach that spans beyond the typical investment team.

We welcome students from all majors and all disciplines.

name: Latinx Theatre Initiative at UT Austin
description:
LTI at UT Austin Mission Statement:

The Latinx Theatre Initiative is a group of students, regardless of race or ethnicity,  that gather together in order to promote Latino and Hispanic art theater pieces or plays in the University of Texas at Austin.

 

We believe that our culture is an essential part of our history, and history can be reminisced throughout the arts. Looking forward to have more Latino theater plays and performance works, we can get to know better who we were and who we are, in order to know who will be become.

 

Besides performing works that share our stories, we look to build community with other students at the University and with the greater Austin area. Different from other theatre traditions, our organizational structures highlight an equitative and shared collaboration amongst our members. We believe that our leaders should inspire and support the group to reach both high artistic standards and communal values outlined below.
summary: The Latinx Theatre Initiative is a group of students, regardless of race or ethnicity,  that gather together in order to promote Latino and Hispanic art theater pieces or plays in the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Sustainability Investment Group
description:
What is SIG?

The Sustainability Investment Group is the premier sustainability and finance group at UT. SIG offers students the opportunity to become leaders in social impact, environmental action, and community investment through their one of a kind curriculum. SIG operates an Austin based impact investing group that injects in local businesses and startups, as well as a public securities based portfolio. Both of these are managed by students and alumni advisors. 

How to join.

All our positions are open to any and all majors. Our fellowship is open to freshman and is a unique experience that teaches you about how sustainability functions in business. Fellows also get the opportunity to interact and work with alumni advisors and sponsors, as well as lead SIG's research group. Our analyst role is open to sophomores and older, and is an opportunity to learn the intricacies of fund management and sustainable finance. Our application will be available in August and will launch on our website and Instagram. 

Alumni & other opportunities. 

In addition to our undergraduate members, we have alumni and advisory board members that interact with our members online and in-person. Currently, we have active alumni at Greentech Capital Advisors, Bain Capital, The Bridgespan Group, Boston Consulting Group, Bain & Co., J.P. Morgan, Morgan Stanley, the United Nations, and more! 

If you have any questions, feel free to join us at one of our info sessions or email us at the address below!
summary: The Sustainability Investment Group is the premier sustainability and finance group at UT. SIG offers students the opportunity to become leaders in social impact, environmental action, and community investment through their one of a kind curriculum.

name: International Relations and Global Studies Council
description: The UT International Relations and Global Studies Council (IRG Council) aims to help students from the International Relations and Global Studies program better engage in their academic and social experience here at The University of Texas. IRG Council, a student organization, has three areas of focus: professional development, academic advancement, and social experience. The group holds weekly meetings to serve the student body of IRG majors by providing valuable panels and events, academic and career guidance, and track-related interaction. IRG Council also hosts social events, creates an avenue of peer mentorship, and coordinates opportunities for students to be a part of an IRG-specific community.
summary: UT IRGC aims to assist IRG students in professional development, academic advancement, and social experience. Through peer and faculty engagement, UT IRGC will enhance a community and consolidate resources within IRG to best serve members of the major.

name: University of Texas Sport Club Association
description:
The Sport Club program consists of sponsored student organizations that comprise the University of Texas Sport Club Association (UTSCA). Each club offers students the opportunity to pursue a higher degree of participation with a group of individuals interested in the same level and type of sport. The UTSCA promotes student participation in a wide variety of physical and athletic activities, contributes to the development of student leadership and provides a bond between individual clubs.

Each club is formed, developed, governed and administered by the student membership of the club working in conjunction with Recreational Sports. While RecSports assists in the development and growth of each club, the emphasis of the program is on student leadership and involvement. The existence of each sport club and the UTSCA is dependent upon student interest and participation.

Click here to view the UTSCA Handbook.

Click here to view RS Internal Services.

Click here to schedule a meeting with staff.
summary: The University of Texas Sport Club Association (UTSCA) consists of sponsored student organizations with Recreational Sports. Each club is formed, developed, governed and administered by the student membership of the club working with RecSports.

name: Getting Radical In The South
description: Getting Radical in the South (GRITS) is intended to foster an environment on the University of Texas campus, specifically within Texas Law, that explores and promotes radical advocacy in the South. Each year we host a multi-day conference that brings together members of the entire University of Texas community, students throughout the South, and professionals working in advocacy spaces. Our conference consists of various speakers and workshops that are aimed at helping students around the country learn about various social justice issues affecting the South, while also equipping them with the tools and skills that will help them to become more impactful advocates.
summary: GRITS is a group housed in Texas Law that brings together the UT community to explore radical legal advocacy in the South. The primary focus is community lawyering, meaning that we partner with social justice advocates working outside of the law.

name: Q++ (Queer Students in Computer Science)
description:
Q++ (Queer Students in Computer Science) is a student-run organization at UT. Our mission is to build community and promote academic and professional success for LGBTQ+ people in tech at UT. Q++ offers professional support through our corporate events, enhances learning through our study sessions, and provides a place for students to be themselves at our socials. We're open to LGBTQ+ students and allies alike!
 
Fill out the Get Involved form on our website for an invite to our Discord, and check out our events list on texasqpp.com to keep track of upcoming events!
summary: Q++ is an organization that aims to empower and provide support for LGBTQ+ students in computer science and technology through academic, professional, and social events.

name: Black Muslims Alliance
description: Black Muslims Alliance [BMA] is intended to help the unseen feel seen. We accept all genders, races, and religions. Our primary focus is to raise the voice for Black Muslims who have felt displaced between different cultures. Of course, we welcome all who are allies and supporters. Our association aims to educate our members of black history in the Islamic world through, but not  limited to, seminars, poetry slams, and workshops. Additionally, we aim to work with other organizations to build strong partnerships. Our socials will consist of, but not limited to, intimate potlucks, halal mixers, and big/little teams. Our main purpose is to provide a group of solidarity and a safe welcome space.
summary: BMA is an all-inclusive group targeted to raise awareness, support, and resources for Black Muslims. We aim to educate our people about the history of Blacks in Islam, as well as, celebrate their successes.

name: Space Law and Policy Society at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: The Space Law and Policy Society at The University of Texas at Austin is committed to helping students learn more about space law and policy, discover opportunities to join the field, and facilitate their growth.

name: Undergraduate Korean Association
description:
Fellow UT students!

 

We are UKA (Undergraduate Korean Association) established in Fall 2019.

 

Undergraduate Korean Association promotes cultural diversity and inclusion through the diffusion of Korean culture and language.

 

We ensure the preservation and growth of the Korean American community at the University of Texas at Austin by providing cultural and professional development opportunities to those with a keen interest in Korean culture.

Please reach out to us through these methods:

- Instagram: @texasuka 

- Email: admin@texasuka.org

- Website: www.texasuka.org

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/texasuka/mycompany/
summary: Undergraduate Korean Association (UKA) provides opportunities for Korean students to form a broad network within the community through various events and projects that will enhance students’ academic, social, and career needs.

name: Texas Figure Skating
description:
TEXAS FSC offers figure skaters the opportunity to meet and train with other figure skaters at UT. The ice rink is our home away from home. We host on-ice practices, off-ice conditioning, skating-related social events, and are in the process of forming a collegiate competitive team branch. All levels are welcome--join us on ice and let's skate!

Visit our website for more information! 

32ºFSC Website!

Want to see more of what we do? Check out our Instagram or Tiktok @texas.fsc !
summary: TEXAS FSC is the premier figure skating club at the University of Texas at Austin. We are dedicated to creating a vibrant community of skaters and promoting the sport of figure skating.

name: Social Entrepreneurship Learning Lab Fellowship
description:
The Social Entrepreneurship Learning Lab (SELL) Fellowship is an intensive eight-week fellowship program for undergraduate students attending the University of Texas at Austin who want to build social impact-focused organizations to solve the BIG problems.
 
The Fellowship Program consists of:
  • Seminars. Hear and get mentorship from other changemakers working in the field to solve social issues.
  • Workshops. Lessons on design thinking, social entrepreneurship, and ideation.
  • Projects Ideation. Work together with other fellows to sketch out big ideas together and get them tested in the field!
Do you want a hands-on approach to learning problem-solving techniques and the key to building a social-impact oriented venture? Fill out our interest form to learn more!
summary: The SELL Fellowship is an intensive eight-week fellowship program for undergraduate students attending the University of Texas at Austin who want to build social impact-focused organizations to solve the BIG problems.

name: Bangladeshi Students Association at the University of Texas at Austin
description: The Bangladeshi Students Association at the University of Texas at Austin, abbreviated to BDSA, is intended to provide a community to students, faculty, and staff who would like a platform to celebrate Bangladesh, the Bangladeshi identity, and Bangladeshi culture. The objectives include, but are not limited to providing networking and socializing opportunities among those with the interests stated, providing a platform for incoming international students to ask for assistance with transition, facilitating the celebration of cultural events, and representing Bangladesh on campus.
summary: The Bangladeshi Students Association at UT Austin, (BDSA), is intended to provide a community to students, faculty, and staff who would like a platform to celebrate Bangladesh, the Bangladeshi identity, and Bangladeshi culture.

name: TX NLGJA
description: TX NLGJA is UT's student chapter of the National Association of LGBTQ+ journalists. We provide professional development in the journalism field, discuss LGBTQ+ issues in the news and host socials. Students from any background/major are welcome.
summary: NLGJA at UT Austin strives to improve coverage of LGBTQ issues and increase representation in newsrooms. Everyone is welcome to join, regardless of sexual orientation, gender, or major.

name: The Daily Texan
description: The Daily Texan is the student newspaper at The University of Texas at Austin. We produce a paper twice a week that is distributed campus-wide, and in addition, we produce content and stories for publication on our website and social channels. With over 100 staffers across 13 departments are on staff each semester. Both in print and online, the Texan is responsible for keeping the UT community informed and holding university officials accountable.
summary: The Daily Texan student paper at the University of Texas at Austin. Both in print and online, the Texan is responsible for keeping the UT community informed.

name: Texas Design, Build, Fly
description: We are an organization that designs, builds, and flies a new RC aircraft every year! We want to teach any and all students who are interested in RC aircraft about how a RC aircraft functions and how to build a RC aircraft!
summary: We are an organization that designs, builds, and flies a new RC aircraft every year! We want to teach any and all students who are interested in RC aircraft about how a RC aircraft functions and how to build a RC aircraft!

name: Pre-health & Distinguished Scholars
description: The primary mission of the Pre-health & Distinguished Scholars within Dell Medical School is to empower undergraduate students throughout their pre-health journey with professional development, community engagement and mentorship opportunities. By interacting with students within our community, we hope to facilitate the transition from high school to college-level courses.
summary: The primary mission of the Pre-health & Distinguished Scholars is to empower undergraduate students throughout their pre-health journey with professional development, community engagement and mentorship opportunities.

name: Texas Brain Exercise Initiative
description: Texas Brain Exercise Initiative is a student-led organization that focuses on preventing memory loss through brain exercise. Pioneered by neuroscientist Dr. Ryuta Kawashima, it has been found that doing simple math and reading aloud for just 30 minutes a day, 5 times a week, caused marked improvements for those with Alzheimer’s. Most showed improvements in cognitive function as tested by Mini-Mental State Examinations (MMSE) and Frontal Assessment Batteries (FAB). Many began to feel happier and improvements in communication and cognition were recorded. Drawing from this, Texas BEI works to create an inter-generational bond with seniors at various nursing homes around Austin. We are made up of over 200 student members who work directly with 10 assisted living and nursing care facilities throughout Austin. Daily packets filled with cognitively stimulating activities, including simple math, writing, and "read-alouds," are completed by each senior with the help of UT students, in one-hour sessions over 20 times per week. Not only does the Brain Exercise Initiative hope to improve the memory of those suffering from Alzheimer’s, but we also create an invaluable, all-too-rare avenue for inter-generational service, discussion, and collaboration between the seniors of Austin and the students of UT. This organization is open to all students in all majors, including graduate students displaying interest. Membership includes all volunteering privileges, general meetings, socials, and fundraising events, all of which help our members engage and contribute to the growing Austin and UT community.
summary: Texas Brain Exercise Initiative is a chapter of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that recruits volunteers to utilize specific brain exercises as an intervention to improve cognitive function in those with Alzheimer's Disease.

name: Live Design Graduates
description: The organization has been created to support the graduate students of the Live Design program within the Theatre and Dance Department.  The main function is to support the yearly portfolio event at the end of the spring semester called "Live Design Showcase," Thesis projects and student productions.  Additionally it will serve as a way to facilitate and maintain the Live Design Graduate Studio in the F.L. Winship building.
summary: Graduate Students of the Live Design program within the Theatre and Dance Department

name: Texas Product Engineering Organization
description:
Hey! We're 🤠 Texas Product Engineering Organization (TPEO). We teach students of all majors how to build modern technology products through courses over software engineering, ui/ux design, and product management and software projects that positively impact the local Austin/UT community.

TPEO recruits incoming freshmen and sophomores looking to go into UI/UX design, software engineering, and product management from all majors. 

To learn more about what TPEO is, follow us on@txproduct
summary: We are a group of students at UT Austin who are building, designing, and launching software products to solve real-world problems in our community.

name: University of Texas at Austin Police Department (UTPD)
description:
Founded in 1968, The University of Texas at Austin Police Department (UTPD) continues to uphold its mission to serve and protect. The department has the same authority and responsibilities as most municipal police organizations and has challenges unique to a large, nationally recognized university community.

UTPD employs more than 178 dedicated individuals interested in making a positive difference on campus. In keeping with its community-oriented policing philosophy, the department regularly partners with faculty members, staff members and students on crime prevention and law enforcement programs. UTPD is accredited through the Commission on Accreditation for Law Enforcement Agencies.

We are located at 2201 Robert Dedman Dr, directly east of the Darrell K Royal-Texas Memorial Stadium on the northeast corner of Clyde Littlefield and Robert Dedman Dr. Paid parking is available in the Manor Garage.
summary: Founded in 1968, The University of Texas at Austin Police Department (UTPD) continues to uphold its mission to serve and protect.

name: University Housing and Dining
description: University Housing and Dining hosts programs and events for residents and the campus community aimed at enriching the student experience on the Forty Acres. From craft nights and educational panels to black light bingo and pop-up petting zoos, our diverse range of programming is filled with fun activities, entertainment, food, giveaways and more. Event times vary – and some events will recur – so hopefully you can catch one that fits into your schedule!
summary: University Housing and Dining plans a number of fun opportunities for our residents and campus community to build connections during their time on the Forty Acres.

name: Law Students for Black Lives
description:
Through advocacy, outreach, and policy reform, Law Students for Black Lives works to eradicate racial disparities in America’s criminal justice system, amplify Black voices, and empower communities in the ongoing fight against institutional and systemic racism.

Our vision is an American society free from all forms of institutional and systemic racism, with a focus on eliminating racial discrimination in policing, education, healthcare, and housing.
summary: Law Students for Black Lives is a student-run organization dedicated to using the law to challenge and eliminate racial disparities in the criminal justice system, amplify Black voices, and empower communities in the ongoing fight against racism.

name: Graduates for Underrepresented Minorities
description:
Graduates for Underrepresented Minorities (GUM) seeks to create a supportive community among underrepresented minority graduate students and post-docs. We strive to promote professional growth and provide leadership and outreach opportunities in Austin.

Follow our Instagram @gum_ut_austin for our most up to date information!
