We also regularly send emails via the UT listserv. 

1. Go to utlists.utexas.edu/sympa
2. Click “Search for Lists” and search for “GUM
3. Click gum@utlists.utexas.edu then click “Subscribe”
summary: Graduates for Underrepresented Minorities (GUM) goal is to create a supportive community among underrepresented minority graduate students.

name: Hazing Prevention and Education
description:
In order to shift the campus culture concerning hazing, we have developed the Horns Against Hazing initiative.  Horns Against Hazing organizes awareness-raising events, develops mass communication messages, and designs workshops and presentations that both demystify hazing behaviors and promote positive culture change.

 

Initiatives include: 

Horns Against Hazing Student Advisory Council

 

National Hazing Prevention Week Event Series: 9/22-9/25, 2025

 

Alternatives to Hazing Week Event Series: Coming Spring of 2026!

 

Horns Against Hazing: Leading the Charge Workshop Series: Fall and Spring 

 

Hazing Prevention and Response Badge: Students can earn the Hazing Prevention and Response digital badge by participating in our Horns Against Hazing    workshop series. To earn the badge, students must engage in at least 4 hours (any combination of workshops) to earn the Hazing Prevention and Response badge. This badge can be posted on Linkedin or social media to show employers your dedication to building safe and healthy communities! 

 

 

 

To learn more about hazing prevention efforts across campus, visit hazing.utexas.edu.

Or Email denali.diaztorres@austin.utexas.edu
summary: The Hazing Prevention and Education strives to address hazing through a comprehensive lens and collaborative action.

name: Austin Sunshine Camps at The University of Texas at Austin
description:
Some goals of Austin Sunshine Camps at The University of Texas at Austin include: 

- Foster community among UT students with similar passions to make an impact in Austin community

- Develop student leaders that advocate for inclusive and motivated culture and community on campus

-Train UT student members to be qualified camp counselors to mentor youth of underrepresented communities in Austin OR become fundraising champions for the organization

Follow us at @asc_ut on Instagram! JOIN OUR SLACK if you are interested to stay up to date with our meetings, events, and socials! SLACK JOIN LINK
summary: Austin Sunshine Camps is the only free overnight camp for historically underserved youth. Members of ASC at UT will either serve as a camp counselor or as a fundraising champion for the nonprofit, growing as a leader and making an impact in Austin, TX.

name: BridgeTexas
description:
BridgeTexas is all about connecting students with diverse views and fostering understanding through thoughtful discussions. As a student-led organization, we're passionate about bringing together college students with different viewpoints to discuss today's pressing issues in a positive and understanding atmosphere. We aim to make politics and social issues accessible and engaging for everyone, regardless of their prior experience or knowledge.

We achieve this by hosting conversations where students can explore different perspectives, practice critical thinking, and engage with ideas that may differ from their own. We also organize larger events featuring club leaders, professors, or other notable figures who can inspire our community with thought-provoking discussions.

Our name, Bridge, symbolizes our mission: we're not here to push anyone to a particular viewpoint or to change anyone's mind, but to help everyone learn from each other's unique and diverse perspectives. Our discussions are built on three main principles:

  1. "Diversity of Ideas": We believe that engaging with a wide range of ideas helps us grow and develop our own beliefs.
  2. "Listening Communities": When someone is speaking, we listen to understand their point of view, not to argue against it. Rather than debating, BridgeTexas hosts an informal open forum.
  3. "Responsible Discourse": We focus on discussing ideas, not attacking people, and always treat each other with dignity and honesty.

Join us at BridgeTexas to be part of a welcoming community that embraces learning, empathy, and the power of respectful dialogue. Together, we can build bridges and create a more understanding and cooperative world.
summary: BridgeTexas brings together students to engage in semi-informal discussions about today's political issues. Through this, students learn the experiences from people of different backgrounds while discussing U.S. policy and campus issues.

name: Hook'd Magazine
summary: Hook'd Magazine is a student-led fashion and culture magazine aimed a giving budding creatives hands-on experience in niche creative and business spaces. We give our members a platform to gain exposure into related fields without needing prior experience

name: Girls Who Code College Loops at UT Austin
description: Girls Who Code College Loops at UT Austin is a chapter of the national Girls Who Code organization. We aim to provide all students (women, non-binary people, and male allies), particularly those with limited or no prior coding experience, the opportunity to learn skills that they may not get the chance to elsewhere. With mentoring at local Austin schools, entry-level projects, technical workshops, and community-building socials, we aim to bring together people into a community in order to learn topics like game development, web development, programming languages, machine learning, and more industry relevant topics! If you're interested, connect with is at https://linktr.ee/utgirlswhocode or our Discord at https://discord.com/invite/jWqkWQ6.
summary: Girls Who Code at UT is a community empowering students to break into tech through hands-on projects, local mentorship, and collaborative learning. We foster an inclusive space for building skills, confidence, and meaningful connections in tech.

name: Texas Sport Management Club
description: The purpose of this club is to provide students with professional development opportunities such as resume building workshops and guest speakers to inform students about the sports industry. Members will have opportunities to attend these professional development events, giving them a deeper understanding of the sports industry and allowing them to learn about potential careers in the industry. In the past, we have welcomed industry leaders from organizations such as WME, CAA, the NFL, the New Orleans Saints, Texas Athletics, The Athletic, and ESPN.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide students majoring in Sport Management and other related fields enhanced educational and professional development opportunities. Members will have opportunities to attend in-service meetings and events.

name: Texas Visual Arts Collective
description: Texas Visual Arts Collective is composed of an Art department, an Advertising department, an Events and Operations department, and a Philanthropy department. The Art department is made up of student artists that learn from each other and individually create artworks to fundraise for our chosen cause at our annual benefit show. The other departments will be responsible for organizing and promoting the benefit show and/or any other events that TVAC chooses to host. Our goal is to support social justice causes and educate members of our UT and Austin communities regarding these issues.
summary: Texas Visual Arts Collective is a student organization made up of artists and allies producing visual artwork, , to provide aid to social justice causes.

name: Kendra Scott Women's Entrepreneurial Leadership Institute Student Board
description: The KS WELI Student Board (SB) was founded to represent the needs and experiences of students at UT to the KS WELI. The SB provides feedback and ideas to ensure KS WELI programming and events are in line with the interests of students. The SB creates engaging programs focused on equipping attendees, empowering women, and building community, while providing resources and opportunities for students to connect with their passions. Working alongside KS WELI, the Student Board hopes to work towards a more diverse, female-led, and entrepreneurial future.
summary: The KS WELI Student Board represents the needs and experiences of students at UT to the Kendra Scott Women's Entrepreneurial Leadership Institute.

name: LHBlacks
description: LHBlacks is intended to; foster a community among underrepresented students affiliated with the Butler School of Music, highlight and uplift members of our community, and reach out to expand membership and recruitment within the Longhorn Band Showband of the Southwest
summary: LHBlacks is intended to; foster a community among underrepresented students affiliated with the Butler School of Music, highlight and uplift members of our community, and reach out to expand membership and recruitment within the Longhorn Band.

name: Partners in Health Engage at UT Austin
description:
Partners In Health Engage at UT Austin is a team of dedicated and motivated UT students who have a desire to aid in the fight towards healthcare equality. We believe that all individuals, no matter
where they live, have a fundamental economic, social, and cultural right to a universal standard of health. 
 
We call our Congressional representatives, circulate petitions, educate our fellow students, share resources, and raise funds - all in support of the right to health movement. We believe that together, we can build a more just and equitable world, where everyone has access to the care they need. Members can expect to learn about global health and healthcare disparities through guest speakers and curriculum. By learning we educate ourselves and learn how to best educate our student peers on on the healthcare related inequalities that exist in the world today. Join us and help build the movement.
 
How PIH Engage is Building the Right to Health Movement
summary: Partners In Health Engage at UT Austin is a team of dedicated and motivated UT students who have a desire to aid in the fight towards healthcare equality. We believe that together, we can build a more just and equitable world. Help build the movement!

name: Project Sunshine at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
Click on the world icon below this description to be taken to our chapter website for more information!

 

At Texas Project Sunshine, we are driven by a single goal; to bring smiles to the faces of pediatric patients all across the country. By partnering with local hospitals in the Austin area and the national Project Sunshine chapter, we coordinate all sorts of fun-filled events for children with medical challenges. We strive to create family-centered opportunities for joy, connection, socialization, and memory-making, thereby making the hospital a positive environment for kids and parents alike.

 

Contingent upon completing volunteer training, volunteers donate their time to create program materials and deliver programs. Our involvement has the goal of restoring a crucial sense of normalcy to the pediatric healthcare environment. Project Sunshine at the University of Texas at Austin strives to provide fun, engaging activities for children over the age of 3 years old while offering respite to their caregivers.
summary: Project Sunshine at the University of Texas at Austin intends to provide educational, social, and recreational events for children with medical challenges along with their families in the Austin area by volunteering with neighboring pediatric hospitals.

name: Readying Impactful Student Entrepreneurs
description:
Interested in entrepreneurship, education, or volunteering? Check us out! Readying Impactful Student Entrepreneurs teaches underserved high schoolers in the Austin area about entrepreneurship, financial literacy, and professional development. We help students acquire the skills necessary for a bright future.

 

Mission Statement: Readying Impactful Student Entrepreneurs empowers underserved high schoolers in Austin to achieve their academic and professional goals after graduation. We partner with local high schools to connect students with mentors from UT Austin, helping them discover their passions, develop leadership skills, and cultivate an entrepreneurial mindset. Through mentorship and learning, we prepare students for college, equip them with essential soft skills, and inspire them to create positive impact in their communities.
summary: We empower high schoolers in underserved communities through entrepreneurship, financial literacy, and professional development, equipping them with an entrepreneurial mindset to overcome challenges and create meaningful impact in their communities.

name: Longhorn Voices Book Club
description: Longhorn Voices Book Club is intended to serve as a means of building community, reading diverse books, promote recreational and creative activities, and foster discussions. It is targeted, but not restricted to, those in STEM education to serve as an entertaining break from rigorous course demands. This organization aims to make members more well-rounded by immersing them in literature and the arts. This book club focuses on reading Own Voices novels, that allow for readers to gain first-hand insight into the lives of marginalized groups in the form of fiction. Members will then attend meetings to discuss the book. The ultimate goal of Longhorn Voices Book Club is to make everyone feel included and heard.
summary: Reading and discussing entertaining #ownvoices books in an environment that aims to make everyone feel included and heard.

name: International Student Mentorship Program at UT
description: The International Student Mentorship Program at UT (“ISMP at UT”) is intended to provide guidance for international students by connecting them to mentorship opportunities, a community among international students, and resources such as helpful workshops and webinars. For example, new students can be matched with an upperclassman, graduate student, UT staff, UT alumnus and/or working professional in Austin, TX, where the mentor will aim to meet weekly for a period of a few weeks (subject to the mentee’s preferences) to help the mentee navigate through their college and graduate school experience at UT Austin. This organization is the UT Austin chapter of International Student Mentorship Program, a nonprofit Christian organization, though the group is open to members of any religious background or affiliation.
summary: International Student Mentorship Program at UT is a student organization that is passionate about guiding international students through transitioning to America, academics, and life by providing resources and mentorship for international students.

name: Longhorn Art Therapy Club
description: Members of the Longhorn Art Therapy Club experience the therapeutic benefits of art through various mediums such as painting, drawing, sculpting, collage making, LEGO Building, etc. Museum and gallery visits, hearing from local art therapists, and learning about the field of Art Therapy are other components of our Club. The Club also provides a safe place to create, be social, and relieve stress through art-making.
summary: Longhorn Art Therapy Club utilizes the therapeutic benefits of art through various mediums such as painting, drawing, sculpting, collage making, etc. Our other main objective is to learn about and bring awareness to the field of Art Therapy.

name: (un)Jaded
description:
(un)Jaded is a mental health and service organization intended to destigmatize mental health and encourage social advocacy amongst the AAPI community. (un)Jaded recognizes the need for a culturally aware space that is committed to celebrating Asian identities, addressing intersectional issues, and promoting greater community involvement. Membership is open to self-identified Asians, Pacific Islanders, Asian Americans, and allies attending the University of Texas at Austin.

(un)Jaded will advocate for and educate on its values of...

  • Mental Health
  • Social Advocacy
  • Community Outreach

2025 Membership Update: If you're interested in joining, please visit @unjadedatx on Instagram to learn more! Sign up to join our mailing list, and we'll keep you updated once Recruitment begins. Please do NOT request to join the org through Hornslink.
summary: (un)Jaded is a mental health and service organization intended to destigmatize mental health and encourage social advocacy amongst the AAPI community and its allies.

name: The E. Lee Walker Fellowship at Texas
description: The E. Lee Walker Fellowship at Texas exists to cultivate conscientious and persistent student action in the pursuit of community engagement. Addressing systemic social issues requires an enduring commitment and a deep intimacy with the affected community. Unfortunately, college students often operate on short, semester-long timelines which usually prohibit meaningful community engagement. The E. Lee Walker Fellowship at Texas breaks down this barrier by facilitating long-term student initiatives that are supported by and amplify community mentors and leaders. The Fellowship achieves this goal through a multistage process that emphasizes design thinking, critical theory, and mentorship. The three phases of the process are Education, Application, and Implementation.
summary: The E. Lee Walker Fellowship at Texas exists to cultivate conscientious and persistent student action in the pursuit of community engagement.

name: New Works Student Council
description:
The New Works Student Council produces and supervises the Cohen New Works Festival (NWF). The NWF is a biennial, week-long showcase of new work created by students at The University of Texas at Austin held in various locations in and around the UT campus. It is not just an event, but a celebration of a continuously ongoing process–the creation of new work.

Our Vision The Cohen New Works Festival, produced biennially, provides a space for artists to take risks, builds bridges between the educational and professional communities, and cultivates a diverse artistic community for students. In the spirit of playwright and professor David Mark Cohen, the festival celebrates and challenges the meaning of “new” in order to encourage the personal growth and empowerment of the next generation of art-makers and leaders. 

Our Values 

● Collaboration: The Cohen New Works Festival encourages new creative partnerships across many disciplines and builds relationships across the Department of Theatre and Dance, the College of Fine Arts, and the University of Texas at Austin. 

● Growth: The Cohen New Works Festival seeks to create a more robust festival experience by engaging our audiences through inventive new experiential formats and by planning for the expansion of future festivals. 

● Equity: The Cohen New Works Festival aims to center the collective power and experiential knowledge that already exists in our UT arts community by affirming our commitment to the principles of diversity and inclusion. 

● Risk: The Cohen New Works Festival supports artists’ creative autonomy and enables them to pursue artistic risk through clear, timely communication and the effective development and distribution of resources. 

● Discourse: The Cohen New Works Festival creates new avenues for conversation and critical inquiry within our community and engages the national artistic community through partnerships with guest artists. 

The Cohen New Works Festival was named in honor of David Mark Cohen in 2001, former head of playwriting for The University of Texas at Austin Department of Theatre and Dance. Cohen was killed in a car crash on December 23, 1997, but during his life, he was an adamant supporter of new work. In his honor, the Cohen New Works Festival continues to explore the endless possibilities of devised and collaborative new work.

Since 2001, the Cohen New Works Festival has supported and showcased over 400+ student-generated projects, developed the artistic community for 15+ years by igniting student-generated work, financially supported student projects over the summer with our Go! The grant program, and have hosted over 17,000+ attendees in the previous festivals.  

Today, the Festival has evolved to include not just plays, but all mediums of student-generated work. It is the largest festival of its kind, run and organized entirely by a committee of graduate and undergraduate students with the support of faculty co-producers.
summary: The New Works Student Council produces and supervises the Cohen New Works Festival, which is a biennial, week-long festival that is student-run, student-led, and showcases New works of students at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Greek Meds
description:
The Greek Meds organization stands to provide support to Greek life individuals looking for a community of like-minded scholars who aim to join the medical field. This organization acts as a catalyst for these goals through providing philanthropic, scholarship, and leadership based opportunities to all of its members. 

 

MEMBERSHIP FORM: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfNk2DExTu3AcfUEtO-P-AUXdVUavrkefQuMVMD0JMsGAHZTQ/viewform?fbclid=PAZXh0bgNhZW0CMTEAAafH6Z_nL6ncEDAyltGeUQIUoo50sz2TvlctXryj90cxWA3i5d2YtMe5MqCLBQ_aem_tOeP-jIudCNWLuviHIA6LA
summary: An organization at the University of Texas composed of sorority and fraternity students with the common ultimate goal of joining the medical field.

name: Admissions Welcome Center
description:
At the Welcome Center

Your Longhorn journey starts the second you enter. Check out a vibrant mural by Austin artist Judy Paul. See the Vo Family Auditorium, established by global businessman and proud Texas Ex Andrew Phong Vo, who founded the Texas Iron Spikes. Explore the university’s history, long-standing Longhorn traditions and what makes each of our colleges and schools unique through the plaques, timelines and memorabilia displays.

 

Whether you’re going on a campus tour, turning in a document for your application or visiting for an appointment with an admission counselor, your experience of the Forty Acres starts here.

 

The Admissions Welcome Center is also home to our Texas Tour Guides. If you are interested in learning more about Texas Tour Guides or applying you can join our interest form here.

 

Our application for Summer 2022 tour guides is now live! You are able to apply to become a tour guide here [URL: https://utexas.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_71KJjPegFwqYmAm].
summary: The Admissions Welcome Center serves as the front door to the university for prospective students and their families. Located on the ground floor of the Perry-Castañeda Library in the heart of campus.

name: Engineering and Computational Learning of Artificial Intelligence in Robotics
description: ECLAIR is dedicated to helping all robot-loving and passionate AI students transform their unique ideas into reality. We provide the robotics parts, membership, training, and community to give students everything they need to succeed. Members will learn about many aspects of robotics and AI and become a part of one of the most open-minded and supportive groups on campus. We believe robotics should be accessible to everyone, so any major can join, any skill level can join, and you're never expected to pay a single cent of your own money. If you have a cool robotics project you want to build, come join us! You can apply to lead a project every semester, and we'll cover every cost you might incur along the way. You can join our Discord here!
summary: Engineering and Computational Learning of Artificial Intelligence in Robotics (ECLAIR) is a CS organization dedicated to helping students of all backgrounds transform their robotics and AI ideas into reality through student-led projects funded by ECLAIR

name: Longhorn Comic Books
description:
Do you enjoy Marvel, DC, or any other comic related movies/shows? But have no idea where to start with the actual comics?
-

Then this club is for you! We want to introduce members to new titles from Marvel, DC, as well as independent publishers! Meet fellow comic newbies and explore different genres within the medium!
-

Our general meetings are on every other Thursday @ 7PM! We take votes and suggestions at our regular meetings on what to read next!
-

This club is primarily for American comics, but some members also read manga :)

-

Please join! We have a mix of novice and expert members so don’t be shy! Can’t wait to meet you!
-

Join our discord to learn more: discord.com/invite/3k5sRDbMfR
summary: A club for discussing all types of American comic books! These include titles from Marvel, DC, and independent publishers! Meet and socialize with fellow enthusiasts who are trying to explore the world of comics!

name: Music Appreciation Club at UT
description:
The Music Appreciation Club at (UT MAC) is a organization which provides a platform for people to diversify their tastes in music and to also get some insight into the culture, background, or the music itself. Members will listen to a weekly album selected through voting and come together to discuss both the album and the history behind it. Other activities will be general listening parties, members presentation on an artist or genre history, etc.

The discord linked below is the quickest way to access anything related to MAC. We also have an Instagram linked as well!

https://discord.gg/bvTgbgN7Xw
summary: This is an organization which provides a platform for people to diversify their tastes in music and to also get some insight into the culture, background, or the music itself.

name: Kontrol Crew Texas
description:
Do you love to dance? Do you love K-Pop? Looking to find a team here at UT? 

Well you've come to the right place. WE ARE KTX!

KTX is a cover/performance based group that specializes in K-Pop and Urban dance. We take all levels of dancers and help you continue to grow in your dance journey. We pride ourselves in creating a space that is safe and inclusive for all dancers. Come join us as we continue to Kontrol The Stage!
summary: KTX is a performance and competition-based Urban and Korean Pop dance organization that aims toward the advancement of our member's skills as well as aiding to connect all lovers of dance and performance.

name: Texas Robotics Graduate Students
description:
We aim to foster community among graduate students and connect students to opportunities (tours of robotics companies, speaker series, socials, and more).

To join our email list, head here and search for TRGS and press subscribe.
summary: A student org to promote community, opportunities, and professional development for graduate students in Texas Robotics.

name: TX MedstART
description:
TX MedstART is an organization devoted to bridging the gap between traditional healthcare delivery/service and the arts. We believe that the fine arts can be used to enhance wellness and one's quality of life. We follow this mission by volunteering and collaborating with various nonprofits, hosting speakers to educate us, and informing the public on the way art can be integrated with wellness. We are also committed to establishing a close-knit community of individuals interested in both the arts and/or health professions. 

We are accepting members for Spring 2026! Please fill out this interest form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScYgehjegwiuib-Q_ZYJM2NIai1KGreRj0biZzgRfCrsTa5Sg/viewform?pli=1 https://forms.gle/MFoubQdFsg92DZBR9

Join our slack!: https://join.slack.com/t/txmedstart/shared_invite/zt-3r6wnyo2c-WP3lft1h0c~Yl5FMXb2A5g
summary: TX MedstART is a community of philanthropists who aim to use our creativity to promote wellness and uplift artists. Through this organization, we hope to bridge the gap between arts and healthcare via community projects, fundraising events, and art!

name: Longhorn Furries
description:
We are a student group for people who are interested in art and costumes of anthropomorphic animals (commonly referred to as "furries"). Our main purpose is to be a social group where people can express themselves and make new friends. Our primary function is a space where people can share, create, and participate in furry media. We host various social events throughout the year such as twice-weekly meetings, holiday events, bowling nights, video game events, a yearly zine of member's art, photography, and writing, and more!

Discord: https://discord.gg/cVuf4jhE96
Other Socials: https://longhorn-furries.carrd.co/
summary: We are a student group for people who are interested in art and costumes of anthropomorphic animals (commonly referred to as"furries"). We are looking to host social events around campus.

name: The Dell Medical School Student Senate
description: The purpose of the Dell Medical Student Senate is to facilitate effective representation of our diverse interests, foster a vibrant, health-conscious school culture, and empower future physician leaders in their personal, professionaland academic development
summary: Facilitate representation of our diverse interests, foster a vibrant, health-conscious school culture, and empower future physician leaders in their personal, professional, and academic development.

name: Title IX Office
description:
The Title IX Office seeks to provide leadership pertaining to the Title IX regulations, state laws, and University policies, as well as develop and implement best practices for prevention, training, resources, and support services at The University of Texas at Austin.

The Title IX Office offers training and professional development opportunities for all UT students. Workshops are offered both virtually and in-person. Our workshops are tailored to the needs of each group and offer an engaging space to learn about policies, process, and prevention:

Title IX Basics
An overview of Title IX, SB 212 (Texas Education Code 51.252), and UT Austin’s HOP 3-3031 policy. This course covers reporting responsibilities, support strategies, and available resources. Customizable for students, staff, and faculty.

Trauma-Informed Care
This workshop explores how trauma affects the body and mind, and offers best practices for supporting a trauma-informed campus. 

Supportive Measures Workshop
Designed for employees, this session covers how to support students involved in Title IX cases.

Are We Okay? Communicating Boundaries
An interactive workshop on setting and respecting personal boundaries. Participants learn to recognize and navigate communication challenges. 

Title IX and Retaliation
Explores how to recognize and address retaliation under Title IX in both academic and workplace settings, promoting a safe environment.

In Her Shoes
An immersive experience educating participants on domestic violence through the lens of survivor stories. Groups make real-time decisions based on scenarios, fostering empathy and advocacy for survivors. 

You can request a workshop at anytime!
summary: Provide leadership pertaining to the Title IX regulations, state laws, and University policies, as well as develop and implement best practices for prevention, training, resources, and support services at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Middle Eastern and North African Law Students Association at the University of Texas at Austin
description: Open to all students, the Middle Eastern and North African Law Students Association at the University of Texas at Austin creates a community of students, provides networking and educational opportunities, and addresses the interests and concerns of the MENA community.
summary: Open to all students, the Texas Middle Eastern and North African Law Students' Association creates a community of students, provides networking and educational opportunities, and addresses the interests and concerns of the MENA community.

name: Texas Swifties
description: Texas Swifties is dedicated to enjoying and discussing Taylor Swift, her music, and everything in between through meetings and events. We bring people who may not know each other together to talk about the thing we all have in common: our love of Taylor Swift. Meetings center around discussing the singer's songs, albums, and conspiracy theories while having fun in a safe environment. Events that we may hold outside of meeting include but aren't limited to: movie nights, listening parties, trivia/gameshow nights, and much more.
summary: Texas Swifties is dedicated to enjoying and discussing Taylor Swift, her music, and everything in between! We bring people together through our mutual love of the music industry, Taylor herself.

name: Because Inquiry Propels Our Curiosity
description:
The mission of BIPOC is to increase the diversity of BIPOC leaders in science as there is a consistent lack of BIPOC representation in research. Our promotion of BIPOC inclusivity in research will occur through the avenues of networking, mentorship, research opportunities, and so much more! If you are interested in research and want to support our mission, then join our community! Meetings in WCL Balcony Room 3.106, every other Monday, 5-6.

 

Join our Slack to hear about important events and announcements!

https://join.slack.com/t/bipocresearch-9ue8375/shared_invite/zt-1vex1ep9s-6w8LDVIHeYJipTp_GWqZwg  

 

Click on the buttons below to access to our shared google calendar and Instagram where we post about our events!
summary: Building Inclusive Leadership in Science Together!

name: Texas Undergraduate Sales Organization
description: The Texas Undergraduate Sales Organization (TUSO), a McCombs Student Organization at UT Austin, is dedicated to cultivating a community of students passionate about professional sales and business. We are committed to supporting our members in achieving their career aspirations by offering valuable learning experiences and fostering personal and professional growth.
summary: Texas Undergraduate Sales Organization (TUSO) seeks to build a community of students interested in professional sales and business. It strives to support students in their career goals and provide opportunities for learning and growth.

name: Texas Momentum
description:
Texas Momentum enables the next generation of entrepreneurs to build and scale groundbreaking products. 

Hacktimes - Thursdays 6-8pm

Demo Day - April 17th 6-8pm -  Final pitches featuring Momentum's startups, prominent VCs, VC-backed startups looking to hire. 

  1. Equipping student founders with the talent and resources they need to accelerate their startup growth and development.
  2. Providing students with hands-on experience through impactful projects and work at startups.
  3. Fostering a community of creatives, innovators, and builders who are going to change the world.
summary: Momentum provides semester-long internships to talented UT students at top quality startups. Momentum's student-founded companies have collectively raised over $1M.

name: Texas Sigma Alpha Omega
description: Texas SAO is a Christian social sorority. We are rooted in Christ and in sisterhood!  We encourage one another to grow in love through life in Christ.  Some of the ways in which we fellowship include Bible studies, social activities, retreats, and service projects. Texas Sigma Alpha Omega is a great way to get involved and enjoy college life while remaining true to your identity as a daughter of the King!
summary: Texas SAO is a non-denominational Christian social sorority that is rooted in Christ and sisterhood. We encourage each other to grow in the Lord and in the Word while holding one another accountable through friendship!

name: Longhorn Riichi
description:
Longhorn Riichi is an organization centered around the competitive tabletop game Riichi (Japanese Mahjong). Riichi offers a dynamic blend of psychological and statistical strategies. Its fusion of skill and chance contributes to its global appeal, serving as the focal point in various media like anime and games.

Longhorn Riichi hosts regular Riichi-focused events, including game sessions, strategy discussions, and tournaments. Our mission extends beyond hosting events -- we aim to promote and popularize Riichi in the West and connect our members with the broader Riichi community, including introducing our members to statewide leagues and tournaments.

Membership requires no fee or prior experience; we are happy to teach you at our meetups! Members can elect to support the club with a paid membership and receive perks like a choice of club T-shirt, eligibility for leaderboard prizes, etc.

Please join our Discord server, our primary hub of information and discussion.
summary: We center on Riichi, a competitive mahjong variant fostering psychological and statistical strategies. Alongside game sessions, we hold strategy talks and join tournaments. Come to us to learn and play Riichi for free!

name: Acacia Fraternity at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
The motto of Acacia is Human Service. What that means is that we encourage every member from pledge to active to try and make a difference in the community. We welcome new ideas, events, and plans whether that be helping at the food bank, raising money for charities, or hosting toy drives. We value the interests of our members and try to come together as a unified group to help make change. That said,  aside from what we do in the community, we also try and build lifelong friendships and brotherhood. Whether that be watching a football game, playing pool, or hitting up Top Golf, we put just as much into our members as we do the community. 

So if you can see yourself as a future Acacian or have any questions fill out our interest form below!
summary: Acacia is a men's fraternity that aims to change the stereotypes placed on fraternities. Our motto: Human Service, embodies  who we are and what we do. We place an emphasis on  helping the community while also focusing on creating a lifelong brotherhood.

name: Reformed University Fellowship International at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
Reformed University Fellowship International (RUF-I) is intended to welcome international students and scholars to the university campus, city, and country through welcome events and exploration of culture and customs within a community.

During the semester, we regularly hold dinner and social events designed for international students from any religious/cultural background, where we provide FREE dinner followed by activities intended for community building. As a Christian ministry, we also have recurring Bible studies for those who are interested. Also, we have welcoming events throughout the semester, where students engage in social activities and get to know each other's culture and customs. Past welcoming events include camping, international potluck, Easter egg coloring, and ice skating.
summary: Reformed University Fellowship International (RUF-I) is intended to welcome international students and scholars to the university campus, city, and country through welcome events and exploration of culture and customs within a community.

name: Atidna at the University of Texas
description:
Atidna at the University of Texas seeks to provide Jews/Arabs and Israelis/Palestinians at UT the ability to interact with one-another to build a bond as cousins rather than enemies. The group will provide informative lectures based on shared cultural/historical elements between the two groups, social volunteer based events in which Jews and Arabs can seek to solidify their bond through community service, and roundtable discussions between members on a given subject relevant to progressing the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

ABC Nightline Feature Story

 

Click Here to Join Our Discord
summary: Atidna at UT (a combination of the Hebrew word Atid (future) and the Arabic word na (our) to mean "our future") is dedicated to unifying Jews/Arabs at UT to promote a shared culture/history and to provide roundtable discussions between the two groups

name: Asian American Journalists Association at UT
description:
The Asian American Journalists Association at the University of Texas at Austin aims to advance diversity in newsrooms and ensure fair and accurate coverage of communities of color by providing a community for journalists of color at UT and support for AAPI student journalists and students from all backgrounds.

In building a community, our student organization holds several types of events, including but not limited to: general meetings, where we introduce topics and meet members, guest speaker events, where we invite outside speakers to come talk to our organization, workshops, where we both teach and work together with our members on journalism and other topics, and socials, where we build community through friendship and fun events.

AAJA at UT is also focused on ensuring fair coverage of communities of color, which occurs during discussions of recent events at all of our meetings and through the input toward the coverage of AAPI communities at local news outlets, including but not limited to the Daily Texan and its Diversity and Inclusion Board.
summary: The Asian American Journalists Association at UT aims to advance diversity in newsrooms by providing a community for journalists of color at UT and support for AAPI student journalists and students from all backgrounds.

name: Network for Collaborative Oncology Development and Advancement
description:
NCODA Professional Student Organization Chapters are dedicated to enhancing students’
understanding of oncology by:
● Raising awareness of the roles that oncology pharmacists play in medically integrated
dispensing (MID) organizations
● Working with NCODA and local pharmacy school faculty/administration to promote
awareness to industry-focused internship and other opportunities while complying with the university’s solicitation and co-sponsorship policies
● Creating networking opportunities with clinical and industry professionals as well as
NCODA team members
● Creating new educational materials that will help cancer patients nationwide
● Participating in community service events through NCODA-led initiatives/partnerships
● Offering students the opportunity to participate at NCODA international meetings and
present research
● Facilitating site-visits and shadowing opportunities at membership practices
summary:
NCODA Professional Student Organization Chapters are dedicated to enhancing students’
understanding of oncology and providing resources to help students advance their careers within pharmacy.

name: Law Students for Life at The University of Texas at Austin
description: Law Students for Life at The University of Texas at Austin is an organization of law students passionate about the protection and inherent value of innocent human life from conception until natural death. By hosting on-campus speaker events, we seek not only to offer a pro-life perspective on current legal and ethical debates but also to foster civil discourse about timely issues. We are committed to serving the vulnerable in our community, and we facilitate practical volunteer opportunities to help our members engage in the pro-life movement.
summary: Law Students for Life at The University of Texas at Austin is an organization of law students passionate about the protection and inherent value of innocent human life from conception until natural death.

name: CliftonStrengths for Students
summary: CliftonStrengths for Students is an educational program that uses Gallup's world-renowned CliftonStrengths assessment to measure 34 research-validated talent themes and provides resources tailored to students to develop their talents into strengths.

name: National Retail Federation Student Association at UT Austin
description: The National Retail Federation (NRF) Foundation Student Association is a national organization that connects college students interested in retail careers, such as merchandising, buying, marketing, and product development, with industry professionals and opportunities. It offers networking events, scholarships, career resources, and access to the annual NRF Foundation Student Program in New York City, where students meet top retail companies and leaders. As part of this national organization, our goal is to bring these opportunities to The University of Texas at Austin, helping students explore and discover what a career in retail could mean for them.
summary: The National Retail Federation Student Association at UT Austin aims to provide students interested in careers in the retail industry with unique educational programs, networking and scholarship opportunities, and access to the latest industry research.

name: Deaf Culture Alliance at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
Deaf Culture Alliance's mission is to bring together Deaf students, Hard of Hearing students, and students with a passion for ASL and Deaf Culture. We host ASL socials, work on projects together, have discussions about Deaf Rights and education, and work on making positive changes in the community. We strive to be both a professional and social space for students to come together and share their common passions. Going beyond personal interests, Deaf Culture Alliance's mission is to create a more equitable and accessible campus for minority students.

 

Add our SOCIAL CALENDAR to stay up-to-date!
summary: DCA's mission is to bring together Deaf students, HoH students, and students with a passion for ASL/Deaf Culture. We work on projects together, have discussions about Deaf Rights and education, and work on making positive changes in the community.

name: Crochet 4 a Cause
description: Crocheters 4 a Cause is a philanthropy based crochet circle dedicated to giving back. Our mission is to enhance members crochet skills while producing products and garments for charity. Members will be expected to attend all general meetings and contribute to the donations to our chosen charity. Members will also be encouraged to attend our group socials and crochet workshops to enhance learn different crochet techniques, enhance their skills, and social with their fellow members.
summary: Crocheters 4 a Cause is a philanthropy based crochet circle. The goal of this organization is to enhance members crochet skills while producing products and garments for charity. Members will be expected to attend meetings and contribute to the donation.

name: Texas Immersive Institute
description:
Texas Immersive Institute (TXI) is a program within the Moody College of Communication at the University of Texas at Austin designed to create responsible, responsive, and remarkable solutions through the power of immersive storytelling. We empower artists, scientists, and humanists (aka Explorers) with access to cutting-edge technology and research to design experiences that address significant challenges in today’s world.

 

Connect: We build community.

Throughout the year, TXI hosts innovative events for the UT and Austin community. Explorer-in-Residence (Fall semester) invites a prominent figure from the immersive media and technology community to engage with the UT campus for a week. This esteemed guest sparks lively discussions with students and faculty, enriches classroom experiences as a guest lecturer, and culminates as the keynote speaker at TXI’s Explorer-in-Residence Night. The Social Impact BuildFest (Spring semester) is a multi-day event that welcomes students from all majors to collaborate on creating novel solutions for social issues in our community. This event is open to everyone, regardless of their technical background. The TXI Immersive Showcase marks the culmination of the academic year and the TXI’s course sequence. Students in the advanced class produce this immersive experience which is open to the Austin community.

 

Play: We play to experiment.

Play is the heart of TXI. We invite and encourage people to visit the Texas Immersive Lab, located on the 5th floor of the Jesse H. Jones Communication Center. Our lab is open to all UT students and faculty who want to explore, play, and learn using the latest Extended Realty (XR) technologies and products. It is a safe and welcoming environment that aims to break down the hesitations one may have regarding these new tools and technologies.

We hold free workshops and open lab/demo nights during the school year for anyone who wants to improve or practice their digital literacy skills. Visit us soon!

 

Learn: We learn to innovate.

Texas Immersive is an innovative research institute that expands teaching and learning beyond the physical and time-bound, traditional format. We offer unique courses that specialize in the immersive field to teach students how to become experience designers, strategists, and technologists. UT students from all majors are welcome to apply!

Read more about our courses here 

 

Research: We research to discover.

TXI is a hub for scholars conducting experiential, interactive, and story-driven projects. We welcome client briefs from academia and various industries including healthcare, sustainability, education, business, entertainment, among others, to partner with us on addressing real-life problems through an immersive experience designed primarily with our audience in mind. We aim for these audiences to be inspired and feel empowered to act and help us make a change in the world.
summary: The Texas Immersive Institute aims to be a leading voice in the future of communication and media. Using a mixed-methods approach in our creative applications and research projects, we combine traditional communication methods with technology tools.

name: Delta Theta Mu
description: The purpose is to unite a diverse group of underrepresented cultures and ethnic backgrounds, create a sisterhood in which members form strong bonds, share our beliefs and morals, and help members grow as individuals.
summary: The purpose is to unite a diverse group of underrepresented cultures and ethnic backgrounds, create an organization in which members form strong bonds, share our beliefs and morals, and help members grow as individuals.

name: Dinner with 16 Longhorns
description: Dinner with 16 Longhorns is a unique event designed to bring the UT campus community together over dinner and meaningful conversation. Dinner with 16 Longhorns is an opportunity for students to explore commonalities, learn about their peers, and meet people they may not typically encounter on campus.
summary: Dinner with 16 Longhorns is an opportunity for students to explore commonalities, learn about their peers, and meet people they may not typically encounter on campus.

name: First-Year & Transfer-Year Experience
description: The First-Year Experience Office supports every single first-year student as they make The University of Texas at Austin their new home. Our office leads First-Year and Transfer Interest Groups (FiGs & TrIGs) to create a seamless transition to the Forty Acres for new Longhorns. We also coordinate Signature Courses (UGS 302 and UGS 303), the University Lecture Series, and other 360 Connections!
summary: The First-Year Experience Office supports every single first-year student as they make Our office leads First-Year and Transfer Interest Groups (FiGs & TrIGs) to create a seamless transition to the Forty Acres for new Longhorns.

name: Let's Crush Medical Debt at UT Austin
description:
Let’s Crush Medical Debt at UT Austin is dedicated to ensuring that individuals facing medical crises are not further burdened by financial hardship. Our volunteers support patients by working directly with hospital financial assistance programs, helping complete applications that can lead to partial or full relief of medical debt. In addition, members contribute through fundraising, marketing, and community outreach to expand access to these resources across Central Texas.

Beyond direct service, we engage in legislative advocacy to address the systemic causes of medical debt. Through partnerships with organizations like RAISE Texas, we participate in storybanking initiatives, track healthcare-related legislation, and advocate for policies that expand access to financial assistance and protect patients from aggressive debt collection practices.

Our mission is to mobilize undergraduate and graduate students to eliminate medical debt and help individuals navigate the complex healthcare financial system after a medical crisis. We are committed to making financial assistance free, fair, and accessible, both in Texas and nationwide.
summary: Let’s Crush Medical Debt at UT Austin is dedicated to ensuring that individuals facing medical crises are not further burdened by overwhelming financial hardship.

name: Health Law Society at The University of Texas
description:
*LAW STUDENTS ONLY*

Health Law Society at The University of Texas (THLS) is dedicated to addressing the interests and concerns of health law students at the Law School. These interests are not limited to but include healthcare law and policy, disability rights, and medical-legal partnerships in government agencies, nonprofits, hospitals, law firms, and other entities.

THLS is an academic, professional, and social law student organization. THLS is dedicated to serving health law students at The University of Texas School of Law and the health law community at large. THLS also works very closely with other student organizations in coordinating various educational and social events.
summary: Health Law Society at The University of Texas is an organization for students who may be interested in health law. We host events including academic discussions about health law and student panels on careers in the field.

name: Longhorn Bakers
description:
Longhorn Bakers is a community of student bakers welcoming individuals of all skill and experience levels. Our club (1) gives back to the local community through baking, (2) provides members the opportunity to learn baking skills and techniques, (3) provides members the opportunity to socialize and connect through baking, and (4) exposes members to diverse cultures, diets and traditions through baking. We host baking meetings, social meetings, and charitable bake sales among other forms of outreach through baking. 

Want to get involved?

Fill out our interest form: https://forms.gle/x8K4iKDzztzjinnw6

Join our Discord server: https://discord.gg/JcG3Jdv6bW

Follow us on Instagram: https://instagram.com/longhornbakers?igshid=YmMyMTA2M2Y=
summary: Longhorn Bakers is a community of student bakers of all experience levels. We are dedicated to giving back to the local community through baking, while honing our baking skills, learning about new foods, and connecting with each other.

name: UXPA Student Chapter
description:
UXPA Student Chapter helps students explore user experience through real-world projects, mentorship, and community. We offer workshops, events, and talks, connecting members with alumni to build skills, refine interests, and prepare for UX careers.

The following are the "Wings" of theUXPA Student Chapter organization that you can be a part of:

 📝 Marketing & Communications: Will work on emails (design templates too!) for all communications (internal & external), manage socials (LinkedIn, Discord, IG), and communicate with UT staff about event logistics (location, food, etc.) in collaboration with the events team.

 👨 Design: Will work on event posters (including general events and team-only socials), website design, and social media graphics.

 👋 HR & Public Relations: Will network to bring in speakers to conduct talks/workshops at UXPA @ UT Austin. This will also include maintaining consistent dialogue with student members and the local UXPA (Austin) chapter.

✨ Events: Will consolidate current students' needs, organize events, and manage the organization's calendar.
summary: UXPA Student Chapter helps students explore user experience through real-world projects, mentorship, and community. We offer workshops, events, and talks, connecting members with alumni to build skills, refine interests, and prepare for UX careers.

name: Longhorn Harm Reduction Coalition
description: Longhorn Harm Reduction Coalition is a student organization supported by the Impact Factory and UT SHIFT, helping students get involved in harm reduction efforts through education, service, and advocacy. Our goal is to help students build a network of peers where they feel educated and supported in harm reduction efforts. This includes bringing educated speakers to discuss harm reduction techniques when it comes to substance use and helping students get involved in service and advocacy opportunities.
summary: Longhorn Harm Reduction Coalition is a student organization supported by the Impact Factory and UT SHIFT, helping students get involved in harm reduction efforts through education, service, and advocacy.

name: Texas Pre-Occupational Therapy Community
description: The Texas Pre-OT Community serves as a resource for Pre-OT students when navigating their graduate school application process and a place to connect students to fulfilling shadowing and volunteering opportunities throughout the Austin community.
summary: The Texas Pre-OT Community serves as a resource for Pre-OT students when navigating their graduate school application process and a place to connect students to fulfilling shadowing and volunteering opportunities throughout the Austin community.

name: Texas Society Of Unconventional Drummers
description:
We're SOUnD, UT's only performative percussion and experimental music ensemble. We play anything with musical potential: paint buckets, paper cups, boomwhacker tubes, and more! Every semester we perform at various campus events, host outreach concerts, and produce our own show.

 

Inspired by the Blue Man Group and Harvard THUD, SOUnD's biggest goal is to spread our love for music. We take anyone interested in playing with us - no percussion experience required.
summary: UT's newest musical group focused on experimenting with alternative percussion and unconventional musical techniques.

name: Electrical Computer Engineering and Business Association
description:
The mission of the Electrical Computer Engineering and Business Association is to provide a welcoming, safe, and nurturing environment for all ECB students to bond, relax, study, and grow together as well as provide opportunities for professional development.

ECBA hosts various events for ECB students year-round.

  • General meetings - hang out with fellow ECB students and bond over food, games, interview preparation, and homework.
  • Destress Before the Test - ECB students have many tests, but fear not! ECBA is here to help you study, with upperclassmen answering your questions and calming your nerves while you get to munch on treats.
  • Special guest speakers - the intersection of EE and business is very fascinating. Come to our guest speaker events and learn valuable advice from them.
  • Networking opportunities - ECB students are in high demand from employers who want EE and business-savvy students. Meet them through ECBA-sponsored networking events! All outside employers and company representatives will solely be focused on providing advice and information on their companies and will not be soliciting internships or other company opportunities.
  • Mock interviews, career planning/advice, and resume reviews - ECBA offers all members the ability to get mock technical and case interviews, resume reviews, and general career planning and advice.
  • Special events - come join ECBA at various special events, like holiday parties, company immersions, etc.
summary: The Electrical Computer Engineering and Business Association (ECBA) is a student organization for all students jointly majoring in Honors Electrical Engineering and Business Honors.

name: The Texas Business Law Society
description: Texas Business Law Society (TBLS) is dedicated to providing the highest quality educational, networking, and professional development opportunities for law students interested in transaction work. Organization activities include guest lectures, training clinics, competition teams, networking events, career-advancement opportunities, and social events both on and off of campus. TBLS is focused on enhancing the legal career of the law students at The University of Texas. TBLS will abide by all University rules related to solicitation, co-sponsorship, and guest speaker policies.
summary: Texas Business Law Society (TBLS) is dedicated to providing the highest quality educational, networking, and professional development opportunities for law students interested in transaction work. TBLS will abide by all University rules.

name: Texas Energy Capital
description:
Texas Energy Capital is a student-managed investment organization at the University of Texas at Austin focused on providing students exposure to the energy industry through management of an energy-investment portfolio.

The objectives of the investment organization are to:

Provide students with the opportunity to learn about careers in energy finance and investment banking

Create an expansive network of alumni for students to learn and connect with

Provide students with the opportunity to invest real capital

Teach students the foundational elements of an investment pitch
summary: Texas Energy Capital is a student-managed investment organization at the University of Texas at Austin focused on providing students exposure to the energy industry through management of an energy-investment portfolio.

name: Texas Equity Group
description:
Texas Equity Group (TEG) is a student-run organization started at UT in 2007 that manages a diversified US equity portfolio of ~$16,000. Through general meetings, underclassman education sessions, and stock pitches, TEG introduces and enhances financial concepts, portfolio analysis, and investment strategy among its members.

TEG is an admissions-based club accepting applications at the beginning of each semester. More information about TEG and its current members can be found at our website.
summary: Texas Equity Group is the premier investing group at UT with the mission of fostering and advancing our members' interest and education in finance.

name: The Corral
description: The Corral is a student led organization that will serve as the official student section of The University of Texas basketball. The Corral aims to enhance the gameday environment and create a strong basketball culture at The University of Texas. The Corral is inclusive and on gameday encompasses all student groups and all student supporters of Texas Athletics.
summary: The Corral is a student led organization that will serve as the official student section of The University of Texas basketball. The Corral aims to enhance the gameday environment and create a strong basketball culture at The University of Texas.

name: SKY Meditation and Wellness
description:
SKY Meditation and Wellness club is aimed at promoting happiness, wellbeing, harmony in diversity, service and leadership, creating a stress-free and violence-free, happy campus. We conduct Leadership, well-being, social & de-stress sessions on a weekly basis to manage stress from the body and the mind through fun activities, group discussion, relaxation techniques (meditation, breath-work, light stretches), insights into laws that govern the mind and community service.

 

Neither at home nor at school are students taught how to deal with stress in healthy ways. Research suggests that proactive approaches to stress-reduction, enhanced social connection, and service-based engagement with community can significantly enhance health, happiness and quality of life for students.  Our innovative life-skills training programs combine breathwork and meditation with interactive discussions and experiential processes so students gain valuable skills to better navigate the personal, social, and academic landscape and pressures of college.

Through breath work, meditation, and self-awareness practices, students learn to:

  • Clear and relax the mind
  • Increase personal resilience by managing negative emotions and stress
  • Increase energy and capacity naturally
  • Gain equanimity and broader perspective on life when facing challenges
  • Reduce anxiety, depression and improved mental-health
summary: SKY Meditation and Wellness club is aimed at promoting happiness, wellbeing, harmony in diversity, service, and leadership, by creating a stress-free and violence-free happy campus through evidence-based SKY breathing and meditation techniques.

name: Last Writers at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
The Last Writers is a service-oriented organization at the University of Texas that allows students to interview interested hospice and palliative care patients in Austin. Students write the life stories of their paired patient and family in a memoir format over the course of a student semester. The finished memoir is published as a physical hardcover copy and presented to that patient and their family at no cost. We hope to aid in the grieving and healing through companionship and by providing a lasting memento for family and friends. Members will learn more about narrative medicine, the intersection between the humanities and medicine, improve patient interactions, and gain service hours.

 

Feel free to email lastwritersatut@gmail.com with any questions! Thank you!
summary: Last Writers connects students with interested hospice patients to create a memoir that encapsulates their life stories. Members will learn more about narrative medicine and the intersection between the humanities and medicine.

name: Longhorn Mycological Society
description:
Welcome to the Longhorn Mycological Society!

Our purpose is to create a community for all students interested in topics related to fungi and mushrooms at UT Austin through social engagement, educational opportunities, and mentorship.

We host a variety of events including mushroom walks, cultivation kit workshops, arts and crafts, cooking and gaming socials, educational events, and so much more!

Our social activities, academic support, professional networking and development, and career resources are open to any and all who share a passion for mycology and related topics.

If you would like to be a member, just click "Join" on this Hornslink page and follow our Instagram @longhornmyco ! We don't communicate on Hornslink much, so this is the best method of knowing when we are hosting events, and further general discussions occur on our Discord!

Check out our linktree here: https://linktr.ee/longhornmyco
summary: The Longhorn Mycological Society is a community for all interested in topics relating to the study of fungi and mushrooms on the forty acres through social engagement, shared professional resources, and mentorship.

name: The Alzheimer's and Dementia Organization at The University of Texas at Austin
description: The Alzheimer's and Dementia Organization (ADO) exists to connect college students to the Alzheimer's Association to serve needs through volunteering, educate young students about neurological disease, and raise money to further research for an eventual end to the disease. The organization will host gatherings with researchers and members of the Association to foster connections between the student organization and the AA.
summary: We wish to increase awareness and advocacy, volunteering opportunities, and foster education for and about Alzheimer's and other dementias at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Texas Striking Laboratory
description: The Striking Laboratory is intended to (provide a safe space for students to train in various types of striking arts in a free form playground style manner. While providing a means to develop skills in sparring, training, and teaching.
summary: The Striking Laboratory is intended to provide a safe space for students to train in various types of striking arts in a free-form playground-style manner. At the same time, providing a means to develop skills in sparring, training, and discipline.

name: Occupy Campus Ministry at the University of Texas at Austin
description: Occupy is a diverse campus ministry that exists to usher in a new era of worship and relationship with God on and off campus. We host many events such as worship nights, grow groups, services, social gatherings, and community service projects.
summary: Occupy is a diverse campus ministry that exists to usher in a new era of worship and relationship with God on and off campus. We host many events such as worship nights, grow groups, services, social gatherings, and community service projects.

name: UBUNTU
description: “Ubuntu” is a Zulu word that means “I am because you are”. This is the understanding that we are who we are through the individuals that we interact with throughout our lives. Ubuntu embodies the notions of compassion and humanity towards others. We are a dance team that specializes in dance forms across the African Diaspora. Ubuntu serves to empower and build an uplifted community of young dancers through the performing and the spreading of culture from the African diaspora throughout UT campus and Austin area.
summary: “Ubuntu” is a Zulu word that means “I am because you are”. We are a dance team that specializes in dance forms across the African Diaspora. Ubuntu serves to build an uplifted community of young dancers through  performance and love of African culture.

name: Texas Finance Team
description: Texas Finance Team intends to provide members a community for improving one’s understanding of fundamental finance and for establishing a network to Texas Finance Team alumni. The organization also provides an environment for upperclassmen members to participate in mentorship for the underclassmen members. Texas Finance Team aims to assist members in business competition success, competitive career placements, and academic excellence.
summary: We strive to develop our member base by encouraging active involvement and providing individualized mentorship, helping our members grow their finance skills, and preparing them for recruiting in a variety of industries.

name: The National Association of Black Accountants at The University of Texas at Austin
description:
The National Association of Black Accountants (NABA, Inc.) is a nonprofit professional association dedicated to advancing the careers of not only Black professionals but all professionals in accounting, finance, and related business fields. Founded in 1969, NABA represents the interests of over 200,000 members on a national level within the United States, including entry and mid-level accountants, auditors, financial professionals, senior managers, executives, firm partners, and students.

NABA's mission is to engage, empower, and educate business leaders by providing a platform to enhance skills and elevate voices. The organization focuses on talent development, innovation in business, and fostering an inclusive culture. Through various programs and initiatives, NABA supports its members in achieving professional excellence and inspires future generations of leaders
summary: The National Association of Black Accountants empowers and educates all professionals in the fields of accounting, finance, and business by providing networking opportunities, leadership programs, and promoting inclusivity within the industry.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances at the University of Texas at Austin
description: Hello everyone! We are VAW Global Health Alliances at the University of Texas at Austin, we strive to promote equitable and sustainable healthcare worldwide! If you like to travel and want to give back to the community, check us out!
summary: Hello everyone! We are VAW Global Health Alliances at the University of Texas at Austin, we strive to promote equitable and sustainable healthcare worldwide! If you like to travel and want to give back to the community, check us out!

name: Always Texas
description:
Always Texas' mission is to create a space where all students are welcomed and celebrated. We work to create an organization with a diverse membership in traits such as race, class, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, identity, and experience. Authenticity and inclusivity drive our organization's core, and we are committed to upholding our 7 foundational values: spirit, service, inclusivity, community, progressivism, traditions, and love. 

We strive to serve all working-class people and marginalized communities, such as BIPOC (Black Indigenous, and People of Color), disabled folks, and those who identify as part of the LGBTQIA+ community. As a spirit organization at the University of Texas at Austin, we work to build an inclusive culture that encourages, supports, and empowers students to be their most authentic selves and find comfort in all their identities.
summary: Always Texas is a spirit, service, and social organization that strives to create an inclusive community of UT students where a diverse group of students feels welcome. Our mission is to serve working-class, BIPOC, and LGBTQIA+ communities in ATX.

name: Engineering Student Life
summary:
Engineering Student Life 
Cockrell School of Engineering

name: ADAPT Texas
description:
ADAPT Texas is a student-led organization at the University of Texas at Austin that applies engineering and innovation to create real-world solutions for accessibility challenges. Through hands-on projects, community partnerships, and advocacy efforts, we empower individuals with disabilities by modifying devices, developing assistive technology, and fostering an inclusive environment. Our team collaborates across disciplines to turn ideas into impactful solutions, ensuring that technology serves everyone.

 

Our website: https://adapttexas.org/
summary: ADAPT Texas creates, adapts, and develops tools for individuals with disabilities, advocate for communities, and create change, one person at a time.

name: Longhorn Squash Club
description:
Welcome to the home of the premiere squash university of the South.

Through regular, high-quality programming, Longhorn Squash Club promotes awareness, engagement, and participation with the international sport of squash at all levels of play. Our diverse and inclusive culture provides a fun, healthy, and fulfilling community experience that can be enjoyed by all.

We offer free weekly sessions with some coaching to get you started! If you're an advanced player, we also sport a competitive travel team. Sign up and reach out to us to get on court!

Tuesdays 7:30-9:00PM @ Greg Gym

Rice vs. Texas
summary: Welcoming all students and faculty to participate in the international sport of squash.

name: Korean American Student Association at Austin
description:
안녕하세요!

Welcome to the Korean American Student Association.  Our mission is to provide an inclusive environment where members can build life-long relationships through cultural activities.

We strive to build life-long friendships in a community that promotes diversity and understanding.

Be a contributor to the expansion of asian awareness and make a difference at UT.
summary: KASA provides Korean Americans and all those interested a safe space to network and learn about the unique experiences of living in America as an Asian minority.  We strive to eliminate negative stereotypes by expanding the outreach of Korean culture.

name: Longhorn Puzzle Club
description: Be a part of an organization with like-minded people who enjoy doing puzzles or want to unwind and take a break from schoolwork. Please join our Instagram (@longhornpuzzleclub) for updates about meetings and socials!
summary: A club for students who are interested in puzzles or getting their puzzle on!

name: Allied Scholars for Animal Protection at The University of Texas at Austin
description: Allied Scholars for Animal Protection (ASAP) is a student organization that educates, trains, and mobilizes student advocates in the field of animal protection. We provide the infrastructure, organization, and resources that enable students to effectively advocate for human and nonhuman animals alike.  To become a member and staying up to date on events and meetings, please fill this form here: alliedscholars.link/utaustin
summary: Allied Scholars for Animal Protection (ASAP) is a student organization that educates, trains, and mobilizes student advocates in the field of animal protection. We help students effectively advocate for animals.

name: Kappa Theta Pi at the University of Texas at Austin
summary:
UT Austin's premier co-ed technology fraternity centered around five pillars: professional development, alumni connections, social growth, technological advancement, and academic support.

Open to all majors.

name: Active Minds at The University of Texas at Austin
description: Active Minds is the nation's premier nonprofit organization promoting mental health for young adults. Through education, research, advocacy, and a focus on young adults, Active Minds is opening up the conversation about mental health and creating lasting change in the way mental health is talked about, cared for, and valued in the United States.
summary: Dedicated to building stronger communities through education, research, advocacy, and a focus on young adults, Active Minds is opening up the conversation about mental health and creating lasting change in the way mental health is talked about.

name: Reggaeton Dance Club at UT
description: love to dance, sing, or simply be Latinx?? Then Reggaeton Dance Club at UT Austin is your home away from home! RDC@UT is a safe place for all to dance, sing, and celebrate being Latinx. Stick around as this new org on campus spreads some great music, moves, and vibes! This is not just a dance club, but a place to express yourself, find joy, and family in other students from around the world‼️ Being a part of RDC@UT will involve advocacy work, fundraising, dance rehearsals, socials, and festivals!! Come join and be a part of this new & fresh journey aiming to give all Latinx/BIPOC students or lovers of Reggaeton a platform to grow, excel, and achieve their dreams with the help of art.
summary: The Reggaeton Dance Club at UT is a safe place for all to dance, sing, share creativity, and celebrate being Latinx. Through the power of dance, music, and positive, energetic Latin vibes, our members will be able to find a home to be unapologetic.

name: Ridgetop College
description: Ridgetop College is getting off the ground and seeking students who want to grow together in what it means to be a Christ-centered community that serves students and the city of Austin. It’s our hope that this ministry will be a catalyst for community building among students and residents of the city and truly reflect the beautiful diversity of the University and the city of Austin.
summary: We envision a campus ministry that is centering on Christ, serving our neighborhood, and sending the next generation.

name: Teaching Refugees to Understand English at UT Austin
description:
TRUE is a student-led volunteer organization that tutors English to young refugees within AISD. Every week, TRUE students tutor English to a small group of elementary refugee students for one hour. TRUE students pick a time that they are available each week and are paired with a group of refugees at an AISD school based on that time. Lesson plans are provided by TRUE, but transportation is not, so students will have get to their assigned tutoring location themselves. Carpooling opportunities may be provided.

For more information:
