https://docs.google.com/document/d/13t6irjbkaR212jkeHAboCO59iXj8tcX96QqBFwIfGg0/edit?usp=sharing
summary: Teaching Refugees to Understand English at UT Austin is a service organization that teaches English to young refugees at various schools around AISD. Every week, TRUE students tutor English to refugees students for one hour.

name: Texas Success for Austin Youth Organization
description:
Texas SAYO is an organization that provides mentoring and tutoring services to students from communities in the Austin area. Their programs offer one-on-one tutoring, mentoring, and academic coaching, providing students with the tools they need to excel in their studies. In addition, we provide healthcare education to local underserved programs to increase awareness and prevention. Texas SAYO's services are tailored to meet the unique needs of each student.

In addition to academic support, Texas SAYO offers college application assistance, career guidance, and opportunities for students to participate in extracurricular activities, internships, and community service projects. Their mission is to empower students to become leaders in their communities and achieve their personal and professional goals.
summary: Texas SAYO is a mentoring and tutoring organization in Austin that supports students from diverse backgrounds with academic support and resources to promote their success. Their programs empower students with skills and knowledge to achieve their goals.

name: The Students of East Africa
description: Students of East Africa strives to sustain the cultural and intellectual representation of East Africans on campus. Through biweekly meetings and regular events, our organization creates spaces where students can experience a sense of community and belonging. Our events will enables SEA to further promote cultural and intellectual representation as well as raise awareness of current issues in East Africa not only to our members but also to the greater UT community.
summary: Students of East Africa strives to sustain the cultural and intellectual representation of East Africans on campus. Through biweekly meetings and regular events, our organization creates spaces where students can experience a sense of community.

name: Texas Creative Union Project
description:
The Texas Creative Union Project is intended to help people navigate their artistic endeavors in a community setting as a creative collective. TCUP assists artists in their business journey by organizing fun, low-risk art markets with accommodating vending fees & by creating a redistribution system of small-business supplies. <3

Applications open every fall!
summary: TCUP is a collective organization aimed to support student artists by providing a welcoming art market community to grow and learn together.

name: Austin STEM Buddies
description: Welcome to Austin STEM Buddies! We go to elementary schools in the Austin area and teach different STEM concepts to the kids! Volunteer with us to make slime, elephant toothpaste, and more to inspire elementary kids in STEM and watch their excitement grow!
summary: We aim to promote STEM exploration in elementary and secondary students around the Austin area.

name: Vamos Tennis
description: We introduce elementary students to the sport of tennis by partnering with schools that are located in under-resourced and minority communities. The members of Vamos Tennis will work together to promote the sport of tennis throughout the Austin community through weekly volunteer outreach efforts. Additionally, Vamos Tennis is partnered with the United States Tennis Association (USTA) through USTA's School Tennis Program.
summary: We are partnered with the United States Tennis Association (USTA). Our goal is to introduce tennis to as many kids as possible. We work with elementary schools in the Austin area, visiting campuses weekly on Fridays.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Incorporated
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity is a professional organization of college-educated men with similar ideas and like attainments.

name: Out of State, International, Transfer Student Agency of Student Government
description:
Please Do **NOT** Request Membership through HornsLink! If you would like to stay connected, please follow us on instagram (@iotsa.agency)!

Current Directors: 

Arantxa Espinosa Rivera, arantxa@utexas.edu 

Ezinne Osemene, Sreeja.arora@gmail.com   

 

IOTSA’s Mission:

The International, Out of State, and Transfer Student Agency (IOTSA) advocates and provides support and a community space for students from other states, international students, and students who have transferred from other universities. We also ensure students from these communities are given a platform to advocate for university policies, programs, and initiatives that best serve them!

  • IOTSA offers personalized support to new students from out-of-state, international backgrounds, and transfer students, helping them navigate campus life and connect with essential resources.
  • We connect with students and assess their needs through regular interactions and feedback sessions, ensuring that out-of-state, international, and transfer students have a voice in campus matters.
  • We coordinate subcommittees focused on the specific needs of out-of-state, international, and transfer students, encouraging more student involvement in the agency’s initiatives.
  • We plan and host events that build a supportive community such as orientation workshops, information panels, mentorship programs, and the first-semester kickoff fair to foster community and provide essential information and support.
  • We collaborate with university offices such as the Office of Admissions, Texas Global and New Student Services to advocate for policies and services that address the unique challenges faced by out-of-state, international, and transfer students.
summary: The International, Out of State, and Transfer Student Agency (IOTSA) advocates and provides support and a community space for students from other states, international students, and students who have transferred from other universities.

name: Governmental Relations Agency of Student Government
description:
Please Do **NOT** Request Membership through HornsLink! If you would like to stay connected, please follow us on instagram (@utsg.govrelations)!

Current Directors: 

Leah Tharakan, leahtharakan@utexas.edu   

Sara Allen, saraallen@utexas.edu 

 

GRA’s Mission:

The Governmental Relations Agency (GRA) raises student awareness and involvement in government at the city, state and federal level, to interact with governmental entities and policy groups. We also work in conjunction with the Student Government executive and legislative branches to relay the official voice of the student body as expressed in official Student Government policies, positions, and stances to government entities. 

GRA facilitates invaluable nonpartisan connections, conversations, and understanding between Longhorns and their government. Students in GRA are divided into Local, State, and Federal committees in which they work with their peers in the completion of a project to cultivate Longhorn civic engagement. Beyond this, GRA hosts panels, town halls, and workshops, providing platforms for students to engage directly with policymakers, experts, and community leaders on pressing political issues. GRA seeks to spark conversation, highlight political accessibility, and activate the UT student body into active, engaged citizens. Whether you are passionate about local issues or national policies, GRA provides a platform to make a meaningful impact and ensure that student voices are heard at every level of government.
summary: The Governmental Relations Agency (GRA) of Student Government raises student awareness and involvement in government at the city, state and federal level, to interact with governmental entities and policy groups.

name: Mental Health Alliance
description:
Please Do **NOT** Request Membership through HornsLink! If you would like to stay connected, please follow us on instagram (@utsgmentalhealth)!

Current Directors: 

Krystal Zhong, yanzh0ng@utexas.edu 

Vipin Eapen, veapen05@gmail.com   

 

MHA's Mission: 

The Mental Health Alliance (MHA) furthers advocacy, action, and awareness of mental health issues on campus. Through student-led initiatives, events, and legislation, MHA aims to promote mental health advocacy, ensuring that every member of the UT Austin community has access to the necessary resources for individualized mental health support!

  • Assist in initiatives to raise mental health awareness through campus events and social media campaigns such as distributing Speedway Sleep Kits and hosting Divine Canines sessions where students can unwind with therapy dogs.
  • Collaborate with student organizations to promote mental health education.  
  • Advocate for accessible, inclusive mental health services on campus, supporting the university's efforts to provide comprehensive care for all students.
  • Work in partnership with such entities as University Health Services, Counseling and Mental Health Center (CMHC), and other university offices and organizations to advance efforts of advocacy, action, and awareness surrounding mental health and mental illness.
summary: The Mental Health Alliance (MHA) furthers advocacy, action, and awareness of mental health issues on campus.

name: Dell Medical School - Student Affairs
summary: Student Affairs staff and office in the Dell Medical School

name: Food Security Agency
description: • Food Security Agency is a collaborative movement, led by students within the University of Texas at Austin, to ensure a just, thriving, zero waste food system for members of this campus. We believe that every member of the UT Austin community matters deeply, that food is essential to learning, and that a healthy food system is the foundation for community wellbeing. The Food Security Agency is committed to realizing Tomorrow’s Texas by engaging the UT Austin food system, from production to purchasing to logistics to consumption to resource recovery.
summary: Food Security Agency

name: International Engineering Education (IEE)
description:
The Cockrell School's International Engineering Education (IEE) Office coordinates with faculty and international institutions and helps students apply and participate in engineering-specific study abroad programs, in addition to other exchange programs offered by UT Austin.

International Engineering Education (IEE) programs prepare Texas Engineers for the global marketplace. International experience contributes to the development of entrepreneurs and leaders who innovate technology and manage people across cultural borders, as well as assess and implement solutions to global challenges and needs.

 

IEE offers UT credit, enhances soft skills, and broadens personal and professional networks through the following programs: short term programs and semester exchanges.

 

For more information, contact the IEE office at iee@engr.utexas.edu or (512) 232-9675.

Visit the IEE office at EER 2.608
2501 Speedway, Austin, TX
summary: The Cockrell School's International Engineering Education (IEE) Office coordinates with faculty and international institutions and helps students apply and participate in engineering-specific study abroad programs, in addition to other exchange programs.

name: Longhorn Federal Bar Association
description: The Longhorn Federal Bar Association seeks to 1) create a community for Texas Law students interested in federal government practice, 2) promote knowledge about government and government adjacent legal developments, and 3) aid students searching for career opportunities in federal practice and firms working with clients before the federal government.
summary: The Longhorn Federal Bar Association seeks to create a community for Texas Law students interested in federal government practice and aid students searching for career opportunities in federal practice and firms working with clients before the federal.

name: Plaintiffs' Advocacy & Litigation Society
description: Provide opportunities for students to learn about plaintiff-side career paths and connect with practitioners and other students interested in plaintiffs' advocacy.
summary: Provide opportunities for students to learn about plaintiff-side career paths and connect with practitioners and other students interested in plaintiffs' advocacy.

name: Muslim Mental Health Organization
summary: Foster a community of young Muslim men and women in enriching the importance of mental health within our religion. While confronting cultural and religious stigma, the topic of mental health will be discussed and spread within a personal and wide impact.

name: Undergraduate Law Journal for Minority Women
description: The Undergraduate Law Journal for Minority Women is an organization that looks to uplift and empower women of color and minority women through teaching and publishing undergraduate law journal articles that allow students to explore law in a unique way. Our goal is to provide a platform for women to express their thoughts and ideas on legal issues and to promote diversity and inclusion in the legal profession.
summary: A University of Texas at Austin organization uplifting and empowering women of color by helping them explore law through legal writing.

name: Undergraduate Linguistic Society
description: The Undergraduate Linguistic Society of the University of Texas at Austin (henceforth the ULS) aims to provide community for undergraduates here at UT who are interested in Linguistics, and language more broadly and to connect students to faculty, research opportunities and the interdisciplinary applications of linguistics, as well as our field more generally.
summary: The Undergraduate Linguistic Society aims to assist undergraduates at UT in becoming involved in the linguistic community through connecting students with faculty, research opportunities, and other students interested in linguistics.

name: Thai Lao Cambodian Student Association
description: Join us in promoting our endangered oral traditions, culinary techniques, and more! Our mission extends beyond preservation as we wholeheartedly engage with local communities and engage in social activity. If you're interested in learning about TLC and making friends, find us on Instagram at @utexastlc :)
summary: TLC's mission is to spread awareness of our rich Southeast Asian culture while creating a fun, inviting envorenment. All are welcome!

name: Texas Undergraduate Consulting Group
description: Texas Undergraduate Consulting Group (TUCG) seeks to build and promote leaders with strong logical skills and human-oriented decision-making. We strive to provide quality business and consulting education to all students on campus and expose business knowledge to business and non-business majors in the process. TUCG was created as a mechanism to build leaders through experience. Through service to peers, the Austin community, and small businesses across Texas, our members are trained through experience to lead with logic, empathy, and strong decision-making skills.
summary: Texas Undergraduate Consulting Group focuses on equipping student leaders with efficient problem-solving skills, elite communication, and excellent teamwork abilities. TUCG is geared toward creating meaningful impact across campus and its clients.

name: Student Affiliates for School Psychology
description: The purpose of SASP shall be to present UT school psychology graduate students as a unified body in order to discuss issues and concerns that affect students, both professionally and academically; to provide opportunities to develop and nurture leadership skills; to allow student mentorship and occasions for sharing relevant resources; to foster professionalism during training; to institute a means to affect changes within the department/program; to sponsor speakers and extracurricular training; to establish links with professional organizations and school psychology professionals in the community; and to afford networking opportunities for school psychology graduate students.
summary: The purpose of SASP shall be to present UT school psychology graduate students as a unified body and to connect them to one another.

name: Tech Consulting Group at The University of Texas at Austin
description:
Technology Consulting Group (TCG) is a McCombs Affiliated Student Organization that inspires and equips a new generation of business and technology leaders by developing our members with client projects and a hands-on, comprehensive curriculum. Each semester, our consultants deliver pro-bono strategy and technology solutions for a wide range of organizations.

 

We build community beyond project work through a range of social events and shared traditions designed to help members connect. These include structured programs, including Fall Retreat, Secret Santa, and IM Sports, along with casual socials throughout the semester that create opportunities to meet others across cohorts.

 

Recruitment information:

We welcome all majors to apply, and no prior background is required!

 

Freshmen and sophomores apply to the Fellowship and learn consulting skills through our comprehensive curriculum covering general consulting knowledge, professional development, and a technical overview. After completing the fellowship, fellows become consultants.

 

Juniors apply to become consultants on a project team and create impact for real organizations. Gain hands-on industry experience by working on 10-week-long cases with a wide variety of companies. Consultants are continually trained through weekly consultant workshops.

 

Make sure to follow us on Instagram and fill out our Interest Form to keep up with recruitment!

 

Check out our Linktree for all relevant links!
summary: TCG is a family of students who are passionate about the intersection of business and technology. We strive to cultivate a tight-knit community of business and technology leaders who are well-prepared to deliver high-impact solutions in the industry.

name: Texas herScript
description: Texas herScript is dedicated to empowering Black women in technology by providing community, mentorship, and career development opportunities. Through programs such as herCollective mentorship, techCollective K–12 outreach, herDesign technical workshops, and herStudy academic support sessions, we create pathways for members to grow personally, academically, and professionally. Our organization hosts monthly meetings, social events, service initiatives, and career‑focused engagements while advocating for representation, equity, and belonging within the tech industry.
summary: A campus organization supporting Black women in technology through community, mentorship, professional development, and technical workshops while fostering empowerment, skill‑building, and inclusive tech pathways.

name: Reading Aces
description:
Reading Aces is a volunteering organization dedicated to promoting and enhancing child literacy and comprehension. We take books to various recreation centers in Austin and partner volunteers with a child that will then teach them how to read, either by reading to them or encouraging them to read to the volunteer. Sessions are offered Monday - Thursday with rides provided! We strive to make the volunteering process as least stressful as possible, and require no weekly commitment. Come volunteer with us once a semester or every week, we'll be happy to have you!

View more and sign up to volunteer at readingaces.org
summary: A volunteering organization dedicated to promoting child literacy. The organization partners with various recreation centers in Austin to teach their children how to read. Volunteering sessions are offered Monday - Thursday, with rides provided

name: Women in Data and Statistics
description: Women in Data and Statistics is dedicated to providing educational resources, career opportunities, and an inclusive community for all interested in data analytics, data science, research, and statistics. We are open to all majors, knowledge and experience levels, and are gender inclusive.
summary: Women in Data and Statistics is dedicated to providing educational resources, career opportunities, and an inclusive community for all interested in data analytics, data science, research, and statistics.

name: Longhorn Neurotech
description:
Welcome to Longhorn Neurotech, a student organization with a singular focus: facilitating your entry into the world of neural engineering. Our purpose is to provide you with the knowledge, experiences, and connections necessary for a successful career in academia or industry.

Our club is dedicated to creating and curating educational content that ensures a deep understanding of neural engineering concepts. We believe that a strong foundation is crucial for your growth in this complex field. We have an extensive onboarding process that ensures you are able to meaningfully contribute to one of our year-long projects. 

Furthermore, we understand the significance of networking. Longhorn Neurotech provides opportunities to connect with guest speakers who are established professionals in neural engineering. Conferences and events organized by our club offer a chance to broaden your perspectives and create connections that may shape your future.

In essence, Longhorn Neurotech is your stepping stone into the neural engineering domain. We invite you to join us in this journey of learning, collaborating, and networking, as we equip you with the tools to thrive in the exciting realm of neural engineering.
summary: We are a neural engineering club dedicated to preparing our members for careers in academia and industry through competitions, education, and networking.

name: Youth Justice Alliance
description: The Youth Justice Alliance is dedicated to supporting aspiring first-generation lawyers. We work diligently with members who have a lack of access to the legal profession to ensure the success of their future legal careers. YJA will provide a community of aspiring first-generation lawyers. YJA is a great opportunity to meet people from similar backgrounds, build networks in the legal field, and gain hands-on experience for your resume.
summary: An organization to support aspiring first-generation lawyers.

name: Longhorn Urbanists
description:
Longhorn Urbanists aims to advocate, educate, and empower UT students to work towards a more walkable, accessible, and livable urban community! We host discussions, renowned guest speakers, tours around Austin, and so much more; come and join LU!

NEXT GENERAL MEETING: TUESDAY, APR 7TH @ RLP 1.104

OFFICER APPLICATIONS OPEN: LINK

Please follow our Instagram & join our Discord to stay up to date with all LU-related discussions/meetings.

https://www.instagram.com/longhornurbanists/?img_index=1

https://discord.gg/9tUdTm2GXR
summary: The Longhorn Urbanists is intended to advocate, educate, and empower for a more walkable, accessible, and liveable urban environment.

name: National Association of Black Journalists - UT
description: National Association of Black Journalists - UT is tailored toward Black students interested in Communications. We host Black professional speakers and cultivate an incredible community at our biweekly meetings. All majors are welcome!
summary: National Association of Black Journalists - UT is tailored toward Black students interested in Communications. We host Black professional speakers and cultivate an incredible community at our biweekly meetings. All majors are welcome!

name: Disability Cultural Center
description: The Disability Cultural Center supports disability community and education at UT. All members of the university community are welcome to engage with the DCC as we work to improve access and belonging for disabled students, faculty, staff and celebrate disability as a valued culture and identity.
summary: The Disability Cultural Center supports disability community and education on campus.

name: Texas Mental Health Initiative for South Asians
description:
MHISA is a grass-roots organization committed to addressing the societal and structural barriers which prevent South Asian-Americans from accessing mental health care. Our primary goals are to:

1) Educate South Asian communities about the relationships between mental health, substance abuse, and gun violence

2) Decrease the marginalization of South Asians struggling with mental health illnesses

3) Advocate for evidence-based policies which reduce gun violence in relation to mental illness
summary: MHISA is a grass-roots organization committed to addressing the societal and structural barriers which prevent South Asian-Americans from accessing mental health care.

name: Kendo Association at The University of Texas at Austin
description:
The KAUT aims to offer UT students, faculty, and staff the opportunity to practice Kendo, or "Way of the Sword” in Japanese. The purpose of Kendo is to discipline the human character through the application of the principles of the katana. Whether you're a seasoned practitioner or brand new to kendo, our association offers an inclusive and vibrant space to embrace the way of the sword, build friendships, and immerse yourself in a rich cultural experience!

Practice Schedule (Checkout our instagram and website for more detail!):

Tuesday 7:00 pm - 9:00 pm at WCP 2.310
Thursday 7:45 pm to 9:45 pm at PURE Yoga North Austin
Saturday 9:30 am to 11:00 am at Austin Recreation Center

Discord: https://discord.gg/2apvXFSvaF

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/utaustinkendo/
summary: Our goal is to offer UT students, faculty, and staff the opportunity to practice Kendo, or Japanese fencing.

name: Texas Entertainment Association
description: A preprofessional student organization that seeks to educate and prepare students for a career in entertainment.
summary: A preprofessional student organization that seeks to educate and prepare students for a career in entertainment.

name: Music United
summary: Music United seeks to work with disabled and/or financially underprivileged students (grades K-5) and teach them music for free. We hope to inspire them with what they accomplish throughout their musical journey and unite a diverse population of people.

name: Collaboration for Undoing Racism in Environmental Sciences
description: CURES is a fledgling campus organization geared towards providing students with a toolbelt of techniques for fighting environmental racism either in outstanding advocacy or in their everyday lives. The organization is defined by weekly meetings which consist of a short lecture series designed to provide background on environmental justice and its effects on local communities and strategies for advocacy. Between these meetings, volunteer opportunities are coordinated with local grassroot organizations in collaboration with underrepresented communities, such as PODER Austin. Another goal we are putting together to achieve is guest speakers from faculty who have had experience with environmental justice or have dedicated a portion of their career to the movement, and hopefully guest speakers from out of campus as well to provide insight to various environmental justice issues in a broader sense.
summary: CURES is geared towards providing students with a toolbelt of techniques for fighting environmental racism either in outstanding advocacy or in their everyday lives.

name: The L.O.V.E. Club
summary: We are a social organization that holds space for lesbians and allies to celebrate and uplift lesbians at UT. L.O.V.E. stands for Lesbian On-Campus Visibility & Engagement, which are the two areas of inclusivity we most concern ourselves with improving.

name: Texas Nu Rho Psi
description:
Nu Rho Psi is the only nationally-recognized honor society in the area of brain research. The study of the brain is one of the noblest of academic pursuits because a deep and true understanding of the principles by which the brain works contributes directly to the quality of our lives and the well-being of future generations.

The purpose of Nu Rho Psi is to:

  • encourage professional interest and excellence in scholarship, particularly in neuroscience;
  • award recognition to students who have achieved such excellence in scholarship;
  • advance the discipline of neuroscience; encourage intellectual and social interaction between students, faculty, and professionals in neuroscience and related fields;
  • promote career development in neuroscience and related fields;
  • increase public awareness of neuroscience and its benefits for the individual and society; and
  • encourage service to the community.

Meaning of the Nu Rho Psi symbols:

Membership The Greek letters of our honor society, “ΝΡΨ”, designate the study of neuroscience – the interdisciplinary science that engages our members in the study of the Nervous System and is the unifying focus of this honor society. The Greek letter Nu (Ν, ν) stands for the noun Nous (pronounced “noose”), meaning “Mind”. The letter Rho (Ρ, ρ) stands for the verb Ρueaoai (pronounced “hruesthigh”) meaning, “to cure”. The letter Psi (Ψ, ψ) stands for the noun Psyche (pronounced “psuke”), meaning “life”.
summary: Nu Rho Psi (NRP) is the National Honor Society in Neuroscience. NRP offers numerous opportunities in research, community outreach, and scholarships.

name: Texas Diabolo Association
description: Texas Diabolo Association is a Chinese yo-yo or diabolo performance team at UT Austin that blends the traditional past time of diabolo with modern and traditional Chinese music. We aim to spread Asian American culture through the performing arts. Members are encouraged to learn about and perform the traditional activity for school wide events, or other organization's events such as TASA's night market and CSA's Mid Autumn Festival.
summary: Texas Diabolo Association is a Chinese yo-yo or diabolo performance team at UT Austin that blends the traditional past time of diabolo with modern and traditional Chinese music. We aim to spread Asian American culture through the performing arts.

name: Texas Education Society
description:
Texas Education Society is an organization where every member is imperfectly perfect.
Our Leaders will Lead, Guide, Challenge, protect, and cultivate young Leaders to
empower others to be their best genuine self! This community will provide a sense of
family, a sense of belonging, and a sense of togetherness. When one joins this
organization they will learn, network, and gain leadership skills to enhance their
potential.
summary:
Our Leaders will Lead, Guide, Challenge, protect, and cultivate young Leaders to
empower others to be their best genuine selves. This community will provide a sense of togetherness. Members will network and gain skills to enhance their potential.

name: EcoCAR at UT
description:
Visit our website to learn more: 

https://sites.utexas.edu/ecocar/
summary: EcoCAR at UT modifies the latest production vehicles, integrating new software and hardware in a competition against other universities

name: Texas Political Consulting
description:
Mission Statement:

At Texas Political Consulting, our team of dedicated and experienced UT Austin students provides bi-partisan, pro-bono political analysis and strategic consulting services to campaigns, organizations, and non-profits. 

 

Our Goals:

—Support our local community by working with non-profit, developing, and future-focused organizations and campaigns.

—Leverage our array of skills and expertise to assist entities that may not have the financial resources to hire professional consultants, thereby promoting a more inclusive and equitable political landscape. 

—Conduct thorough, in-depth research and analysis for our clients to aid in crafting effective and successful campaigns.

—Provide our members with impactful professional experience in the political realm so they can build their portfolios and gain hands-on experience.

—Facilitate the development of valuable relationships between students, mentors, and professionals to create a supportive community that fosters success.
summary: Our team of dedicated and experienced UT Austin students provides multi-partisan, pro-bono political analysis and strategic consulting services to campaigns, organizations, and non-profits.

name: Hispanic Public Relations Student Association
description:
Our Mission

To advance the practice of Hispanic/Latinx public relations as a communications and marketing discipline; to serve as the voice for students and rising professionals working in the communications field nationwide; and to empower its members through resources, education and a community that will support them throughout their careers.
 
Why were we founded?
Inclusivity and representation are more important now than ever.
In 2018, HPRSA was founded to cultivate a community that uplifts students’ cultural identities. We believe that inclusive and accessible programs like HPRSA can inspire a new wave of incoming professionals from diverse backgrounds.
summary: To advance the practice of Hispanic/Latinx public relations as a communications and marketing discipline. To serve as the voice for students and rising professionals working in the communications field nationwide.

name: SPIC MACAY Chapter at UT Austin
description: The Chapter serves as a platform to showcase the rich Indian culture by facilitating performances of Indian classical music and dance at UT Austin, putting together workshops, and bringing together a community of students who appreciate cultural heritage.
summary: Society for the Promotion of Indian Classical Music and Culture Amongst Youth is a global volunteer-led effort that seeks to have the youth experience and appreciate the rich Indian culture and heritage embodied in Indian classical art forms.

name: Horns Lens - Photography Org
description:
This photography org is a creative space for members to learn from each other and to put skills into practice during general meetings and outings where we explore Austin through our lenses.

Join our Discord!

 

https://join.slack.com/t/hornslensutph-hzn9063/shared_invite/zt-2fpshj8zm-AIcrqujts3_Zu5oUPHbIhw
summary: This photography org is a creative space for members to learn from each other and to put skills into practice during general meetings and outings where we explore Austin through our lenses.

name: Veterinary Pharmacy Practice Association
description: Veterinary Pharmacy Practice Association intends to promote awareness for the increasing growth in the field of veterinary pharmacy, to collaborate with other professionals in the field of veterinary medicine, pharmacy, compounding, industry, and public health, to help our local community by participating in events that support animals in need, and to develop foundational skills for all members to become leaders in their perspective fields one day.
summary: Veterinary Pharmacy Practice Association is an organization designed to bring awareness to the field of veterinary pharmacy and it's roles within the community. Our focus is to collaborate with other health professionals and help animals in need.

name: The Searcher
description:
The Searcher is an undergraduate humanities research journal at the University of Texas at Austin, sponsored by the Department of English.

We aim to provide an outlet and resource for undergraduate students interested in the humanities to publish scholarly work and familiarize themselves with the peer-review journal process. By offering a broad platform for multiple disciplines, we hope to highlight how meaningful themes emerge and span across the humanities.
summary: UT Austin's first undergraduate humanities research journal, accepting scholarly research in the humanities for publicatoin.

name: Bright Stars of Texas
description: Bright Stars of Texas is a mental health focused organization dedicated to promoting mental health initiatives through education and advocacy and fundraising for nonprofit organizations, providing opportunities for members to get involved on campus and the greater Austin community. We also serve as a place for UT Austin students to build genuine friendships and camaraderie amongst one other in a supportive environment where they feel valued and uplifted. This is a safe space for all who join, allowing members to come as they are and be welcomed with open arms.
summary: Bright Stars of Texas seeks to unite the UT Austin community in support of mental health initiatives, cultivating friendships, and fostering school spirit. We aim to create a campus environment where students feel valued, connected, and empowered.

name: Beyond Frames Productions
description:
Welcome to Beyond Frames Productions (BFP)! We are dedicated to building a community of filmmakers and providing them an opportunity to gain experience in film production, make connections, and expand their resume.

 

DEPARTMENTS

 

Film

 

Every member will be automatically accepted to the Film Department. This allows everyone the opportunity to be involved in our productions, workshops, and professional events!

 

Publicity + Events

 

Each member will be able to sign-up for work within the Publicity and Events Department(s). Opportunities to work with these departments (e.g., volunteering at events) will be posted in our GroupMe and on our socials. This allows anyone the opportunity to strengthen their abilities outside of filmmaking!

 

OPPORTUNITIES

 

Sponsored Productions (Headliners)

 

Every academic year, BFP will select one or two student productions to sponsor. The selected production(s) will receive: (1) sponsored meals and craft services, (2) assistance with crewing, and (3) facilitated connections with established filmmakers in Austin.

 

Productions are selected at our annual Pitch Night, where accepted members have the opportunity to pitch their script to BFP. This event occurs within the first month of the semester. So, if you’re interested in pitching, start working on a script!

 

Following production, we host our annual End-of-Year Screening, where selected productions AND other submitted films from members are premiered at the theatre.

 

Events

 

In addition to our headlining productions, BFP offers numerous other opportunities. All events are exclusive to members (unless stated otherwise).

 

As part of the onboarding process, all members participate in the Set 101 Workshop. This workshop serves as a basic “crash-course” for new filmmakers. It also ensures that every member of BFP can be confident in their ability to function on set.

 

This year, we will also be hosting multiple events monthly. These events are intended to build the knowledge base of our filmmakers and allow members the chance to connect with each other.

 

All events are exclusive to BFP members.

 

(2) Student-led Workshops: Educational workshop sessions hosted by experienced student filmmakers in their respective department.
(1) Industry Professional Guest Speaker
(1) Social Event

 

APPLICATION PROCESS

 

BFP accepts members annually at the beginning of the fall semester.

 

Steps to Apply

 

1. Attend an Info Session at the beginning of the fall semester.
2. Fill out and submit your application by the deadline.

 

If accepted…

 

3. Pay your member dues and join our GroupMe!
4. Attend the first Member Social, 24-Hour Race, and Set 101 Workshop.

 

NOTICE: We do NOT accept applications through HornsLink! We host our application process through Google Forms.

 

COMMUNICATION

 

Follow us on Instagram so you can stay up-to-date on everything we have going on or email us if you have any questions.
summary: Student Film Organization at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Christian Business Student Council
description: Christian Business Student Council (CBC) seeks to provide young professionals the space to glorify God through the exploration of fields, cultivation of talents, and integration of Christ and career. Through its speaker series, professional development workshops, and specialized technical training programs, CBC hopes to cultivate a community for Christian students to advance themselves toward success within their chosen professions for His glory.
summary: Christian Business Student Council (CBC) seeks to provide young professionals the space to glorify God through the exploration of fields, cultivation of talents, and integration of Christ and career.

name: Environmental Justice Collective
description: We are the Environmental Justice Collective (EJC), and we seek to protect people and the planet by empowering students to engage with the environmental movement through an intersectional perspective. Created in the Fall of 2018 by students of color, the EJC was born out of the need to center issues of race, class, gender, ability, and other social issues within environmentalism. We do this through educational workshops, community building events, volunteering, and integrating social and environmental justice into UT’s curriculum.
summary: The Environmental Justice Collective seeks to protect people and the planet by empowering students to engage with the environmental movement with an intersectional lens.

name: Probate, Guardianship Law, Estates, and Trusts Society
description: PGLETS seeks to connect law students with resources and practitioners regarding probate, estate planning, wealth management, guardianship, fiduciary litigation, and civil commitment law. PGLETS aims to inspire young attorneys to explore careers in probate practice, increase access to opportunities in these practice areas, and provide a point of contact between law students and the probate community. PGLETS also aims to create a strong culture of participation in pro bono and ad litem work among graduating probate attorneys, as well as provide grassroots legal education within the community about how awareness of these areas of the law can protect an individual’s financial security, wellbeing, and civil rights.
summary: The Probate Courts process matters involving the civil and property rights of deceased and incapacitated persons. PGLETS is the primary point of contact for Texas Law students to learn, network, and volunteer in the world of probate and estate planning.

name: Students of Caribbean Ancestry
description: Students of Caribbean Ancestry (SOCA) at The University of Texas at Austin seek to promote, share, and spread various cultures present throughout the Caribbean Sea. Members of SOCA, regardless of origin, share a common interest in wanting to gain a deeper understanding of Caribbean culture and strive to remain dedicated to promoting Caribbean culture and traditions in an inclusive and welcoming environment.
summary: Students Of Caribbean Ancestry spreads various cultures across the Caribbean Sea through meetings, socials, dance workshops, and more! SOCA welcomes all students with opened arms!

name: Texas embrACE
description: Texas embrACE is a support group for students on the asexual/aromantic spectrum as well as questioning individuals and allies. We have weekly meetings and occasional social activities.
summary: Texas embrACE is a support group for students on the asexual/aromantic spectrum as well as questioning individuals and allies.

name: Longhorn Legal Coalition
description:
The mission of Longhorn Legal Coalition (LLC) is to guide pre-law students through the discovery of law-related opportunities at the undergrad level and beyond. Through workshops, networking events, and mentorship opportunities, our members will build a strong foundation of professional skills while exploring various legal fields and industries. LLC is a space for like-minded, driven individuals to learn and grow together to reach their career goals.

 

PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT

Workshops

At General Meetings, LLC will host various Professional Development Workshops aimed at cultivating the skills necessary to succeed in all future academic and occupational endeavors. These seminars will be tailored towards pre-law students and future legal professionals. Some of the topics that will be covered include:

  • Professional communication
  • Résumé-building
  • LinkedIn profiles
  • Networking
  • Job searches
  • Cover letters
  • Internship applications
  • Interview preparation
  • Accepting an offer

Mentorship

We will also offer a mentorship program within LLC, where newer and more experienced members will connect in an environment that allows them to learn from each other. Mentees will be adopted by mentors with similar interests, who have advanced further in their professional pursuits and can offer perspective and advice to their mentees. Mentors will have the opportunity to advance their leadership abilities, while mentees will be in an advantageous position to learn how to succeed in their ventures.

 

CAREER EXPLORATION

Guest Panels

LLC strives to create a setting where undergraduate students can discover many of the diverse career paths available to them in the field of law. We will achieve this through inviting panels of legal professionals to speak about their work and industries, including, but not limited to:

  • Criminal
  • Entertainment
  • Government
  • Corporate
  • Immigration
  • Healthcare
  • Real Estate
  • In-House Counsel

In conjunction with professional development, LLC aims to provide access to internship opportunities in the legal field that are available to undergraduates. We will send announcements and connect students to these avenues as they arise.

 

Professor Lunches

Another unique amenity that LLC offers is Professor Lunches. We create schedules where Active Members can RSVP for time slots to sit and eat lunch with current law school professors! This will grant members insight into the law school experience and give them a chance to learn from a specialist in their industry.

 

LSAT

Partnership

LLC is proudly LSAT Demon’s Official Premier Partner at the University of Texas at Austin. Through this collaboration, our members unlock discounts on course content, access to a Score Improvement Challenge with reward incentives, and biweekly study sessions. LLC remains committed to providing our members with any and all pre-law resources, and our exclusive partnership with LSAT Demon is yet another way we are able to deliver on that promise.

 

PHILANTHROPY

Partnership

LLC has officially partnered with Court Appointed Special Advocates (CASA) of Travis County as our dedicated philanthropic cause. CASA's goal is to advocate for children who have experienced abuse or neglect by speaking up for their best interest in court, at school, and in our community. CASA educates and empowers community volunteers who ensure each child's needs remain a priority in an overburdened child welfare system. When the state steps in to protect a child's safety, a judge appoints one of our volunteer advocates to make independent and informed recommendations and help the judge decide what is best for the child.

 

Internal

Aside from our partnership, LLC also hosts our own independent events to support our local Austin community. We create an impact by preparing and donating home-cooked meals, cleaning up West Campus, writing letters to refugee children, and so much more.
summary: Longhorn Legal Coalition is an association of pre-law students aimed to cultivate professional skills and explore career opportunities through exclusive workshops, networking events, and mentorship opportunities.

name: Revive Texas Ministries
description:
Revive Texas puts on monthly events for students of all religious backgrounds to come and learn about Jesus. Our vision is threefold:

1) Unite and gather Christian students on campus

2) Reach the nonbeliever with the gospel

3) Empower students to take a next step with Jesus
summary: Revive Texas exists for all college students to experience Jesus Christ and enrich their relationship with Him.

name: Texas Materials Research Society
summary: The mission of Texas Materials Research Society (MRS) is to represent and further the interests of MSE graduate students and organize department-wide events to build community within the Texas Materials Institute.

name: Texas Pre-CAA Association
description: Texas Pre-CAA Association is a community for students interested in pursuing the Anesthesiologist Assistant profession. We are open to all students who are considering a future in the healthcare field. Our goal is to provide necessary information about AA schools, shadowing opportunities, and assistance with professional school applications.
summary: TPCA aims to provide prospective Certified Anesthesiologist Assistants (CAAs) students with resources, opportunities, and assistance in their pre-health journeys.

name: Longhorn Students For Israel
description: Longhorn Students for Israel serves as the primary, student-led channel for pro-Israel advocacy and engagement on campus, aiming to bring together various campus organizations and their student leaders to help form an informed and interconnected student population. Dedicated to educating and uniting students through a comprehensive understanding of the modern State of Israel, LSFI provides a holistic range of educational programs, speaker events, dialogic engagement, and uniform support for all pro-Israel activity on campus.
summary: Longhorn Students for Israel stands at the University of Texas at Austin, committed and dedicated to educating and uniting students through a comprehensive understanding of Israel by providing a diverse range of educational programs, speaker events

name: Texas Materials Scientists and Engineers
summary: TMSE aims to unite students from diverse majors with a passion for materials science and engineering, fostering a community where they can cultivate interests and skills, and ensuring access to crucial resources and knowledge for individual success.

name: American Statistical Association at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
At our association, we are passionate about nurturing a community of undergraduate students with an interest in statistics and data science.

Through workshops, we empower our members with practical skills in statistics and coding in different languages to aid in preparation for industry or graduate school aspirations. We also host social events to provide a platform for like-minded individuals to connect, share experiences, and forge lasting connections.

Meeting locations specified in the 'Upcoming Organization Events' section.
summary: ASA fosters a community of students curious to learn more about statistics and data science through fun social gatherings, workshops, and events or travel.

name: Interpreters in Healthcare
description: Interpreters in Healthcare is an organization that focuses on helping a population of patients who may not be able to communicate their needs, symptoms, etc. with healthcare providers. Our organization aims to help patients by translating for them in the healthcare setting.
summary: Interpreters in Healthcare aims to help patients by interpreting for them primarily in Spanish in the healthcare setting. We seek to become experienced interpreters by getting our certifications through this organization and effectively helping others.

name: Texas Forensics - Debate
description:
The purpose of Texas Forensics - Debate, or Texas Debate, is to support students competing in collegiate policy debate in the National Debate Tournament and Cross-Examination Debate Association circuit competitions.

To attend a meeting, please email Annemarie for date/time information (contact listed below)
summary: The purpose of Texas Forensics - Debate, or Texas Debate, is to support students competing in collegiate policy debate in the National Debate Tournament and Cross-Examination Debate Association circuit competitions.

name: American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative
description:
The mission of the American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative (ALCSI) is to expand awareness of, and improve access to, lung cancer screening for people at high risk. They pursue this goal through a blend of:

  • Community outreach: Empowering students (high school, college, medical) and chapters nationwide to raise awareness and connect eligible individuals with screening centers.

  • Advocacy: Collaborating on legislation (e.g., HR 1192, Senate resolutions) and public campaigns to support broader screening coverage and national recognition.

  • Research: Partnering in clinical studies (like INSPIRE) and promoting evidence-based guidelines to broaden screening eligibility criteria.

They execute this mission with a national structure that includes university chapters and regional directors steering grassroots actions, ranging from educational events, health fairs, to campaigns like “White Ribbon Relay” and “Plus One.” This blend of education, policy work, and research fosters early detection and health equity in lung cancer screening across the U.S.
summary: ALCSI raises awareness and improves access to lung cancer screening through outreach, advocacy, and research. They engage students, support chapters, host events, and push for policy change to promote early detection and health equity nationwide.

name: The International Society for Pharmaceutical Engineering
description:
ISPE is the global industry leader in connecting pharmaceutical knowledge to deliver manufacturing and supply chain innovation, operational excellence, and regulatory insights to enhance industry efforts to develop, manufacture, and reliably deliver quality medicines to patients.

ISPE seeks to provide solutions to complex pharmaceutical industry challenges through manufacturing innovation, member and workforce development, technical, regulatory, and compliance collaboration.
summary: The International Society for Pharmaceutical Engineering is a professional organization aimed at providing educational/career opportunities to those looking to pursue a career in pharmaceuticals or biotech.

name: The Ridge | College Ministry at the University of Texas at Austin
description: The Ridge | College Ministry is a college ministry branched from Austin Ridge Bible Church with the purpose of reaching college students on campus regardless of their association with Austin Ridge Bible Church.
summary: The Ridge | College Ministry is a college ministry branched from Austin Ridge Bible Church

name: Peer Assistance and Leadership Students
description: PALs (Peer Assistance and Leadership) intends/seeks to cultivate young leaders and create a peer-to-peer mentoring program between UT Austin students and area Austin high schools. As PALs students, we are determined to learn how to properly mentor younger students and establish a functional peer to peer program between high school and college students. The goal of PALs is to cultivate students to make a difference in their own communities and peers’ lives.
summary: PALs (Peer Assistance and Leadership) intends/seeks to cultivate young leaders and create a peer-to-peer mentoring program between UT Austin students and area Austin high schools.

name: Normandy Scholars Program
description:
The Frank Denius Normandy Scholar Program (NSP) studies the causes, conduct, consequences, and contemporary representations of the Second World War from multiple national perspectives.

 

The NSP brings together eighteen students from different backgrounds and different majors each spring semester who take the same five courses, share the same challenges, and travel together nearly a month in May with the NSP faculty.  Beyond the classroom discussions, the readings and paper assignments, the guest lectures, the film series, and the opportunity to visit WWII sites in Europe, perhaps the most striking feature of the program is the personal development and intellectual growth it fosters.
 
Students who meet the program requirements and are looking for an extraordinary educational experience are invited to inquire and apply.
summary: The Frank Denius Normandy Scholar Program (NSP) studies the causes, conduct, consequences, and contemporary representations of the Second World War from multiple national perspectives.

name: Alpha Sigma Phi
description: Alpha Sigma Phi is a nationally recognized and one of the oldest Greek fraternities that began at Yale University in 1845.
summary: Male fraternity

name: Lapis Ladies
description: Lapis Longhorns intends to be a space to empower, unite, and celebrate Jewish women on campus. Our mission is to create a place where female Jewish students have the ability to share the Jewish values of Hesed, Emet, and Kehila. Lapis Longhorns strives to promote each of the aforementioned values alongside academic excellence, mutual and emotional support, leadership, and lifelong friendships after our years on the Forty Acres.
summary: Lapis Longhorns intends to be a space to empower, unite, and celebrate Jewish women on campus. Our mission is to create a place where female Jewish students can share the Jewish values of Hesed and Kehila while forming lifelong friendships!

name: Pi Kappa Phi
description: To create an uncommon and lifelong brotherhood that develops leaders and encourages service to other for the betterment of our communities.
summary: To create an uncommon and lifelong brotherhood that develops leaders and encourages service to other for the betterment of our communities.

name: The Last Reformers
description:
Welcome to The Last Reformers, a non-denominational Christian Bible study club at UT Austin! Our mission is to create a space where students can dive into Scripture, ask tough questions, and grow in faith together.

Why Join Us?

  • Inclusive Community: Whether you’ve read the Bible your whole life or are opening it for the first time, you are welcome here.

  • Flexible Scheduling: We know college life is busy, so we offer flexible meeting times to accommodate your classes and exams.

  • Genuine Connection: Beyond study, we focus on building supportive friendships that last throughout your time at UT.

Feel free to reach out with any questions or just to say hello! We look forward to meeting you.
summary: A welcoming non-denominational Christian community at UT Austin dedicated to exploring the Bible together. Join us for engaging discussions and fellowship designed to fit a busy student’s schedule.

name: ColorStack at UT Austin
summary: We’re a tech nonprofit cultivating the strongest cross-campus community of its kind to deliver community building, academic support, and career development opportunities at scale.

name: Children's Oral Health Advocacy Project
description: Our mission is to educate the youth community by traveling to different elementary, middle, and high schools in the Austin area. We envision a world where every individual has the opportunity to lead a healthy life, regardless of their circumstances.
summary: Children's Oral Health Advocacy Project (COHAP) is a pre-dental organization at The University of Texas at Austin that serves the children of Austin. We dedicate our service to illuminating the path to healthier communities through oral hygiene education

name: Sports and Entertainment Law Society
description: The Sports and Entertainment Law Society at the University of Texas seeks to connect UT students with opportunities for networking, employment, and education in the fields of sports and entertainment law.
summary: The Sports and Entertainment Law Society at the University of Texas seeks to connect UT students with opportunities for networking, employment, and education in the fields of sports and entertainment law.

name: Black Ambitious Women Supporting Excellence
description: At BAWSE, (Brilliance, Ambition, Wisdom, Support, and Excellence) we stand as a beacon of inspiration and solidarity for every black woman on campus. Our mission is to nurture the inherent and brilliant, fuel the boundless ambition and guide, with the collective wisdom of our community. We extend  unwavering support, fostering an environment where every voice is heard, every dream is valued , and every journey is honored.
summary: At BAWSE, (Brilliance, Ambition, Wisdom, Support and Excellence) we stand as a beacon of inspiration and solidarity for every black woman on campus.

name: Indigo Collective@UT
description:
What is the Indigo Collective? A tight-knit community that produces creative work under a universally-applicable collective theme.

  • Purpose:
    • To educate over lesser-known topics from a variety of angles under the theme.
    • To foster inspiration, care, and awareness to the public.
    • To reduce barriers for access and production of creative work.
    • To build dedicated networking opportunities for the broader community.
    • To carve a space that takes care of each other and equitably provides a safe place for expression.

 

  • Goals:
    • Provide support and some amount of funding to a yield of {X} projects started in the collective.
  • Work out collaborative partnerships with various organizations, businesses, and firms to build continuous funding and services for our collective members to use.
  • Accept all potential and interested candidates who exhibit they would be a best-fit for the collective. 
  • Use our non-profit as outreach and build opportunities for Austin-non-UT creatives to participate in the work the collective does. 
  • Provide future employment opportunities to creatives broadly through the non-profit to support and foster an environment where it is possible to follow creative passions in the midst of an environment that devalues creative work
  • Produce creative work under the organization that deviates away from commoditization and focuses on its meaning and intentionality 
  • Build accountability measures in place through our Resolution and Compliance branches.
  • Each member will be seen as equal to each other, regardless of the work they do.

 

The collective will have two branches, Logistics, and Resolution and Compliance. A member in one of these branches cannot be a member of the other branch to avoid conflicts of interest.

 

  • Logistics:
    • Responsible for the functioning of the organization. 
    • Takes care of background procedural work in regards to budgeting, marketing, working with community members/external organizations, and providing production support to projects. 
    • There will be a position called “Collective Organizer”, which is solely responsible for the coordination of the branch and initiating tasks. Other than that, they are an equal member of the branch. 
    • In any event a policy or action done by logistics is investigated by Resolution and Compliance, the final decision by R&C will be binding.




  • Resolution and Compliance:
    • Responsible for resolving disputes inside of the organization.
    • This branch will be responsible for reviewing policies, complying with relevant policies in the event of notification, and any and all reports made to the organization.
    • For every and all reports, a proper investigation will take place. Assignment of investigators (members of the branch) will attempt to choose non-conflicting members.
    • There will be a position called “Lead Adjudicator”, which is responsible for coordinating the branch and making final resolutions in the event a dispute was appealed with sufficient reason and evidence. 
    • This branch is not equipped to investigate claims it does not have governance over (see: Title IX investigations), nor making decisions that have involved other outside agencies. In any event, it will defer investigation and decisions to outside governing agencies accordingly, and a determination made will be the recommendation the branch uses for its own decision.
summary: A tight-knit community that produces creative work under a universal theme to educate over lesser known topics, to foster public inspiration, to increase access to creative work production, and build a safe equitable environment for all types of students

name: Wax Magazine
description:
WAX is an Austin-based literary zine, a community for writers, and a creative exercise -- a space to color outside the lines. We produce a biannual literary/art zine (one issue a semester). Our publication philosophy encourages members and contributors to exercise broad creative freedom and to playfully explore their crafts beyond their own comfort zones. We emphasize fiction, but we accept any genres/topics.

WAX also seeks to curate a space for local writers and artists to mingle, share resources, and learn together through monthly workshops. We aim to provide opportunities for sharing and improving writing/drawing in a stress-free and casual environment.
summary: WAX is an Austin-based literary zine, a community for writers, and a creative exercise -- a space to color outside the lines. We produce a biannual literary/art zine.

name: TableTalk
description:
TableTalk is an initiative to help people figure out the lives they want to live and develop the communication skills to live them well. We meet, have discussions about thought-provoking films, and routinely hold public speaking workshops.

Meetings are every other Wednesday, 6:00 - 9:00 pm in Joynes Room A.

Join our group me (where we are most active) + follow us on instagram!

Join our slack for event announcements, meeting summaries, and more.
summary: TableTalk is an initiative to help people figure out the lives they want to live and develop the communication skills to live them well. We meet to have inspired discussions about thought-provoking films and routinely hold public speaking workshops.

name: Students Demand Action
description:
We shouldn’t have to worry about getting shot while grocery shopping, working, jogging, sitting in class, worshipping, watching a movie, walking to school, or sleeping. Students Demand Action is a non partisan group working towards a future free from gun violence, and your voice and energy can help us get there.

We are powered by the commitment and leadership of students all around the country. We welcome all young people to join us in taking action against gun violence.
summary: We’re young activists committed to ending gun violence in our communities. We’ve grown up in the midst of America’s gun violence crisis. We know that gun violence is preventable and will rise up on campus to help end it.

name: GIVE at The University of Texas at Austin
shortName: GIVE at Texas, GIVE at UT/ UT Austin, or GIVE at Austin
description: GIVE at UT Austin seeks to increase awareness of GIVE Volunteers and fulfill our mission to empower global citizens and ignite sustainable change worldwide. We want to create a safe space for students to discuss global issues at our meetings. Our goal is to promote sustainable volunteer efforts, globally minded initiatives, environmentally conscious lifestyles, and expand our comfort zones through adventures. We hope to provide students with the opportunity to participate in local volunteer events, group discussions, and social events that allow them to learn and build a community here at UT Austin. We also want to encourage students to take the leap and potentially become more involved with GIVE Volunteers by providing them with support and resources about the organization and its opportunities.
summary: Our goal is to create an environment where like-minded people can come together, thrive, dive into their curiosities about the world, and make a good impact. We seek to provide sustainable volunteer opportunities and collaborate with other student orgs.

name: Vertical Flight Society at UT
summary: We are an organization focused on designing and building a scale, vertical take-off aircraft to participate in the annual VFS Design, Build, Vertical Fly competition. We also host speakers and attend indsutry-specific networking events!

name: Girls Club of Texas at Austin
description: Girls Club is an inclusive community for women in sports. We strive to provide a welcoming, safe, fun place for women and girls in a space that has historically been male-dominated and where women have been underrepresented. In our organization, members can broaden their knowledge, acquire new skills, connect with peers, and find a sense of community within the university-level sports industry. Our organization is a sisterhood united by a common goal: navigating an industry that has historically been inaccessible to women.
summary: Girls Club of Texas at Austin is an organization focused on creating a supportive space and network for women in sports! For us, this can mean that you work in sports, play sports, or just enjoy watching sports!

name: Community Engagement Collective
description: The Community Engagement Collective ("The Collective") is a network of students passionate about community engagement and service, and serves as a gateway to connecting students to the Austin community so that they can also create meaningful impact while at UT Austin. As a sponsored student organization within the Volunteerism and Service-Learning office of the Office of the Dean of Students, the Collective oversees two signature programs - The Project and Alternative Breaks - as well as coordinates civic engagement and general service events for students. Our organization is non-hierarchical and works as a shared partnership of goals and responsibilities. Members are able to join of ten committees: Civic Involvement, Service, Trips, Culture, Outreach, Development, Operations, Volunteer Management, Logistics, or Community Relations.
summary: The Community Engagement Collective is a network of students passionate about community engagement and service. Our vision is that all UT students can be active citizens of the Austin community while enrolled and can create meaningful impact.

name: Longhorn Digital Marketing
description: Longhorn Digital Marketing is a student collective designed to uplift and educate the University of Texas community to pursue careers in the digital marketing space.
summary: Longhorn Digital Marketing is a student collective designed to uplift and educate the University of Texas community to pursue careers in the digital marketing space.

name: Texas Swedish Club
description:
The Texas Swedish Club provides a setting in which those interested can get to know their classmates on a more personal basis while practicing Swedish. Students enrolled in the Swedish course are encouraged to meet on their own outside of class for fika (the Swedish word for ‘coffee + conversation’). In addition, the Club provides events, such as film screenings and general meetings, for students to meet and talk with other UT students, including those who have previously taken Swedish and those who are interested in learning Swedish in the future.

To keep in touch join our GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/104935641/AxUG1rVI 

Meetings will be held from 12-1pm at BUR 234 every other Friday starting September 5th, 2025!
summary: The Texas Swedish Club provides events, such as film screenings and general meetings, for Swedish students to meet and talk with other UT students, including those who have previously taken Swedish and those who are interested in learning in the future.

name: Zine Workshop & Education Club
description: The Zine Workshop & Education Club provides an immersive experience for its members, delving into the rich history and evolution of zines (specifically in punk culture, feminist movements, advocacy of marginalized groups, and political issues). In addition to learning about the origins and cultural significance of zines, club members will also be actively involved in the hands-on process of crafting their own zines through collage, drawing, and other mediums. Through this interactive and engaging approach, participants have the opportunity to not only gain knowledge about zines but also to express their creativity, special interests, and individual perspectives by producing their own unique zines.
summary: The Zine Workshop & Education Club immerses members in the history and evolution of zines, while simultaneously providing a space where folks of all skill levels and majors can create their own zines.

name: Legal and Social Work Alliance Journal
description:
The Legal and Social Work Alliance Journal intends to foster interdisciplinary collaboration with the shared goal of informative outreach. Functioning as an editorial based partnership, social work students and pre-law students will study and share the injustices children face in the foster care system through a legal and social lens. This organization gives its members invaluable research experience while further developing strong analytical writing skills.

Regardless of major, we urge all students interested in law and/or child advocacy to apply to both writing and editing positions. It’s important to recognize the worth of different skill sets uniting for a common purpose. 

Active members will attend biweekly meetings where writing and editing contributions will be shared and assessed. The executive committee will lead these meetings. To respect our members' time, our meetings will be enriching and efficient.
summary: Functioning as an editorial based partnership, social work students and pre-law students will study and share the injustices children face in the foster care system through a legal and social lens.

name: Alchemind Texas
description:
Alchemind brings together students passionate about psychiatry and neuroscience, fostering a close-knit community dedicated to destigmatizing addiction and mental health disorders. What sets us apart is our vibrant community culture, where members collaborate to educate both students and the public on cognitive development and often misunderstood topics in addiction and mental health. Through interactive seminars, expert guest panels, and community service, we actively engage with these critical issues while integrating essential resources on campus for UT students. Alchemind isn’t just a group; it’s a united community where ambitious individuals connect with one another through intimate events such as socials, peer mentorships, and networking opportunities. Together, we create a supportive environment that shares a passion for these sciences and takes meaningful action to address misrepresentation in the field.

Our community emphasizes cognitive development/wellness and psychiatry/destigmatization. The former primarily focuses on teaching members and the public how to scientifically keep their mind healthy. The latter strives to educate students on the complexities and correlations between addiction and mental illness, working to accurately reverse issues that are often misrepresented.
summary: Alchemind unites students passionate about psychiatry and neuroscience, working to destigmatize addiction and mental health. Our workshops, peer mentorships, and networking events, create a vibrant community culture that builds long term connections.

name: Texas Helping Hand
description:
Our mission is to foster a dedicated student community committed to practical public service, organizing students to volunteer and advocate with local Austin nonprofits assisting the unhoused. We aim to address service gaps and contribute meaningful solutions to the challenges faced by selected nonprofits, building a strong network of students passionate about making a tangible impact in our community.


By staying closely connected with frontline organizations, we aim to support their efforts to provide shelter, food, and clean clothing to the unhoused, helping make Austin a city that addresses homelessness with compassionate, thoughtful solutions. The organizations we will be working with include Keep Austin Fed, Texas Homeless Network, and Mission Accomplished.

Beyond direct service, we take a deeper approach through policy analysis and research into the root causes of homelessness. Our Meet Your Neighbor project furthers this mission by interviewing unhoused Austinites about their hobbies, favorite foods, and personal stories, humanizing their experiences and challenging the stigma surrounding homelessness in our city.


For our members, Texas Helping Hand is also a space for personal growth. Students are encouraged to develop leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills while gaining hands-on experience that prepares them for their careers. By aligning personal growth with meaningful social impact, they gain hands-on experience that prepares them for their careers, empowers them to grow as future leaders, and allows them to make a tangible difference in their community.
summary: Texas Helping Hand organizes students to support Austin nonprofits addressing homelessness. Through volunteering, fundraising, and tackling service gaps, we help nonprofits focus on their mission to provide critical services to those in need.

name: Table Tennis Club
description:
We are a friendly table tennis club welcome to all skill levels! Come out to our regular meetups, casual games, and friendly tournaments to improve your skills, meet new friends, and have fun playing one of the most exciting sports around!

Check us out on Instagram and join the groupme for consistent updates and/or discussion!!

https://www.instagram.com/tabletennisclub_ttc/
summary: We are a friendly table tennis club welcoming all skill levels! Come out to our regular meetups, casual games, and friendly tournaments to improve your skills, meet new friends, and have fun playing one of the most exciting sports around!

name: Design in Health
description:
Because the course-load for the pre-health pathway is so demanding, and the extracurriculars and jobs that are expected to build a strong applicant resume are so time consuming, many students neglect their passion for art and creativity. With this lack of time, it is often overwhelming for pre-health students to join organizations for artists who are highly technically skilled, and are often taking courses that teach them these techniques. In addition, art minors require portfolios that many pre-health students do not have, and to make one would indicate extensive time commitment. 

In frustration of this lack of opportunity, we found the Masters in Design in Health program in the College of Fine Arts, whose mission speaks deeply to our personal philosophies of how art is so incredibly vital in communication within medicine. From systems of interactions between doctors and patients, to architecture and interior design, art interlinks the emotions of the human experience to the physical environment patients are affected by in a healthcare setting. With the advancement of automated technologies and decreasing human interaction, creativity is a necessity in recognizing the humanity of both patients and healthcare workers. Subjects such as the importance of human centered design, how creativity and design is vital to productivity, organization, and the growth of workers, and environmental design, such as hospital architecture and interior design affects how patients heal and their mental health, are just some of topics we would hope to educate our members about. 

We will host guest speakers, craft socials, art/music therapy volunteer opportunities, and a semester project of a zine on a topic in design in health!
summary: A partnership with the Design in Health Masters program in the College of Fine Arts, DIH emphases the vitality of creativity and human centered design in healthcare.

name: Swear2Care at UT
