description: Swear2Care at UT Austin is a student-led chapter of the national Swear2Care initiative, dedicated to supporting college students' mental health and well-being. Through engaging and restorative activities like peer connection programs, creative expression, and community service, we aim to reduce loneliness and foster a stronger sense of belonging on campus.
summary: Swear2Care at UT Austin supports college students' mental health through peer connections, creative expression, and advocacy, fostering a sense of belonging and reducing loneliness on campus.

name: Mens Basketball Club
description: A platform for competitive basketball enthusiasts at the University of Texas at Austin
summary: Mens Club Basketball is dedicated to providing a competitive platform for basketball enthusiasts at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Celebration Young Adults at the University of Texas at Austin
summary: CYA is a co-ed Bible study group under the Young Adult ministry at Celebration Church. Our mission is to foster a community where young adults can deepen their faith, grow their understanding of God's Word, and build meaningful relationships.

name: Criminal Law Association
description: Criminal Law Association is an organization for students to gain more knowledge on civil rights, racial equality, and the criminal justice system. If you are passionate about the legal field and want to learn more about criminal law, this org is for you!
summary: Criminal Law Association is an organization for students to gain more knowledge on civil rights, racial equality, and the criminal justice system. If you are passionate about the legal field and want to learn more about criminal law, this org is for you!

name: Texas Cellos @ ATX
description:
Founded in 2022 as a self-led group of cellists from the UTUO cello section, Texas Cellos @ ATX has grown into a vibrant cello choir ensemble, primarily composed of non-music major cellists from across the University of Texas. Our mission is to create a supportive and fun community, welcoming cellists from diverse fields and skill levels to celebrate our shared passion for music and cello playing. While we focus on the joy of playing together, we also take pride in our high-performance standards. As a cello choir, we are part of what many consider to be one of the most harmonious and powerful ensemble types, showcasing a unique sound that only a same-instrument group can create. Our repertoire includes both original and arranged pieces, from classical masterpieces to contemporary pop.

We are currently accepting applications for Spring 2026! See our LinkTree for more details
Any questions about the org feel free to email cellos.at.texas@gmail.com
summary: Texas Cellos at Austin is a dynamic cello choir featuring mostly non-music majors from UT fostering a fun, supportive community for cellists across multiple majors, celebrating our passion through high-quality performances of classical and pop music.

name: Meow Mates
description: Meow Mates is a student organization with a mission to promote the welfare of stray cats around the UT campus. We offer students volunteering and fundraising opportunities to assist in the care of our campus cats, in addition to socials to meet other members in a fun and welcoming group sharing a passion for animal welfare. We are also committed to educating our community about responsible and humane pet care to enrich the lives of both animals and people. All cat-loving people are welcome to join our general meetings and events! Come make a difference in our community.
summary: Meow Mates is a volunteer organization dedicated to assisting the stray cat population on campus and promoting stray cat welfare in general.

name: Plan II Film Club
description: A film club for Honors and non-Honors students to meet and watch and discuss films together.
summary:
Basement film screenings.
Basement conversations. Basement people.
Everyone is welcome. Every Sunday in Joynes.

name: West Campus Collective
description: WCC launched Fall 2023 and adopted a mutual aid framework to decrease community food waste while feeding students who experience food insecurity; meals are outsourced from organizations' food surplus and repurposed after hours at no cost to university students. Solves inaccessibility to food for university students who are economically disadvantaged and seeks to narrow barriers like these which affect energy levels, overall health, and scholastic performance, set to feed 1,000 students in a semester. Working under a mutual aid model that defies socioeconomic status divisions and incorporates the collaboration of co-op housing, Greek life, student organizations, and the general population of UT students.
summary: WCC launched Fall 2023 and adopted a mutual aid framework to decrease community food waste while feeding students who experience food insecurity; meals are outsourced from the organization's food surplus and repurposed after hours at no cost to students.

name: The Longhorn Design Lab
description: The Longhorn Design Lab is a community of engineers and designers that make products that are truly human centered. Unburdened by the profitability constraints most firms are forced to adhere to, LDL members focus on making physical products that solve problems in people's lives.
summary: The Longhorn Design Lab is a student-led product design firm that focuses primarily on providing pro-bono design solutions for organizations in the Austin area, as well also supporting in-house personal projects pitched by student members.

name: The International Association of Business Communicators Chapter at the University of Texas at Austin
description: The International Association of Business Communicators Chapter at the University of Texas at Austin serves as a professional student-led organization dedicated to the success of the Longhorn community across sectors of the business communications field.
summary: The International Association of Business Communicators at the University of Texas at Austin chapter serves as a professional student-led organization dedicated to the success of the Longhorn community across sectors of the business communications field.

name: Cassidy Joined for Hope at the University of Texas
description: The Cassidy Joined for Hope at the University of Texas organization seeks to prevent young adult suicide. We are dedicated to providing resources, support, and education to the UT Austin community. Suicide prevention requires a community-wide effort, starting with students. By challenging the stigma and breaking the silence, we initiate life-saving conversations. Our goal is to educate students and faculty about warning signs through mental health awareness events. The Cassidy Joined for Hope Foundation raises awareness through student-led ‘Joined for Hope’ Clubs. The Cassidy Joined for Hope at the University of Texas organization promotes hope, kindness, and open discussions about mental health. We extend awareness through speaking engagements and community events designed to Break the Silence on suicide.
summary: Cassidy Joined for Hope at the University of Texas is a mental health and suicide awareness organization aimed at providing resources, educating, and supporting the students of UT. We promote hope, kindness, and open discussions about mental health.

name: Sustainable Building Initiative
description:
The Sustainable Building Initiative (SBI) is a professionally driven, student-led consultancy founded at the University of Texas at Austin in 2024. Born from a passion to solve real-world infrastructure challenges, SBI unites students from every academic discipline to function as a single, cohesive company. We partner with clients to engineer, design, and develop innovative building projects.
 
As a student organization, SBI aims to drive innovation in sustainable infrastructure and empower the next generation of industry leaders. Our mission is to provide hands-on consultancy experience that delivers tangible value to our clients while developing our members' practical skills. We bridge the gap between academic theory and professional practice, tackling the technical, environmental, and economic aspects of sustainable development.
 
Our vision and goals for SBI are to become the premier student-led consultancy for sustainable development in the region. In the short term, we provide unparalleled hands-on learning experiences through our departmental project work in engineering, architecture, finance, and more. Long term, our goal is to expand our project portfolio, advance cutting-edge research in sustainable materials and design, and advocate for green building practices that serve society.
 
Become a part of our dynamic and growing organization at The University of Texas at Austin to gain invaluable, real-world skills and help shape a more sustainable future. Join a mission-driven team dedicated to making a tangible impact, one project at a time.

Submit our team application form: https://forms.gle/qzciHKXKUUkVZHwSA
Join the Discord to stay updated: https://discord.com/invite/869DjwYvd5
summary:
Research Driven, Professionally Inspired, and Student Powered
Ran by all majors and a student-led consultancy that designs and engineers real-world sustainable building solutions for clients. With a project portfolio valued at over $25M

name: Texas Perfect Pair
description: Perfect Pair fosters one-on-one, intergenerational connections between older adults and college students. We strive to reinforce a mutual sense of community and purpose for our pairs: a goal that underlies our efforts to combat loneliness and improve overall health and wellbeing. We achieve this by ensuring that our pair connections are personalized: based on shared background and interests. Simultaneously, we encourage pairs to re-engage with their passions by offering individualized and accessible programming. We hope for older adults and students to feel that their life perspective and quality of life have been meaningfully enhanced by their experience in our program.
summary: We pair student volunteers with seniors at assisted living homes based on similar backgrounds, experiences, and hobbies to foster a meaningful intergenerational connection that combats loneliness and improves overall health and well-being.

name: Pharmacology and Toxicology Social and Outreach Committee
summary: The Pharmacology and Toxicology Social and Outreach Committee is group dedicated to advancing science education in local Austin schools. In addition to our educational outreach, we are known for organizing social events to foster a sense of community.

name: Texas Big Dippers
description: Two-Step and Country Swing at the University of Texas at Austin
summary: @txbigdippers on Instagram

name: Texas Society of Professional Engineers at the University of Texas at Austin
description: Texas Society of Professional Engineers (TSPE) at the University of Texas at Austin is a professional engineering organization dedicated to educating students on the importance and process of becoming a licensed professional engineer. As a student chapter of TSPE, we provide valuable opportunities through guest lectures, interactive committees, and social and networking events. Our members gain valuable knowledge on the licensing process, and valuable networking and leadership skills. Join us to connect with your peers, enhance your skills, and pave the way for a successful engineering career!
summary: Texas Society of Professional Engineers (TSPE) at the University of Texas at Austin is a professional engineering organization open to all engineering disciplines, and focused on educating students about the importance and process of becoming licensed.

name: Texas Writing Society
description: Texas Writing Society is the premier writing organization that is preserving a student writing community through events, speakers, and collaborative projects. Join the Slack to be kept updated about events and projects!
summary: Texas Writing Society is the premier writing organization that is creating and preserving a student writing community through events, speakers, and collaborative projects.

name: Women in Sports & Entertainment
description: Women In Sports & Entertainment (WISE) was created for women of all backgrounds, experiences, and identities who are passionate about making their mark in sports and entertainment industries that have long lacked accessible spaces where women feel seen, supported, and prioritized. Our purpose is to unify and uplift by creating a community where ambition is encouraged, leadership is shared, and growth both personal and professional is always within reach. What sets us apart is our commitment to accessibility, with no dues, no required meetings, and no barriers to entry. By simply showing up, members gain access to exclusive opportunities, hands-on projects, internships, and lasting connections. From professional development and creative collaborations to socials and resume-ready experiences, everything we do is interactive, authentic, and designed to support women in stepping into spaces where they belong.
summary: Women In Sports & Entertainment is a student-run organization dedicated to advancing women’s careers in the traditionally male-dominated fields of sports and entertainment. WISE provides opportunities to support their professional development in college.

name: Baháʼí Club at the University of Texas
description: Welcome to Bahá'í Club! Our mission is to create a vibrant and inclusive community based on universal principles such as unity in diversity, the nobility of each individual, and independent investigation of the truth. We aim to provide a space for students of all backgrounds and beliefs to learn about the Bahá'í Faith and engage in meaningful and uplifting conversations and service to the community.
summary: Our mission is to create a vibrant and inclusive community that will provide a space for students of all backgrounds and beliefs to learn about the Bahá'í Faith and engage in meaningful and uplifting conversations and service to the community.

name: The New Project
description: The New Project is a multi-media creative community founded on the bases of a new inclusive creative environment. The New Project supports and promotes its members through creative projects, networking and communal events, and developmental workshops.
summary: The New Project is a multi-media creative community founded on the bases of a new inclusive creative environment. The New Project supports and promotes its members through creative projects, networking and communal events, and developmental workshops.

name: Texas Energy Consulting
summary: Student run energy consulting group that offers members opportunities to gain practical knowledge in the industry through pro-bono projects, case competitions, mentorship programs, and more.

name: Horns Sports Debate
description: The Sports Debate Society is a dynamic club for sports enthusiasts who love to discuss, analyze, and celebrate their favorite games. Focused on soccer, basketball, and football, our club offers exciting opportunities like weekly debates, game watch parties, and friendly competitions through app-based point systems (club-only). We foster connections among members, especially freshmen, through shared sports passion and intramural team interest. Our recognition programs, such as Debater of the Month, and leadership roles—Game Analyst, Event Organizer, Debate Coordinator—help members build resumes and develop valuable skills. Join us to connect, compete, and cheer together!
summary: The Sports Debate Society brings fans together to debate soccer, basketball, and football; enjoy watch parties; and compete in app-based challenges. It’s a great place to meet people, share your passion for sports, and build lasting friendships.

name: Longhorn GeoGuessr
description:
At Longhorn GeoGuessr, you'll be able to learn how to guess professionally in the game. We'll show you how to look at a location's geography, culture, and infrastructure and recognize where you are in the world. With the Duels feature of the game, you'll be able to test your skills against your friends and compete in intercollegiate tournaments free of charge, making the game accessible to everyone interested.

 

Join this Discord server if you'd like to be a part of this org!

https://discord.gg/b75uavKdEk
summary: Longhorn GeoGuessr is a community for people passionate in GeoGuessr and geography. This club will teach members how to play professionally and create a recreational and social space to learn more about the world.

name: Lifelong Learning with Friends at the University of Texas at Austin
description: Lifelong Learning with Friends is a 501(c)3 organization that provides inclusive educational and social programs to both UT Austin students and adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities. For over a decade, over 1,700 UT Austin volunteers have participated in our programs, paving the way for special needs adults to be included in higher education opportunities.
summary: Lifelong Learning with Friends is a 501(c)3 organization that provides inclusive educational and social programs to both UT Austin students and adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: Hispanic Pre-Dental Society
description: The Hispanic Pre-Dental Society intends to build a community of undergraduate students from any background seeking dentistry as a professional career.  We aim to provide support, resources, tips, and guidance for our members that they can use to enhance their experience and knowledge.  As a society, we will encourage academic success and involvement in the Austin community, along with the encouragement and celebration of our cultural backgrounds.  We are committed to breaking barriers and stigmas often viewed in dentistry as well as making a great impact on the future of Hispanic dentists.
summary: The Hispanic Pre-Dental Society fosters diversity in the dental field by supporting students through applications with resources and mentorship while giving back through volunteering to aid the community.

name: Changing Health, Attitudes and Actions to Recreate Girls
description: The idea behind CHAARG is simple: a group of students on campus that try new workouts together every week. We believe that working out can [+ should!] be fun ++ we know that working out is a lot better with friends! CHAARG Chapters quickly turned into groups that do a lot more than workout together – socials, formals, + retreats have created true communities on campus that lead to lifelong friendships.
summary: The idea behind CHAARG is simple: a group of students on campus that try new workouts together every week. We believe that working out can [+ should!] be fun ++ we know that working out is a lot better with friends! CHAARG Chapters quickly turned into gr

name: Pediatric Pharmacy Association
description: The mission of the Pediatric Pharmacy Association is to advance pediatric pharmacy practice, support the health and wellbeing of children, and promote safe and effective medication use in children through Collaboration, Advocacy, Research, and Education.
summary: The mission of the Pediatric Pharmacy Association is to advance pediatric pharmacy practice, support the health and wellbeing of children, and promote safe and effective medication use in children through Collaboration, Advocacy, Research, and Education.

name: Longhorn Lemons Racing - Orange Dames
description: Intro Powerpoint
summary: Longhorn Lemons Racing-Orange Dames is an engineering organization focused on racing $500 cars in the 24 Hours of Lemons series. We emphasize safety and motorsports education while not taking ourselves too seriously.

name: Run for St. Jude
description: RFSJ is a student-led run club that also fundraises for the annual St. Jude Austin Walk. Our group runs are 3 miles and take place on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday evenings. No running experience is needed, and beginners are strongly encouraged to join. Our pace groups usually include the following times: 8:45, 9:45, 10:45. All communication takes place on GroupMe, so interested members should click on the following link: https://groupme.com/join_group/112117105/oRa2suE9
summary: RFSJ is a student-led run club that also fundraises for the St. Jude Austin Walk. Our group runs are 3 miles and take place on Monday, Wednesday, and Thursday evenings. No running experience is needed, and beginners are strongly encouraged to join.

name: The Oral Care Project
description: Our mission is to bridge the gap between financial barriers and the prioritization of oral health. Through donation drives and fundraising efforts, and working alongside community outlets, we aim to diminish oral health disparities by making essential oral care more accessible to those in need.
summary: We bridge the gap between financial barriers and the prioritization of oral health. Through donation drives and fundraising efforts, and working alongside community outlets, we aim to diminish oral health disparities by making oral care accessible.

name: Health and Society Collective
description: The Health and Society Collective is a student organization at The University of Texas at Austin that fosters community among Health and Society majors while welcoming students from all majors interested in the connections between health, culture, policy, and inequality. We offer opportunities for leadership, community service, and professional development to help students explore diverse perspectives on health and work toward their academic and career goals.
summary: Health and Society Collective aims to build community among Health and Society majors and welcomes all students interested in the social, cultural, and policy aspects of health through opportunities for leadership, service, and professional development.

name: Creative Writers at UT
description: We are a community of student creative writers open to all mediums (fiction, nonfiction, poetry, screenwriting, etc.). We provides fun opportunities to share and receive feedback on personal projects, network with other writers, and get connected with resources such as writing competitions, internships, and networking and publication opportunities.
summary: Community of creative writers at UT.

name: Longhorn Splatoon
description:
Welcome to Longhorn Splatoon! Whether you're a casual player, have played competitively, or even aren't quite sure what Splatoon is, not to worry! We aim to foster an inclusive, welcoming environment that is open to all skill and interest levels. If you're interested in becoming a general member, simply click the "Join" button on this page!

 

Most of our announcements about events, tournaments, etc. are communicated through our Discord server. Be sure to join here! https://discord.com/invite/7MbxscbbQX

 

Other important information can also be found on our Linktree: https://linktr.ee/longhornsplatoon

 

If you are interested in playing with us competitively and/or want to join us for in-person tournaments, tryouts for the competitive teams will be held at the start of the fall and spring semesters. No prior experience is necessary! See the Discord for more details.

 

Additionally, if you are interested in helping out or getting involved with Longhorn Splatoon, feel free to DM one of our officers on Discord!
summary: Longhorn Splatoon is a social, gaming organization that aims to foster a welcoming environment for anyone that is interested in the Splatoon game series! General meetups where we hang out and play Splatoon are held every other Friday.

name: Texas Supports Live Music
description:
In the Live Music Capital of the World, students deserve a space to dive into the live music scene. Whether you are a musician, a creative, or just curious about how shows come together, there is a place for you at Texas Supports Live Music. If you have a skill or want to try something new, you are welcome to join (no experience required!). We host jam sessions, put on on-campus shows, and regularly bring in guest speakers to share insight into every side of the live music world for both musicians and non-musicians.

This is a great organization to join if you want to network with student musicians, connect with other creatives, and be part of a community that is passionate about live music. Members get hands-on experience with everything it takes to put on a show, from forming bands and naming projects to designing flyers, logos, and other visual branding, as well as producing sound, setting up stages, running events, and tearing down. Through collaboration and real-world experience, members develop skills, confidence, and knowledge about navigating the live music scene while contributing to the music culture in ATX.

If you'd like to learn more, be sure to follow us on Instagram (@txsl.m) and stay alert for tabling dates and recruitment meetings!
summary: What's up! Want to be part of the live music scene without needing tons of experience? Join TSLM to play, create, and work together on real student-led shows.

name: The Global Business and Economics Club
description:
Join the UT Global Business and Economics Club!

Each meeting features guest speakers who discuss how their business or economics degree has influenced their career while offering valuable advice to college students as they navigate their careers. Past speakers have included executives from Salesforce, Silicon Valley, PwC, and the Jackson Walker Law Firm.

Contact mb68996@my.utexas.edu for more information.
summary: The Global Business and Economics Club seeks to enable its members to help each other better understand global business and economic issues, build their global network and explore career opportunities together.

name: Texas Arts for Aid
description:
Texas Arts for Aid is a student organization that combines creativity with community service, using sustainable arts and crafts to make a meaningful impact on marginalized communities throughout Austin. Through multiple service workshops each semester, we create projects that directly benefit those in need, from holiday cards for senior centers to educational art kits for underserved schools, always emphasizing the use of sustainable and upcycled materials. Our organization builds community through bi-weekly general meetings, collaborative workshops, and social events that unite artists and crafters across campus, while our regular fundraising initiatives like student art marketplaces and craft fairs support ongoing service projects.

 

Check out our Instagram! @texasafa
summary: Texas Arts for Aid is a student organization that serves marginalized Austin communities through sustainable arts and crafts practices. Members unite to create positive social impact by repurposing materials, sharing artistic skills, and fostering care.

name: Texas German Club
description: Hallo! We are a student-run club for anyone interested in the German language and culture. We generally hold weekly meetings centered on facets of German culture, as well as "Stammtische" where students can practice their German and eat German-inspired cuisine.
summary: The Texas German Club offers a network for students interested in German language and culture.

name: Texas Marketrics - Quantitative Finance
summary: Texas Marketrics is a student organization dedicated to fostering a community of students passionate about quantitative finance. Our goal is to equip members with advanced technical skills in finance by applying mathematics, statistics, and programming

name: Texas Women in Investing
description: Texas Women in Investing will provide a space for everyone, specifically women, to learn about personal finances, wealth management, and overall investing skills to help teach others the importance of investing.
summary: Texas Women in Investing will provide a space for everyone, specifically women, to learn about personal finances, wealth management, and overall investing skills to help teach others the importance of investing.

name: Longhorn League of Legends
description:
Welcome to Longhorn League of Legends, your home for casual and competitive League of Legends here at UT Austin! Whether you’re a ranked grinder, ARAM warrior, or someone who is looking to get into the game, Longhorn LoL is here to connect you with other League players of all skill levels and backgrounds.

 

Every semester, we host tournaments, socials, watch parties, and more! Our competitive esports teams represent UT in CLOL and are supported by our competitive staff and the Alienware Esports Gaming Arena on campus. For our staff, Texas LoL offers opportunities to explore the esports industry, build leadership skills, and work on exciting projects and events. 


Come join our discord server (https://discord.gg/KsFgz6Eb6z) to make some new friends and learn more about Texas LoL! We’re constantly planning to bring you better events and tournaments, so be sure to keep an eye on our #announcements channel for updates. :)
summary: Longhorn LoL is UT Austin's hub for casual and competitive League players of all skill levels. We host tournaments, socials, and watch parties every semester. Join our Discord to connect with fellow players and stay updated on all things collegiate LoL!

name: Texas Line Dance
description: Texas Line is intended to provide a supportive environment for people interested in line dancing in order to establish and grow an inclusive community at The University of Texas at Austin. Our goal is to foster an appreciation for line dancing at every skill level by teaching foundational knowledge for the art and sport of line dancing with aims to grow dancers’ confidence.
summary: Texas Line is intended to provide a supportive environment for people interested in line dancing by teaching dances weekly and hosting line dance socials.

name: Longhorn Escape Society
description: Longhorn Escape Society is an organization all about escape rooms! Come join us in learning about escape rooms and solving them. Everyone is welcome to join, and members are encouraged to join us on our semesterly escape room trip!
summary: Longhorn Escape Society. An escape room-based organization!

name: Heart2Art
description: Heart2Art is open to all students! Within Heart2Art, students uplift the lives of the less fortunate by creating and delivering handmade artwork. Our mission is to create meaningful connections between young artists and communities in need, including orphans, the elderly, the sick, and those living in poverty. With love and unity, students create personalized pieces of art to uplift those facing hardship. We also emphasize learning and empathy, offering opportunities for students to understand the complex experiences of those they are helping. By blending compassion with creativity, we aim to make a lasting impact on those who need it most!
summary: Everyone deserves the gift of love and belonging. Heart2Art allows students to send and create meaningful art for the underprivileged, orphans, the sick, and the elderly.

name: Longhorn Poker
description: Longhorn Poker is UT Austin's first-ever poker club. Our goal Consists of two main areas: learning and development. One of our primary focuses is to provide a safe and friendly environment for players to learn the game of poker. As players learn the foundations of poker, we want to support them along their poker journey by providing workshops and development to allow them to become better players and understand that the skills learned in poker translate to many areas of life.
summary: UT Austin's premier poker club, designed to create an inclusive and friendly space for individuals to learn and play poker

name: The Memory Mural Project
description: At The Memory Mural Project, we are dedicated to raising awareness and fostering a sense of unity and compassion for individuals and communities affected by Alzheimer's disease. We believe that the power of shared memories and collective support can help alleviate the challenges and isolation often associated with this condition. Our mission is to create a tangible expression of love, hope, and remembrance through our interactive mural for Alzheimer's awareness. We invite participants to share cherished memories by writing them on heart-shaped pieces of paper. These are then folded into petals, reminiscent of forget-me-not flowers, symbolizing the respect we hold for those impacted by Alzheimer's. Assembled on a canvas, the floral murals are displayed around the city to educate others on the realities of this condition and foster a sense of unity for those impacted by the disease.
summary: Our mission is to create a tangible expression of love, hope, and remembrance through our interactive mural for Alzheimer's awareness.

name: Texas Science Bowl
description: The Texas Science Bowl hosts an annual invitational competition in the style of the National Science Bowl. The organizational mission is to foster interest in science through fast-paced, buzzer-style competition. The primary areas of science are biology, chemistry, earth and space science, math, and physics.
summary: The Texas Science Bowl hosts an annual invitational competition in the style of the National Science Bowl. The organizational mission is to foster interest in science through fast-paced, buzzer-style competition.

name: Longhorn Developers
description:
Interested in joining Longhorn Developers? Fill out our Interest Form to stay updated with recruitment opportunities! https://longhorn-developers.notion.site/21dd9cb37133811e8fc4d4c4ad4b14d8

Are you ready to reimagine and reshape our UT experience? Longhorn Developers (LHD) is here to do just that! We're a collective of Longhorn problem-solvers dedicated to collaboratively designing and developing technology solutions that tackle student problems at UT Austin. We do this through organizing and establishing different project teams, each aimed to address a specific problem. While our projects are open source and welcome contributions from all corners of GitHub, as a member of Longhorn Developers, you'll have the opportunity not just to decide which UT experience we tackle next but also to be hands-on in making it happen with a community of UT student developers.
summary: Home of the UT Registration Plus Chrome Extension! Longhorn Developers (LHD) is a community of passionate students at UT Austin dedicated to building innovative solutions that address challenges faced by students.

name: Blacklisted Bookworms
description: A group dedicated to fighting book censorship and promoting literacy within our community. In light of recent efforts to ban books in schools and libraries, we aim to provide a platform for discussing and defending banned books through our Banned Book Club meetings, our "Underground Cafe." Our mission also includes ensuring everyone has access to books, hosting philanthropy events for nonprofits with similar values, and offering volunteer opportunities related to our cause. We also host social events throughout the academic year-- non-book related ones-- to help build a community of like-minded individuals who are passionate about defending free expression and access to literature. We know college students are busy, so attendance at our Underground Cafe meetings is not mandatory; it will count as more of a social point for anyone who wants to participate!
summary: A group dedicated to fighting book censorship and promoting literacy within our community.

name: Longhorn Sim Racing
description:
Longhorn Sim Racing (LSR) is UT Austin’s premier organization for students passionate about competitive sim racing, motorsports, and virtual driving experiences. Our mission is to create an inclusive and dynamic community where members can race, improve their skills, and compete at a high level—all while having fun.

Whether you're a seasoned sim racer or just getting started, LSR offers opportunities for all skill levels. We host casual races, structured league competitions, and special events across popular sim racing platforms such as iRacing, Assetto Corsa, F1, and Gran Turismo. Members also have access to coaching, setup guides, and strategy discussions to refine their technique and gain a competitive edge.
summary: Longhorn Sim Racing (LSR) is UT Austin’s premier organization for students passionate about competitive sim racing, motorsports, and virtual driving experiences.

name: Texas Friendly Fridays
description: Friendly Fridays is a volunteer organization with a mission to provide meaningful conversation, companionship, and connection to residents in nursing homes. Every Friday, we visit a local nursing home for an hour to spend quality time with the residents. Students with friendly pets are welcome to bring them along as their companions.
summary: Friendly Fridays is a volunteer organization with a mission to provide meaningful conversation, companionship, and connection to residents in nursing homes by visiting them every Friday.

name: Collective Studios
description:
REACH OUT TO US ON OUR INSTAGRAM @collective.atx


Collective is a dynamic creative studio where storytelling, filmmaking, and cultural expression converge. Each year, we produce three original films, post weekly marketing and narrative shorts on our socials, help our members build their portfolios and hone their creative skills, and organize engaging club events to foster collaboration and innovation. With a strong focus on social media presence, we craft narratives that captivate, content that resonates, and art that leaves a lasting impression. Driven by bold ideas, we explore uncharted creative territories, blending mediums to shape projects that inspire, challenge, and connect with audiences on a deeper level.
summary: Collective is a student-led filmmaking studio dedicated to craft compelling narratives, produce engaging content, and spark meaningful community through filmmaking, social media, and expression.

name: Hooked Media Strategy
description:
Hooked Media Strategy is a digital marketing agency dedicated to driving community impact through collaboration, creativity, and strategic growth. We help our clients amplify their mission and connect with their audience by enhancing their online presence and increasing visibility.

 

We specialize in full-service social media management and digital PR. By partnering with local nonprofits and small businesses, we help strengthen the Austin community while equipping our clients with the tools to grow.

 

Feel free to email hookedmediastrategy@gmail.com with any questions! Thank you!
summary: HMS fosters collaboration and creativity to inspire community impact. We enhance clients’ online media presence to amplify missions, connect with audiences, and support Austin nonprofits and small businesses through full-service social media services.

name: Longhorn osu!
description:
Longhorn osu! is a community centered around one of the largest online PC rhythm games, osu! We welcome players of all skill levels and backgrounds to have fun and improve together!

Every semester, we hold several social meetups to bring our community together. We also run a couple of tournaments throughout the year for beginners and veterans alike, and we also form teams for a collegiate tournament.

Come join us on our Discord!, which is our primary hub for our community.
summary: The home of the online rhythm game osu! community at UT

name: Ratio Christi at the University of Texas at Austin
description:
Ratio Christi's mission is to equip university students and faculty to give historical, philosophical, and scientific reasons for following Jesus Christ. The purpose of this Ratio Christi Chapter is to cultivate the intellectual voice of Christ and the Christian worldview at the university, and to do this by encouraging stimulating dialogue and intellectual investigation which aid in answering life’s pressing questions, especially where those questions find their answers supported by the academic disciplines of study including but not limited to: culture, history, science, philosophy, and theology. The goal will be to foster critical thinking, the use of logic, and evidential and philosophical tools in the pursuit of truth in understanding the world and religious beliefs. The Chapter will encourage and strengthen the faith of Christian students and faculty while sharing Christ’s message and love with those who have not yet accepted Him. Ratio Christi intends not to compete with other ministries, but it instead seeks to serve and support other ministries. The Chapter will exist as an asset for all by facilitating amicable discussions around worldview matters while taking into account the plethora of diverse paradigms and backgrounds found at the University of Texas at Austin. 

Introducing Ratio Christi
summary: Ratio Christi exists to equip university students and faculty to give historical, philosophical, and scientific reasons for following Jesus Christ. We aim to foster critical thinking and amicable dialogue in the pursuit of truth and deeper understanding.

name: Students Supporting Israel at University of Texas at Austin
description: Students Supporting Israel (SSI) is a pro-Israel international campus movement that supports the State of Israel. Our mission is to be a clear and confident pro-Israel voice on college campuses and to support students in grassroots pro-Israel advocacy. Join our new UT Austin Chapter to make an everlasting difference on our campus, we can't wait to meet you!
summary: Students Supporting Israel is an independent, nonpartisan, grassroots campus movement that was created BY students, FOR students, in order to organize a strong and united pro-Israel front on college campuses.

name: 2400 Nueces Residence Hall Council
description: 2400 Nueces Residence Hall Council (RHC) is a student-led organization that represents the residents of a specific residence hall. The council works to foster a sense of community, enhance the on-campus living experience, and advocate for student needs.
summary: 2400 Nueces Residence Hall Council (RHC) is a student-led organization that represents the residents of a specific residence hall. The council works to foster a sense of community, enhance the on-campus living experience, and advocate for student needs.

name: 0fficial Pulse Club
description:
0fficial Pulse Club is the first-ever fragrance organization, uniting students who share a passion for the art and science of scent. The club was born out of a love for creativity, community, and storytelling through fragrance.

We provide a space for members to dive into the fascinating world of perfumery, blending scientific principles with artistic expression. From crafting signature perfumes and candles to learning the intricacies of fragrance composition, our activities are designed to spark curiosity and innovation. Every scent tells a story, and we encourage our members to create their own while exploring the chemistry, history, and cultural significance of perfumes.

Our club is open to students from all academic disciplines who are eager to learn, create, and connect with like-minded individuals. Members benefit from hands-on workshops, access to resources for fragrance crafting, and opportunities to contribute to the club's creative and organizational efforts.

Whether you're an aspiring perfumer, a business, advertising or graphic design student who's looking for professional experience, a lover of scents, or simply curious about the fusion of science and art, the UT Perfume Club is the perfect place to express your creativity and discover the magic of fragrance. Join us and be part of a growing community that celebrates the beauty of storytelling through scent.

Looking for members to join our dynamic teams:
Finance & Social Team: Budget management (fundraising), member dues, and organizing socials.
Creative Team: Design club materials, perfume labels, and provide creative ideas.
Preferred skills: 3D-Modeling, Graphic Design (Adobe Creative), Perfumery
PR/Adv Team: Promote our events and grow our community through social media and advertisement.
Preferred skills: Filming (Short Form Contents)

We host monthly general meetings featuring fragrance or candle-making workshops, with additional meetings scheduled as needed to accommodate student and team availability.
summary: The UT Perfume Club blends science, art, and storytelling through fragrance. Open to all, we craft perfumes, explore scent chemistry, and foster creativity. Join us to connect with like-minded students and discover the magic of fragrance at UT Austin!

name: Texas Students for Refugees
description: Students for Refugees seeks to provide aid for refugees in the Austin area and offer meaningful opportunities for the students at The University of Texas at Austin. Additionally, it aims to champion awareness and education about the ongoing refugee crisis caused by persecution, conflict, violence, and human rights violations. It strives to deepen understanding of migration issues—pre-migration, transmigration, and post-migration. This organization empowers students to volunteer, advocate, and foster community participation for this vulnerable population, in collaboration with numerous agencies dedicated to assisting refugees.
summary: Students for Refugees empowers students to volunteer, advocate, and foster community participation for the refugee population in collaboration with numerous agencies dedicated to assisting refugees.

name: Catalyst Consulting & Strategy Group
description: Catalyst Consulting and Strategy Group (CCSG) seeks to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and professional practice by offering consulting services to nonprofits, small businesses, government entities, and for-profit firms while fostering the growth of future business leaders. CCSG intends to create a supportive and collaborative community where members can grow professionally, build lasting relationships, and create a network of future industry leaders.
summary: Catalyst Consulting and Strategy Group (CCSG) seeks to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and professional practice by offering consulting and fostering the growth of future business leaders.

name: Central Asian Society
description:
The Central Asian Society (CAS) is a student organization committed to building a strong and inclusive community for students with a background in or interest in the diverse cultures, histories, and traditions of Central Asia and Eurasia. CAS serves as a platform for cultural exchange, fostering connections among students from different backgrounds while deepening awareness and appreciation of the region’s rich heritage.

This includes, but is not limited to, countries such as Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan, Turkmenistan, and Tajikistan, as well as neighboring regions spanning Eastern Europe, the Caucasus, and Western Asia. CAS seeks to highlight the shared histories, cultural ties, and unique traditions of these regions while creating a welcoming space for students to connect, learn, and celebrate their heritage.
summary: The Central Asian Society (CAS) is a student organization dedicated to fostering a community for students with a background or interest in the cultures of Central Asia/Eurasia.

name: Texas Product Catalyst
description:
At Texas Product Catalyst, we’re more than just an organization, we’re a place where students explore the world of tech and product. Whether it’s product management, marketing, development, or design, there’s something here for everyone.

Our mission is to help students grow through our 3 C's:

Career – Empowering members with industry insights, expert-led workshops, and connections with professionals.
Curriculum – A 12-week program packed with product management fundamentals, workshops, and real-world projects.
Community – Build strong relationships and work together on meaningful projects while sharing a passion for tech.

TPC is where skills are sharpened, ideas come to life, and members achieve their goals.
summary: Texas Product Catalyst helps individuals break into tech through insights on product management, marketing, development, and design. We offer workshops, projects, and competitions, connecting students with resources and networks for success.

name: ASCEND
summary: Aerial Solutions for Combating Emergencies and Natural Disasters

name: Longhorn Baja Racing
description: Longhorn Baja Racing is UT Austin's Baja SAE team. Baja SAE is a collegiate engineering competition where student teams design, build, and race a single-seat off-road vehicle. The competition, organized by SAE International, challenges teams to create a rugged, durable car that can handle rough terrain while optimizing performance, safety, and efficiency. Teams compete in events like acceleration, maneuverability, endurance, and design presentation, testing both their engineering skills and teamwork.
summary: Longhorn Baja Racing is UT Austin's professional chapter of the Baja SAE competition. Baja SAE is a collegiate engineering competition where student teams design, build, and race a single-seat off-road vehicle.

name: Lambda Pi Eta UT Chapter
summary: LPH's goal is to promote academic excellence in communication studies, foster interest in the communication field, facilitate discussions and exchange of ideas, and build strong relationships between faculty and students.

name: Texas Retail & E-Commerce Ventures
description:
For the first time at UT Austin, students interested in e-commerce and digital entrepreneurship have a place to collaborate, learn, and grow together. From building online stores on platforms like Shopify, Amazon, and Etsy to mastering SEO, digital marketing, and tools like Google Analytics and Facebook Ads, Texas REV has you covered.

We’re kicking off a Guest Speaker Series with industry leaders & entrepreneurs soon—stay tuned!
summary: Students interested in e-commerce and digital entrepreneurship have a place to collaborate, learn, and grow together. From building online stores on platforms like Shopify to mastering digital marketing. Texas REV has you covered.

name: Rooted Collective
description:
We are an immigrant student liberation collective providing immigrant students with equitable access to resources, services, and opportunities.

Membership: If you're interested in joining/attending our social events and/or workshops, please follow us on Instagram to hear about our events: @rooted.immigrant.collective! Fill out our interest form, and we'll keep you updated once the semester begins.
summary: We are an immigrant student liberation collective, a student-led initiative. We support and advocate for the undocumented student community.

name: Student Medical Reserve Corps at UT
description: The Student Medical Reserve Corps at UT aims to enhance the health and preparedness of the University of Texas at Austin community by organizing and training volunteers to respond to public health crises. We offer a variety of trainings, including Stop the Bleed, Emergency To-Go Bag preparation, and Psychological First Aid, to equip our community with the skills needed to handle emergencies effectively.
summary: The Student Medical Reserve Corps at UT is an organization that strives to create a group of volunteers (comprising of faculty, staff, students, and community leaders) to be prepared to assist in public health issues and emergencies on and around campus.

name: Alliance on Latinx Mental-Health Awareness
description:
Alliance on Latinx Mental-Health Awareness (ALMA) is a student-led organization seeking to promote and advocate for the awareness, resources, and community for advocacy of equitable mental health care for Latinx people and other underserved communities.

 

How to be an active member?

- Fill out the membership interest form and receive a confirmation welcome email!

- Pay $12/semester dues.

- Attend 5 general events (3 general meetings, 2 social events).

**By completing each objective above, you will maintain your membership.

 

Though our organization's name has Latinx in it, we advocate for any and all communities that are underserved. Regardless of your cultural and racial background, everyone is welcome to become a part of ALMA.

 

"Alma" means soul in Spanish, which represents the embodiment of all the abundant cultures on campus and ALMA's core initiatives for healing our minds, bodies, and souls through equitable care practices. 

 

If you are interested in becoming a member, fill out this form!

(ALMA) Alliance on Latinx Mental-Health Awareness: Membership Interest Form

 

Spring 2026 General Meeting & Event Schedule

 

Any organizations that would like to collaborate with us, please email almaoncampusatx@gmail.com.

 

Friendly Disclaimer/Reminder: We are not affiliated with UT's CMHC, nor are we qualified to provide counseling services. This group is focused on advocacy and support, but we are not a replacement for professional assistance. By joining this organization, we can help you find professional help if you need it, and joining our organization is an excellent way to get started on your journey to recovery or surround yourself with an accepting community of friends who will listen.

 

¡Hacerlo con ganas!
summary: Alliance on Latinx Mental-Health Awareness (ALMA) is a student-led organization seeking to promote and advocate for the awareness, resources, and community for advocacy of equitable mental health care for the Latinx and other underserved communities.

name: Longhorn EMS
description:
1. To work with Austin/Travis County EMS (ATCEMS), Austin Fire Department (AFD) and The University of Texas Police Department (UTPD) to provide emergency medical care to anyone who should suffer illness or injury on the University of Texas Campus or within close proximity thereof.
  2. To coordinate with ATCEMS, in the case that any injured party might require immediate transport to a hospital. 
  3. To provide public health education at The University of Texas at Austin and in the Austin community.
summary: Our mission is to provide excellence in emergency response and education to the University of Texas and greater Austin community.

name: Texas Space Society
description:
Here’s a completely revised version reflecting TSS’s new focus on high-altitude balloon payloads:

The Texas Space Society (TSS) is evolving into a hands-on, engineering-driven organization focused on the design, integration, and flight of high-altitude balloon payloads. Instead of concentrating on external competitions, TSS will now provide students with the opportunity to build real flight hardware and conduct near-space missions reaching altitudes of 80,000–100,000 feet.

Through these missions, members will work on multidisciplinary teams responsible for payload structures, thermal protection, power systems, avionics, communications, and data analysis. Students will gain practical experience in systems engineering, testing, mission planning, and post-flight recovery—mirroring the workflows used in professional aerospace programs.

High-altitude balloon projects give students direct exposure to near-space environments, including low temperatures, low pressures, and high radiation levels. By designing experiments and flight systems to withstand these conditions, members will develop strong technical foundations while learning how to manage risk, iterate on designs, and collaborate effectively across subteams.

Beyond the technical work, TSS will connect students with aerospace mentors, researchers, and industry professionals, creating opportunities for career development and professional growth.

By joining TSS, Longhorns become part of a mission-focused community committed to hands-on aerospace innovation and expanding student access to real near-space flight experience.
summary: The Texas Space Society (TSS) is designed to promote National Space Society competitions. Through industry-level competitions, members of TSS will engage in complex, futuristic design challenges that closely mimic the dynamics of the aerospace teams.

name: Longhorn Asset Management
description: The Premier Hedge Fund Simulation Club at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: The Premier Student-Led Hedge Fund Simulation at UT Austin

name: First-Gen Longhorns
description: Through the First-Gen Experience Program, First-Gen Living Learning Community, and campus-wide events, we provide meaningful support, engaging programming, and access to resources. Our office connects first-gen students to a strong community and helps them thrive throughout their college journey.
summary: First-Gen Longhorns is committed to supporting and celebrating first-generation college students at UT Austin.

name: IDSA at UT Austin
description: The Industrial Designers Society of America (IDSA) is an organization that promotes the practice and education of industrial design.
summary: Industrial Designers Society of America student chapter.

name: Survivor Texas
description:
Survivor Texas is a student-led social strategy competition at UT Austin where players face weekly challenges, form alliances, and navigate blindsides in a multi-week test of adaptability and resilience. Cast members compete to outlast the rest, while crew design challenges, film, edit, and host events. With opportunities to play, join crew, or enjoy socials, Survivor Texas is more than a game—it’s a community built on strategy, teamwork, and unforgettable experiences.

Interested in cast or crew? Apply on our Interest Form!

 

Want to check us out? Watch the premiere of our newest season!
summary: Survivor Texas is a student-led social strategy competition where players outwit, outplay, & outlast each other through challenges, alliances, and blindsides in the ultimate test of adaptability and resilience. Compete, join crew, or enjoy social events!

name: Longhorn Finance Association
description: Longhorn Finance Association is a student-led organization dedicated to equipping undergraduate students with the skills and knowledge needed to succeed in the finance industry. Through interactive lessons and workshops, members gain real-world experience that prepares them to pursue careers in finance.
summary: Longhorn Finance Association is an organization designed to help students prepare for a career in finance.

name: Texas Sports Consulting Group
summary: Texas Sports Consulting Group seeks to bridge the gap between business and sports by offering consulting services to local Austin sports organizations and UT sports clubs.

name: Hill Country Strong
description:
Hill Country Strong is a student organization at The University of Texas at Austin focused on supporting Central Texas communities impacted by flooding and natural disasters. Founded in response to the devastating 2025 floods, our mission is to mobilize students to provide meaningful relief through direct volunteer work, fundraising, and long-term recovery efforts.

 

We organize service trips to the Hill Country region including Kerrville, Ingram, and nearby areas where we assist with cleanup, supply distribution, rebuilding projects, and coordination with local partners. Our team works closely with community leaders, nonprofits, and emergency response organizations to ensure our efforts are helpful and well-organized.

 

Whether you want to volunteer in the field, help with planning and logistics, or contribute to outreach and awareness, Hill Country Strong offers opportunities for students from all backgrounds to get involved. There are no membership dues, and participation is flexible based on your availability and interests.

 

We are building a community of students committed to standing strong for our neighbors before, during, and after the storm.
summary: Hill Country Strong is a UT Austin org that coordinates student-led volunteer efforts to support communities affected by flooding in Central Texas.

name: Investigative Reporters and Editors at UT
description:
Investigative Reporters and Editors at UT (IRE) is an organization that hopes to increase interest in investigative journalism through connecting students with professionals and other passionate students. IRE at UT is open to all!

It is a student chapter of the professional organization, Investigative Reporters and Editors, which aims to “foster investigative journalism across the country through training, conferences, resources, and more”.

To learn more about future events, join the slack channel : IRE at UT
summary: Investigative Reporters and Editors at UT (IRE) is an organization that hopes to increase interest in investigative journalism through connecting students with professionals and other passionate students. IRE at UT is open to all!

name: CTRL Room
description: CTRL Room is an organization focused on showcasing art by marginalized communities. CTRL Room aims to foster community by holding meetings every other week, focused on celebrating all facets of art by these communities. Additionally, our main focus is to provide perfromance opportunities for marginalized artists to showcase their work on a greater stage.
summary: CTRL Room seeks to amplify BIPOC, LGBT+, and female identifying  voices through producing performances, and club meetings, celebrating work by these groups.

name: Verdatronics
description:
Verdatronics is a student-led organization dedicated to developing advanced smart infrastructure solutions through engineering, computer science, and entrepreneurial collaboration. Our mission is to design, prototype, and deploy scalable systems that leverage AI, sensors, embedded hardware, and automation to address real-world challenges across multiple sectors.

We structure our work into specialized departments: Smart Homes, Agriculture, Finance, Marketing & Sales, and Legal & Business Operations. Each department is responsible for driving projects from concept to implementation. This framework allows us to operate with the rigor of a startup while fostering interdisciplinary learning and innovation.

Our projects span diverse domains. In smart homes, we design energy- and water-efficient automation systems, HVAC optimization packs, and grid-interactive load management tools. In agriculture, we develop autonomous gardens, precision irrigation systems, and AI-driven monitoring platforms to improve sustainability and yield. Beyond these sectors, we explore applications in renewable energy, urban infrastructure, and connected devices, extending our technology to larger-scale problems such as microgrids and distributed resource management.

Verdatronics emphasizes hands-on prototyping and impact-driven engineering. Teams focus on building tangible solutions, whether integrating IoT hardware, creating embedded software pipelines, or training machine learning models, ensuring our technologies are both functional and scalable.

By bridging academic research, entrepreneurial practice, and practical engineering, Verdatronics positions itself as more than a campus club: it is a launchpad for next-generation smart infrastructure technologies.
summary: Verdatronics is a student-founded organization developing innovative smart infrastructure solutions, from smart homes to agriculture and beyond, using AI, sensors, and automation to tackle real-world challenges and create scalable, impactful technologies

name: Lawtina Network
summary: Lawtina is a pre-law organization originally founded at St. Mary’s University in San Antonio, TX. It is beginning to make its way through other schools in chapters and has now reached the University of Texas at Austin. It is designed to empower, support,

name: The African American Nursing Student Association
description:
The African American Nursing Student Association (AANSA) at The University of Texas at Austin is dedicated to empowering and supporting Black nursing students through academic, professional, and social development. We provide a welcoming and inclusive community where members can find mentorship, encouragement, and resources to help them thrive in nursing school and prepare for future careers in healthcare.

Our purpose is to create opportunities for connection and growth by offering educational workshops, peer support, leadership development, and networking events with healthcare professionals and alumni. AANSA also advocates for cultural awareness and competency in nursing by highlighting health issues that disproportionately affect the Black community and encouraging dialogue about diversity, equity, and inclusion in healthcare.

In addition to professional development, AANSA places a strong emphasis on community engagement and service. We partner with campus organizations, local clinics, and community groups to support wellness initiatives, volunteer efforts, and outreach events. Through these activities, members develop leadership skills, build lasting friendships, and contribute positively to both the University and the greater Austin community.

Ultimately, AANSA strives to cultivate future nurses who are not only academically prepared, but also compassionate, culturally aware, and committed to advancing health equity.
summary: The African American Nursing Student Association at UT Austin empowers Black nursing students through mentorship, community, and professional development while promoting diversity, inclusion, and cultural awareness in nursing.

name: University Accounting Organization
description: The University Accounting Organization seeks to create an inclusive, collaborative community for undergraduate accounting students at The University of Texas at Austin. Our mission is to provide professional development opportunities, foster peer support, and connect students with industry professionals across public accounting, corporate finance, and advisory roles. UAO aims to strengthen the identity of accounting students within McCombs and leave a lasting impact on the program and its future students.
summary: The University Accounting Organization seeks to create an inclusive, collaborative community for undergraduate accounting students at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Foundation Communities on Campus
description:
Foundation Communities on Campus is a student organization dedicated to connecting UT students with meaningful volunteer opportunities through Foundation Communities, a local nonprofit serving over 9,000 Austin residents with affordable housing, education, health, and financial stability programs.

Members volunteer in programs such as health coverage enrollment, after-school tutoring, ESL classes, care teams, and food pantries, while also gaining access to rideshares, certifications, leadership positions, and professional development. The organization serves as a campus hub for students to build community, expand equity-focused service, and create lasting impact in Austin.
summary: FCC connects students with volunteering in housing, health, food, and education programs supporting 9,000+ Austinites through a local non-profit, Foundation Communities. Whether it's for a few hours or a long-term commitment, there's a place for you!

name: South Asian Women Empowerment
description: South Asian Women Empowerment serves as a catalyst for South Asian Women to bridge the growing gap between our communities and cultures. Being a part of a singular South Asian community tends to evoke a lot of pride and honor in our culture. There is a general misunderstanding that we should be pitted against one another due to this honor when we should combine forces and celebrate the unities and differences. Therefore, this organization fosters that growth and evolution in coming together as a whole and fostering a community right here at UT. We are open to all South Asian communities, not just any specific one. We hope to promote our organization as one that welcomes all despite a lack or abundance of knowledge in your own or other South Asian communities, and we hope to foster a sense of togetherness, friendship, support, and community through this organization.
summary: South Asian Women Empowerment is an organization driven by the need for South Asian communities, and especially women, to bridge the spreading gap between our uniting cultures and identities. This org caters to all South asians and fosters community.

name: National Council of Negro Women, UT Austin Section
description:
The purpose of the National Council of Negro Women (NCNW) collegiate section is to provide a platform for Black women students and allies to engage in leadership, service, and advocacy that uplifts our communities. On campus, NCNW works to foster sisterhood, promote academic excellence, and celebrate cultural heritage while addressing the social, political, and economic issues that impact students of African descent.NCNW collegiate sections prepare students to be change-makers who carry forward the organization’s legacy of unity, empowerment, and progress.

Today, the NCNW programs are grounded on a foundation of critical concerns that are now “NCNW Priorities.” Our organization promotes education, encourages entrepreneurship, financial literacy, and economic stability; educates women about health and promotes access to healthcare; and advocates for civic engagement and sound public policy, as well as social justice.
summary: It is a part of NCNW’s mission to lead, advocate for, and empower women of African descent, their families, and communities. It was founded in 1935 by Dr. Mary McLeod Bethune, an influential educator and activist.

name: Texas Sri Lankan Student Association
description:
Texas Sri Lankan Student Association seeks to increase awareness of Sri Lankan culture at the University of Texas at Austin. Our ultimate mission is to foster a strong appreciation for diversity and the preservation of Sri Lankan culture on the campus.

 

We just founded this organization, so this is an exciting time for us! We'll mostly be hosting social events, and are open to ideas and suggestions if members have any. We don't have point requirements, you can attend our meetings and socials as you are available. Also, you do not have to be Sri Lankan to join, if you are interested in learning about the country and the culture, you are more than welcome to join us!

If you are interested in joining, please fill out the Texas SLSA 25-26 Membership Form!

We hope to see you around!
summary: Texas Sri Lankan Student Association seeks to increase awareness of Sri Lankan culture at the University of Texas at Austin. Our ultimate mission is to foster a strong appreciation for diversity and the preservation of Sri Lankan culture on the campus.

name: Longhorn Scrap Society
description: A journaling, scrapbooking, and craft club that celebrates the art of imperfection.
summary: A journaling, scrapbooking, and craft club that celebrates the art of imperfection.

name: Honors Consulting Association
description: At Honors Consulting Association, we aim to bridge the gap between academia and the local community by providing high-impact consulting services that address real-world challenges. As students from the University of Texas at Austin, we harness the diverse expertise and interdisciplinary perspectives of our student consultants to deliver innovative, effective solutions for local businesses and organizations. We hope to empower students to apply their academic knowledge beyond the classroom and strengthen bonds with the Austin and Texas communities.
summary: We connect UT Austin students with local businesses and organizations, delivering innovative solutions to real-world challenges. We empower students to apply academic knowledge beyond the classroom while strengthening community ties.

name: Vibe Coding Club
summary: Texas Vibe Coding is for everyone—beginners to advanced coders—who want to learn, collaborate, and build real projects that can change the world. Join us for workshops, app-building, and a community that turns ideas into impact.

name: HEAL: Health, Ethics, and Law.
description: HEAL (Health, Ethics, and Law) is a student organization at The University of Texas at Austin dedicated to exploring the complex intersections of medicine, law, and ethics. Through speaker panels, case study discussions, mock trials, debates, advocacy workshops, and community outreach, HEAL fosters interdisciplinary dialogue and civic engagement. Our goal is to empower students with the knowledge and skills to critically examine ethical and legal challenges in healthcare and to promote health equity and informed policy-making.
summary: HEAL aims to foster interdisciplinary dialogue on health, ethics, and law through speaker events, case studies, debates, advocacy, and community outreach. We empower students to explore ethical and legal issues in healthcare.

name: Longhorn Padel
description: Longhorn Padel is dedicated to building a vibrant padel community in Austin, starting with the students of The University of Texas at Austin. Our mission is to create opportunities for Longhorns to discover and enjoy this fast-growing sport through a mix of tournaments, social events, and collaborative partnerships with local padel clubs and businesses. By offering regular chances to play, meet new people, and compete in a fun and welcoming environment, we aim to give students a new way to stay active and engaged on campus.
summary:
Longhorn Padel seeks to build a padel community in Austin, providing opportunities
for UT students to play padel through tournaments and social events, helping the sport grow in the US. Longhorn Padel is open to all students regardless of skill level.

name: Texas Future Lawyers
description:
Texas Future Lawyers is a student organization at The University of Texas at Austin dedicated to preparing undergraduates for the academic, professional, and ethical demands of a future in law. We provide a community for students interested in legal careers while prioritizing two core pillars: academic development and philanthropy.

Academically, Texas Future Lawyers equips members with tools and resources to excel in their journey toward law school. We host weekly LSAT practice sessions and workshops, panels with practicing attorneys and law students, and career-focused events that expose members to diverse areas of law. Our goal is to build confidence, competence, and clarity for students pursuing the legal profession.

Equally important is our commitment to service. Texas Future Lawyers emphasizes the role of law in advancing justice and community well-being by organizing philanthropic initiatives, such as legal literacy workshops, volunteer opportunities, and fundraising for causes that expand access to legal resources.
summary: Texas Future Lawyers is a UT Austin student organization that prepares undergrads for law school and beyond. We focus on academics through weekly LSAT workshops, career panels, and professional development, while also giving back through philanthropy.

name: Unwiring Minds
summary: Unwiring Minds explores the mind through hands-on education, research, and service. We connect students with real-world neuroscience, facilitate debates with leading experts, and support community-centered learning and outreach across Austin.

name: Blessing on Campus
summary: Blessing on Campus is a Christian student organization that gathers for Bible reading, prayer, and fellowship. We especially provide a supportive community for Korean-American second-generation students who want to grow in faith together on campus.

name: Longhorn Mindful Miles
description:
Longhorn Mindful Miles (LMM) is a student-led, social and community-based organization at The University of Texas at Austin dedicated to promoting both physical and mental well-being through the simple, restorative act of walking. Our mission is to create a supportive space where students can prioritize their health, build meaningful connections, and find balance amidst the demands of academic life.

We believe that movement is medicine, not only for the body, but for the mind. By organizing group walks around campus and in the Austin community, Longhorn Mindful Miles provides a consistent and accessible outlet for stress relief, mindfulness, and social connection. Our walks are intentionally designed to be inclusive and low-pressure, welcoming students of all fitness levels and backgrounds to join in.

At its core, Longhorn Mindful Miles is more than just a walking club; it is a community committed to fostering resilience, friendship, and holistic health. Whether you are looking to clear your head, boost your mood, or simply connect with others in a positive and supportive environment, Longhorn Mindful Miles is here to walk alongside you.
summary: LMM is an organization dedicated to providing a community where students can improve their physical and mental wellness by stepping outside and taking walks with other students.

name: Texas Claude Builder Club
description: Claude Builder Clubs are inclusive communities for all students interested in exploring AI development, developing technical skills, and learning about the benefits and risks associated with AI. CBCs are a space for passionate students to build their own projects while connecting with peers who can elevate their ideas. CBC members are AI thought leaders on their campuses and beyond.
summary: Texas Claude Builder Club is intended to empower UT students to build the future with AI. Through workshops, hackathons, and collaborative projects, we aim to show what responsible AI development looks like in practice while building community.

name: The Cyber Directive
description:
The Cyber Directive is intended to function as a student-run, interdisciplinary academic publication dedicated to exploring the evolving intersection of cybersecurity technology, law, and public policy. Its mission is to provide a national forum for the next generation of leaders to develop and present innovative solutions, bridge disciplinary divides, and critically engage with the complex challenges shaping our digital future. The Cyber Directive is dedicated to disseminating the research that will prepare this vanguard to defend critical infrastructure, secure national interests, and chart new courses in the study and application of cybersecurity against emerging threats.

The Cyber Directive publishes rigorous, forward-looking work that elevates student perspectives and fosters collaboration across campuses, sectors, and fields. By convening voices from across the country, it aims to influence the conversations—and the policies—that will fortify the United States of America’s cybersecurity in the United States for years to come.
summary: The Cyber Directive is a student-led, interdisciplinary academic journal that focuses on the intersection of cybersecurity law, policy, and technology. We provide students nationwide a platform to share research and collaborate with like-minded peers.

name: Hooked on Running
summary: We're a student-led running group that supports runners of all levels. Beyond running, we're dedicated to giving back to our community. So, if you like running or want to give it a try, join our org for the betterment of your health and your community!

name: Scholars of Finance
description:
Scholars of Finance (SOF) is a national organization that equips the next generation of principled financial leaders through education, mentorship, and community. With chapters on 60+ university campuses across the U.S, SOF provides students with a network of members, alumni, and industry professionals committed to values-based leadership in finance.
