SOF hosts weekly meetings, where members engage with corporate speakers, participate in leadership workshops, and discuss topics related to integrity, purpose, and long-term impact in the financial industry. We offer opportunities to join mentorship programs and national events that provide hands-on development and connection with professionals across the country.

 

Our chapter also organizes social events throughout the semester, creating space for members to build meaningful relationships and grow within a community of like-minded peers.
summary: Scholars of Finance develops the next generation of principled leaders in finance. Through leadership training, mentorship, and professional development, we equip students with the skills and values to pursue finance careers with integrity and purpose.

name: IPO Investing at UT
description:
IPO Investing at UT is dedicated to preparing students for the future of financial markets by exploring the next generation of IPOs. We believe the coming wave of public offerings will be shaped by artificial intelligence, biotechnology, and other frontier technologies that are redefining the global economy.
Our mission is to help students understand how these innovations move from private startups to publicly traded companies, and how investors can evaluate them with clarity and discipline. We combine traditional market analysis with forward-looking perspectives on AI-driven industries, digital platforms, and the businesses that will define the next decade.
Through case studies, research projects, and collaborative discussion, members gain insight into how emerging companies scale, how technology influences valuation, and how investors can navigate markets in a world transformed by automation and data.
IPO Investing at UT is also a community. We connect students with peers, alumni, and industry leaders who share a vision for the future of finance. Our organization is a place where students can build practical skills, ask bold questions, and prepare to lead in an era where the companies going public today will shape the world of tomorrow.
summary: IPO Investing at UT is dedicated to preparing students for the future of financial markets by exploring the next generation of IPOs.

name: The Student Solidarity Network
summary: The Student Solidarity Network is a organization looking to provide for UT Austin students through a mutual aid network. In doing so, we hope to educate the community and foster an environment of solidarity through engaging events.

name: Texas Corporate Law Collective
description:
Through interactive sessions and applied case problems, we prepare students to analyze real-world legal issues faced by businesses. This is meant to mimic the nature of law school courses and exams.

Please view our website to apply.
summary: We provide a structured and practical Business Law curriculum designed to help students understand the legal principles that govern commercial activity.

name: Parkinson's Pals
summary: Aims to form intergenerational connections between college students and Parkinson’s patients to ease isolation. Pairs meet weekly for two months, in person or online. Matching forms are used to help align shared interests between the student and patient.

name: Texas Sociological Association
description: The Texas Sociological Association is a professional student organization dedicated to creating pathways for students to apply sociology in their careers through academic exploration, professional development, and research opportunities. Our mission is to provide a space that fosters education and connections for anyone interested in sociology!
summary: A professional student organization dedicated to creating pathways for students to apply sociology in their careers through academic exploration, professional development, and research opportunities.

name: Pivot Point Central
description:
Underlying Problems

  • We believe that the trend of declining mental health in teens and young adults has a strong correlation with mental and physical isolation. 
  • Especially in a new environment like college, almost 100% of freshmen feel lost and lonely at some point of their freshmen year.
  • Numerous sophomores to senior years also feel the same.

 

Our mission: We are trying to spread awareness of this mental health decline in college students, targeting the UT campus, preventing excessive mental and physical isolation that are leading to depression, anxiety, and various harmful effects.

 

Our Goal: To make UT a community where you truly can find your identity through reflection, the “Why?” in your goals, and focus more on your well-being than putting all your eggs in one basket (academics, internships, career opportunities, relationships with one particular group or person) 

 

Format/Events:

 

We will hold general informational sessions a few times during the semester, allowing people to join at different times.

 

We will hold general meetings weekly/bi weekly, in these sessions, we will talk about psychological studies and general tips and personal stories of people to prevent isolation, to prevent social anxiety, and beyond 

  • Rotate themes: (1) psychology insight, (2) student story, (3) skill mini-workshop (time management, stress control, etc.).

 

We will also have social events throughout each month a few times, 

Ex. Barton Springs hangout 

Ex. Social at Chipotle/Cabo bobs, etc 

Ex. Ping Pong tournament (Winner get $ Prize)

 

Members also can edit the “Ideas/Currently” Document and suggest ways to improve the organization, which will be checked by exec board (Secretary or Resources) once a week
summary: Pivot Point Central: A UT organization focused on improving the overall mental health of students on campus, preventing excessive isolation that leads to depression, anxiety, etc. Spreading Awareness is our number 1 goal.

name: Hooked on Service
summary: Hooked on Service is a club to serve, uplift, and empower in all areas of our community. We provide service opportunities at least twice per month in all types of volunteering, in order to cultivate a spirit of philanthropy in our members!

name: ATX Food Hub
description: Our mission is to strengthen connections between food resource organizations near UT by integrating community feedback, offering real-time updates on available resources, and empowering students to seek the support they need. We carry out this mission through three committees. The Outreach & Engagement Committee builds partnerships with food resource organizations, spreads awareness through class talks and resource cards, and supports students with SNAP applications in collaboration with UT Outpost. The Research Committee studies how students use the map, develops research proposals on food resource usage, and presents findings at conferences. The Map Improvement Committee enhances the usability and design of the map and website, while also sharing regular newsletters with important information about food resources around campus.
summary: We provide students with a comprehensive, updated overview of the wide range of resources they can access while offering local nonprofits valuable insights into existing resource distribution, helping to identify gaps and improve management strategies.

name: Texas Voices of Equity
description:
Voices of Equity's mission is to educate, inspire, and empower communities and future healthcare leaders on health equity issues through impactful content, storytelling, and actionable community-based initiatives. By educating and raising awareness on local health equity issues, we can implement community-based initiatives that provide actionable steps to alleviate health disparities among diverse populations.


Members of VoE will identify health disparities, unite community members/students passionate about the social determinants of health, and implement sustainable solutions to create long-lasting change through four committees: Philanthropy, Community Outreach, Education, and Media.

https://youtu.be/PJEX8nYyXBU
summary: Texas Voices of Equity is a service and community awareness-based organization dedicated to educating and empowering students to provide actionable steps to improve health equity among diverse populations.

name: Texas Fintech Collective
description:
Texas FinTech Collective (TFC) is an interdisciplinary, pre-professional organization at The University of Texas at Austin that prepares undergraduate students for high-impact careers at the intersection of finance, technology, and business strategy.

TFC is divided into three hands-on verticals:

  1. Product Team: Operates like a fintech startup, designing and developing an original product over the course of a semester. The team is split into a technical subteam (frontend/backend developers) and a strategy subteam (go-to-market planning, product marketing, and UX research). The goal is to build and launch a minimum viable product (MVP) in-market, with real-world deployment and user feedback.
  2. Consulting Team: Functions like a boutique consulting firm that serves a single real-world fintech client each semester. Members are trained in case structuring, client communication, and problem-solving frameworks modeled after MBB firms (McKinsey, BCG, Bain). The team delivers business strategy recommendations — such as pricing, growth, or GTM — in a final deck and client presentation.
  3. Investing Team: Mimics the operations of a fintech-focused investment fund. Members conduct in-depth equity research, build models, and pitch long or short theses on public or private fintech companies. Analysts are responsible for writing formal investment memos and simulating trades in a team-managed portfolio. The team also runs internal investment committee simulations and pitch competitions.

Across all three verticals, members receive specialized training through crash courses and weekly workshops, enabling them to build technical skills, business acumen, and confidence in real-world settings. All work culminates in a final semester showcase with external guests, faculty, and industry partners.

TFC is designed to:

  • Provide elite, career-relevant experience in finance, product, and consulting
  • Foster a tight-knit, supportive community that combines ambition with mentorship
  • Operate with the intensity, structure, and prestige of a professional team

By bridging theory and practice, TFC aims to be the top fintech-focused undergraduate organization in the country — where students graduate with the mindset, network, and portfolio to thrive in the world's most competitive industries.
summary: Texas FinTech Collective (TFC) is an interdisciplinary, pre-professional organization at The University of Texas at Austin that prepares undergraduate students for high-impact careers at the intersection of finance, technology, and business strategy.

name: Longhorn Banking Group
description: We help train the next generation of longhorns for WSFM and IB recruitment. We hold 6+ technical training sessions in the spring for prospective LBG members so that our application process tests students not on current knowledge, but the ability to quickly learn it.
summary: We help train the next generation of longhorns for WSFM and IB recruitment.

name: Pink First at the University of Texas at Austin
description: Pink First aims to empower women through advocacy and promotion of comprehensive health care. It understands the importance of encompassing preventative healthcare and holistic well-being especially in communities where healthcare is not always accessible. Pink First recognizes the vital role many women play in their families and the deep emotional and practical challenges that arise during illness. Pink First seeks to bring light to common illnesses such as breast cancer that affect many families. Through care, community, and connection, Pink First hopes to empower healing for women and provide strength for the ones who walk beside them.
summary: Pink First empowers women with advocacy, preventive care, and holistic health, highlighting illnesses like breast cancer while supporting afflicted families through care, community, and connection.

name: Iswarah
description: Our mission is to enable our donors to create a profound impact on the lives of struggling civilians in the Middle East and around the globe. With each purchase of an Iswarah product, we pledge to donate 100% of the profits to humanitarian aid. In appreciation of donor support, we offer our donors the freedom to select the products of their choice as a gesture of gratitude. Together, we aspire to instill hope and provide much-needed assistance to those in need, working towards fostering positive change and transforming the lives of affected marginalized communities.
summary: Iswarah is a registered nonprofit organization aimed to create a profound impact on the lives of struggling civilians. With each purchase of an Iswarah product, 100% of the profits are donated as humanitarian aid to regions that need it the most.

name: Texas Research Association
description: Texas Research Association is a student-run organization which facilitates research through pathways such as AI and Biology. We focus on research to foster technical expertise and produce publishable, impactful work.
summary: Texas Research Association is a student-run organization which facilitates research through pathways such as AI and Biology. We focus on research to foster technical expertise and produce publishable, impactful work.

name: Feminist Policy Alliance Austin
description:
The Feminist Policy Alliance (FPA) is an inclusive, student-led community at the LBJ School of Public Affairs dedicated to examining and addressing policy issues that impact women and historically marginalized communities through an intersectional lens. FPA is grounded in the principles of equity, justice, and representation, and seeks to create space for thoughtful dialogue, research, and collaboration on how public policy can be used as a tool for social change.

Through events, speaker series, workshops, and community engagement, FPA works to educate and empower students to become advocates for inclusive and equitable policy. The organization serves as a platform for policy analysis, advocacy, and leadership development, connecting the LBJ School with the broader University of Texas campus and the Austin community.

Our mission is to ensure that the perspectives and experiences of women are meaningfully included in policy conversations and decision-making processes, and to prepare future policymakers to lead with awareness, empathy, and a commitment to justice.
summary: The Feminist Policy Alliance is an inclusive community for students focusing on policy issues affecting women. FPA brings education and advocacy to the LBJ School, the University and the Austin community.

name: Foundation for International Medical Relief of Children
summary: FIMRC is a non-profit humanitarian organization that strives to expand access to healthcare to children and families with limited access. As a chapter, we work towards actively supporting our local and global communities through service & volunteering!

name: Keep It Doable
description: K.I.D Keep it Doable partners with free and low-cost pediatric clinics (Lirios Pediatrics and Volunteer Health Clinic) to offer free, hands-on Family Nutrition & Exercise classes rooted in MyPlate guidance. Each week, parents and children learn simple meal-planning strategies, at-home workouts, and behavior-change techniques followed by virtual check-ins and a kid-friendly reward system to reinforce new habits. Our members will work to volunteer and run these classes to increase engagement and help educate children and their families on healthy, doable, and cost-efficient nutrition and fitness practices.
summary: K.I.D Keep it Doable partners with free and low-cost pediatric clinics (Lirios Pediatrics and Volunteer Health Clinic) to offer free, hands-on Family Nutrition & Exercise classes rooted in MyPlate guidance.

name: Ascend Consulting Group
summary: The UT Austin chapter of Ascend Consulting Group is a student-run consulting organization that partners with tech startups to provide real-world solutions.

name: Dogs Without Borders Foundation
description:
Two countries. One mission: give every dog a chance.

 

We are a small nonprofit working in both Central Mexico and Central Texas, united by one deep commitment — to protect and uplift the lives of dogs in need.

 

In San Miguel de Allende, we begin our work where it matters most: the streets.
We sponsor free spay and neuter campaigns to reduce suffering and overpopulation. We also rescue vulnerable puppies, giving them a second chance through loving foster care, full medical treatment, and the support they need to heal and thrive.

 

In Austin, Texas, our focus shifts to advocacy and empowerment.
We raise funds to provide training scholarships for shelter dogs, helping them become adoptable companions. We also organize the transport of rescued dogs from Mexico to find forever homes in the U.S.


And because we believe in compassion on every level, we invite University of Texas students to be part of our mission. Students have the opportunity to walk our rescue dogs and visit Austin-area animal shelters to give long-overlooked dogs the exercise, companionship, and hope they deserve. Beyond hands-on care, students help organize campus awareness events, adoption outreach, fundraising campaigns, and social media efforts, gaining the chance to make an immediate impact. We also host volunteer days that double as a mental health break with purpose — a way to connect with pups, meet fellow animal advocates, and bring change across borders!

Please fill out the interest form below so we know your availabilities:

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe0wLsfFY6E4qeITUNrOwvm0qI6sVIaPvRPgpU_VDp2TbrbYw/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=106251602059216970503
summary: Miss your dogs at home? Looking for a break from campus or a mental health day? Join us! Visit Austin shelters to walk long-overlooked dogs, play with cats, and spend time with our rescue pups from Mexico while connecting with other animal lovers!

name: Euphony at Texas
description: Euphony is a student organization at The University of Texas at Austin dedicated to fostering a collaborative, creative, and welcoming community around programming, artificial intelligence, and emerging technologies. Through workshops, co-working sessions, demos, and community events, Euphony aims to inspire students to explore novel tools, build impactful projects, and connect with peers who share a passion for innovation.
summary: Euphony is dedicated to fostering a collaborative, creative, and welcoming community around programming, artificial intelligence, and emerging technologies.

name: 725 Dream at the University of Texas at Austin
description: 725 Dream at UT Austin is a student-led chapter dedicated to increasing accessibility to U.S. citizenship. Rooted in the belief that financial barriers should never stand in the way of opportunity, we work to support and empower aspiring citizens through advocacy, outreach, and community partnerships. The chapter connects students with meaningful service and engagement opportunities, builds collaborations with local law firms and organizations, and raises awareness on campus about immigration issues. By mobilizing resources, knowledge, and compassion, 725 Dream at UT Austin seeks to create a stronger, more inclusive community where every aspiring citizen has the tools and support to achieve their dream.
summary: 725 Dream at UT Austin increases accessibility to U.S. citizenship by providing advocacy, outreach, and partnerships that empower aspiring citizens and expand opportunities in our community.

name: Mindful Medicine
description:
While many students pursuing medicine dedicate themselves to mastering rigorous STEM coursework, the practice of medicine requires more than scientific knowledge alone. Physicians must also cultivate empathy, communication skills, and an understanding of the human experience, qualities that are often underemphasized in traditional undergraduate and graduate curricula.

The Mindful Medicine Club seeks to bridge this gap by fostering reflection, storytelling, and dialogue among future healthcare professionals. Through writing workshops, group discussions, and interactive activities, members will explore the human side of medicine while strengthening their ability to listen and connect with others. The organization will also host guest speakers from the medical field who will share personal experiences and insights on the importance of narrative and empathy in clinical practice.

Importantly, this organization is not limited to pre-medical students; it welcomes members from all academic backgrounds who are interested in the intersections of health, storytelling, and the human experience. By combining science with narrative, the club will provide a space for students to develop the skills and perspectives necessary to become not only knowledgeable professionals but also compassionate and empathetic individuals.
summary: A community for students to explore the human side of medicine through storytelling, reflection, and dialogue. Open to all majors interested in empathy, health, and the power of narrative.

name: The Storytellers
description:
The Storytellers’ mission is to harness the power of storytelling to turn the lived experiences of youth into illustrated children’s books that educate, inspire, and foster empathy.

 

The storytellers partners with local non-profits and schools in the Austin community. By engaging youth from these establishments, we provide a structured creative outlet for reflection and personal growth, while honoring and amplifying their voices. Through this process, we seek celebrate diverse perspectives and share authentic stories that resonate with children and families everywhere.

 

UT students volunteer as authors, helping youth craft manuscripts, or as illustrators, bringing those stories to life through art.
summary: The Storytellers work with Austin hospitals, nonprofits, and schools to turn children’s real stories into illustrated books. UT students join as authors, guiding manuscripts, or as illustrators, bringing each child’s story to life through original art.

name: The ASH Collective
description:
The ASH Collective aims to cultivate a diverse and committed group of UT student volunteers who serve weekly at the Austin State Hospital. At its core, this organization values long-term commitment, believing that sustained involvement fosters the most meaningful relationships between volunteers and adolescent patients. Furthermore, by emphasizing diversity within its volunteer base, this organization strives to reflect a wide range of identities, increasing the likelihood that each patient can connect with someone who resonates with their background or experience. Finally, by utilizing enthusiastic college students, The ASH Collective harnesses the unique ability of young adults to relate to adolescents and youth, often sharing similar ages, interests, and cultural touchpoints, which helps build authentic and supportive connections.

 

Common volunteer activities include leading games of basketball, kickball, soccer, and other sports. Other activities include playing board and card games, hosting arts and crafts, and simply chatting with patients and residents.
summary: The ASH Collective fosters long-term, meaningful relationships between UT students and adolescent patients at Austin State Hospital through volunteering, grounded in consistency, empathy, and trust.

name: NOIRE
description:
Noire is an organization for UT’s Black creatives to build their own personal brands through networking, collaborative projects, and professional development opportunities. Our vision is to be a creative network for Black creative to elevate themselves and their brand, whether it’s their personal brand or business.

We aim to provide resources, networking events, resume and portfolio building opportunities to ALL Black creatives at UT. We aim to foster a strong creative network and circulate wealth within the community. Our mission is to empower and assist the next Black creative visionaries in succeeding in their future careers.

This organization is open to ALL majors and classifications.
summary: Empowering Black creative visionaries the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Unmute Journal
description:
Unmute seeks to provide students at the University of Texas at Austin—both undergraduate and graduate and of any major, college, or discipline—a new platform to demonstrate their passions and scholarship on various musical subjects. Submitted works can be of any format, ranging from formal, academic papers to non-academic pieces including poems, narratives, anecdotes, and brief philosophical musings. Musical content may belong to any subject even tangentially related to music, including but not limited to the following areas:

  • Musicology
  • Ethnomusicology
  • Philosophy of music
  • Musical ethnography 
  • Music education
  • Ensembles
  • Music performance and historical performance practices
  • Music theory
  • Composition
  • Theatre and dance
  • Music administration
  • Music therapy
  • Commercial music
  • Music production and industry
  • Music science
  • Non-music studies/miscellaneous creative content

For the first time ever at the University of Texas, students can enjoy an accessible platform in which their voices can be heard through a medium not currently available to them. Additionally, any student at UT Austin can apply to serve on the journal’s operations and logistics team. The peer-reviewing and editorial processes will be provided by volunteer graduate students in the Butler School of Music’s Association for Graduate Ethnomusicology and Musicology Students (AGEMS) and Music Theory division.

Unmute explores the profound ideas, inherent tensions, fleeting emotions, and candid realities of engaging with music. From the quiet dedication in practice rooms to the vastness of stadium performances, and everything in between, our journal amplifies the often-hidden facets of the art. Nascent or mature, scholarly or creative—Unmute is a place where diverse musical meditations converge.
summary: Unmute seeks to provide students at the University of Texas at Austin—both undergraduate and graduate and of any major, college, or discipline—a new platform to demonstrate their passions and scholarship on various musical subjects.

name: UTea
summary: Tea and tea culture appreciation/social club

name: Grain Photo Club
description: Grain Photo Club is a student-led community for anyone at UT with an interest in photography, regardless of major or experience level. We create space to connect with others, exchange ideas, and explore the many ways photography shapes how we see the world. Throughout the year, members have opportunities to share their work, hear directly from established photographers, and contribute to a collective culture of creativity. The club culminates in an end-of-year exhibition, celebrating the diverse voices and visions within our community.
summary: Grain Photo Club is a space for UT students of all majors who share an interest in photography. We focus on building community, sharing perspectives, and finding inspiration together. Whether through conversation, collaboration, or showcasing our work.

name: Texas Equity Management
description:
We serve to educate members through experiential learning opportunities, including the use of digital stock portfolios through “paper trading” and active participation in stock pitches. We further aim to develop knowledge of market trends and economic factors by providing consistent coverage of macroeconomic developments and their potential impact on financial markets through our meetings.

Through these meetings and events, we strive to better our members with analytical and decision-making skills that will prepare them for a financial career. Texas Equity Management is a community of curiosity and professionalism that offers students a supportive network of peers and fellow academics.
summary: Texas Equity Management (TEM), aims to foster financial literacy, investment knowledge, and professional development to establish this organization as a collaborative environment for students interested in equity markets and financial analysis.

name: Midtown College at the University of Texas at Austin
description: Midtown College seeks to extend the freedom and family of Jesus among college students at the University of Texas
summary: Midtown College seeks to extend the freedom and family of Jesus among college students at the University of Texas

name: Responsible Intelligence Network
summary: Our mission is to empower college students to develop and apply AI responsibly. RIN creates a space to explore AI technologiesOur mission is to empower college students to develop and apply AI responsibly. RIN creates a space to explore AI technologies,

name: Vietnamese Culture & Language
summary: This organization serves to promote and preserve Vietnamese customs by upholding the values of cultural involvement and educational enrichment in Vietnamese culture and Vietnamese language.

name: Texas Minority Sports Association
description: The Texas Minority Sports Association (TMSA) is a student-led organization at The University of Texas at Austin dedicated to empowering underrepresented students to break into the competitive sports industry. Through professional development, mentorship, and networking opportunities.
summary: The Texas Minority Sports Association aims to empower and equip underrepresented students with the knowledge, connections, and resources necessary to break into the competitive sports industry.

name: Texas National Arab American Medical Association
description:
Texas NAAMA NextGen is the University of Texas at Austin chapter of the National Arab American Medical Association (NAAMA). Our organization empowers students pursuing careers across healthcare: medicine, dentistry, nursing, pharmacy, public health, and allied health, through mentorship, professional development, leadership training, cultural engagement, and a supportive community.

Membership is open to all UT Austin students, regardless of nationality or background. While our mission uplifts Arab American healthcare leaders, we believe diversity strengthens healthcare and enriches our community.

What We Offer

  • Leadership Opportunities: Shape a growing chapter while building your resume and practical leadership skills useful for competitive health-related applications.

  • Mentorship & Career Growth: Access NAAMA’s national network of 4,000+ healthcare professionals for one-on-one mentorship, shadowing, application guidance, and career advice across specialties (medicine, dentistry, psychiatry, pediatrics, neurology, surgery, public health, and more).

  • Exclusive Speaker Events: Hear from physicians, clinicians, researchers, and admissions leaders (including assistant deans) who share specialty insights and evidence-based strategies for strong applications.

  • School Tours & Information Sessions: Join virtual and in-person tours and info sessions at medical, dental, and other health-science schools to learn about curricula, facilities, and admissions processes.

  • Scholarships & Test-Prep Benefits: Learn about NAAMA scholarships (merit, need, service) and member-only test-prep benefits with leading providers such as DATBooster and The Princeton Review (including course, tutoring, and admissions support options, plus complimentary consultations where available).

  • Cultural & Social Programming: Celebrate Arab American heritage, build community, and expand your network through cultural events and socials open to all members.

  • Graduation Recognition: Earn a sage-green graduation cord by meeting participation requirements, recognizing your commitment and impact.

  • National Affiliation: As a Texas NAAMA NextGen member, you are connected to NAAMA’s national network, unlocking conferences, leadership development, and collaboration with chapters across the country.

Texas NAAMA NextGen prepares students for impactful, culturally informed careers in healthcare while fostering belonging, service, and leadership. Join us to engage with mentors, grow your skills, and contribute to a diverse, supportive community at UT Austin.
summary: Texas NAAMA NextGen supports all UT Austin students pursuing healthcare careers with mentorship, leadership roles, scholarships, test-prep discounts, speaker events, cultural programs, health profession school tours, and national NAAMA connections.

name: Triangle Fraternity
description:
What is Triangle?

Triangle is a social and professional STEM fraternity that was founded by 16 civil engineering students at the University of Illinois in 1907.

Established at UT fall of 2025, our chapter is a facilitator for likeminded men to find brotherhood, intellectual vitality and career development opportunities. 

 

Who can join?

Men majoring in the STEM fields, regardless of background, who are invested in establishing connections and fostering lifelong friendships.

 

When do we meet?

We usually meet twice per week: at chapter meetings on Sunday and for our weekly event.

During chapter meetings we discuss pressing matters pertaining to the fraternity, and we share our ideas and goals for the growth of our members and organization. 

Our weekly events vary; from socials like bowling, volleyball, game nights, baking competitions and parties, to service events such as park cleanups and volunteering for STEM events, Triangle men do it all!

 

What separates us from other fraternities?

With us, potential members have the opportunity to partake in a community of intelligent and ambitious STEM students who stimulate both academic and social growth in their peers. As Triangle’s newest chapter, all members have the opportunity to shape our culture and traditions through a shared vision. This unique opportunity allows all members to engage in the founding of a prestigious STEM organization. To top it off, Triangle has a robust 38,000-man alumni network, mostly composed of men in the STEM industries. Notable alumni include William R. Hoskins (inventor of the aluminum can), Frederick Kappel (Chairman of AT&T, served in the Johnson and Nixon administrations, recipient of the Presidential Medal of Freedom), Steven L. Miller (Former CEO and President of Shell Oil Company) and Micheal Morhaime (Co-founder of Blizzard Entertainment, the videogame developer of World of Warcraft and Overwatch).
summary: Triangle was founded in 1907 by 16 civil engineers. Established at UT in fall 2025, it unites STEM men seeking brotherhood, growth and career support. Members meet twice weekly for chapter business, socials and service. Join to help shape a new legacy.

name: Texas Marine Robotics
summary: Texas Marine Robotics (T.M.R.) is a UT student club building an autonomous surface vessel (ASV) for competition. We bring together engineers and programmers to learn, collaborate, and apply their skills to real-world robotics challenges on the water.

name: 1967 Latina Run Club
description: The 1967 Latina Run Club is a student-led organization at UT Austin that creates a safe and empowering environment for anyone interested in running, no matter their pace or experience. We host two runs each week: one “Hot Girl Walk” group for a more social, relaxed pace, and one faster-paced running group for those seeking more intensity. Beyond running, we host one to two socials each month and two philanthropy events each semester to give back to our UT and Austin community. We also love partnering with other Latino organizations on campus to strengthen connections and increase our sense of community. Our goal is to promote physical health, mental wellness, and belonging while giving students a supportive space to connect, stay active, and build friendships.
summary: The 1967 Latina Run Club is a student-led organization that creates a safe, empowering space for runners of all levels. We host weekly runs to promote health, community, and wellness.

name: Hooked on Harmonies
description:
Hooked on Harmonies is a co-ed vocal group that performs pop and contemporary music for the Austin community. The group focuses on outreach performances at schools, hospitals, nursing homes, community events, and charity fundraisers, bringing joy and connection to people of all ages.

Members work together in group arrangements, building musical skills while making friends and having fun. By performing familiar songs that everyone loves, Hooked on Harmonies creates memorable experiences and supports local causes through music and community engagement.
summary: Hooked on Harmonies is a co-ed vocal group performing pop and contemporary hits for the Austin community, sharing music and giving back through performances at schools, hospitals, nursing homes, and charity events.

name: Latin American Aspiring Law Student Association
description: The Latin American Aspiring Law Student Association at the University of Texas at Austin is   dedicated to advancing advocacy, empowerment, and educational access for Latino undergraduate students with aspirations in the legal field. We strive to cultivate a supportive and inclusive community that not only uplifts Latino voices but also equips members with the tools and confidence to navigate the complex journey of pre-law and law school. Through mentorship, leadership development, and culturally grounded programming, we aim to inspire meaningful engagement, amplify representation, and create lasting connections within and beyond the university.
summary: LAALSA is dedicated to advancing advocacy, empowerment, and educational access for Latino undergraduate students with aspirations in the legal field. We strive to cultivate a supportive and inclusive community that uplifts Latino voices.

name: Clean Cycle Initiative
description: The purpose of Clean Cycle Initiative is to empower girls in Nepal and India through education and access to hygienic menstrual resources.
summary: Clean Cycle Initiative is intended to aid girls in under-resourced public schools in Nepal and India by providing virtual education on menstrual cycles, making environmentally-friendly pads, and sending handmade care packages.

name: PEA at UT Austin
description:
PEA stands for Personal Effectiveness Association, a certified 501 (c)(3) non-profit organization designed to support and improve individual mental health through exercise and knowledge of fitness and nutrition. Whether you are an athlete, or have never been to the gym, this is the place for you.  

As college students, we've noticed there are limited opportunities for teenagers and young adults to address anxieties and everyday worries. Local students are invited to join us as we go on runs, do yoga, and explore various bodyweight exercises together while communicating about whatever may be on your mind in this safe environment.
summary: We are an organization designed to support and improve individual mental health through exercise. Whether you are an athlete, or have never been to the gym, this is the place for you! All UT Austin are invited to join us.

name: Society of First Principles
summary: Society to discuss important topics of philosophy, religion, and political philosophy.

name: Civics Law & Policy Society
description: The Civics Law & Policy Society seeks to foster intellectual inquiry and interdisciplinary dialogue. It will serve as a preparatory forum and promote the virtues of integrity, justice, and ethical reasoning for individuals pursuing a career in law. The Society will celebrate all forms of civic engagement and examine how the legal profession can benefit communities. Through service-based learning projects, members will provide for community needs and be introduced to important concepts such as pro bono initiatives. Members will also have the opportunity to attend lectures featuring distinguished legal scholars. Through these experiences, society members will gain meaningful connections with legal professionals and deepen their understanding of jurisprudence. Furthermore, the Society will host forums for debate and inquiry about law, ethics, and social justice. These forums preserve a tradition of intellectual fellowship and philosophical inquiry regarding the foundations of law within the Society. Preserving the spirit of inquiry ensures the maintenance and development of all members’ moral foundation. The refinement of Society members’ ethical reasoning skills will also aid members when facing legal ambiguity in their careers. The Society will further cultivate intellectual tradition by encouraging undergraduate research in jurisprudence and civic engagement. Society members will have the opportunity to submit their work for peer review and publication in a Society-sponsored journal. Ultimately, The Civics Law & Policy Society aims to prepare its members for meaningful contributions to the field of law through intellectual fellowship, service, and philosophical inquiry.
summary: The Civics Law & Policy Society fosters inquiry, integrity, and civic engagement through service, debate, research, and lectures, preparing members for ethical leadership and meaningful contributions in the field of law.

name: Fynix
summary: Social organization that prioritizes uplifting local small businesses and the community among UT students, as well as donating/volunteering at local charities, while also promoting Austin culture and fun

name: American Adages Society
description:
The American Adages Society (AAS) at the University of Texas at Austin is a student-led organization devoted to exploring the wisdom embedded in timeless sayings. Founded on the belief that adages are more than old phrases - that they are living vessels of cultural memory - the AAS studies these expressions as windows into history, language, and the shared human experience.

Our mission is to decode the origins, meanings, and transformations of adages while revealing their enduring relevance in modern life. We approach adages as cultural and linguistic artifacts, tracing how they shape identity, behavior, and thought across generations. By connecting linguistic study, philosophy, literature, and storytelling, the AAS aims to revive the art of concise wisdom in a world overwhelmed by noise.

Members participate in discussions, guest lectures, creative projects, and collaborative research that bridge disciplines and foster critical, deeper reflection. Together, we build an evolving archive of American adages - preserving insight, humor, and truth distilled through centuries of human experience.

As a member, you'll help establish the society's foundation, projects, and legacy at our university. More than a club, this is a community of thinkers exploring how collective language and inherited wisdom can illuminate a strong path forward.
summary: A student-led society at the University of Texas exploring the wisdom of adages through discussion, research, and creativity, reviving timeless sayings as a bridge between language, culture, and modern thought.

name: TexasDayTrading
summary: UT's very first organization working to teach undergraduate students the lucrative hobby of day trading and all it has to offer.  At TDT, we will help individuals trade based on catalysts, make descriptive models, and hold weekly market updates.

name: Texas Health Education Advocacy and Law
description: Texas Health Education Advocacy and Law is intended to bridge the critical gap between law, medicine, and public health by bringing together students across disciplines to collaborate, learn, and prepare for careers addressing today's most pressing health challenges. Through interdisciplinary dialogue, research, community initiatives, and professional development, Texas HEAL equips future lawyers, healthcare providers, policymakers, and public health professionals with the tools to understand how legal and health systems intersect, advocate effectively for health equity, and create meaningful change in communities that need it most.
summary: Texas Health Education Advocacy and Law is intended to bridge the critical gap between law, medicine, and public health by bringing together students across disciplines to collaborate and learn.

name: KSEA Chapter at the University of Texas
description:
We seek to foster communication, academic and vocational collaboration, and cultural connection among Korean American and allied students, faculty, and professionals in the STEM fields. The organization supports career development, scientific exchange, and community outreach aligned with the mission of KSEA Headquarters.

 

Please join our mailing list: https://utlists.utexas.edu/sympa/subscribe/ksea
summary:
Promote the application of science and technology for the general welfare of society
Help Scientists and Engineers develop their full career potential
Serve the Korean-American Communities

name: Children's Advocacy Initiative
summary: CAI is a student-led organization focused on advocacy, education, outreach, and research related to child abuse prevention and child welfare.

name: Texas Midshipmen
description: Texas Midshipmen is the student organization representing the Naval Reserve Officers’ Training Corps. Our purpose is to support the development of midshipmen, promote leadership and professionalism, and engage the campus community.
summary: Texas Midshipmen is the student organization representing the Naval Reserve Officers’ Training Corps. Our purpose is to support the development of midshipmen, promote leadership and professionalism, and engage the campus community.

name: Texas Dodgeball Club
description: The Texas Dodgeball Club provides a place to play dodgeball year-round and create a fun loving community. We are open to all UT Austin students, regardless of prior experience with playing dodgeball. We play weekly scrimmages, travel to compete in tournaments against other universities, and have social events outside of the court.
summary: The Texas Dodgeball Club is a year-round co-ed dodgeball club that has weekly scrimmages and yearly tournaments. Open to anyone regardless of playing experience.

name: Alpha Tau Omega
description:
The ATO Experience: 

If you’re looking for a place where you belong, a place to have fun, and a place where you can grow and develop in meaningful ways with like-minded individuals, ATO is that place.

But more than a place, Alpha Tau Omega is an opportunity. It allows you to be part of something much bigger than a college experience, a brotherhood that makes a difference and a brotherhood with a strong values-based foundation.

Since 1865, Alpha Tau Omega has been a provider of amazing opportunities. More than 234,000 men have become ATOs and taken advantage of an experience that is like nothing else.

As America’s Leadership Development Fraternity, ATO provides a great opportunity for young men who are looking for a truly unique fraternity experience.

https://youtu.be/MEKbgQ0scK4
summary: Alpha Tau Omega is founded on the values of brotherhood, scholarship, and integrity. ATO fosters leadership, moral character, and service while uniting men through a commitment to love and respect.

name: Redeemed Campus Fellowship
summary: The organization aims to help its members grow spiritually. It also provides career guide and mentorship.

name: Hispanic Latino Student Association
description:
The Hispanic Latino Student Association at The University of Texas at Austin helps create a welcoming space where all students celebrate and appreciate culture. HLSA offers social student events, mentorship opportunities through the Familia mentoring system, and academic excellence programming, as well as opportunities to develop as community leaders through HLSA-organized volunteer initiatives. The organization recognizes that many UT Austin students face challenges, including coming from low-income backgrounds or navigating college in general. Through mentorship and community building within HLSA, the organization strives to make the college experience more accessible for every member.

HLSA events include campus-wide cultural showcases featuring festivals that honor traditions such as Día de los Muertos, hosting social gatherings designed to foster meaningful student connections, and academic workshops that provide resources for success. These experiences collectively foster a deep sense of celebration in Hispanic and Latino heritage while strengthening connections between students here within the UT Austin community. HLSA serves to help members build lasting friendships and honor cultural traditions throughout the UT Austin community.
summary: The Hispanic Latino Student Association is to provide a space where all students can celebrate and appreciate culture while also fostering a strong community among students, by hosting volunteering, social, and academic events.

name: Heart Club @ UT
description:
Heart Club offers students unique opportunities to lead, serve and champion cardiovascular health and wellbeing while improving their own. Members gain career insights, earn recognition and awards, hone their marketing and communication skills, support survivors and caregivers and earn community service hours. Heart Clubs are advancing health and hope for everyone, everywhere and are guided by a commitment to four key pillars: leadership, impact, fundraising and well-being.

Heart Club activities can include, but are not limited to, leading Hands-Only CPR and AED awareness events, educating peers about stroke prevention, organizing heart-healthy food drives, spreading gratitude to healthcare workers, volunteering at local American Heart Association events, raising donations in support of the American Heart Association, hosting health fairs and collaborating with other student organizations to promote healthier lifestyles and the connection between mind, heart and body.
summary: Heart Club is a chapter of the American Heart Association and allows students to advocate for cardiovascular health in their communities and gain leadership, fundraising and volunteering experience.

name: Longhorn Sportsmans Club
description: Longhorn Sportsmans Club is a community for UT students who love the outdoors. We bring together hunters, anglers, and conservation minded students to share skills, plan trips, and explore the wild places of Texas. Whether you grew up in the country or are brand new to outdoor sports, our club offers opportunities to learn, connect, and get outside. We host events ranging from fishing tournaments and hunting trips to outdoor education events.
summary: Longhorn Sportsman's Club seeks to create a unified community of outdoorsmen at The University of Texas that fosters opportunity and fellowship to its members.

name: Texas Album of the Week
description: Texas Album of the Week explores the range, importance, and artistry of music by hosting discussions about a new album each week. Each week will feature a different genre (hip-hop, pop, rock, etc.). Members nominate albums that fit the chosen genre, then vote to select which album the group will listen to. During meetings, we examine the album’s impact by analyzing its lyrics, production, craftsmanship, and recurring themes. Members are encouraged to share their personal connections to the album with the group. Our goal is to highlight the spectrum of self-expression that music provides its listeners.
summary: Texas Album of the Week is an organization that seeks to provide a space for music lovers to explore the diversity and importance of music by encouraging healthy discussions of a different album every week.

name: Consciousness Studies Circle
description:
The Consciousness Studies Circle is an interdisciplinary academic student organization dedicated to philosophical and scientific inquiry into consciousness, self, and reality. With a focus on non-dual theories of consciousness, we engage with a wide range of Eastern and Western perspectives, spanning ancient and modern theories across philosophy and science. Our goal is to bring together students and faculty from diverse fields — AI, philosophy, neuroscience, physics, and beyond — for open discussions, reading circles, and guest lectures. Activities include:

  • Academic discussions and reading circles on nondual philosophical texts
  • Engagement with contemporary theories of mind and consciousness
  • Guest talks by philosophers, scientists, AI researchers, and scholars of nondual philosophies 

The Consciousness Studies Circle welcomes all sincere enquirers, regardless of background or prior knowledge, to engage in thoughtful study and open discussion.
summary: The Consciousness Studies Circle provides a platform for academic discussion and philosophical inquiry into consciousness, nonduality, and related topics in philosophy of mind, cognitive science, and AI.

name: UTeach Theatre Spotlights
description: UTeach Theatre Spotlights seeks to integrate any and all individuals of the UTeach theatre program to connect further with peers who share the same goal of becoming the future educators of theatre. UTeach Theatre Spotlights is primarily a social organization that focuses on establishing fellowship through mentoring, monthly check-ins, and bi-monthly events.
summary: Spotlights is a program dedicated to helping establish fellowship between students in the UTeach Theatre program.

name: Texas Trading Group
description: Texas Trading Group is designed to help students understand how financial markets operate across equities, fixed income, FX, commodities, and derivatives, from core fundamentals to real world execution. Members will develop a semester long pitch trade proposal, build technical market skills, and analyze both historical and current market case studies. Guest speakers, including C-suite executives and McCombs professors, will share their experiences and break down how macroeconomic drivers move assets.
summary: Texas Trading Group is a club where students learn markets through stock and rates pitches, market updates, a mock portfolio, and guest speakers. It’s the place to build real trading skills and connect with others interested in finance.

name: Women Entrepreneurship
description:
WE exists to empower the next generation of entrepreneurs and leaders, by offering them the tools, community and exposure needed to launch and grow entrepreneurial ventures.

Core Activities

  • Entrepreneurship Program: WE runs an exclusive program for entrepreneurs—offering cohort-based support, workshops, mentoring and networking.

  • Tools & Knowledge: The organization provides educational content, lectures and opportunities to learn from experienced entrepreneurs, investors and executives.

  • Networking & Community: WE builds a professional platform for connections among members and external leaders, creating a supportive ecosystem.

  • Ambassador Program: They invite leaders passionate about entrepreneurship to become Ambassadors, advocating for WE in their communities
summary: We are an organization dedicated to promoting entrepreneurship. WE aspires to help, guide, and inspire people to fulfill their entrepreneurial potential by providing skills & knowledge, exposure to role models, and a supportive community.

name: Culture Shock: The African American Culture Committee
description: We, the members of Culture Shock: The African American Culture Committee, in recognition of the importance of cultural awareness, leadership development, and community engagement, establish this organization to promote unity, service, and empowerment among students. We are committed to fostering an environment that celebrates African American culture and heritage, encourages meaningful dialogue, and supports the personal and academic growth of all members.
summary: Culture Shock: The African American Culture Committee fosters belonging, leadership, and cultural pride through community, service, and events that celebrate African American heritage and empower students to connect and grow.

name: Tamil Cultural Association
description: The Tamil Cultural Association is a student-led organization dedicated to celebrating and promoting Tamil language, heritage, and traditions. Our mission is to create a welcoming community where students can connect with Tamil culture through music, dance, food, festivals, and service.
summary: Join us to celebrate Tamil culture, movies, dance, and food, while making memories that will last a lifetime at the Forty Acres!

name: Music as Medicine
summary: Music as Medicine is intended to promote the well-being of patients and staff of healthcare facilities through our music. Bringing together musicians, volunteers, healthcare professionals and patients, we aim to create a supportive environment in Austin.

name: Self Betterment Club at Texas
summary: There is a big financial literacy crisis, fitness crisis, and emotional wellness crisis. Self Betterment Club at Texas focuses on teaching and holding individuals accountable to self-better themselves in those three aspects–finance, fitness, psychology.

name: Fit to Practice
description: Fit to Practice is a UT Austin law student organization dedicated to improving the physical, mental, and emotional well-being of law students. Our mission is to help law students become the most physically capable versions of themselves so they can excel in every other aspect of life — academically, professionally, and personally. We host biweekly meetings on wellness topics such as stress management, sleep, nutrition, and efficient training strategies tailored to the demands of law school. We also organize low-pressure fitness meetups, including group runs, recreational sports, and strength-focused sessions, to build healthy habits in a supportive, judgment-free environment. Our goal is to create a strong community centered on health, resilience, and sustainable success throughout law school and beyond.
summary: Fit to Practice is a UT Austin law student organization that helps law students become the most physically capable versions of themselves so they can excel in every other area of life through wellness-focused meetings and low-pressure fitness meetups.

name: The Diplomatic Table
description: The Diplomatic Table is intended to create an inclusive, respectful space for students at The University of Texas at Austin to engage across political, cultural, and ideological differences. The organization seeks to promote civil discourse, intellectual humility, and global awareness through moderated roundtable discussions, guest speakers, and professional development opportunities related to diplomacy, international affairs, and public service. Each student discussion will be mediated by a foreign service officer to train members with methods backed by real-world experience.
summary: The Diplomatic Table creates an inclusive, respectful space for students at The University of Texas at Austin to discuss a variety of policy topics mediated by foreign service officers (US diplomats).

name: Modern Latino Orchestra
description: The Modern Latino Orchestra is an organization dedicated to celebrating Latino culture and integrating our music across the campus of the University of Texas at Austin. We are focused on playing music that your mom would listen to while cleaning or working, such as salsa, reggaeton, Latin pop, Meregue, etc., ranging from the 1970s to the current day. We are looking for proficient musicians and anyone willing to contribute in other ways not related to music. Anyone is welcome!
summary: The Modern Latino Orchestra is an orchestra made to spread Latino music styles across campus and connect its members and listeners to Latino culture.

name: River of Life Chinese Young Adult Fellowship
description: The River of Life Chinese Young Adult Fellowship is established to provide a supportive community for Chinese young adults and Mandarin-speaking international students. Grounded in Christian values, the fellowship seeks to foster spiritual growth, personal development, and meaningful connections among its members. The fellowship organizes seminars addressing a wide range of topics, including academics, career development, physical and mental well-being, financial stewardship, as well as relationships, dating, and marriage. In addition to these seminars, the fellowship also offers a variety of social and community activities designed to cultivate fellowship, mutual encouragement, and holistic growth.
summary: The River of Life Chinese Young Adult Fellowship supports Chinese young adults and Mandarin-speaking students through faith-based community, seminars, and activities promoting spiritual, personal, and social growth.

name: Texas Amnesty International
description: Texas Amnesty International is dedicated to joining a global movement of millions of people demanding human rights for all people. We are open to all UT Austin students interested in advocacy, regardless of major or background. We host biweekly meetings where we provide human rights education and participate in various campaigns. Members have the opportunity to meet like-minded activists and gain hands-on experience in advocacy efforts.
summary: Texas Amnesty International is a human rights activism organization that helps students get involved in the fight for human rights for all people. We participate in campaigns in order to demand for the protection and expansion of human rights.

name: Texas Bowling Club
description: Texas Bowling Club at The University of Texas at Austin is a student-run organization that brings together bowlers of all skill levels, whether you’re picking up a ball for the first time or you’ve been competing for years. Our goal is to create a welcoming community where members can have fun, improve their game, and connect with other Longhorns who love bowling. We host regular practices and meetups focused on skill development, consistency, and confidence on the lanes. Members can learn fundamentals, work on spare shooting and lane play, and get feedback from more experienced bowlers in a supportive, low-pressure environment. Just as importantly, we emphasize good sportsmanship, teamwork, and a positive culture where everyone feels included. Texas Bowling Club also plans to participate in bowling leagues, giving members a structured way to bowl regularly, track improvement, and enjoy friendly competition. For those who want to take it further, we provide opportunities to compete in local events and intercollegiate tournaments and to represent UT Austin as part of our Competitive Team through an established tryout and selection process. Whether you’re looking for a new hobby, a consistent league to join, or a chance to compete and travel, Texas Bowling Club offers a place to build skills, friendships, and Longhorn pride—one frame at a time.
summary: Texas Bowling Club at UT Austin welcomes all skill levels for weekly practices, leagues, social events, and Intercollegiate tournaments. Build skills, friendships, and sportsmanship while representing the Longhorns in tournaments.

name: Nuclear Is Clean Energy
description: Nuclear is Clean Energy (NiCE) is dedicated to educating the public about nuclear energy by engaging students across all disciplines to promote its safe, efficient, and innovative role in the clean-energy sector. For more information, join our Slack.
summary: Nuclear is Clean Energy (NiCE) is dedicated to educating the public about nuclear energy by engaging students across all disciplines to promote its safe, efficient, and innovative role in the clean-energy sector. For more information, join our Slack.

name: Women Leaders in Communication
description:
Women Leaders in Communication (WLC) is a student organization at The University of Texas at Austin focused on developing confident, ethical, and effective leaders through the lens of communication. While WLC centers and uplifts women’s leadership, the organization is open to students of all majors, backgrounds, and identities who are interested in strengthening their communication and leadership skills.

WLC provides members with opportunities for professional development, mentorship, and community-building through speaker panels, workshops, networking events, and collaborative discussions. The organization emphasizes communication as a foundational skill across industries, including law, business, public service, media, and advocacy, and encourages members to explore how strong communication can be used for leadership, impact, and positive change.

By creating an inclusive and supportive environment, WLC aims to foster meaningful connections, promote interdisciplinary learning, and empower students to grow academically, professionally, and personally during their time at UT.
summary: Women Leaders in Communication (WLC) is a student organization at UT Austin dedicated to uplifting and developing leaders across disciplines through professional development, mentorship, and community-building centered on effective communication.

name: Pro Bono Pathways
description: Pro Bono Pathways intends to dedicate time and resources to fundraising money for Pro Bono law firms and contribute to the possibility of members in our community receiving a better shot at legal justice that would not be able to receive it otherwise. We are an open organization seeking any and all UT students wanting to be involved in making friends and getting active while giving back to our community. We have weekly community walks, monthly meetings, and semesterly walk-a-thons and socials.
summary: We are an organization open to all students passionate about raising money for our community.

name: AMS at UT Austin
description:
The Texas Atmospheric Society (TexasAtmos) is the official student chapter of the American Meteorological Society at the University of Texas at Austin. We are a student-led organization dedicated to building a supportive community for anyone interested in atmospheric science, meteorology, and climate,  regardless of major or experience level.

We offer professional development through workshops, networking events, and guest speaker seminars featuring industry leaders, as well as opportunities for community outreach and collaboration with fellow Longhorns who share a passion for weather and climate.

Membership is completely free and open to all UT Austin students. To join, simply attend one of our meetings!

We meet every Monday at 5:00 PM in the Barrow Conference Center (JGB 4.102).
summary: The AMS Student Chapter at UT Austin will build a welcoming community for students interested in atmospheric and related sciences. Our goal is to foster connection, collaboration, and professional growth while serving as a hub for learning & networking.

name: The Philosophy Society at The University of Texas
description: The Philosophy Society at the University of Texas intends to be a social student organization that has the express purpose of proliferating philosophical thought, inquiry, and discussion. Seeking to provide a space and platform for students of all majors, backgrounds, and opinions to share and engage in philosophical discussion, participate in events to learn more about the discipline, and host activities for members to socialize and promote the diffusion of different philosophical perspectives.
summary: The Philosophy Society at the University of Texas intends to be a social student organization that has the express purpose of proliferating philosophical thought, inquiry, and discussion. We host discussions for all majors to partake in conversations.

name: Engineers in Business
description: Open to all STEM majors, but students of Cockrell School of Engineering or students with a tech background are especially encouraged to apply. We will host monthly meetings, hold guest speaker events, and workshops regarding networking. Engineers in Business is the first student organization on UT campus to connect engineering disciplines with the business world.
summary: Engineers in Business is a student organization dedicated to equipping engineering students with ample resources in order to ensure their success in the cut throat business world.

name: Billable Laughs Stand-Up Comedy
description: Billable Laughs is UT Law’s stand-up comedy group. We write jokes, workshop them, and try them on real humans at open mics. We meet biweekly, hit local comedy shows, and put on an annual UT Law showcase. If you want to get funnier and more confident speaking, this is it.
summary: Billable Laughs is UT Law’s stand-up comedy group. We meet biweekly to write + workshop jokes, hit open mics, attend comedy shows, and host an annual law school showcase. Open to students, alumni, and staff.

name: Young Navigators of Tomorrow
summary: YNT is a social organization that creates inclusive, and uplifting entertainment opportunities for young people and the local community. YNT organizes events, activities, and community initiatives that foster connection, and promote social well-being.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi Beta Sigma Chapter
description:
Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Gender Inclusive Professional Fraternity exists nationally to foster a gender-inclusive brotherhood for undergraduate students interested in law and government.

Our brotherhood is open to all undergraduate focuses of study. Chapters are devoted to developing students professionally and academically to be leaders in the world that follows graduation.
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi is a pre law and government professional fraternity that focuses on fostering brotherhood and upholding community, service, and academic/professionalsuccess.

name: Precious Plastic TX
description: Our mission is to fight plastic pollution from the bottom-up & make recycling more personal and accessible.
summary: Precious Plastic Texas aims to bring city-wide awareness to waste recovery efforts by implementing an open-source process to upcycle plastic waste at UT. We will be building a line of machines to shred plastic and convert them into useful products.

name: Viridis Fund
description: The Viridis Fund aims to teach students fundamentals of holistic investment management through thematic and sector-based investments in a student-managed portfolio. Through a curriculum of advanced financial concepts, students will be able to effectively valuate public companies and propose investments for the fund.
summary: The Viridis Fund aims to teach students fundamentals of holistic investment management through thematic and sector-based investments in a student-managed portfolio.

name: Hispanic Undergraduates in Geological Sciences UT
description: HUGS UT is an org dedicated to serving the undergraduate Hispanic student population in the geoscience and related fields at UT. HUGS UT is open to students of any background, regardless of their ethnicity, race, etc., and is an org dedicated to creating a safe space that can garner academic and professional success within its community. HUGS UT is run by students and is for students and everyones unique scholastic journey by showcasing the accomplishments of fellow students and faculty who represent the Hispanic communtiy.
summary: A professional, social, and academic organization catered to showcasing Hispanic student cultural and community excellence with a diverse and open approach to community building.

name: Youth Aid Initiative
description: Our mission is to provide meaningful, hands-on assistance to vulnerable communities while raising awareness of the humanitarian challenges these groups face. The purpose of the Youth Aid Initiative is to create structured opportunities for volunteer engagement, community partnership, and advocacy, working with nonprofits that support displaced and vulnerable populations in regions affected by conflict, poverty, and war. Our aims include fostering a campus culture of service, promoting informed dialogue on displacement and immigration issues, and equipping members with practical experience in nonprofit collaboration and outreach.
summary: The Youth Aid Initiative is dedicated to supporting and serving victims of war, refugees, and low-income immigrant households through fundraising, volunteer service at local resettlement agencies, and further community engagement events.

name: Texas Hindi-Urdu Club
description: • The Texas Hindi-Urdu Club aims to create a relaxed environment for Hindi- and Urdu-speakers to improve their command of the languages in a non-academic context. This club is open to people of all levels of fluency, from beginners to native speakers. The Hindi-Urdu Club recognizes the importance of speaking a language to gain confidence in one's proficiency. The club thus has weekly suggested topics and related key words which allow members to expand their vocabulary and comfortability in advanced subjects. Through community and connection, the Hindi-Urdu Club hopes to empower Hindi- and Urdu-speakers to improve their skills in these spoken languages.
summary: The Texas Hindi-Urdu Club is a place for Hindi- and Urdu-speakers to come together to practice speaking their languages and form friendships with others of similar cultural and language backgrounds.

name: Students Concerned About Bad Stuff
description: Scabs is committed to limiting and stopping bad stuff students are concerned about across campus. Open to all UT Austin students interested in conservation. regardless of major or background. We will have monthly service projects, volunteer opportunities, and meetings. Members have the opportunity to voice there concern and act on them as a part of a bigger group.
summary: SCABS is a UT Austin student organization dedicated to campus conservation and collective action. Open to all majors, the group empowers students to voice their concerns and address them through monthly meetings, volunteer opportunities.

name: Texas Royale
description:
Texas Royale is the official community for Clash Royale players at UT Austin. Whether you are a casual player looking for a 2v2 partner or a competitive grinder, our goal is to create a vibrant space where students can socialize and compete.

We host regular social nights and campus tournaments designed for all skill levels. Join us to find your clan, improve your play, and connect with fellow players across the University
summary: Texas Clash Royale Club

name: HealthMaze
description:
Health Maze is made possible with the help of dedicated volunteers to provide valuable resources and expand our reach to serve the overlooked and underserved communities.

Whether you're passionate about healthcare, community outreach, or supporting our mission, there’s a role for you at Health Maze! Your contribution, big or small, helps us bring healthcare access to those who need it most.
summary: At HealthMaze, our mission is to make it easier for individuals to access the medical care they need. We are passionate about improving healthcare outcomes for underserved communities and are dedicated to making a meaningful impact.

name: Texas Hispanic Association of Pre-Pharmacists
description: The Hispanic Association for Pre-Pharmacists endeavors to cultivate a supportive community among undergraduate students who aspire to pursue pharmaceutical sciences as their professional career path. Though "Hispanic" is a key cultural pillar within the organization's name and identity, the group extends a warm welcome to students of any background, embracing them as part of nuestra familia. This inclusive approach fosters a strong social support system, where members can learn from both practicing pharmacists and fellow pharmacy students. Through shared experiences and mentorship, students gain valuable insights into the Hispanic experience within the field and discover how to serve their communities through their chosen vocations effectively. We are deeply committed to providing crucial representation, ensuring equity, and "quenching the thirst" for invaluable professional insights. Through these dedicated efforts, HAPP will continue to be a transformative space, empowering those who enter to emerge fully prepared for their future endeavors. Furthermore, we welcome all individuals who seek to deepen their understanding of Hispanic culture and community, recognizing that cultural exchange enriches everyone involved. We are deeply committed to pursuing equity within the workforce, fostering a profound understanding of individuals from all walks of life, and proudly showcasing our rich heritage and unique identities. With this commitment to equity, we recognize that true equality stems from understanding and mutual respect. This foundation allows each individual to shine brightly, like a star, knowing they are valued and operating on an equal playing field.
summary: The Hispanic Association of Pre-Pharmacists is dedicated to building a strong community for future pharmacists and pharmaceutical scientists. We welcome all backgrounds, fostering nuestra family while providing professional, and mentorship opportunities.

name: 360 Strategy Texas
description: Grounded in the belief that human challenges require human-centered solutions, we combine design thinking principles, advanced AI models, and the diverse expertise of UT Austin students to deliver innovative and impactful consulting services. We offer consulting training through professor and professional lecture series for general members, and hands-on consulting work with analyst and officer members. We work directly with other student organizations, local businesses and non profit organizations. Our analysts are given the opportunity to be placed in 3 pillars: Business Strategy, Engineering, or Creative. In your team you will work with clients to deliver solutions for your client.
summary: We are a pioneering consulting student organization focused on delivering projects to clients through a unique framework of understanding the root of the issue first: Design Thinking.

name: Civic Leadership Council
description: The purpose of the Civic Leadership Council is to address student concerns and further the development of the School of Civic Leadership. Our goals include organizing events to unite SCL students, promote career-readiness, offer leadership experience, work with student organizations within the college, and foster relationships between students and faculty. Through student-led initiatives, the Civic Leadership Council aims to provide students with meaningful experiences regarding their interests and the tools to successfully advocate for the school. Additionally, as a member of UT’s Senate of College Councils, CLC provides a voice to its students by representing student opinion on campus-wide and college-specific issues.
summary: The Civic Leadership Council serves as the legislative voice of the School of Civic Leadership, representing student opinions and facilitating a strong relation between, the school, faculty, students, and university community.

name: Medical School for Kids
description: The Medical School for Kids Outreach Chapter is a student-led community service organization that  aims to introduce children to the world of healthcare and medicine. Through interactive events such  as Mini White Coat Ceremonies, Teddy Bear Clinics, and Health & Hygiene Fairs, the chapter aims  to educate and inspire the next generation about medical sciences in a fun and engaging manner.
