The  organization will provide hands-on experiences that promote medical knowledge and healthy  lifestyles among children in the Austin community.
summary: The Medical School for Kids Outreach Chapter is a student-led community service organization that aims to introduce children to the world of healthcare and medicine through interactive events such as mini white coat ceremonies, teddy bear clinics, etc.

name: Salud para Todos
description: This organization is designed to bring together students interested in healthcare careers who want to learn Spanish medical terminology and patient communication. Living in a state with a large Hispanic population, I believe it is important to understand and respect Spanish language and culture so that healthcare professionals can provide the best possible care. The club focuses on two main components: learning medical Spanish and participating in outreach efforts to support healthcare initiatives in underserved Spanish-speaking communities in Austin.
summary: This club is for pre-health students who want to learn Spanish medical terminology and patient communication. Two components: learning medical Spanish and participating in outreach efforts to support health in underserved Spanish communities in Austin.

name: Texas Financial Futures
description: Texas Financial Futures is an organization that lets you master the fundamentals of finance while paying it forward to the broader community. As a member, you’ll learn practical skills in budgeting, investing, savings, and smart money management through interactive workshops. You’ll then put those skills into action by mentoring a 13–16-year-old student in an underserved Texas community, working together on hands-on projects and fun finance competitions. Along the way, you’ll build leadership experience, make meaningful connections, and become part of a community rooted in fun, finance, and service here at UT Austin.
summary: Learn real finance skills and mentor teens in underserved communities. Build your knowledge, gain leadership experience, and make an impact—all while having fun and growing at UT Austin.

name: Marvel Rivals at UT Austin
description:
Marvel Rivals at UT Austin is a student led organization dedicated to building a community that welcomes all around the hit video game Marvel Rivals. Our organization's goal is to bring together players of all interests and skill levels to establish UT as one of the leading colleges in popularizing and competing in Marvel Rivals.

We aim to create a space where students can gather, socialize, and find other people to play Marvel Rivals with. We understand that it can be difficult for students to meet new people with similar gaming interests, and because of that, our organization exists to help create a space where friendships can be made through playing Marvel Rivals. Marvel Rivals at UT Austin is open to everyone.

Since the game is free to play, we encourage anyone with an interest in video games, Marvel comics, or those who are new to gaming to join our organization!
summary: Marvel Rivals at UT Austin is dedicated to fostering a friendly environment built around a collected interest in the video game Marvel Rivals. Members can join up for casual matches, or tryout for the competitive team.

name: National Association for Business Economics at UT Austin
description: NABE is dedicated to creating a community of like-minded pre-professionals interested in developing skills and exploring diverse career opportunities. NABE is open to all UT students regardless of major, but business/economics students may benefit more. Our members will have the opportunity to learn from professionals in business and economics through guest speakers at our monthly meetings and to develop pre-professional skills through our resume workshops, mock interviews, and professional headshots.
summary: NABE is dedicated to creating a community of like-minded pre-professionals interested in developing skills and exploring diverse career opportunities. NABE is open to all UT students regardless of major, but business/economics students may benefit more.

name: Venezuelans at UT Austin
description: Venezuelans at UT Austin (VenzA) is a cultural and social organization dedicated to celebrating Venezuelan heritage and fostering community on campus. We host cultural events and social gatherings that highlight our traditions, connect Venezuelan students, and share our culture.
summary: Venezuelans at UT Austin celebrates our culture through events and gatherings that connect Venezuelan students and foster a strong community.

name: Crisis Aid Network
summary: Crisis Aid Network (CAN) unites students passionate about healthcare, service, and crisis response. Through outreach, volunteering, and education, CAN empowers compassionate leaders to support communities and make a real impact in times of need.

name: Impulse Performance Team
summary: Want a taste of idol life? Impulse Performance Team is the only dance group that teaches you how to balance singing and dancing at the same time on stage! Dancing only parts and singing only parts are always available for those who don't want to do both!

name: Behavioral and Social Data Science Society
description: We’re a professional and social student org at UT Austin for anyone interested in Behavioral & Social Data Science. We connect students with peers, faculty, and alumni, explore careers, host networking events, and offer advising. All majors welcome!
summary: We’re a professional and social student org at UT Austin for anyone interested in Behavioral & Social Data Science. We connect students with peers, faculty, and alumni, explore careers, host networking events, and offer advising. All majors welcome!

name: The Folklore Society
description: The Folklore Society at the University of Texas at Austin is a student-led organization for anyone passionate about folklore, cultural traditions, and expressive cultural practices. Members explore the stories, songs, customs, beliefs, and everyday traditions that shape communities both locally and around the world. Through events, discussions, workshops, storytelling sessions, and collaborative projects, the society provides a welcoming space to share and learn about diverse folk cultures, preserve traditions, and connect with others who appreciate the richness of cultural narratives. Participation offers opportunities to engage with themes such as oral histories, music, games, foodways, and folklore research while building community with fellow students across disciplines.
summary: The Folklore Society at the University of Texas at Austin is a student-led organization for anyone passionate about folklore, cultural traditions, and expressive cultural practices.

name: Students at the Well Austin
summary: Students at the Well Austin is committed to making Jesus known and exalted on UT campus. Our vision is to raise up disciples who reproduce disciples who impact the world for the glory of God. Exalt. Disciple. Send.

name: The National Security Collective
description: The National Security Collective will be an inclusive and academic space for anyone interested in discussing National Security from all backgrounds. This will take form in our meetings being an hour-long student-led roundtable discussion on any topic pertaining to National Security. These topics will be suggested and presented by members of any level in the organization and selected based upon their importance and academic diligence.
summary: A Collective of the most passionate students in National Security pursuing further public discussion. Building a space where every voice is heard, where any student from any status can propose and present a topic through their own academic diligence.

name: The Veneka Initiative
description: The Veneka Initiative is a student-led fundraising and advocacy organization dedicated to supporting Venezuelans. The organization’s purpose is to raise funds and raise awareness–by selling Venezuelan arepas– for the Saludos Connection nonprofit that provides humanitarian, legal, and social assistance to Venezuelans affected by political and economic challenges. In addition, The Veneka Initiative seeks to promote awareness and education on the conditions in Venezuela through participation in the organization. Through ethical fundraising, education, and collaboration, the organization aims to foster informed advocacy, civic responsibility, and meaningful impact within UT Austin.
summary: The Veneka Initiative is a fundraising and advocacy organization dedicated to supporting Venezuelans. The organization’s purpose is to raise funds and awareness by selling Venezuelan arepas and donating proceeds to the Saludos Connection nonprofit.
